target_output.title: GL Reconciliation Automation Tool Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python Celery automation tool that reconciles daily general ledger entries across core banking, card processing, and payment network batch files. The tool ingests ISO 20022 camt.053 XML statements and SWIFT MT940 files nightly, applies a configurable in-memory matching engine against tolerance thresholds, posts balanced journal entries for matched items, and routes unmatched exception items to operations team queues by exception type. Operations staff work exceptions via a Flask queue UI with Keycloak-authenticated access; exceptions above $10,000 require dual-approver sign-off enforced by a PostgreSQL database trigger. Each completed reconciliation run generates a SOX Section 404 compliant PDF attestation report stored to S3 for internal audit retrieval.
target_output.architecture_overview: Batch-driven architecture on ECS Fargate with three task types: ReconciliationBatch (Celery Beat scheduler triggering nightly ingestion, matching, and exception routing tasks), ExceptionQueueAPI (Flask WSGI app for queue UI and approval workflow), and a weekly ArchivalWorker for S3 Glacier transitions. Celery uses Redis as broker and result backend. All reconciliation data persists in RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with ledger_entries partitioned by reconciliation_date. Keycloak enforces MFA-backed RBAC with segregation of duties between operators and approvers. PostgreSQL row-level security makes the audit_log table append-only. PDF attestation reports generated by WeasyPrint per run and stored to S3 with 7-year retention for SOX compliance. Nightly batch has checkpoint monitoring with PagerDuty escalation at 05:30 UTC if completion is at risk.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5.x, Flask 3.x, SQLAlchemy 2.x with Alembic migrations. File parsing: lxml for ISO 20022 camt.053 XML, python-mt940 for SWIFT MT940. Report generation: WeasyPrint for PDF attestation reports. Database: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (us-east-1) with range partitioning on ledger_entries. Cache/broker: ElastiCache Redis 7. Storage: S3 for source files and PDF reports. Auth: Keycloak with MFA. CI/CD: Jenkins, ECR, CloudFormation. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty. Infrastructure: Terraform for all AWS resources.
target_output.functional_feature_map: IngestionEngine: parses ISO 20022 camt.053 and SWIFT MT940 files from S3, normalizes to LedgerEntry format, stores to partitioned PostgreSQL table, records SourceFileManifest with SHA-256 hash. MatchingEngine: in-memory hash-map matching on transaction_ref, then amount+value_date proximity fallback within per-source-system tolerance thresholds; posts matched journal pairs; marks exceptions. ExceptionRouter: classifies exceptions by root cause code, assigns to operations_queues by routing rules. ExceptionQueueAPI: Flask routes for queue browsing, exception claim, dual-approver sign-off (enforced by DB trigger), resolution posting. ReconciliationCloseTask: generates PDF attestation report via WeasyPrint, stores to S3, records key in reconciliation_runs. AuditLogger: append-only write to audit_log on every operator action and batch event.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate cluster (reconciliation-batch-worker tasks, exception-queue-api tasks, archival-worker tasks). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ private subnet (ledger_entries partitioned by month, reconciliation_runs, exception_items, source_file_manifests, operations_queues, tolerance_thresholds, approval_actions, audit_log). ElastiCache Redis 7 private subnet (Celery broker and result backend). S3 source-files bucket (ISO 20022 and SWIFT MT940 files, 90-day lifecycle to Glacier). S3 reconciliation-reports bucket (PDF attestation reports, 7-year retention). ALB (HTTPS, Keycloak-authenticated routing to exception-queue-api). Secrets Manager (DB credentials, Keycloak client secret, 30-day rotation). Keycloak (identity provider, MFA, RBAC). Datadog (APM, alerting). PagerDuty (on-call escalation).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Reconciliation Batch: Celery Beat triggers IngestionTask at 02:00 UTC. IngestionTask reads ISO 20022 and SWIFT MT940 files from S3 source-files bucket, parses each using lxml and python-mt940, writes LedgerEntry rows to ledger_entries partition for current reconciliation_date, records SourceFileManifest with SHA-256 file hash. MatchingTask runs at 03:15 UTC: loads all unmatched LedgerEntry rows for reconciliation_date into memory, builds hash map keyed by transaction_ref, matches core-banking entries to payment-network counterparts, applies amount+value_date proximity fallback for near-matches within tolerance_thresholds, writes matched pairs in single PostgreSQL transaction, sets match_status=exception for unmatched rows. ExceptionRoutingTask at 04:45 UTC: reads exception rows, classifies by exception_code (timing_difference, amount_mismatch, missing_counterparty, duplicate_detected), assigns to operations_queues. ReconciliationCloseTask at 05:15 UTC: queries reconciliation_runs, exception_items, approval_actions, and source_file_manifests, renders HTML attestation template, generates PDF via WeasyPrint, stores to S3 at reconciliation-reports/{date}/attestation_{run_id}.pdf, updates reconciliation_runs.attestation_report_s3_key. Exception Sign-off: Operator claims exception from queue (status=in_progress), reviews GL entry details, posts first approval. For exceptions above $10,000, a second distinct approver must post approval — PostgreSQL trigger on approval_actions INSERT rejects same-user second approval and validates amount threshold. On second approval, exception status transitions to resolved, correcting entry is queued for posting, AuditRecord written.
target_output.data_model: ledger_entries (id UUID PK, reconciliation_date DATE PARTITION KEY, source_system ENUM core_banking/card_processor/payment_network, transaction_ref VARCHAR, amount NUMERIC(18,4), currency CHAR(3), value_date DATE, counterparty_account VARCHAR, raw_file_id UUID FK, match_status ENUM unmatched/matched/exception, matched_pair_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). reconciliation_runs (id UUID PK, reconciliation_date DATE UNIQUE, status ENUM in_progress/matched/exceptions_pending/closed/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, matched_count INT, exception_count INT, attestation_report_s3_key VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). exception_items (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, ledger_entry_id UUID FK, exception_code ENUM timing_difference/amount_mismatch/missing_counterparty/duplicate_detected, amount NUMERIC(18,4), queue_id UUID FK, status ENUM open/in_progress/resolved/escalated, resolution_notes TEXT, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). source_file_manifests (id UUID PK, reconciliation_date DATE, file_name VARCHAR, file_format ENUM iso20022/swift_mt940/csv, s3_key VARCHAR, file_hash_sha256 CHAR(64), received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ingestion_status ENUM pending/ingested/failed). operations_queues (id UUID PK, queue_name VARCHAR, exception_codes JSONB, assigned_team VARCHAR, sla_hours INT). tolerance_thresholds (id UUID PK, source_system VARCHAR, exception_code VARCHAR, amount_tolerance NUMERIC(18,4), date_tolerance_days INT, updated_by VARCHAR, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). approval_actions (id UUID PK, exception_id UUID FK, approver_id VARCHAR, action ENUM approved/rejected, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, notes TEXT; DB trigger prevents same approver_id for second approval on same exception_id when amount > 10000). audit_log (id UUID PK, actor_id VARCHAR, action_type VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, details JSONB, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ; append-only via RLS).
target_output.api_design: Flask ExceptionQueueAPI endpoints (Keycloak Bearer token required): GET /api/v1/queues/{queue_id}/exceptions?status=open (list exception items for operator). PATCH /api/v1/exceptions/{id}/claim (operator claims item, sets status=in_progress). POST /api/v1/exceptions/{id}/approvals (submit approval action; body: {action, notes}; DB trigger enforces dual-approver). GET /api/v1/runs/{date}/attestation-report (audit-viewer: returns S3 presigned URL for PDF download). GET /api/v1/runs?from_date&to_date (list reconciliation runs with status and exception counts). GET /api/v1/admin/tolerance-thresholds (admin: view configured thresholds). PUT /api/v1/admin/tolerance-thresholds/{id} (admin: update threshold, writes AuditRecord). All endpoints log to audit_log on every write operation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak SSO with MFA enforced for all roles. RBAC: reconciliation-operator (queue browsing, claim, first approval only), reconciliation-approver (second approval for exceptions above $10k), reconciliation-admin (threshold configuration), audit-viewer (read-only reconciliation runs and report download). Dual-approver SOX control: PostgreSQL trigger on approval_actions table rejects INSERT where approver_id matches an existing approval record for the same exception_id and exception amount exceeds $10,000. Audit log append-only: PostgreSQL row-level security policy grants INSERT to audit_writer role, DENY UPDATE and DELETE to all roles including service account. AES-256 encryption at rest for RDS and S3. TLS 1.3 for all service-to-service and client-to-API communication. PDF attestation report content: reconciliation_date, source file names and SHA-256 hashes from source_file_manifests, total matched_count and matched_amount_sum, exception_count grouped by exception_code, table of all resolved exceptions (exception_id, amount, exception_code, approver_1_id, approver_1_timestamp, approver_2_id if applicable, approver_2_timestamp, resolution_notes), attestation statement block (reconciliation-admin username, attestation_timestamp, run_id, statement text: 'I attest that the above reconciliation run has been completed and reviewed in accordance with SOX Section 404 financial controls'). Report stored to S3 reconciliation-reports bucket with 7-year lifecycle policy. SOX Section 404 audit readiness: all exception items have immutable approval records; attestation PDF signed by admin identity; source file integrity verifiable via SHA-256 hashes in manifest.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1 private subnets. Task definitions: reconciliation-batch-worker (Celery worker + Beat, 4 vCPU / 8GB, auto-scaling 2-6 tasks), exception-queue-api (gunicorn Flask, 1 vCPU / 2GB, ALB target group, 2 tasks minimum), archival-worker (scheduled task weekly, 1 vCPU / 2GB). Jenkins CI/CD: flake8 + mypy, pytest (90% coverage gate), Docker build, ECR push, CloudFormation change set deploy. Alembic migration applied as ECS one-off run-task before new app version goes live. Rollback: previous ECR image redeploy via CloudFormation stack rollback. Environments: dev (t4g.medium RDS single-AZ, local Redis via Docker Compose), staging (Multi-AZ RDS, production-scale data seed, performance test confirming batch completes within 3.5 hours for end-of-month volume), production (RDS Multi-AZ, auto-scaling). End-of-month procedure: pre-scale batch workers to 6 tasks at 01:00 UTC on last day of month; monitor all checkpoint alarms manually. Secrets Manager rotation: DB credentials and Keycloak client secret rotated every 30 days automatically.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: Celery task traces for IngestionTask, MatchingTask, ExceptionRoutingTask, ReconciliationCloseTask — track task duration, retry count, and outcome. Custom Datadog metrics: reconciliation_matched_count (gauge), reconciliation_exception_count (gauge by exception_code), exception_queue_depth (gauge by queue_name), batch_checkpoint_lag_minutes (gauge per checkpoint). Datadog dashboard: daily reconciliation run timeline (matched vs exception counts), exception queue depth by team, batch checkpoint status for current run. PagerDuty alerts: IngestionTask failure (immediate), MatchingTask failure (immediate), batch not completed by 05:30 UTC (immediate, P1), exception_queue_depth > 100 for 2+ hours (P2). Structured JSON logs from all Celery tasks and Flask API with trace_id, run_id, and actor_id for correlation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.r6g.large: ~$350/month. ElastiCache Redis cache.r6g.large: ~$150/month. ECS Fargate batch workers (2 tasks avg, 4 vCPU/8GB): ~$180/month. ECS Fargate API tasks (2 tasks, 1 vCPU/2GB): ~$40/month. S3 (source files + reports + Glacier archival): ~$30/month. Secrets Manager, CloudWatch, Datadog: ~$80/month. Total estimated: ~$830/month. End-of-month scale-out (6 workers for 48 hours) adds ~$90/month amortized.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): IngestionEngine for ISO 20022 camt.053 files, MatchingEngine with static tolerance thresholds, exception_items table and ExceptionQueueAPI with single-approver workflow, Keycloak authentication, RDS and Redis on ECS Fargate, Jenkins CI, Datadog APM, PagerDuty checkpoint alerts. Phase 2 (4 weeks): SWIFT MT940 file ingestion, dual-approver constraint via PostgreSQL trigger, SOX-compliant PDF attestation report generation via WeasyPrint, audit_log append-only RLS, tolerance threshold admin UI, S3 archival workflow. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Configurable exception routing rules in tolerance_thresholds, end-of-month scaling procedures, audit-viewer role and report download API, correcting GL entry posting integration, staging performance test suite.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict type annotations with mypy. All Celery tasks idempotent: re-running a task for an already-processed reconciliation_date must be a no-op (check for existing reconciliation_run record before starting). Alembic migration scripts must include down-migration rollback logic for all schema changes to ledger_entries, reconciliation_runs, and exception_items. pytest with 90% coverage gate; financial calculation unit tests use fixture ledger entry datasets covering edge cases (exactly at tolerance threshold, exactly at $10,000 dual-approver boundary). Flask routes must not perform database writes directly — all writes go through service layer to ensure AuditLogger is always called. WeasyPrint PDF generation tested against a golden PDF fixture in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: In-memory MatchingEngine loads all ledger_entries for reconciliation_date into memory — for end-of-month volumes with 3x normal entry count, memory usage could exceed Fargate task limit (8GB). Mitigation: benchmark at 3x volume in staging; if memory is exceeded, switch to batched in-memory matching processing 500,000 entries at a time. Risk: RDS failover during MatchingTask aborts the in-progress transaction; Celery retry logic reschedules the task but adds 30+ minutes to batch completion, risking the 06:00 UTC deadline. Mitigation: enhanced monitoring on RDS failover events with immediate PagerDuty alert and manual intervention runbook for out-of-hours recovery. Tradeoff: Celery Beat single scheduler is a single point of failure for batch triggering; mitigate with S3 event notification as secondary trigger for IngestionTask if Beat fails to fire within 15 minutes of scheduled time.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed WeasyPrint over ReportLab for PDF attestation report generation due to HTML/CSS template approach enabling easier format updates without Python PDF layout code. Confirmed RDS range partitioning on reconciliation_date over table-per-month approach to keep Alembic migrations simpler. Confirmed Celery Beat as primary scheduler with S3 event secondary trigger rather than EventBridge Scheduler to avoid adding another AWS service dependency.

metadata.notes: Round 4 GL reconciliation tool. Resolves ISS-003 SOX attestation report PDF format. ISS-001 matching performance, ISS-002 dual-approver DB trigger, ISS-004 end-of-month scaling resolved in prior rounds.

profile.domain: Commercial Loan Underwriting Desktop Tool

project_goal.value: Local-first desktop application for commercial loan underwriters consolidating financial spreading, covenant tracking, and risk rating with offline capability and nightly sync to core lending system
project_goal.rationale: Underwriters work offline in client meetings; central sync required for loan committee review

target_users.value: Commercial loan underwriters at the bank, typically 150 users across 12 regional offices
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in workflow discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Offline-first requirement mandates native desktop; web app cannot guarantee offline document access

capabilities.rationale: Full local processing stack with auth and central system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync conflict resolution plus complex financial calculation engine

risk_level.rationale: Financial data for live loan decisions; data integrity failures create regulatory and credit risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Borrower tax returns, audited financials, and credit memos are non-public financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Azure AD authentication required; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO with MSAL; RBAC roles: underwriter, senior_underwriter, credit_officer, admin
access_model.rationale: Bank SSO mandate; role controls editing vs approval rights on loan files

feature_scope.value: Financial spreading from tax returns and audited statements, DSCR/leverage/liquidity ratio calculation, covenant schedule entry and breach alerting, risk rating scorecard, credit memo generation, nightly sync to core lending platform
feature_scope.rationale: Full underwriting workflow from document intake to credit memo submission

mvp_scope.value: Financial spreading with DSCR calculation, basic risk rating scorecard, credit memo draft, and nightly sync to central PostgreSQL
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers core underwriting workflow for Q2 pilot with 20 users in Chicago office

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption for SQLite at rest via SQLCipher, TLS 1.3 for sync API, MSAL token storage in Windows Credential Manager, no plain-text PII on disk
security_baseline.rationale: OCC and SOX minimum controls for non-public financial data on endpoint devices

frontend_stack.value: Electron 30 with React 18 and TypeScript; Tailwind CSS for UI components
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in React; Electron provides cross-platform desktop with Chromium renderer

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express for local IPC server; sync adapter in Node.js calling central REST API
backend_stack.rationale: Single language stack (TypeScript) across renderer and main process simplifies development

data_platform.value: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher for local store; PostgreSQL 16 on Azure for central lending database
data_platform.rationale: SQLite handles offline-first with zero network dependency; PostgreSQL for multi-user central record

hosting_target.value: Self-hosted on bank-managed Windows 10/11 workstations; auto-update via Electron-updater from Azure Blob Storage
hosting_target.rationale: Bank policy prohibits cloud-hosted desktop app; updates through internal CDN

privacy_retention_policy.value: Loan file data retained 7 years per OCC guidance; local SQLite DB purged after successful central sync + 90-day grace; audit logs retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OCC lending record retention requirements and SOX audit trail obligations

future_scope.value: Automated OCR extraction from scanned financial statements, integration with credit bureau APIs for automated spreading pre-fill, mobile companion for read-only review
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap pending MVP success

constraints.value: Must run offline on Windows 10/11; no internet dependency during underwriting sessions; auto-update must not interrupt active underwriting sessions
constraints.rationale: Underwriters work in client offices with restricted internet; update interruption causes workflow loss

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting; local app log file rotated daily; sync job results posted to Datadog from central API side
observability_baseline.rationale: No real-time monitoring on client devices; crash telemetry via Sentry is the pragmatic baseline

execution_preference.value: 4-week sprints; desktop app released bi-weekly via internal auto-updater channel
execution_preference.rationale: Longer sprint for desktop testing cycle; staged rollout via updater channels

compliance_context.value: OCC lending guidance for commercial credit; Regulation B ECOA adverse action documentation; SOX Section 302/404 for financial data integrity controls
compliance_context.rationale: Commercial lending data and adverse action documentation trigger OCC, ECOA, and SOX obligations

project.goal: Offline-capable desktop underwriting workstation with nightly sync to central lending system
project.target_users: 150 commercial underwriters across 12 regional offices
project.timeline: MVP in Q2, full rollout Q4

frontend.state: Zustand for local state; React Query for sync status

backend.framework: Express for IPC server
backend.api: REST to central PostgreSQL sync API on Azure

security.auth: MSAL with Azure AD; tokens in Windows Credential Manager
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 at rest; TLS 1.3 for sync

data.sync: Timestamp-based optimistic sync with conflict detection

devops.ci: GitHub Actions build + code-sign + publish to Azure Blob
devops.hosting: Self-hosted Windows workstations; electron-updater staged channels

constraints.update: No forced restart during active loan file session

open_questions.conflict_strategy: Last-write-wins vs three-way merge for concurrent edits on same loan file

confirmed_decisions.spreading_engine: Custom TypeScript calculation engine; no third-party spreading library
confirmed_decisions.code_signing: EV certificate from DigiCert for Windows SmartScreen trust

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first mandate is the dominant product constraint and correctly drives the SQLite local store decision. The MVP scope covering spreading + risk rating + credit memo maps directly to the core underwriting workflow. Covenant tracking deferred to Phase 2 is acceptable for the pilot cohort.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SQLite with SQLCipher is the right local store choice. The main IPC architecture via Electron main process prevents renderer-process crashes from corrupting the database connection. The sync conflict detection strategy must be resolved before MVP; last-write-wins is inadequate when two underwriters edit the same borrower record from different offices.
reasoner_reviews.security: MSAL token storage in Windows Credential Manager is the correct approach; avoid localStorage in the renderer. SQLCipher key derivation must use PBKDF2 with a per-device salt stored in the OS keychain, not a static application secret. The prior plan lacked a clear key rotation procedure for SQLCipher.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 7-year retention is legally mandated. The 90-day local grace period post-sync is pragmatic but must be enforced by the app, not left to the user. Auto-update channel must gate production rollout behind the beta channel with a 5-day soak period.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan did not address the sync conflict scenario for concurrent loan file edits. This is a real operational risk when a senior underwriter and an analyst are both working on the same file from different devices. The fix_report must address this.

backend.service_design: Electron main process hosts an Express IPC server on localhost:4719 (random port bound at startup, communicated to renderer via contextBridge). Modules: spreading_engine (financial ratio calculations), sync_adapter (nightly push to central API), loan_file_manager (CRUD on SQLite), auth_manager (MSAL token lifecycle).
backend.api_patterns: IPC over contextBridge for renderer-to-main; REST POST /sync/push and GET /sync/pull from main to central Azure API; sync uses If-Modified-Since headers and ETag for conflict detection.
backend.failure_handling: SQLite WAL mode prevents corruption on crash. Sync failures are logged locally and retried on next scheduled run. MSAL token refresh failure triggers re-auth prompt without losing the active document state.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly sync scheduled via node-cron at 02:00 local time; sync job acquires a file lock to prevent concurrent runs; sync result (success/failure/conflicts) written to sync_log table.

frontend.app_structure: Electron renderer SPA with React Router v6 for section navigation: Dashboard, Loan Files, Spreading, Risk Rating, Covenants, Credit Memo, Sync Status.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard shows active loan files and sync status. Spreading view displays side-by-side financial statement years with auto-calculated ratios. Risk Rating scorecard with weighted factor sliders. Credit Memo editor with template sections and attachment list.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active loan file context and user preferences. React Query for IPC calls to main process API with optimistic updates on local mutations. Sync status atom updated by main-process push events via ipcRenderer.
frontend.performance_notes: Lazy-load spreading calculation worker in a Web Worker to prevent UI thread blocking on large financial datasets. Virtual list for loan file history tables with >500 rows.

security.auth_design: MSAL PublicClientApplication with system browser redirect for initial login. Token cached in Windows Credential Manager via MSAL node extension. Silent token refresh in main process every 45 minutes.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher encryption key derived from user PIN + device ID via PBKDF2 (310,000 iterations, SHA-256). Key stored in OS keychain (Windows Credential Manager), never written to disk in plaintext.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every loan file open, save, and export event written to audit_log table with user_id, timestamp, action, and loan_file_id. Audit log shipped to central PostgreSQL during nightly sync. Read-only access to audit_log for non-admin roles.

data.entities: loan_files (id, borrower_name, loan_number, assigned_underwriter_id, status, created_at, updated_at, synced_at). spreading_worksheets (id, loan_file_id, fiscal_year, statement_type ENUM tax_return/audited/internal, line_items_json, dscr, leverage_ratio, liquidity_ratio). risk_ratings (id, loan_file_id, scorecard_version, factor_scores_json, composite_score, rated_by, rated_at). covenant_schedules (id, loan_file_id, covenant_type, threshold, measurement_frequency, last_measured_at, breach_flag). sync_checkpoints (id, loan_file_id, last_synced_at, central_etag, conflict_flag).
data.storage_design: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher for all local data. WAL mode enabled. Separate schema migration table managed by custom migrator run at app startup. Central PostgreSQL 16 on Azure holds the authoritative record after sync.
data.retention_and_deletion: Local SQLite records soft-deleted 90 days after successful sync to central. Hard delete runs as nightly maintenance job. Central PostgreSQL enforces 7-year retention via pg_partman range partitioning on created_at with archive-to-Azure-Blob after Year 3.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds signed NSIS installer for Windows. Artifacts published to Azure Blob Storage in versioned channel folders (beta/, production/). electron-updater polls channel feed every 6 hours. Production channel updated only after 5-day beta soak with no critical Sentry crashes.
devops.ci_cd_design: Pipeline stages: lint (ESLint + tsc --noEmit), test (Jest unit + integration against in-memory SQLite), build (electron-builder), code-sign (EV cert via DigiCert Keylocker), publish to Blob. PR gate: all stages must pass. Release: tagged commits only.
devops.observability_stack: Sentry Electron SDK for crash reporting with source maps. Sync job results written to structured JSON log file and shipped to Datadog via Datadog Agent on the central server side. Local app log at %APPDATA%/UnderwritingTool/logs/ rotated daily, retained 30 days.
devops.rollback_strategy: electron-updater supports rollback to previous channel version via admin CLI flag. Rollback triggered by Sentry alert threshold: >5% crash rate in 1 hour on new version.

ISS-001.title: Sync conflict resolution strategy undefined
ISS-001.detail: The previous plan specified timestamp-based optimistic sync but did not define behavior when two underwriters edit the same loan_file record from different devices between sync windows. Last-write-wins will silently overwrite work.

history.note: Identified during architect review; no resolution in plan v1

ISS-002.title: SQLCipher key derivation uses static app secret
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan referenced a static application-level encryption key for SQLCipher. Security review flagged this as a critical control failure; key must be per-device and user-derived.

history.note: Flagged by security specialist
Resolved: PBKDF2 per-device key derivation with OS keychain storage now documented in security plan

focus_issues.title: Sync conflict resolution strategy undefined
focus_issues.detail: The previous plan specified timestamp-based optimistic sync but did not define behavior when two underwriters edit the same loan_file record from different devices between sync windows.

latest_recommendations: Define a concrete conflict resolution protocol for concurrent loan file edits; recommend three-way merge with conflict notification to both underwriters
Document SQLCipher key rotation procedure triggered on user password change

previous_plan.title: Commercial Loan Underwriting Desktop Application - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: An offline-first Electron desktop application for commercial loan underwriters that handles financial spreading, risk rating, and credit memo drafting with SQLite local storage and nightly sync to the central Azure PostgreSQL lending database.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron 30 with React 18 renderer communicating to a Node.js Express IPC server in the main process. SQLite with SQLCipher for local encrypted storage. Nightly sync job pushes completed loan files to central PostgreSQL on Azure via REST API authenticated with MSAL tokens.
previous_plan.data_model: loan_files, spreading_worksheets, risk_ratings, sync_log. SQLCipher encryption applied at database level with application-level key.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: MSAL Azure AD auth; SQLCipher AES-256 with static application secret (flagged); TLS 1.3 for sync API; OCC and SOX compliance noted.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision of a commercial loan underwriting desktop app. The critical unresolved issue is the sync conflict resolution strategy for concurrent edits by multiple underwriters. I need to define a concrete three-way merge approach with conflict notification. ISS-002 (SQLCipher key) was resolved in round 1 and I must confirm that fix is carried forward. The architecture is otherwise sound: Electron + React + SQLite local, nightly sync to Azure PostgreSQL. I must ensure the plan is domain-specific throughout with financial spreading, covenant tracking, and credit memo workflows.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit three-way merge conflict resolution protocol to the sync_adapter design. When a push detects the central ETag has changed since the local sync_checkpoint, the adapter fetches the central version and performs a field-level three-way merge using the common ancestor stored in sync_checkpoints.ancestor_snapshot_json. Unresolvable conflicts (same field edited on both sides) surface a ConflictResolutionDialog to the underwriter showing both versions side by side with field-level diff highlighting. The winning version is chosen by the user and logged to audit_log with conflict_resolved action. Affected sections: workflows, data_model, system_components, security_and_compliance.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent edits on the same loan_file by different underwriters will surface a clear conflict resolution UI rather than silently overwriting work, eliminating the data integrity risk identified in ISS-001.

target_output.title: Commercial Loan Underwriting Desktop Application - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first Electron 30 desktop application for 150 commercial loan underwriters that consolidates financial spreading (DSCR, leverage, liquidity ratio calculation from tax returns and audited statements), covenant tracking, risk rating scorecards, and credit memo authoring in a single encrypted local-first tool. Nightly sync to the bank's central Azure PostgreSQL lending database uses three-way merge conflict detection to safely handle concurrent edits across regional offices. Authentication via Azure AD MSAL with SQLCipher AES-256 encryption keyed per-device via PBKDF2 in the Windows OS keychain satisfies OCC, ECOA, and SOX financial data controls.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 30 main process hosts a Node.js 20 Express IPC server on a randomly assigned localhost port (communicated to the React 18 renderer via contextBridge at startup). The renderer is a TypeScript SPA handling the underwriting workflow UI. All financial data persists to a SQLite 3 database encrypted with SQLCipher using a PBKDF2-derived per-device key stored in Windows Credential Manager. A nightly sync adapter (node-cron, 02:00 local) pushes completed loan files to the central PostgreSQL 16 instance on Azure via REST using If-Modified-Since and ETag headers. Conflict detection compares local sync_checkpoints.central_etag against the server response; divergent ETags trigger a three-way merge using the stored ancestor snapshot. Azure AD MSAL handles authentication via system browser redirect with silent token refresh every 45 minutes.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop shell: Electron 30 (Chromium renderer + Node.js 20 main). UI: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, React Router v6, Zustand, React Query. IPC server: Express 4 in main process. Local DB: SQLite 3 + SQLCipher (AES-256). Central DB: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure. Auth: MSAL node extension + Azure AD. Scheduling: node-cron. Build/distribution: electron-builder, NSIS installer, EV code-signing via DigiCert. Observability: Sentry Electron SDK, Datadog Agent (server side). CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Financial Spreading module imports borrower tax returns (1040/1065/1120) and audited financial statements, maps line items to standardized templates, and auto-calculates DSCR, leverage ratio, and current liquidity ratio across up to 5 fiscal years. Risk Rating Scorecard presents weighted factor inputs (management quality, industry risk, collateral coverage, cash flow consistency) and computes a composite risk grade (1-9 scale). Covenant Schedule Tracker enters and monitors financial covenants with configurable thresholds and measurement frequencies, flagging breaches for underwriter review. Credit Memo Editor assembles narrative sections, financial summary tables, and risk grade into a structured document exportable as PDF. Sync Status Panel shows last sync time, conflict log, and pending upload queue.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (Node.js 20 runtime, Express IPC server, MSAL auth manager, node-cron sync scheduler, SQLite/SQLCipher connection pool). Electron renderer process (React 18 SPA, Zustand state, React Query IPC client). SQLite 3 + SQLCipher local encrypted database (WAL mode, schema migrator at startup). Sync Adapter (conflict detector using ETag comparison + three-way merge engine referencing ancestor_snapshot_json). Azure PostgreSQL 16 central lending database (authoritative record post-sync). Azure Blob Storage (installer artifacts and electron-updater channel feeds). Sentry Electron SDK (crash telemetry with source maps).
target_output.workflows: Financial Spreading Workflow: Underwriter opens a loan_file, navigates to Spreading, selects statement type (tax_return/audited/internal) and fiscal year. Uploads PDF or enters figures manually into standardized line-item template. Spreading engine calculates DSCR = Net Operating Income / Total Debt Service, leverage = Total Debt / EBITDA, and current liquidity ratio in real time. Calculated ratios are saved to spreading_worksheets with the underwriter's ID and timestamp. Nightly Sync with Conflict Resolution: At 02:00 local time the sync adapter acquires a file lock, iterates loan_files with synced_at < updated_at, and POSTs each to the central REST API. Server returns ETag. If the ETag differs from sync_checkpoints.central_etag, the adapter fetches the central version, performs field-level three-way merge using ancestor_snapshot_json, and if unresolvable conflicts exist, writes a conflict_record to the local conflict_queue table. On next app open, the underwriter is presented with a ConflictResolutionDialog showing both versions field-by-field; chosen resolution is posted to the server and logged to audit_log.
target_output.data_model: loan_files (id UUID PK, borrower_name, loan_number VARCHAR, assigned_underwriter_id, status ENUM draft/in_review/submitted/approved/rejected, created_at, updated_at, synced_at). spreading_worksheets (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, fiscal_year INT, statement_type ENUM tax_return/audited/internal, line_items_json TEXT, dscr REAL, leverage_ratio REAL, liquidity_ratio REAL, created_by, created_at). risk_ratings (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, scorecard_version VARCHAR, factor_scores_json TEXT, composite_score REAL, risk_grade VARCHAR, rated_by, rated_at). covenant_schedules (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, covenant_type VARCHAR, threshold REAL, measurement_frequency ENUM monthly/quarterly/annual, last_measured_at, breach_flag BOOLEAN). sync_checkpoints (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, last_synced_at, central_etag VARCHAR, ancestor_snapshot_json TEXT, conflict_flag BOOLEAN). audit_log (id UUID PK, user_id, action ENUM open/save/export/sync/conflict_resolved, loan_file_id, detail_json, timestamp). Loan financial entities: financial_statements (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, statement_year INT, statement_type ENUM income/balance_sheet/cash_flow, total_revenue_cents BIGINT, net_income_cents BIGINT, total_assets_cents BIGINT, total_liabilities_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3)), credit_decisions (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, recommended_loan_amount_cents BIGINT, approved_amount_cents BIGINT, interest_rate_bps INT, dscr NUMERIC, ltv_ratio NUMERIC, credit_score INT, decision ENUM approved/declined/referred, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ, decided_by_underwriter_id UUID), payment_schedules (id UUID PK, loan_file_id FK, payment_number INT, due_date DATE, principal_cents BIGINT, interest_cents BIGINT, total_payment_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3)).
target_output.api_design: Central sync REST API (Azure-hosted): POST /sync/v1/loan-files/:id (push local record, returns ETag and conflict status). GET /sync/v1/loan-files/:id (fetch central version for merge). GET /sync/v1/loan-files/:id/ancestor (fetch ancestor snapshot for three-way merge). POST /sync/v1/audit-log/batch (ship audit_log records). GET /sync/v1/loan-files?underwriter_id=X&modified_since=T (pull remote updates). IPC API (localhost Express): POST /api/loan-files, GET /api/loan-files/:id, PUT /api/loan-files/:id, POST /api/spreading/:loanId, GET /api/risk-rating/:loanId, GET /api/sync/status.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 encryption on SQLite database. Encryption key derived via PBKDF2 (310,000 iterations, SHA-256, per-device salt) from user PIN + device fingerprint; key stored in Windows Credential Manager via MSAL node extension keychain API. Static application secret removed (ISS-002 resolved). MSAL PublicClientApplication with Azure AD; tokens cached in Windows Credential Manager. TLS 1.3 enforced for all sync API calls. Audit log records every loan_file access, save, export, and conflict resolution event; shipped to central PostgreSQL nightly. RBAC enforced in main process: underwriter role cannot approve their own credit memos. ECOA adverse action documentation: loan_files.rejection_reason_json captured with required Regulation B factors. SOX controls: audit_log is append-only in SQLite (enforced via trigger); central PostgreSQL audit records immutable after 24-hour window.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: ESLint + tsc --noEmit (lint), Jest unit tests against in-memory SQLite (unit), electron-builder Windows NSIS package (build), DigiCert EV code signing via Keylocker action (sign), publish to Azure Blob Storage versioned channel (publish). Channel strategy: beta/ channel updated on every tagged release. production/ channel promoted after 5-day beta soak with no P0/P1 Sentry events and manual sign-off from QA lead. electron-updater polls channel feed every 6 hours; update download is backgrounded and applied on next app restart (never forced during active loan file session). Environments: dev (local SQLite, mock sync API), staging (central staging PostgreSQL on Azure), production (central production PostgreSQL, DigiCert-signed installer).
target_output.observability: Sentry Electron SDK captures unhandled exceptions and renderer crashes with source maps uploaded during CI. Release tracking in Sentry correlates crash rate to app version to gate production channel promotion. Local structured JSON log at %APPDATA%/UnderwritingTool/logs/app-YYYY-MM-DD.log for sync job results, IPC errors, and conflict events; rotated daily, 30-day retention. Central Datadog dashboard (server-side): sync job duration, records pushed per run, conflict rate per underwriter, audit log ingestion rate. Key alerts: sync failure rate >10% over 3 consecutive nights (PagerDuty to DevOps), Sentry crash rate >5% on new version (triggers rollback consideration).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud hosting cost for the desktop app itself. Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (4 vCores, 16 GB) ~$280/month handles 150 concurrent sync clients with connection pooling via PgBouncer. Azure Blob Storage for installer artifacts and updater feeds: <$10/month. Sentry Team plan: ~$26/month for 150 users. Total central infrastructure: ~$320/month. Scaling: as user count grows beyond 300, add a read replica for reporting queries and upgrade PostgreSQL to 8 vCores; SQLite local stores scale linearly with no shared resource contention.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Electron shell, SQLite + SQLCipher with PBKDF2 key derivation, financial spreading module (tax return and audited statement templates), DSCR/leverage/liquidity ratio engine, basic risk rating scorecard, credit memo PDF export, MSAL Azure AD auth, nightly sync to central PostgreSQL with three-way merge conflict detection, GitHub Actions CI/CD with EV code signing, Sentry crash reporting. Pilot with 20 Chicago underwriters. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Covenant schedule tracker with breach alerting, covenant_schedules table, email notification on breach via central API. Sync conflict resolution UI (ConflictResolutionDialog) GA release to all 150 users. Phase 3 (8 weeks): OCR extraction from scanned financial statements using Azure Document Intelligence, auto-pre-fill of spreading line items from extracted data, admin dashboard for sync health and user activity on central server.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in both main and renderer. No access to Node.js APIs from renderer process; all privileged operations via contextBridge IPC. ESLint with Electron-specific ruleset (no remote module, no node integration in renderer). Jest for unit tests of spreading calculation engine and sync adapter logic; target 85% coverage on financial calculation functions. Playwright for Electron for E2E tests covering spreading entry, sync, and conflict resolution flows. ADR documents required for spreading template schema changes (affects all existing worksheets). SQLite schema migrations managed by custom versioned migrator; never ALTER TABLE in place.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Three-way merge complexity for deeply nested line_items_json. Mitigation: Store line_items_json as an array of keyed objects (field_key, value) to enable field-level diffing; conflict granularity is per field_key not per JSON blob. Risk: SQLCipher PBKDF2 derivation adds ~500ms to app startup on cold open. Mitigation: Cache derived key in memory for session lifetime; re-derive only on lock/unlock. Tradeoff: Electron bundle size (~200 MB) vs web app; accepted because offline-first is non-negotiable for client-site underwriting. Risk: electron-updater fails on restricted networks. Mitigation: Manual installer download link in admin portal as fallback. Risk: 7-year central retention at PostgreSQL scale. Mitigation: pg_partman range partitioning on created_at; archive partitions older than 3 years to Azure Blob as Parquet.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Three-way merge conflict resolution adopted over last-write-wins; ancestor snapshot stored in sync_checkpoints for merge base. PBKDF2 310,000 iterations chosen to meet OWASP 2024 recommendation while keeping cold-start latency under 600ms on minimum-spec bank workstations. electron-updater staging channel with 5-day soak period selected over manual IT deployment for faster patch delivery without compromising stability.

metadata.notes: Round 2 desktop app for commercial loan underwriting; resolves sync conflict strategy (ISS-001) with three-way merge; financial domain throughout.

profile.domain: Consumer Credit Delinquency Prediction ML Research Prototype

project_goal.value: ML-driven early delinquency prediction prototype scoring consumer credit card accounts weekly using payment behavior, utilization trends, and macroeconomic signals to enable proactive outreach before charge-off
project_goal.rationale: Collections strategy team needs 4-6 week lead time before charge-off to run effective outreach programs

target_users.value: Collections strategy analysts, model risk team, and credit risk officers at the bank
target_users.rationale: Internal model governance and operational users

project_class.rationale: Exploratory ML prototype; not production-hardened for real-time decisioning in this phase

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs, frontend
capabilities.rationale: ML pipeline, feature engineering, model serving, analytics dashboard, and batch scoring

complexity_level.rationale: Feature engineering across multiple data sources, model versioning, drift detection, and governance workflow

risk_level.rationale: SR 11-7 model risk; ECOA fair lending exposure; FCRA if credit bureau data used

data_sensitivity.rationale: Credit card account behavioral data is non-public personal financial information

external_exposure.rationale: Internal model governance tool; no external access

access_model.value: Okta SSO; RBAC roles: analyst, model_owner, model_risk_reviewer, admin
access_model.rationale: Bank SSO mandate; model_risk_reviewer role required for SR 11-7 model approval workflow

feature_scope.value: Weekly batch scoring pipeline for 2M active credit card accounts, gradient boosting model on payment behavior and utilization features, macroeconomic signal integration, model performance dashboard with PSI and Gini tracking, model governance workflow for SR 11-7 approval
feature_scope.rationale: Full prototype scope from feature pipeline to governance sign-off

mvp_scope.value: Weekly batch scoring on internal behavioral features only (no credit bureau), gradient boosting model, Streamlit dashboard for analyst review, MLflow model registry
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimizes FCRA compliance complexity for initial prototype; credit bureau integration deferred

security_baseline.value: Snowflake row-level security for account data; Okta JWT on FastAPI endpoints; no raw PAN or SSN in feature store; TLS 1.3 in transit
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data minimum controls; PAN/SSN excluded from ML features per data governance policy

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit 1.35 for analyst-facing model performance dashboard and governance workflow UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Rapid prototyping for internal analytics; data science team proficient in Streamlit

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for model serving and governance API; scikit-learn + XGBoost for model training
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for ML; FastAPI for async model scoring endpoint

data_platform.value: Snowflake for feature store and scoring output; MLflow on ECS Fargate for experiment tracking and model registry
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake centralizes all credit data; MLflow is standard for model lifecycle management

hosting_target.value: SageMaker for batch scoring jobs; ECS Fargate for FastAPI serving and MLflow; Streamlit on ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: SageMaker integrates natively with Snowflake feature store; Fargate for stateless services

privacy_retention_policy.value: Model scores retained 2 years in Snowflake for backtesting; raw account feature snapshots retained 13 weeks rolling; audit log for model decisions retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 2-year lookback needed for model validation; 5-year audit trail for ECOA adverse action traceability

future_scope.value: Real-time scoring via Kinesis stream, credit bureau integration for expanded feature set, automated champion-challenger A/B framework
future_scope.rationale: Production deployment roadmap post-prototype validation

constraints.value: No PAN, SSN, or full account number in feature tables; prototype must pass model risk SR 11-7 review before any production integration; Snowflake compute budget under $2000/month
constraints.rationale: Data governance and regulatory approval gating for any consumer credit ML model

observability_baseline.value: MLflow experiment tracking for all training runs; Grafana + Prometheus for Fargate service metrics; weekly automated model performance report posted to Slack channel
observability_baseline.rationale: SR 11-7 requires documented model performance monitoring; Grafana for infra health

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; model retraining cycle monthly; governance review milestones at week 8 and week 16
execution_preference.rationale: Aligns model iteration cadence with collections strategy cycle and model risk review calendar

compliance_context.value: SR 11-7 model risk management; ECOA/Regulation B fair lending model bias testing; FCRA if credit bureau data added; SOX for financial impact documentation
compliance_context.rationale: Consumer credit ML models trigger federal model risk and fair lending obligations

llm_integration.value: LLM used for automated plain-language model explanation generation: given a scored account and top SHAP features, GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI generates a human-readable risk narrative for collections analyst review (not for adverse action letters)
llm_integration.rationale: Analysts lack ML expertise to interpret SHAP values directly; LLM narrative bridges the gap without replacing model governance controls

project.goal: Weekly batch delinquency scoring prototype with SR 11-7-compliant governance
project.target_users: Collections analysts and model risk team
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks, governance review at week 16

frontend.approach: Multi-page app: model performance dashboard, governance workflow, analyst review queue

backend.framework: FastAPI for model serving and governance API

security.auth: Okta JWT on all FastAPI endpoints
security.data: Snowflake row-level security, no PAN/SSN in features

data.db: Snowflake for feature store and scoring output

devops.hosting: SageMaker for batch jobs, ECS Fargate for services

constraints.governance: SR 11-7 sign-off required before production integration

open_questions.bias_testing: Which fairness metrics to use for ECOA: demographic parity vs equalized odds

confirmed_decisions.llm_provider: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o for narrative generation

reasoner_reviews.product: The prototype scope is appropriate for SR 11-7 initial model development. The weekly scoring cadence aligns with the collections strategy team's outreach planning cycle. Deferring credit bureau integration removes FCRA complexity for the MVP, which is the right tradeoff. The LLM narrative generation for analyst review is a useful augmentation but must be clearly labeled as non-decisional to avoid ECOA complications.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SageMaker Processing Jobs for the batch scoring pipeline is the right choice given Snowflake integration via the Snowflake Connector for Spark. The MLflow model registry on ECS Fargate creates an unnecessary operational burden; consider Databricks MLflow or SageMaker Model Registry instead. The previous plan's SHAP explanation pipeline was not integrated into the governance workflow - model risk reviewers need SHAP summary plots as part of the approval package.
reasoner_reviews.security: Snowflake row-level security must be configured to restrict analyst access to aggregated model_scores only, not raw account feature snapshots with payment history. The Okta JWT validation on FastAPI must include audience claim verification to prevent token reuse across internal services. The LLM narrative generation must not include raw account identifiers in the prompt sent to Azure OpenAI - only SHAP feature names and values.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Snowflake compute cost is the primary constraint. Weekly batch scoring of 2M accounts with feature engineering could consume 300-500 Snowflake credits per run depending on warehouse size. The plan should specify warehouse sizing and auto-suspend policy. $2000/month budget is achievable with an X-Small warehouse and aggressive auto-suspend.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 plan was intentionally flawed: it used the wrong project_class in the contract (listing 'fullstack_app' instead of 'research_prototype') and omitted the llm_integration contract field despite ai_llm being in capabilities. Round 2 must correct both violations and ensure all plan sections reflect the research_prototype classification rather than production app architecture patterns.

backend.service_design: Two Python 3.12 FastAPI services: scoring_api (POST /v1/score with account_ids list for on-demand scoring by analysts) and governance_api (model approval workflow CRUD, SHAP report retrieval). Separate SageMaker Processing Job for weekly batch pipeline: feature_extraction.py -> model_inference.py -> output_writer.py.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/models/{model_id}/score-batch (trigger manual batch), GET /v1/models/{model_id}/performance (PSI, Gini, KS over rolling windows), POST /v1/models/{model_id}/governance/approve, GET /v1/explanations/{account_id} (SHAP values + LLM narrative).
backend.llm_integration_design: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o called from explanation_service.py after SHAP values computed. Prompt template: system='You are a credit risk analyst explaining model predictions. Do not reference individual account IDs.' user='This account scored {score} for 30-day delinquency risk. Top contributing factors: {shap_features_json}. Write a 3-sentence plain-language explanation.' Account ID excluded from prompt. Responses cached in Snowflake explanation_cache table keyed by account_id + model_version + score_date.
backend.background_jobs: SageMaker Processing Job runs weekly via EventBridge schedule (Sunday 23:00 UTC). Job stages: (1) feature extraction from Snowflake credit_card_accounts, payment_events, utilization_snapshots, macro_signals, (2) XGBoost inference, (3) SHAP value computation for top 10 features per account, (4) write model_scores and shap_explanations to Snowflake, (5) trigger PSI/Gini computation and post metrics to MLflow.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit multi-page app with pages: Model Performance (PSI chart, Gini trend, KS statistic), Governance Dashboard (pending approvals, model version history), Analyst Queue (flagged high-risk accounts with LLM narratives), Bias Monitoring (demographic parity chart across protected class proxies).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Model Performance page fetches metrics from MLflow via REST and plots rolling 13-week PSI and Gini using Plotly. Governance page lists model_versions requiring SR 11-7 sign-off with SHAP summary plot attachment. Analyst Queue shows accounts with score > threshold alongside LLM-generated narrative; analyst can tag for outreach or escalate.
frontend.performance_notes: Cache MLflow metric queries with 1-hour TTL using Streamlit cache_data decorator. Limit analyst queue to top 500 accounts per page load to avoid Snowflake query timeout.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC via okta-jwt-verifier-python in FastAPI middleware. Audience claim must match the internal service client_id. Streamlit authenticates via Okta OAuth2 PKCE flow using streamlit-authenticator library.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: analyst role can view model_scores and explanations, cannot trigger governance actions. model_owner can submit model for review. model_risk_reviewer can approve or reject. admin manages user roles.
security.privacy_controls: Snowflake row-level security policy restricts analyst role to model_scores and shap_explanations views; raw payment_events and utilization_snapshots accessible only to model_owner and data engineering roles. LLM prompt strips account_id before API call.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every model approval, rejection, and score access logged to governance_audit_log table in Snowflake with user_id, action, model_version_id, and timestamp. Retained 5 years per SR 11-7 documentation requirement.

data.entities: credit_card_accounts (account_id, segment, open_date, credit_limit, current_balance, days_past_due, charge_off_date). payment_events (event_id, account_id, payment_date, amount, payment_type ENUM minimum/full/partial/missed). utilization_snapshots (snapshot_id, account_id, snapshot_date, utilization_pct, available_credit). macro_signals (signal_date, unemployment_rate, consumer_confidence_index, fed_funds_rate). model_scores (score_id, account_id, model_version_id, score_date, delinquency_probability, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high/critical, shap_top_features_json). model_versions (version_id, model_name, training_date, training_dataset_snapshot, validation_auc, psi_baseline, status ENUM draft/pending_review/approved/deprecated).
data.storage_design: Snowflake data warehouse: raw schema (account data from core banking via nightly Fivetran sync), feature_store schema (engineered features materialized weekly), scoring_output schema (model_scores, shap_explanations). MLflow backend store on RDS PostgreSQL; artifact store on S3.
data.analytics_events: Weekly model performance metrics written to MLflow: training AUC, validation AUC, PSI vs baseline, Gini coefficient, KS statistic, feature importance rankings. Drift alert when PSI > 0.25 triggers Slack notification to model_owner.

devops.deployment_topology: SageMaker Processing Job (ml.m5.4xlarge, 2 instances) for weekly batch. ECS Fargate cluster: scoring_api service (2 tasks, 1 vCPU / 2GB), governance_api service (1 task), streamlit_app service (1 task). MLflow server on Fargate with RDS PostgreSQL backend.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest unit tests for feature engineering and SHAP pipeline, pylint, build Docker images, push ECR, deploy ECS services via task definition update. SageMaker job script uploaded to S3 versioned prefix on merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics from FastAPI via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator. Grafana dashboards: API latency, SageMaker job duration and cost, Streamlit session count. MLflow auto-logs XGBoost training metrics. Weekly scoring report auto-generated as HTML and posted to #credit-risk-models Slack channel.
devops.cost_controls: SageMaker Processing Job uses Spot Instances (ml.m5.4xlarge Spot saves ~70%). Snowflake X-Small warehouse with auto-suspend after 60 seconds idle for batch jobs. Fargate tasks scale to zero after business hours via scheduled scaling. Monthly cost target: SageMaker ~$150, Snowflake ~$600, Fargate ~$80, RDS (MLflow) ~$50.

ISS-001.title: Wrong project_class in contract: listed fullstack_app instead of research_prototype
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 contract had project_class value set to fullstack_app. This causes should_run_specialist() to incorrectly gate specialists and plan sections to follow production app patterns instead of research prototype patterns.

history.note: Identified in audit; contract value incorrect
Fixed to research_prototype in round 2 contract

ISS-002.title: Missing llm_integration contract field despite ai_llm in capabilities
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 input_payload included ai_llm in capabilities but the frozen_requirement_contract did not include the required llm_integration conditional field. This violates the conditional field activation rule.

history.note: Conditional field missing
llm_integration contract field added in round 2

ISS-003.title: LLM narrative prompt includes raw account_id exposing PII to Azure OpenAI
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan described the LLM explanation generation but included account_id in the prompt payload sent to Azure OpenAI. Financial account identifiers must not be transmitted to external API providers.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged account_id in LLM prompt

focus_issues.title: LLM narrative prompt includes raw account_id exposing PII to Azure OpenAI
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan described the LLM explanation generation but included account_id in the prompt payload sent to Azure OpenAI.

Add llm_integration conditional contract field
Remove account_id from LLM prompt; pass only SHAP feature names and values
