previous_plan.title: Consumer Credit Delinquency Prediction ML Prototype - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A research prototype for weekly batch delinquency scoring of 2M credit card accounts using XGBoost gradient boosting on payment behavior and utilization features, with Streamlit dashboard and MLflow model registry. Contract incorrectly listed fullstack_app as project_class and omitted llm_integration field.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: SageMaker Processing Job for weekly batch scoring. FastAPI for on-demand scoring endpoint. Streamlit dashboard for analyst review. MLflow on Fargate for experiment tracking. Snowflake as feature store and output sink.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Okta JWT on FastAPI. LLM narrative generation using Azure OpenAI with account_id included in prompt (flagged as ISS-003). Snowflake row-level security.
previous_plan.data_model: credit_card_accounts, payment_events, utilization_snapshots, model_scores, model_versions in Snowflake.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is round 2 of an ML delinquency prediction research prototype with two critical contract violations fixed (project_class now correctly research_prototype, llm_integration field added) and one high-severity issue to resolve: account_id in LLM prompt. I need to document the prompt privacy fix explicitly in the fix_report and ensure the plan sections use research prototype patterns rather than production app architecture. The flawed quality designation means I should carry forward an intentional flaw - in this case, the plan should have a subtle inconsistency: the Streamlit frontend is described with a backend auth pattern not appropriate for a static Streamlit deployment, reflecting a plan quality issue.

fix_report.action_taken: Modified LLM explanation prompt template in backend specialist subplan and api_design section to exclude account_id. The prompt now receives only SHAP feature name-value pairs and the delinquency probability score. Account-to-explanation linkage is maintained server-side in the explanation_cache table using account_id as key but the identifier is never sent to Azure OpenAI API. Updated security_and_compliance section to document this control explicitly.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Account identifiers are no longer transmitted to Azure OpenAI, satisfying the financial data privacy requirement. LLM narrative quality is unaffected because SHAP feature names and values are sufficient context for explanation generation.

target_output.title: Consumer Credit Card Delinquency Prediction ML Research Prototype - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype for SR 11-7-compliant early delinquency detection on 2M consumer credit card accounts. Weekly SageMaker Processing Jobs extract behavioral features (payment recency, utilization velocity, missed payment streaks) and macroeconomic signals from Snowflake, score accounts using an XGBoost gradient boosting model, and compute per-account SHAP explanations. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o generates plain-language risk narratives for collections analysts from SHAP values only (no account identifiers transmitted). A Streamlit dashboard surfaces model performance metrics (PSI, Gini, KS) and a model governance workflow for SR 11-7 sign-off. ISS-001 and ISS-002 (contract violations) and ISS-003 (LLM PII exposure) resolved in this round.
target_output.architecture_overview: Research prototype architecture with SageMaker Processing Jobs as the primary compute for weekly batch scoring (Sunday 23:00 UTC via EventBridge). Snowflake serves as the data platform: raw schema (account data from core banking via Fivetran), feature_store schema (engineered features materialized by the batch job), and scoring_output schema (model_scores, shap_explanations, explanation_cache). MLflow on ECS Fargate tracks all training experiments and hosts the model registry. FastAPI services on Fargate expose governance and on-demand scoring endpoints. Streamlit multi-page app presents model performance dashboards and analyst queues. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o called post-SHAP-computation to generate analyst-facing narratives; account_id stripped before API call, identifier stored only in local explanation_cache keyed by account_id + model_version + score_date.
target_output.technology_stack: ML: Python 3.12, XGBoost 2.0, scikit-learn 1.4, SHAP 0.45, pandas 2.2. Backend: FastAPI 0.111, Pydantic v2, okta-jwt-verifier-python. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-05-13) via openai Python SDK 1.30. Frontend: Streamlit 1.35, Plotly 5.22, streamlit-authenticator. Data: Snowflake (feature store + scoring output), MLflow 2.13 (experiment tracking + model registry) on ECS Fargate with RDS PostgreSQL backend + S3 artifact store. Infra: SageMaker Processing (ml.m5.4xlarge Spot), ECS Fargate, GitHub Actions CI/CD, EventBridge scheduler.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Batch Scoring Pipeline: SageMaker job extracts 13-week rolling features for all active accounts, runs XGBoost inference, computes SHAP top-10 features per account, triggers LLM narrative generation (account_id-stripped prompt), writes model_scores and explanation_cache to Snowflake scoring_output schema. Model Performance Dashboard: Streamlit page plots rolling PSI, Gini coefficient, and KS statistic over 13-week windows from MLflow metrics; alerts when PSI exceeds 0.25 drift threshold. Analyst Review Queue: Presents top 500 high-risk accounts (score > 0.65) with LLM narratives; analyst can tag for outreach or escalate. SR 11-7 Governance Workflow: model_owner submits model version with SHAP summary plot and performance report; model_risk_reviewer approves or rejects via governance_api with audit trail.
target_output.system_components: SageMaker Processing Job (ml.m5.4xlarge Spot, 2 instances, weekly batch orchestrated by EventBridge). Snowflake cluster (raw, feature_store, scoring_output schemas). FastAPI scoring_api service on ECS Fargate (on-demand batch trigger, SHAP retrieval). FastAPI governance_api service on ECS Fargate (SR 11-7 approval workflow). Streamlit app on ECS Fargate (analyst dashboard and governance UI). MLflow server on ECS Fargate (experiment tracking + model registry, RDS PostgreSQL backend, S3 artifact store). Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (explanation generation with PII-stripped prompts). GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline.
target_output.workflows: Weekly Batch Scoring: EventBridge triggers SageMaker Processing Job at Sunday 23:00 UTC. Feature extraction queries Snowflake for 13 weeks of payment_events and utilization_snapshots per active account. Macro signals (unemployment_rate, consumer_confidence_index) joined from macro_signals table. XGBoost model loaded from MLflow model registry (production alias). Inference produces delinquency_probability scores. SHAP TreeExplainer computes top-10 feature contributions per account. For each account in top 5% risk tier, explanation_service strips account_id and posts SHAP feature name-value dict to Azure OpenAI GPT-4o; narrative response written to explanation_cache with account_id key stored only in Snowflake. model_scores and shap_explanations tables updated. PSI and Gini computed vs baseline and logged to MLflow run. SR 11-7 Governance Review: Model owner runs training experiment, logs metrics to MLflow, generates SHAP summary plot artifact. Submits model_version via governance_api POST /v1/models/{version_id}/submit-for-review with SHAP artifact S3 path. model_risk_reviewer views submission in Streamlit governance page, downloads SHAP plot, reviews demographic parity metrics, approves or rejects via POST /v1/models/{version_id}/governance/decision. Decision logged to governance_audit_log.
target_output.data_model: credit_card_accounts (account_id VARCHAR PK, segment VARCHAR, open_date DATE, credit_limit DECIMAL, current_balance DECIMAL, days_past_due INT, charge_off_date DATE). payment_events (event_id VARCHAR PK, account_id FK, payment_date DATE, amount DECIMAL, payment_type ENUM minimum/full/partial/missed). utilization_snapshots (snapshot_id VARCHAR PK, account_id FK, snapshot_date DATE, utilization_pct DECIMAL, available_credit DECIMAL). macro_signals (signal_date DATE PK, unemployment_rate DECIMAL, consumer_confidence_index DECIMAL, fed_funds_rate DECIMAL). model_scores (score_id VARCHAR PK, account_id FK, model_version_id FK, score_date DATE, delinquency_probability DECIMAL, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high/critical, shap_top_features_json VARCHAR). model_versions (version_id VARCHAR PK, model_name VARCHAR, training_date DATE, training_dataset_snapshot VARCHAR, validation_auc DECIMAL, psi_baseline DECIMAL, status ENUM draft/pending_review/approved/deprecated). explanation_cache (cache_id VARCHAR PK, account_id FK, model_version_id FK, score_date DATE, narrative_text VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP). governance_audit_log (log_id VARCHAR PK, user_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, model_version_id FK, detail_json VARCHAR, timestamp TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: governance_api: POST /v1/models/{version_id}/submit-for-review (model owner submits for SR 11-7), GET /v1/models/{version_id}/performance (PSI, Gini, KS metrics from MLflow), POST /v1/models/{version_id}/governance/decision (reviewer approve/reject), GET /v1/governance/audit-log (compliance report). scoring_api: POST /v1/score-batch (trigger on-demand SageMaker job for subset of accounts), GET /v1/explanations/{account_id} (returns SHAP values + LLM narrative from explanation_cache), GET /v1/models/active (current production model version metadata). All endpoints: Okta JWT required, audience claim validated against service client_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC JWT on all FastAPI endpoints with audience claim validation. RBAC: analyst (read model_scores and explanations only), model_owner (submit for governance), model_risk_reviewer (approve/reject), admin (user management). Snowflake row-level security: analyst role restricted to model_scores and explanation_cache views; raw payment_events accessible only to data engineering and model_owner roles. LLM prompt privacy control: account_id excluded from all Azure OpenAI API calls; only SHAP feature name-value pairs and delinquency_probability score in prompt body. Response stored in explanation_cache keyed by account_id server-side. No PAN, SSN, or full account numbers in any feature table (enforced by data engineering data governance policy). TLS 1.3 for all service-to-service and Snowflake connections. SR 11-7 controls: governance_audit_log append-only (INSERT only for service account, no UPDATE/DELETE privilege). ECOA bias monitoring: demographic parity ratio computed on proxy-based protected class segments before governance approval.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: pytest (feature engineering and SHAP unit tests), pylint, Docker build, ECR push, ECS task definition update for scoring_api/governance_api/streamlit services, S3 upload of updated SageMaker job script. SageMaker Processing Job uses Spot Instances on ml.m5.4xlarge (70% cost savings vs On-Demand). EventBridge rule triggers job at Sunday 23:00 UTC with retry on Spot interruption. Environments: dev (Snowflake dev schema, mock SageMaker job via local Docker), staging (Snowflake staging schema, full SageMaker job with 10K account sample), production (full 2M account batch). Fargate services: scoring_api and governance_api scale 1-3 tasks on CPU 70% threshold. Streamlit scales 1-2 tasks on connection count. MLflow Fargate task always-on during business hours, scales to 0 at 20:00 UTC via scheduled action.
target_output.observability: MLflow auto-logs XGBoost training metrics (AUC, log loss, feature importance) and artifacts (SHAP summary plots, confusion matrix). Prometheus metrics from FastAPI via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator: request rate, latency p50/p95/p99, error rate. Grafana dashboards: API health, SageMaker job duration and Snowflake credit consumption per run. Weekly automated performance report: Python script queries MLflow API for PSI, Gini, KS metrics and posts HTML report to #credit-risk-models Slack via webhook. Alerts: PSI > 0.25 (model drift, pages model_owner), SageMaker job failure (pages DevOps), governance_api error rate > 2% (Grafana alert to Slack).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SageMaker Processing Job (Spot ml.m5.4xlarge x2, ~4 hours/week): ~$120/month. Snowflake (X-Small warehouse, auto-suspend 60s, ~50 credits/week for batch + ad-hoc queries): ~$600/month. ECS Fargate (3 services, scale-to-zero off-hours): ~$80/month. RDS PostgreSQL for MLflow backend (db.t3.medium): ~$45/month. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (5% of 2M accounts = 100K explanations/week, ~$200/month at $0.002/explanation). Total: ~$1,045/month, within $2,000 Snowflake budget constraint.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Feature engineering pipeline for internal behavioral features (payment recency, utilization velocity, missed payment streaks), XGBoost baseline model trained on 24 months of historical data, MLflow experiment tracking, SageMaker weekly batch job, Streamlit model performance dashboard (PSI, Gini), basic Okta auth, governance_api stub for model submission. Pilot scoring on 6-month lookback validation window. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SHAP explanation pipeline integrated, Azure OpenAI LLM narrative generation with PII-stripped prompts, analyst review queue in Streamlit, full SR 11-7 governance workflow with model_risk_reviewer approval, ECOA demographic parity bias monitoring. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Macroeconomic signal integration, champion-challenger framework in governance_api, automated monthly model retraining trigger, real-time scoring API stub (Kinesis integration design for future production phase).
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints required on all functions; mypy strict mode in CI. pytest with parametrized tests for feature engineering functions; 90% coverage target for ML pipeline code. No raw account_id in any LLM prompt; enforced by code review checklist and unit test asserting prompt content. MLflow experiment naming convention: {model_name}_{training_date}_{feature_set_version}. Feature engineering changes require schema version bump in Snowflake feature_store schema and migration script. ADR required for model architecture changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Spot Instance interruption mid-SageMaker batch job. Mitigation: SageMaker checkpoint to S3 every 500K accounts; job resumes from last checkpoint on restart. Risk: Snowflake credit overrun from ad-hoc analyst queries hitting raw feature tables. Mitigation: Query tagging policy + Snowflake resource monitor alert at 80% of monthly budget. Risk: LLM narrative quality degradation on drift. Mitigation: PSI alert already in place; if PSI > 0.25 pause narrative generation until model is retrained. Tradeoff: Streamlit for frontend vs React - accepted for prototype speed; production version would require React for access control at component level. Risk: SR 11-7 review timeline causing delays. Mitigation: Governance workflow documented and model risk team engaged from sprint 1.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ECOA fairness metric: demographic parity ratio selected for prototype given proxy-based class definitions; equalized odds requires confirmed protected class labels, deferred to production. LLM narrative frequency: generated only for accounts in critical risk tier (top 5%) to stay within Azure OpenAI cost budget. MLflow backend: RDS PostgreSQL retained over SageMaker Model Registry to avoid vendor lock-in for the research phase.

metadata.notes: Round 2 ML delinquency prediction prototype; ISS-001 and ISS-002 (contract violations) and ISS-003 (LLM PII) resolved; flawed quality reflects intentional Streamlit auth inconsistency.

profile.domain: Digital Savings Bank High-Performance Marketing Site

project_goal.value: High-performance static marketing site showcasing APY rates, product comparisons, and eligibility checker for a digital savings bank, targeting sub-1-second LCP and Core Web Vitals green scores to maximize organic conversion
project_goal.rationale: Core web vitals are a Google ranking factor; sub-1s LCP is the competitive differentiator for millennial audience acquisition

target_users.value: Millennials (age 28-40) seeking high-yield savings accounts; mobile-first audience with 65% mobile traffic
target_users.rationale: User research from deposit acquisition team confirms mobile-first millennial segment

project_class.rationale: No authenticated sessions or server-side computation; pure static delivery optimized for CDN caching

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery plus analytics tracking; no auth, payments, or backend services

complexity_level.rationale: Static content with React islands for interactive widgets; no server-side complexity

risk_level.rationale: No PII collection beyond email opt-in; no financial transactions on this site

data_sensitivity.rationale: Rate information is public; eligibility checker is client-side only with no data persistence

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing site; no authentication required

access_model.value: No authentication; public read-only access to all pages
access_model.rationale: Static public marketing site; no user accounts

feature_scope.value: APY rate display with CMS-driven updates, product comparison table (savings, money market, CD), eligibility checker widget (state residency + age), A/B test variants for CTAs via Lambda@Edge, email opt-in capture, SEO metadata and structured data for Google rich snippets
feature_scope.rationale: Deposit acquisition team feature requirements for Q2 launch

mvp_scope.value: APY rate pages, product comparison table, email opt-in, basic SEO metadata
mvp_scope.rationale: Eligibility checker and A/B testing deferred 4 weeks after launch due to analytics instrument timeline

security_baseline.value: CloudFront HTTPS only (HTTP redirected to HTTPS), HSTS with max-age 31536000, CSP header blocking inline scripts, no third-party scripts without SRI hash
security_baseline.rationale: Public site minimum security headers; SRI prevents CDN-injected script attacks on financial brand site

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with React islands for interactive components (eligibility checker, rate comparison); TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro zero-JS default maximizes performance; React islands only where interactivity needed

backend_stack.value: No backend server; Lambda@Edge for A/B variant serving and geo-based redirect; headless CMS (Contentful) for APY rate content
backend_stack.rationale: Static site constraint; Lambda@Edge handles dynamic routing at CDN layer without origin server

data_platform.value: No database; Contentful CMS as content source; Google Analytics 4 for analytics event collection
data_platform.rationale: Static site has no persistence layer; all data flows to analytics platform only

hosting_target.value: S3 static hosting + CloudFront with Lambda@Edge; Route 53 for DNS; ACM for TLS certificate
hosting_target.rationale: AWS-native static hosting pipeline with sub-50ms global edge caching

privacy_retention_policy.value: No PII stored on site infrastructure; email opt-ins forwarded to Mailchimp and not retained on AWS; GA4 data governed by Google data retention settings (14 months)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Static site collects no personal data server-side; FDIC disclosure compliance is content-only

future_scope.value: Account opening funnel (authenticated, separate app), interactive savings calculator with compound interest projection, personalized rate widget based on deposit amount
future_scope.rationale: Account opening moves to a separate authenticated application; calculator is Phase 2 island

constraints.value: LCP must score green (<2.5s) on mobile 4G in Lighthouse CI; no third-party JavaScript bundles over 50KB uncompressed; FDIC disclosure must appear on all rate-advertising pages above the fold on mobile
constraints.rationale: FDIC requires disclosures alongside advertised APY; performance gate ensures SEO ranking

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Web Analytics for real-user metrics (CWV: LCP, CLS, FID); Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions blocking on performance regression; GA4 for conversion funnel tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: Real-user CWV monitoring and CI performance gate prevent regression to slow load times

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; Contentful content updates deployed without engineering involvement via webhook-triggered CI rebuild
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing team self-serves rate updates without code changes

project.goal: Sub-1s LCP static marketing site for digital savings bank deposit acquisition
project.target_users: Mobile-first millennials
project.timeline: MVP in 4 weeks, A/B testing at week 8

frontend.performance: Partial hydration, image optimization via Astro Image, font subsetting

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI gate
devops.cms_webhook: Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions rebuild on APY rate publish

security.headers: HSTS, CSP, SRI for all third-party scripts

data.analytics: GA4 via gtag.js with SRI hash
data.cms: Contentful headless CMS for rate cards and product descriptions

constraints.fdic: FDIC disclosure above fold on all rate pages

open_questions.ab_testing: Lambda@Edge A/B vs CloudFront Functions for lower latency variant serving

reasoner_reviews.product: The APY rate CMS-driven update workflow is the most operationally critical feature - rates can change daily and marketing must be able to publish without engineering. The Contentful webhook to GitHub Actions rebuild pipeline is the right pattern. The eligibility checker as a client-side React island is a good performance tradeoff: no server roundtrip, state in component memory only.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with React islands is the optimal stack for this requirement. The zero-JS default pages (homepage, product comparison, APY rates) will achieve sub-1s LCP trivially. Lambda@Edge for A/B testing adds ~5ms at edge versus CloudFront Functions (~1ms); for headline CTA variants the difference is imperceptible, but CloudFront Functions should be evaluated for simpler variant logic to reduce cost. The previous plan had a Lighthouse CI gate but did not specify which CloudFront cache behaviors map to which content types, leaving caching strategy underspecified.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must use nonce-based inline script blocking to allow Astro island hydration scripts without unsafe-inline. The previous plan specified a generic CSP policy; nonce injection via Lambda@Edge on the HTML response is required. SRI hashes for GA4 gtag.js must be pinned to a specific version and updated via Dependabot.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: FDIC disclosure placement is a hard compliance requirement. The Astro layout template must include the FDIC disclosure component in the Hero section above the fold on mobile viewport (375px). Rate page template test in Playwright must assert disclosure element visibility above fold. Previous plan did not include this test.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous round plan was moderate quality overall but the caching TTL strategy was thin. CloudFront behavior configuration for static assets (1-year max-age, content-hash filenames) vs content pages (5-minute TTL for rate pages to allow fast rate updates) was not specified. This must be detailed in round 2.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project structure: src/pages/ (index.astro, savings.astro, money-market.astro, cds.astro, compare.astro), src/layouts/ (BaseLayout.astro with CSP nonce injection, RatePage.astro with FDIC disclosure), src/components/ (RateCard.astro, ProductComparison.astro, EligibilityChecker.tsx React island, EmailOptIn.tsx React island, ABTestWrapper.tsx), src/content/ (Contentful integration via @astrojs/contentful).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage: hero with primary CTA (A/B tested via Lambda@Edge), featured APY rate card, trust signals (FDIC insured badge, BBB rating). Savings product page: rate table with historical APY trend chart (lightweight Chart.js island), FDIC disclosure above fold, email opt-in CTA. Compare page: product feature matrix (pure Astro, zero JS). Eligibility checker: React island, validates state + age client-side, shows product eligibility result without API call.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro Image component for all above-fold images: WebP + AVIF with width/height set to prevent CLS. Self-hosted Inter font subset (latin only) loaded via <link rel=preload>. No Google Fonts DNS lookup. GA4 loaded with loading=async and dns-prefetch. Rate hero section inlined as critical CSS via Astro's style scoped. Lighthouse CI blocks merge if LCP > 2500ms or CLS > 0.1 on mobile preset.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA target. Rate table uses semantic <table> with <caption> for screen readers. A/B test variants maintain identical heading hierarchy. Eligibility checker error messages associated via aria-describedby.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Public read-only access.
security.secrets_management: Contentful API read-only delivery token stored in GitHub Actions secrets; injected as environment variable at build time only. Not embedded in static HTML output.
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront WAF rule group: AWS managed rules for known bad IPs and bot signatures. Rate limit on email opt-in Lambda@Edge handler: 10 submissions per IP per hour.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (static asset origin, versioned content-hash filenames). CloudFront distribution: two behaviors - /assets/* (cache 1 year, immutable) and /* (cache 5 minutes, Vary: Accept-Encoding). Lambda@Edge on viewer-request event for A/B CTA variant cookie assignment. Lambda@Edge on origin-response for CSP nonce injection. Route 53 alias record to CloudFront distribution.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: on push to main - astro build, Lighthouse CI against production-equivalent preview URL (LCP, CLS, FID gate), S3 sync with cache-control headers (assets: max-age=31536000 immutable, pages: max-age=300), CloudFront invalidation for /* on deployment. Contentful webhook: POST to GitHub Actions webhook endpoint triggers rebuild pipeline on APY rate publish event.
devops.observability_stack: Cloudflare Web Analytics (beacon.js, <1KB, no cookies) for real-user CWV. GA4 for conversion events: email_opt_in_submit, cta_click, eligibility_check_complete, product_page_view. Lighthouse CI report artifacts retained in GitHub Actions for 90 days. CloudFront access logs shipped to S3 for traffic pattern analysis.

ISS-001.title: CloudFront caching TTL strategy underspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan noted CloudFront as the CDN but did not differentiate cache TTLs between static assets (content-hashed, should be immutable) and content pages (rate pages need 5-minute TTL to propagate CMS updates quickly). A single cache behavior leads to either stale rate data or inefficient cache invalidation.

history.note: Caching strategy noted as thin in audit

ISS-002.title: FDIC disclosure placement not tested in CI
ISS-002.detail: Compliance requires FDIC disclosure above the fold on all rate-advertising pages. The previous plan mentioned the disclosure in content requirements but did not include a Playwright test asserting disclosure visibility at 375px mobile viewport.

history.note: Compliance note was present but no enforcement mechanism

Add Playwright test asserting FDIC disclosure element is visible above 375px fold on all rate pages

previous_plan.title: Digital Savings Bank Marketing Site - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: An Astro 4 static marketing site for a digital savings bank with React islands for the eligibility checker and email opt-in, hosted on S3 + CloudFront, with Contentful CMS for APY rate management. Caching strategy was a single CloudFront behavior (ISS-001) and FDIC disclosure lacked CI enforcement (ISS-002).
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static build deployed to S3, served via CloudFront. Single cache behavior (generic TTL). Lambda@Edge for A/B variant serving. GA4 for analytics.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions build and S3 deploy. Contentful webhook triggers rebuild. Single CloudFront cache behavior with generic TTL not differentiated by asset type.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HSTS, CSP, SRI headers. FDIC disclosure noted as content requirement but no automated test.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 of a static savings bank marketing site. Both unresolved issues are medium severity: CloudFront caching strategy and FDIC disclosure CI test. Both are fixable in this round. The plan quality is moderate - overall the architecture is sound but I should ensure the caching section and CI pipeline section are detailed enough to address both issues. This is a static_website so there is no backend server, no database, and no server-side runtime - the plan must stay true to these constraints throughout.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined two distinct CloudFront cache behaviors in deployment_and_operations: (1) /assets/* path pattern with Cache-Control max-age=31536000 immutable for content-hashed static assets (JS, CSS, images); (2) /* default behavior with Cache-Control max-age=300 s-maxage=300 for HTML pages, allowing APY rate updates to propagate within 5 minutes of Contentful publish. CloudFront invalidation on deployment covers /* HTML only, not /assets/* (no cost for unchanged assets).
fix_report.expected_outcome: APY rate changes published in Contentful propagate to CloudFront edge within 5 minutes, satisfying marketing update SLA. Static assets are cached aggressively with content-hash filenames for indefinite browser caching, maximizing repeat-visit performance.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Playwright test in CI pipeline asserting fdic-disclosure element is visible (not hidden, not below viewport) at 375x667px mobile viewport on all rate-advertising pages (savings.html, money-market.html, cds.html). Test runs as part of the post-build Playwright smoke test suite in GitHub Actions before S3 deployment. Also added FDIC disclosure as a required slot in the RatePage.astro layout template, preventing rate pages from rendering without the disclosure component.
fix_report.expected_outcome: FDIC disclosure presence above fold is enforced by both template structure (impossible to omit) and automated CI test (visible above 375px fold), satisfying FDIC advertising compliance requirement.

target_output.title: Digital Savings Bank High-Performance Marketing Site - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro 4 marketing site for a digital savings bank targeting sub-1-second LCP on mobile for millennial deposit acquisition. APY rate cards, product comparison tables, and a client-side eligibility checker island are deployed to S3 + CloudFront. Contentful CMS enables marketing to publish rate updates without engineering; a webhook triggers CI rebuild within 2 minutes and propagates to CDN edge within 5 minutes via a 5-minute CloudFront TTL on HTML pages. Static assets use immutable 1-year cache headers with content-hash filenames. FDIC disclosure placement is enforced by layout template and Playwright CI test. Lambda@Edge serves A/B CTA variants. ISS-001 (caching) and ISS-002 (FDIC test) resolved.
target_output.architecture_overview: Zero-backend static architecture: Astro 4 build generates HTML pages at CI time from Contentful CMS content (APY rates, product descriptions) and outputs content-hashed static assets. Artifacts deployed to S3 versioned bucket with two distinct Cache-Control policies: HTML pages at s-maxage=300 for fast rate propagation, /assets/* at max-age=31536000 immutable for browser caching. CloudFront distribution with two cache behaviors routes requests appropriately. Lambda@Edge on viewer-request assigns A/B test cookie for CTA variants; Lambda@Edge on origin-response injects nonce-based CSP header. GA4 analytics and Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon collect CWV and conversion events. No server-side runtime, no database, no authentication.
target_output.technology_stack: Static generator: Astro 4.11 with @astrojs/react (React islands), @astrojs/contentful (CMS integration), @astrojs/sitemap, astro-compress. UI: React 18 (eligibility checker and email opt-in islands only), TypeScript 5.4, Tailwind CSS v3 JIT. CDN: CloudFront with two cache behaviors, Lambda@Edge (Node.js 20). Content: Contentful headless CMS (read-only delivery API). Analytics: GA4 (gtag.js pinned version with SRI), Cloudflare Web Analytics. Testing: Playwright for FDIC disclosure and CWV smoke tests, Lighthouse CI with performance gate. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: APY Rate Pages: Contentful-sourced rate cards showing current APY, minimum balance, and FDIC disclosure; rate table updates trigger CI rebuild via webhook within 2 minutes. Product Comparison Table: Server-rendered Astro component comparing savings, money market, and CD products on 8 feature dimensions; zero JS, pure HTML table with CSS sticky header. Eligibility Checker Island: React island validating state residency (50-state list) and age (>=18) client-side; returns product eligibility result in <100ms with no network call. Email Opt-In Island: React island with form submission to Lambda@Edge handler that validates and forwards to Mailchimp API; rate-limited at 10/IP/hour. A/B CTA Test: Lambda@Edge assigns variant cookie on first visit (50/50 split); Astro conditional renders appropriate headline and button copy based on cookie value passed via CloudFront header.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build system (CI-only; generates static HTML + hashed assets). S3 bucket (static origin, versioned filenames, no public access - served exclusively via CloudFront OAC). CloudFront distribution with two behaviors: /assets/* (immutable 1-year TTL, gzip+brotli) and /* (5-min TTL, gzip). Lambda@Edge viewer-request function (A/B cookie assignment, max 1ms execution). Lambda@Edge origin-response function (CSP nonce injection into HTML response). Contentful CMS (APY rates, product content, SEO metadata). GA4 + Cloudflare Web Analytics (client-side, no server-side data store). Route 53 + ACM (DNS + TLS).
target_output.workflows: APY Rate Update Workflow: Content editor updates APY rate card in Contentful and publishes. Contentful fires webhook to GitHub Actions workflow trigger endpoint. GitHub Actions initiates Astro build, fetching fresh content from Contentful Delivery API. Build completes in ~45 seconds. Playwright FDIC disclosure tests run. Lighthouse CI performance gate runs (LCP, CLS must remain green). S3 sync uploads changed HTML files with Cache-Control: max-age=300 s-maxage=300. CloudFront invalidation issued for /* HTML paths. New rates visible at CloudFront edge within 5 minutes of Contentful publish. User CTA A/B Test Workflow: New visitor arrives at homepage. Lambda@Edge viewer-request function checks for ab_variant cookie. If absent, assigns variant A or B at 50/50 random and sets ab_variant cookie with 30-day expiry. CloudFront passes x-ab-variant header to origin. Astro-generated HTML includes both variants marked with data-variant attribute; Lambda@Edge origin-response strips non-matching variant from HTML before delivery. GA4 event cta_click fires with variant dimension for conversion reporting.
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on site infrastructure. Content entities in Contentful: product_pages (slug, title, meta_description, hero_headline, hero_cta_text_variant_a, hero_cta_text_variant_b). rate_cards (product_slug, current_apy, min_balance, effective_date, fdic_disclosure_text). eligibility_widget_configs (state_allowlist, min_age, excluded_products_by_state). ab_test_variants (variant_id, page_slug, element, variant_a_value, variant_b_value, active). seo_metadata (page_slug, title_tag, meta_description, og_image_url, structured_data_json). Analytics events in GA4: email_opt_in_submit (variant, source), cta_click (variant, page, position), eligibility_check_complete (eligible_products_count), product_page_view (product_slug).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Lambda@Edge functions (not APIs): A/B variant assignment function (viewer-request, <1ms, no external calls). CSP nonce injection function (origin-response, reads HTML response, injects nonce into CSP header and Astro island script tags). Email opt-in handler (Lambda@Edge on /api/email-opt-in POST, validates email format, rate-limits by IP, forwards to Mailchimp API with UTM params). External integrations: Contentful Delivery API (build-time only, read-only). GA4 Measurement Protocol not used; all events client-side via gtag.js.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CloudFront HTTPS enforced (HTTP redirected via CloudFront behavior). HSTS: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. CSP: nonce-based policy injected by Lambda@Edge origin-response function; default-src 'none'; script-src 'nonce-{NONCE}' https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src 'nonce-{NONCE}'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src https://www.google-analytics.com. SRI hashes required for GA4 gtag.js (pinned version, Dependabot weekly version check). CloudFront WAF: AWS managed rule group for common threats, rate limit 100 req/min per IP on /api/* paths. FDIC compliance: fdic_disclosure_text field required in all rate_cards Contentful entries; RatePage.astro layout renders disclosure in Hero section as non-optional required slot; Playwright CI test asserts #fdic-disclosure element bounding box top < 667px at 375px mobile viewport.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow (on push to main and on Contentful webhook trigger): astro build (pulls Contentful content via delivery API), Playwright smoke tests (FDIC disclosure above fold at 375px, eligibility checker renders, email opt-in form submits), Lighthouse CI (--preset=mobile, budget: LCP <2500ms, CLS <0.1, TBT <300ms; blocks merge on failure), aws s3 sync (HTML files with --cache-control 'max-age=300,s-maxage=300'; /assets/* files with --cache-control 'max-age=31536000,immutable'), CloudFront invalidation (/* HTML paths only). Lambda@Edge functions deployed via separate GitHub Actions workflow (on changes to lambda/ directory) using AWS SAM. CloudFront two behaviors: path /assets/* (cache behavior: all methods, TTL min=31536000 max=31536000, gzip+brotli), path /* default (TTL min=0 default=300 max=300, gzip+brotli). S3 origin access controlled via CloudFront OAC (no public S3 access).
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-preserving, cookieless, SRI-verified beacon.js) tracks real-user LCP, CLS, FID across all pages. Weekly CWV report from Cloudflare dashboard reviewed by engineering lead. GA4 tracks conversion funnel: product_page_view -> cta_click -> email_opt_in_submit with A/B variant dimension. Lighthouse CI artifacts (HTML report, performance budget JSON) retained in GitHub Actions for 90 days; performance trend visible in CI history. CloudFront access logs to S3: used for traffic pattern analysis and bot detection, not real-time. Alert: Lighthouse CI failure on any main branch push triggers Slack notification to #web-performance channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3: <$1/month for static asset storage. CloudFront: ~$8/month for 5M monthly page views (data transfer + HTTPS request costs). Lambda@Edge: ~$3/month for A/B and CSP functions at 5M invocations. Contentful: free tier (25K API calls/month, sufficient for CMS). GA4: free. Total: ~$12/month. Scaling is automatic via CloudFront global edge; no capacity planning needed. At 50M monthly views CloudFront cost scales linearly to ~$80/month with no architectural changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Astro 4 project scaffold, Contentful CMS schema (rate_cards, product_pages, seo_metadata), homepage + 3 product pages, APY rate cards with FDIC disclosure layout enforcement, email opt-in React island, Contentful webhook-triggered CI rebuild, two CloudFront cache behaviors (assets immutable + pages 5-min), Playwright FDIC disclosure CI test, Lighthouse CI gate. Launch with APY rates. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Eligibility checker React island, A/B CTA test via Lambda@Edge with GA4 variant tracking, CSP nonce injection via Lambda@Edge origin-response, CloudFront WAF rule group, SEO structured data (BankAccount schema.org markup), Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Savings calculator React island (compound interest projection, no API calls), product comparison page enhancement with sortable table, SRI Dependabot automation for GA4 version pinning, Lighthouse budget tightening (LCP <1800ms target).
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components: no JavaScript in .astro files; interactivity only via React islands with explicit client:load or client:visible directive. New rate pages must use RatePage.astro layout (enforced by Astro layout convention); layout includes non-optional FDICDisclosure slot. Tailwind: no inline styles; utility classes only. TypeScript strict mode. Playwright tests required for any new interactive island before merge. Lighthouse CI budget must not be relaxed; if new feature causes regression, fix the performance before merging. Third-party scripts: no new script without SRI hash and PR review of bundle size impact.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API rate limits during CI rebuild bursts (multiple developers merging simultaneously triggers multiple parallel builds). Mitigation: GitHub Actions concurrency group limiting to 1 concurrent build per branch; queued builds use cached Contentful content when content unchanged. Risk: Lambda@Edge cold start degrading LCP on first visit after deployment. Mitigation: Lambda@Edge functions are lightweight (<50 lines) and warm within 50ms; not on critical path for LCP (A/B runs on viewer-request before CloudFront cache check). Tradeoff: 5-minute HTML cache TTL means rate changes take up to 5 minutes to propagate; accepted over CloudFront invalidation on every publish (cost and latency overhead). Tradeoff: Astro React islands increase JS bundle size vs pure Astro; mitigated by client:visible directive loading islands only when in viewport.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CloudFront Functions vs Lambda@Edge for A/B: Lambda@Edge selected because CSP nonce injection on origin-response is not supported by CloudFront Functions (origin-response event not available). Contentful vs Sanity for CMS: Contentful selected (team familiarity, existing bank contract). Email opt-in forwarding: Mailchimp API over direct CRM integration for MVP; replaced in Phase 3 by direct HubSpot integration per marketing team request.

metadata.notes: Round 2 static savings bank marketing site; ISS-001 (CloudFront caching) and ISS-002 (FDIC disclosure CI test) resolved; no backend or DB throughout as required for static_website class.

profile.domain: SME Lending Platform CI/CD-Powered Loan Decisioning Product Landing Page

project_goal.value: Product landing page for an SME lending platform announcing its revamped CI/CD-powered loan decisioning pipeline, with lead capture form, embedded video explainer, UTM-tracked campaign variants, and GDPR/CCPA consent management to convert small business owners into qualified demo requests
project_goal.rationale: Go-to-market for relaunched decisioning engine; demo requests feed into sales pipeline; EU and CA traffic requires consent compliance

target_users.value: Small business owners (1-50 employees) seeking fast loan approvals; UK, EU, and US audiences
target_users.rationale: Primary ICP for SME lending product; EU/UK traffic requires GDPR consent banner

project_class.rationale: Single-purpose conversion page; no multi-page navigation, no auth, no persistent state

capabilities.rationale: Static landing page with client-side analytics and serverless lead form handler; no auth or backend services

complexity_level.rationale: Single page with form, video, and analytics; no state management complexity

risk_level.rationale: No PII stored server-side; lead data forwarded immediately to HubSpot; no financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Name and email collected for demo requests only; forwarded to CRM immediately; no persistence on Vercel infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing landing page accessible to all audiences

access_model.value: No authentication; public access; consent-gated analytics for EU/CA visitors
access_model.rationale: Landing page is public; GDPR/CCPA require analytics opt-in before GA4 fires for EU/CA visitors

feature_scope.value: Hero section with headline A/B variants, embedded Vimeo explainer video, lead capture form (name, email, company, loan amount range), UTM parameter persistence through form submission, GDPR cookie consent banner with granular analytics consent, CCPA opt-out link, split-URL A/B testing via Vercel Edge Config, GA4 conversion tracking with consent mode
feature_scope.rationale: Full go-to-market landing page requirements for multi-market launch

mvp_scope.value: Hero, lead capture form with HubSpot forwarding, embedded video, GA4 tracking, GDPR consent banner
mvp_scope.rationale: A/B testing and CCPA opt-out deferred 2 weeks to allow analytics instrument validation first

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel (HTTP to HTTPS redirect); HSTS preload; CSP blocking third-party scripts not in allowlist; Vercel serverless function validates lead form input (email format, honeypot field, rate limit)
security_baseline.rationale: Landing page minimum security headers; form abuse prevention on serverless handler

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 static export with Tailwind CSS; no SSR required; output: export
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js static export on Vercel Edge Network; team uses Next.js; Tailwind for fast UI iteration

backend_stack.value: Vercel serverless function for lead form submission handler (Node.js 20); no persistent backend
backend_stack.rationale: Single serverless function handles lead form; forwards to HubSpot Contacts API; no database needed

data_platform.value: No database; HubSpot CRM as lead data destination; GA4 for analytics events
data_platform.rationale: Lead data belongs in CRM immediately; no reason to persist on landing page infrastructure

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network; custom domain via Vercel DNS; automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel native deployment for Next.js; global edge delivery with sub-50ms TTFB

privacy_retention_policy.value: No PII stored on Vercel or AWS infrastructure; HubSpot governs CRM data retention; GA4 data retention 14 months; consent preferences stored in browser localStorage only
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimisation: landing page infrastructure stores no personal data

future_scope.value: Personalized landing page variants by industry vertical (retail, hospitality, construction), dynamic loan range calculator, integration with loan pre-qualification API
future_scope.rationale: Personalization and calculator require authenticated state; planned for separate web app

constraints.value: GDPR cookie consent banner required before GA4 fires for EU visitors; CCPA opt-out link required for CA visitors; page must load in under 2 seconds on 3G mobile (Lighthouse LCP <2500ms); no third-party scripts loaded before consent
constraints.rationale: GDPR Article 7 and CCPA Section 1798.120 impose consent and opt-out obligations; performance is conversion-critical

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for real-user Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID); GA4 with consent mode for conversion events; Vercel Function logs for lead form submission errors
observability_baseline.rationale: Vercel native analytics for performance; GA4 for business KPIs; serverless logs for form debugging

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment via Vercel GitHub integration; preview deployments for every PR; production promotion on merge to main
execution_preference.rationale: Vercel preview deployments allow stakeholder review of copy and layout changes without engineering handoff

project.goal: High-converting SME lending platform landing page with GDPR/CCPA compliance
project.target_users: Small business owners in UK, EU, and US
project.timeline: Live in 3 weeks; A/B testing week 5

frontend.consent: Cookie consent banner with GA4 consent mode
frontend.ab_testing: Vercel Edge Config for split-URL variant routing

devops.environments: Vercel preview per PR, production on main merge

security.form: Honeypot field, email validation, rate limit on serverless handler

data.crm: HubSpot Contacts API for lead forwarding

constraints.gdpr: No analytics before consent for EU/UK visitors

open_questions.video_host: Vimeo vs YouTube embed for GDPR-safe video: Vimeo no-cookie domain preferred

reasoner_reviews.product: The lead capture form with UTM persistence is the highest-priority conversion element. Previous rounds had UTM parameters captured in the form but the HubSpot forwarding function did not include UTM fields in the Contacts API payload, meaning attribution was lost. This must be confirmed fixed in round 3. The A/B test headline variants targeting 500-session threshold for winner selection is a reasonable sample size for the expected 2-5% conversion rate.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel is the correct choice. The Vercel serverless function for lead form submission must handle HubSpot rate limits gracefully (the HubSpot Contacts API has a 100 req/10s limit). Round 2 introduced a retry queue using Vercel KV but the plan did not specify the TTL for queued leads or the retry backoff strategy. Round 3 must close this gap.
reasoner_reviews.security: GA4 consent mode is correctly specified. The previous plan added a honeypot field but did not specify the field name (must be non-obvious, not 'honeypot', to avoid bot detection). CSP nonce approach for inline Next.js scripts must be documented; Next.js static export does not support server-side nonce injection so a hash-based CSP is required instead. Round 2 plan had unsafe-inline in CSP which must be removed.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GDPR consent must block all analytics scripts, not just GA4 - including Vercel Analytics which also sets a cookie. Vercel Analytics has a cookieless mode that is GDPR-compliant by default; this should replace cookie-based Vercel Analytics for EU visitors. The consent banner must persist preference in localStorage and re-check on each page load.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export: single page (pages/index.tsx or app/page.tsx). Components: HeroSection (A/B headline via Vercel Edge Config variant), VideoEmbed (Vimeo no-cookie player), LeadCaptureForm (React Hook Form with Zod validation), ConsentBanner (GDPR/CCPA logic), UTMPersistence (reads URL params into sessionStorage), CTAButton (variant-aware).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single page scroll flow: Hero (headline + subheadline + primary CTA + secondary trust signals) -> Video (Vimeo embed with consent-gated autoplay) -> Features (3-column decisioning pipeline highlights) -> Social Proof (3 SME testimonials) -> Lead Form (name, email, company, loan_amount_range select, hidden UTM fields, honeypot) -> Footer (GDPR consent link, CCPA opt-out, privacy policy link).
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js next/image for hero background with priority=true for LCP. Vimeo embed loaded with loading=lazy and intersection observer trigger to avoid render-blocking. Tailwind CSS purged to <10KB. No web fonts from external CDN; system font stack. Vercel Analytics script deferred. Target: Lighthouse LCP <1800ms on mobile (tighter than 2500ms compliance requirement).
frontend.accessibility_notes: Consent banner trap focus on mount. Form fields with explicit htmlFor associations. Video section has transcript link. CTA buttons have descriptive aria-label including offer context.

security.auth_design: No authentication required. Public page.
security.abuse_prevention: Lead form serverless function: honeypot field named 'website_url' (common enough to fool scrapers, inconspicuous in DOM); server-side email regex validation; Vercel KV rate limit: 5 submissions per IP per hour with 1-hour sliding window. Duplicate email deduplication: check Vercel KV for submitted email within 24-hour window before forwarding to HubSpot.
security.privacy_controls: Consent banner: no GA4 script injection until user accepts analytics. Vimeo no-cookie embed domain (player.vimeo.com/video/{id}?dnt=1). CCPA opt-out link to opt-out-of-sale page. Consent preference stored in localStorage key 'consent_prefs' with version number for future policy changes.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository. Vercel Edge Network serves static Next.js export. Vercel serverless function (Node.js 20 runtime) handles POST /api/lead. Vercel KV (Redis-compatible) for rate limiting and deduplication. Vercel Edge Config for A/B variant configuration (headline_a, headline_b, active_variant_pct).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR - next build (next export), ESLint, Playwright smoke tests (form submit, consent banner display, UTM persistence). Vercel GitHub integration creates preview deployment for every PR. On merge to main: Vercel auto-deploys to production. No manual approval step for landing page deployments.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics (cookieless mode, GDPR-compliant by default) for real-user Web Vitals. Vercel Function logs (accessible in Vercel dashboard) for lead form errors and HubSpot API failures. GA4 with consent mode: fires only after analytics consent granted; consent mode default denied for EU/UK visitors (geo-detected via Vercel Edge middleware). Weekly GA4 report: demo_request_submitted events by UTM source, variant, country.

previous_plan.title: SME Lending Platform Landing Page - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static export landing page on Vercel with HubSpot lead forwarding (UTM attribution resolved in round 2), GDPR consent banner, GA4 consent mode, and Vercel KV retry queue for HubSpot failures. CSP unsafe-inline persisted and retry queue lacked TTL specification.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced. GDPR consent banner with GA4 consent mode. CSP with unsafe-inline (flagged as ISS-002). Honeypot field and rate limiting on lead form handler.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Vercel GitHub integration. Vercel KV retry queue introduced for HubSpot failures (round 2 addition). TTL and backoff for retry queue not specified (ISS-003).
previous_plan.api_design: POST /api/lead serverless function: validates form, deduplicates, forwards to HubSpot Contacts API with UTM properties mapped. Retry queue on HubSpot failure stored in Vercel KV.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 of an SME lending landing page on Vercel. Both focus issues are closeable: ISS-002 (CSP unsafe-inline) requires switching to hash-based CSP which is achievable with Next.js static export; ISS-003 (retry queue TTL/backoff) is a straightforward specification. ISS-001 (UTM forwarding) was resolved in round 2 and I carry it forward. This is a landing_page project class so no backend server, no database - the only 'backend' is a single Vercel serverless function. Plan quality should be strong: all sections detailed, specific to the SME lending/lead gen domain.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced CSP unsafe-inline with hash-based CSP for Next.js static export. Next.js 14 static export generates inline scripts for hydration; extracted script hashes using next build output and pinned in Content-Security-Policy header via next.config.js headers() or Vercel vercel.json headers array. CSP policy now: script-src 'sha256-{hash1}' 'sha256-{hash2}' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://player.vimeo.com; No unsafe-inline. Hash list updated by CI step that runs next build and extracts hashes using a custom script before setting the header value in vercel.json. Consent banner JavaScript that conditionally loads GA4 is a React component using next/script with strategy=afterInteractive; its hash is also pinned.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP no longer permits arbitrary inline script execution, eliminating the risk of consent-bypassing script injection. GDPR compliance posture improved: GA4 cannot be loaded via inline script injection without matching the pinned hash.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Vercel KV retry queue TTL and backoff: lead queue entries set with EX 86400 (24-hour TTL) so stale undelivered leads expire automatically. Lead form handler implements exponential backoff: retry 1 after 1 second, retry 2 after 5 seconds, retry 3 after 30 seconds. After 3 failed retries, lead entry moved to a dead_letter Vercel KV list with EX 604800 (7-day TTL) and a Vercel Cron job (daily at 06:00 UTC) checks dead_letter list and sends alert to Slack #sales-ops-alerts with lead details for manual CRM entry.
fix_report.expected_outcome: HubSpot API failures no longer silently drop leads. Retry queue has bounded lifetime preventing indefinite accumulation. Dead-letter Slack alert ensures ops team can manually recover any leads that exhausted retries.

target_output.title: SME Lending Platform CI/CD Loan Decisioning Landing Page - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: A high-performance Next.js 14 static export landing page on Vercel Edge Network for an SME lending platform promoting its CI/CD-powered loan decisioning pipeline. Converts small business owner visitors into demo requests via a lead capture form that forwards submissions to HubSpot CRM with full UTM attribution. GDPR cookie consent banner gates GA4 analytics for EU/UK visitors (consent mode default-denied); CCPA opt-out link serves California visitors. CSP uses hash-based policy (no unsafe-inline) with hashes extracted by CI post-build script. Vercel KV handles lead form rate limiting, deduplication, and a retry queue with 24-hour TTL and exponential backoff for HubSpot API failures, with dead-letter Slack alerting. All three issues (ISS-001, ISS-002, ISS-003) resolved.
target_output.architecture_overview: Static export landing page built with Next.js 14 and deployed to Vercel Edge Network. Build-time content is hardcoded (no CMS for this single-page landing); A/B headline variants configured via Vercel Edge Config and selected by Vercel Edge Middleware on visitor-request before serving the page. A single Vercel serverless function (Node.js 20) handles POST /api/lead: input validation, honeypot check, Vercel KV rate-limit check, email deduplication, HubSpot Contacts API forwarding with UTM properties, and Vercel KV retry queue on failure with exponential backoff (1s/5s/30s, then dead-letter with Slack alert). GA4 loaded via React consent context: fires only after analytics consent granted; GA4 consent mode default-denied for EU/UK visitors geo-detected by Vercel Edge Middleware. CSP hash-based policy with hashes extracted by post-build CI script and injected into vercel.json headers. No database, no auth, no persistent infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 (static export, output: export), React 18, TypeScript 5.4, Tailwind CSS v3 JIT, React Hook Form 7, Zod for schema validation. Analytics: GA4 with consent mode via @next/third-parties/google, Vercel Analytics (cookieless mode). Video: Vimeo player with dnt=1 parameter (no-cookie embed). Edge: Vercel Edge Middleware (TypeScript), Vercel Edge Config (A/B variant config). Serverless: Node.js 20 Vercel serverless function, Vercel KV (Redis-compatible for rate limiting, deduplication, retry queue). CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Vercel GitHub integration, Playwright for smoke tests. Security: hash-based CSP via vercel.json headers, HSTS preload.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section with A/B Headline Variants: Vercel Edge Middleware reads Edge Config for active_variant (A or B), sets x-variant header, Next.js renders appropriate headline and CTA copy. Variant assignment cookie set with 30-day expiry. Embedded Video Explainer: Vimeo player with dnt=1 (no tracking before consent); autoplay triggered by IntersectionObserver when section enters viewport after consent. Lead Capture Form: React Hook Form with Zod validation (email format, required fields), hidden UTM fields populated from sessionStorage, honeypot field 'website_url', submission to POST /api/lead. GDPR Consent Banner: EU/UK visitor detection via Vercel Edge Middleware (cf-ipcountry header); consent_prefs stored in localStorage; GA4 and Vimeo tracking enabled only after analytics consent. CCPA Opt-Out: Footer link to /opt-out page (static Astro page explaining California rights); no opt-out sale mechanism needed as no data sold.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 14 static build (CI artifact). Vercel Edge Network (global static asset CDN, sub-50ms TTFB). Vercel Edge Middleware (A/B variant routing, geo-detection for consent mode, HTTPS redirect). Vercel Serverless Function POST /api/lead (Node.js 20, HubSpot forwarding, Vercel KV rate-limit and retry). Vercel KV (rate_limit:{ip}, submitted_emails deduplication set, lead_retry_queue list, dead_letter_leads list). Vercel Edge Config (A/B variant config: headline_a_text, headline_b_text, active_split_pct). Vercel Cron Job (daily dead-letter check and Slack alert). HubSpot Contacts API (lead destination). GA4 (consent-gated analytics). Vercel Analytics (cookieless, GDPR-safe).
target_output.workflows: Lead Submission Workflow: Visitor fills lead form (name, email, company, loan_amount_range, hidden utm_source/utm_medium/utm_campaign from sessionStorage). React Hook Form + Zod validates client-side. Form POSTs to /api/lead. Serverless function checks honeypot field (non-empty = bot, return 200 silently). Checks Vercel KV rate limit (5/IP/hour sliding window). Checks deduplication set (email submitted within 24 hours - return 200 silently). Calls HubSpot Contacts API with mapped properties (firstname, email, company, loan_amount_range, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, hs_lead_source=landing_page). On HubSpot success: adds email to deduplication set (EX 86400), returns 200. On HubSpot failure: stores lead JSON in Vercel KV retry queue (RPUSH lead_retry_queue, EX 86400), triggers async retry with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 30s). After 3 retries: RPUSH dead_letter_leads (EX 604800). Daily Vercel Cron job at 06:00 UTC: LRANGE dead_letter_leads 0 -1, posts each to Slack #sales-ops-alerts, LTRIM dead_letter_leads to remove processed entries. A/B Test Workflow: Vercel Edge Middleware reads Edge Config on every request to /. Assigns variant cookie if absent (50/50 Math.random() split). Sets x-ab-variant header. Next.js static HTML includes both headline variants with data-variant attribute; Edge Middleware response header used by client-side React to show the correct variant. GA4 fires cta_click event with {variant: 'A' | 'B'} dimension on button click (consent-gated).
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on site infrastructure. Transient Vercel KV keys: rate_limit:{ip} (String, EX 3600, incremented per submission), submitted_emails (Set, individual entries with EX 86400), lead_retry_queue (List, JSON payloads, EX 86400), dead_letter_leads (List, JSON payloads, EX 604800). HubSpot contact properties mapped by lead form: firstname, email, company, loan_amount_range (custom), utm_source (custom), utm_medium (custom), utm_campaign (custom), hs_lead_status=new, lifecyclestage=lead, hs_lead_source=landing_page. Vercel Edge Config schema: {headline_a_text: string, headline_b_text: string, active_split_pct: number, video_vimeo_id: string}. GA4 events: page_view, cta_click ({variant, position}), video_play ({vimeo_id, seconds_watched}), lead_form_submit ({variant, utm_source, loan_amount_range}), consent_accepted, consent_rejected.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/lead (Vercel serverless function, Node.js 20): Request body: {name: string, email: string, company: string, loan_amount_range: string, website_url: string (honeypot), utm_source?: string, utm_medium?: string, utm_campaign?: string}. Response: 200 OK (success, bot, or duplicate - no content discrimination to prevent enumeration). Response: 429 Too Many Requests (rate limit exceeded - only exposed to legitimate clients; bot attempts return 200). Vercel Edge Config read: GET https://edge-config.vercel.com/{config_id}/item/headline_a_text (internal Vercel SDK call from Edge Middleware). No public API surface beyond /api/lead.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Vercel (all HTTP redirected to HTTPS by Edge Middleware). HSTS: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload submitted to hstspreload.org. CSP (hash-based, no unsafe-inline): default-src 'none'; script-src 'sha256-{hash1}' 'sha256-{hash2}' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://player.vimeo.com; style-src 'self' 'sha256-{tailwind_hash}'; img-src 'self' data: https://i.vimeocdn.com; connect-src https://www.google-analytics.com https://api.hubapi.com; frame-src https://player.vimeo.com. CSP hashes extracted post-build by scripts/extract-csp-hashes.ts and written to vercel.json headers array. Hashes updated automatically on every CI build. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. GDPR: GA4 consent mode default-denied for EU/UK visitors (cf-ipcountry detection in Edge Middleware). Consent preference in localStorage consent_prefs v2. Vimeo embed with dnt=1. CCPA: opt-out link in footer to /opt-out static page. Form abuse: honeypot field named 'website_url', Vercel KV rate limit 5/IP/hour, email deduplication 24-hour window.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on PR: next build (static export), extract-csp-hashes.ts writes hashes to vercel.json (committed back to branch or passed as Vercel env), ESLint + tsc --noEmit, Playwright smoke tests (lead form submission mock, consent banner display and dismissal, UTM persistence into form hidden fields, FDIC-equivalent disclosure visibility). Vercel GitHub integration creates preview deployment per PR with unique URL for stakeholder review of copy/layout. On merge to main: Vercel auto-deploys to production, Edge Config variant config preserved across deployments. Vercel Cron: daily 06:00 UTC dead-letter Slack alert job. Environment variables: HUBSPOT_API_KEY (Vercel secret), SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL (Vercel secret), VIMEO_VIDEO_ID (non-secret). No Terraform or IaC needed; Vercel project settings managed via vercel.json.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics (cookieless, enabled by default, GDPR-safe) tracks real-user Web Vitals: LCP, CLS, FID per page. GA4 with consent mode: fires only after analytics consent; tracks conversion funnel lead_form_submit events with variant and UTM dimensions. Weekly GA4 report: demo request count by utm_source, utm_campaign, a/b variant, country. Vercel Function logs (dashboard): lead form submissions, HubSpot API response times, retry queue entries, rate limit hits. Vercel Cron execution logs for dead-letter processing. Slack #sales-ops-alerts: dead-letter lead notifications with full lead payload for manual CRM entry. Alert: Vercel Function error rate > 5% in 1 hour triggers email alert to engineering lead via Vercel Alert integration.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan: ~$20/month (preview deployments, Edge Config, Vercel KV 256MB, Cron jobs). GA4: free. HubSpot: covered by existing sales team subscription. Vercel KV: 256MB free on Pro, ~$0.10/GB thereafter (landing page usage: <1MB/month at any realistic lead volume). Total: ~$20/month. Scaling is fully automatic via Vercel Edge Network; 10M monthly visitors require zero architectural changes. HubSpot Contacts API rate limit (100 req/10s) becomes a constraint only above 600 submissions/minute; Vercel KV queue handles burst buffering transparently.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): Next.js 14 static export project scaffold, hero section + lead form + video embed + footer, Vercel serverless function with HubSpot forwarding and UTM mapping, Vercel KV rate limiting and deduplication, GDPR consent banner with GA4 consent mode, Vercel Analytics, hash-based CSP via post-build script, Playwright smoke tests, Vercel preview deployments. Launch. Phase 2 (2 weeks): A/B headline variants via Vercel Edge Config and Edge Middleware, GA4 A/B variant tracking, Vercel KV retry queue with exponential backoff and dead-letter Slack alert, CCPA opt-out static page. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Conversion rate optimization based on A/B results; winner headline variant promoted in Edge Config; savings calculator island (compound interest, no API, pure client-side); social proof section with testimonial rotation; Lighthouse budget tightening to LCP <1800ms.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. next.config.js: output: export enforced; no API routes that require SSR (use Vercel serverless functions at /api/ path instead). CSP hash extraction script must run as part of CI next build step and must fail the build if hashes cannot be extracted from the static output. No third-party scripts added without hash pinning and PR review. React Hook Form + Zod for all form validation; no custom validation logic outside Zod schemas. Playwright tests required for any new interactive section before merge to main. Commit convention: conventional commits (feat/fix/chore) for clean Vercel deployment log. Edge Config changes deployed via Vercel dashboard or CLI; do not store A/B config in code to allow marketing to adjust variant split without PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CSP hash breaks after Next.js version upgrade (hydration script hash changes). Mitigation: CI post-build hash extraction runs on every build, not once; hash list is always current before deployment. Risk: Vercel KV dead-letter queue fills if HubSpot has extended outage. Mitigation: 7-day TTL on dead_letter_leads prevents unbounded growth; daily Slack alert ensures ops awareness within 24 hours. Tradeoff: Vercel Edge Config for A/B vs fully static build per variant - Edge Config adds ~2ms latency at edge vs zero for fully static; accepted for the operational simplicity of marketing-team-controlled variant split. Risk: GA4 consent mode default-denied means EU/UK conversion data is estimated via modeled conversion. Mitigation: documented expectation with marketing team; modeled conversions are GDPR-compliant and sufficient for campaign optimization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Hash-based CSP vs nonce-based: nonce requires server-side HTML generation (not available in static export); hash-based adopted with CI automation to prevent manual maintenance burden. Vimeo vs YouTube: Vimeo player.vimeo.com with dnt=1 selected for GDPR safety; YouTube nocookie domain still sets cookies via postMessage. Retry queue persistence: Vercel KV chosen over Upstash Redis for operational simplicity within Vercel ecosystem; 256MB Pro tier is sufficient for any realistic lead queue volume.

metadata.notes: Round 3 SME lending landing page on Vercel; ISS-001 UTM (round 2), ISS-002 CSP unsafe-inline, ISS-003 retry queue all resolved; strong quality with detailed GDPR, CSP, and HubSpot integration specifics.

profile.domain: Commercial Bank Privileged Access Management Web Application

project_goal.value: Privileged access management web application for commercial bank IT operations governing just-in-time access grants, session recording, and break-glass emergency procedures for production database and server access
project_goal.rationale: Replace manual email-based access request process with auditable JIT access controls mandated by SOX ITGC and FFIEC cybersecurity assessment findings

target_users.value: IT operations engineers (200 users), DBAs (30 users), on-call engineers, CISO and security operations team for oversight
target_users.rationale: Primary users are engineers requesting access; CISO and SecOps have read-only audit and alert oversight

project_class.rationale: Browser-based internal tool with real-time session monitoring; SPA with Go backend

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, data, admin_panel, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack app with real-time session recording feed, admin policy management, and strong auth

complexity_level.rationale: JIT credential issuance, session recording proxy, WebSocket real-time monitoring, and multi-step approval workflow

risk_level.rationale: Governs access to production financial systems; compromise enables unauthorized data access or system manipulation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Session recordings may contain credentials, query results, and system configs classified as bank confidential

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only from bank internal network via VPN; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Keycloak with hardware MFA (FIDO2/YubiKey) for all users; RBAC roles: requester, approver, dba, on_call, security_auditor, admin; session timeout 30 minutes idle
access_model.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 requires hardware MFA for privileged access management systems; 30-min idle timeout per FFIEC guidance

feature_scope.value: JIT access request workflow with two-person approval, time-limited credential issuance via HashiCorp Vault, session recording for all privileged sessions via transparent proxy, break-glass self-grant with CISO page-out and auto-revocation at 4 hours, admin policy management for target system definitions and approval routing rules, real-time active session monitoring dashboard, compliance reporting for SOX ITGC evidence
feature_scope.rationale: Full PAM feature set required for SOX ITGC remediation and FFIEC findings closure

mvp_scope.value: JIT access request and two-person approval workflow, Vault credential issuance for PostgreSQL databases, session recording via SSH proxy, break-glass with CISO alert, basic audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the highest-risk production database access scenario for initial SOX audit scope; server access and compliance reporting in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Hardware MFA (FIDO2/YubiKey) required for all logins; TLS 1.3 on all internal connections; all session recordings encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM; HashiCorp Vault for credential issuance with lease TTL; all privileged actions logged to immutable CloudTrail; no plain-text credentials in application memory after issuance
security_baseline.rationale: SOX ITGC AC controls and NIST SP 800-53 AU-2 mandate comprehensive audit logging and encrypted credential management

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript and Vite; Tanstack Query for server state; Zustand for UI state; WebSocket for real-time session monitoring
frontend_stack.rationale: React with Vite for fast dev iteration; Tanstack Query for access request state machine; WebSocket for live session monitoring feed

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework; GORM for PostgreSQL; Vault Go SDK for credential issuance; SSH proxy in Go for session recording
backend_stack.rationale: Go performance and concurrency appropriate for SSH proxy and session recording; Gin for clean REST API structure

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for application state (access requests, approvals, audit log); Redis 7 for active session state and WebSocket presence; S3-compatible object storage for session recording archives
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational access request and audit data; Redis for ephemeral session state; object storage for potentially large session recording files

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in private subnets, VPC-only access, no internet gateway; ALB internal; session recordings to S3 with SSE-KMS
hosting_target.rationale: Internal-only deployment in private VPC; S3 KMS encryption satisfies confidential data requirement for session archives

privacy_retention_policy.value: Session recordings retained 3 years for SOX ITGC evidence; access request audit log retained 7 years; active session state in Redis TTL-matched to credential lease; break-glass events retained indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX Section 404 ITGC evidence retention and FFIEC cybersecurity documentation requirements

future_scope.value: Server access (SSH and RDP) session recording in addition to database, integration with SIEM for real-time anomaly detection, self-service access request mobile app for on-call engineers
future_scope.rationale: Database access is highest risk; server access and SIEM integration are Phase 2

constraints.value: Must not store Vault-issued credentials in application database; credential passed directly to requester and proxy only; deployment within bank VPC with no internet-facing components; all access request state changes must emit CloudTrail events
constraints.rationale: Zero-credential-storage-in-app is a hard security requirement; CloudTrail emission is mandated for SOX audit trail

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks; AWS CloudTrail for all privileged access events; structured JSON logging with correlation IDs; PagerDuty for break-glass events and system health alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog APM for service health; CloudTrail for immutable audit; PagerDuty for break-glass page-out is a hard requirement

execution_preference.value: 4-week sprints; security review gate before each phase release; GitLab CI with mandatory security scan and SAST on all PRs
execution_preference.rationale: PAM system changes require security sign-off; SAST catches credential handling bugs before review

compliance_context.value: SOX Section 404 IT general controls (access management, segregation of duties); FFIEC Cybersecurity Assessment Tool CAT; NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 (account management), AC-3 (access enforcement), AU-2 (audit events)
compliance_context.rationale: SOX ITGC and FFIEC findings drive this project; NIST 800-53 is the control framework

project.goal: JIT PAM system replacing email-based access requests for production DB and server access
project.target_users: 230 IT operations engineers and DBAs; CISO oversight
project.timeline: MVP SOX audit scope in 8 weeks; Phase 2 FFIEC remediation in 16 weeks

frontend.realtime: WebSocket for live session monitoring dashboard

backend.framework: Gin + GORM
backend.vault: HashiCorp Vault Go SDK for PostgreSQL dynamic credentials

security.auth: Keycloak FIDO2/YubiKey hardware MFA
security.credentials: Vault dynamic credentials, never stored in app DB
security.audit: CloudTrail for all access state changes
