devops.ci: GitLab CI with SAST and security gate

constraints.cloudtrail: All access state changes emit CloudTrail events

open_questions.session_recording_format: Asciicast v2 vs custom binary format for SSH session recordings

confirmed_decisions.vault_backend: HashiCorp Vault with PostgreSQL dynamic secrets engine
confirmed_decisions.session_proxy: Custom Go SSH proxy with session recording

reasoner_reviews.product: The two-person approval workflow with break-glass override is the core differentiator from simpler PAM tools. The round 2 plan had a race condition in the approval state machine where two concurrent approval actions could both succeed and issue duplicate credentials. This must be confirmed resolved in round 3. The compliance reporting module for SOX ITGC evidence export is now in scope for this round per the auditor feedback.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Go SSH proxy for session recording is architecturally sound. Round 2 used a polling-based approach for approval status in the frontend which created 2-5 second lag in access grant notification. WebSocket push notification from the backend approval event is the correct fix and was specified as a focus issue. The Vault credential lease TTL must match the access request approved_duration exactly - the previous plan had a fixed 4-hour Vault lease regardless of the approved duration, which allowed credential use beyond the approved window.
reasoner_reviews.security: Break-glass auto-revocation at 4 hours is a hard requirement. Round 2 implemented a cron-based revocation check but cron granularity (1-minute) meant credentials could remain valid for up to 1 minute beyond the 4-hour window. Vault lease TTL should be set to exactly 240 minutes at issuance to guarantee hard expiry without application-layer revocation dependency. The session recording encryption key rotation procedure was missing from round 2 - must be specified for SOX key management controls.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: CloudTrail event emission is a hard constraint. Round 2 emitted CloudTrail custom events via PutEvents but the IAM role had overly broad permissions (cloudtrail:*). The IAM policy must be scoped to cloudtrail:PutEvents only with specific event bus ARN condition. SOX ITGC evidence requires break-glass events are never deletable from CloudTrail; the CloudTrail log integrity validation must be confirmed enabled.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 was strong overall but the approval state machine race condition and polling-based frontend notification are the most impactful remaining issues. The fix_report for round 3 must address the PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE lock on the approval action endpoint to prevent duplicate credential issuance, and the WebSocket push notification for access grant status.

backend.service_design: Go 1.22 with Gin. Domain modules: access_requests (JIT request lifecycle), approvals (two-person approval state machine with SELECT FOR UPDATE locking), credentials (Vault dynamic credential issuance and lease management), session_proxy (SSH proxy with asciicast v2 recording), break_glass (self-grant with CISO page-out), audit (CloudTrail PutEvents and local audit_log), compliance (SOX ITGC evidence report generation). WebSocket hub for real-time session monitoring and access grant notifications.
backend.api_patterns: REST API: POST /api/v1/access-requests (create JIT request), POST /api/v1/access-requests/:id/approve (two-person approval with PG SELECT FOR UPDATE), POST /api/v1/access-requests/:id/revoke (early revocation), POST /api/v1/break-glass (self-grant), GET /api/v1/sessions (active sessions list), GET /api/v1/sessions/:id/recording (stream asciicast). WebSocket /ws/sessions (real-time session events and approval notifications). GET /api/v1/compliance/sox-report (ITGC evidence export PDF).
backend.failure_handling: Vault credential issuance failure: access request transitions to failed state with error_code, CloudTrail event emitted, requester notified via WebSocket. PagerDuty alert on Vault connectivity failure. Approval lock contention: 409 Conflict returned to second approver attempting concurrent approval; frontend shows 'Approval already completed by {approver_name}'.
backend.scaling_notes: PAM system handles 230 users; peak load is incident response scenarios with 20-30 concurrent access requests. ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks handles this comfortably. Session proxy is stateful (SSH connection); affinity routing via ALB sticky sessions on session ID header.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA: pages/AccessRequests (list, create, approve), pages/BreakGlass (self-grant form with MFA re-challenge), pages/Sessions (active sessions dashboard with WebSocket feed), pages/Admin (target system policies, approval routing, user roles), pages/ComplianceReports (SOX ITGC evidence export). Shared: RequestStatusBadge (PENDING/APPROVED/ACTIVE/EXPIRED/REVOKED), SessionTimeline, ApprovalActionPanel.
frontend.state_management: Tanstack Query for access request and session data with 30-second poll fallback. Zustand WebSocket store: session_events[] and approval_notifications[] updated by WebSocket messages. On access request approval, WebSocket message triggers Tanstack Query cache invalidation for the specific request, surfacing approved status without polling lag.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Access Request Flow: Requester selects target system (PostgreSQL prod-db-01), justification, duration (1h/2h/4h), submits. Request enters PENDING_APPROVAL state. Approvers receive WebSocket notification in Sessions dashboard. Approver opens request, reviews justification, clicks Approve (second approval triggers credential issuance). Requester receives WebSocket notification with credential details displayed in modal for 60 seconds then auto-hidden. Break-glass flow requires hardware MFA re-challenge before self-grant proceeds.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Vault-issued credentials displayed in modal for 60 seconds only, then cleared from React state (not persisted in sessionStorage or Tanstack Query cache). Break-glass form disables submit until FIDO2 re-authentication succeeds. WebSocket connection uses Keycloak JWT bearer token in Upgrade request header.

security.auth_design: Keycloak 24 with FIDO2/YubiKey hardware token as second factor for all users. Primary factor: corporate LDAP password via Keycloak LDAP federation. Break-glass procedure requires hardware MFA re-challenge (step-up authentication). OIDC tokens issued with 30-minute expiry; refresh tokens require hardware MFA re-auth after 8 hours.
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced in Go middleware: requester can create requests and view own sessions. approver can approve requests for target systems in their approval scope. dba can approve and create DB-class access requests. on_call role grants break-glass eligibility. security_auditor has read-only access to all audit logs and session recordings. admin manages policies and user role assignments.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every access_request state transition emits a CloudTrail PutEvents call with detail: {request_id, requester_id, target_system, action, approvers, credential_lease_id, timestamp}. IAM role scoped to cloudtrail:PutEvents on specific custom event bus ARN only. CloudTrail log integrity validation enabled (SHA-256 digest files). Local audit_log table in PostgreSQL is append-only (row-level security: GRANT INSERT only, no UPDATE/DELETE). Break-glass events tagged with severity:critical in CloudTrail detail.
security.incident_response_notes: Break-glass trigger: on_call engineer submits break-glass form with FIDO2 re-auth. Backend issues Vault credential immediately (no approval wait), sets lease TTL to exactly 240 minutes, emits CloudTrail event with severity:critical. PagerDuty alert fires to CISO on-call and security operations team within 30 seconds via PD Events API. Session recording starts immediately on proxy connection. Auto-revocation: Vault lease TTL is the hard revocation mechanism; no application-layer cron dependency for break-glass expiry.

data.entities: access_requests (id UUID PK, requester_id FK, target_system_id FK, justification TEXT, duration_minutes INT, status ENUM pending_approval/approved/active/expired/revoked/failed, vault_lease_id VARCHAR, approved_at, activated_at, expires_at, created_at). approvals (id UUID PK, access_request_id FK, approver_id FK, action ENUM approved/rejected, approved_at, comment TEXT). target_systems (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, system_type ENUM postgresql/redis/ec2, host, port, vault_role VARCHAR, requires_approver_count INT DEFAULT 2, approval_routing_group_id FK). sessions (id UUID PK, access_request_id FK, proxy_session_id VARCHAR, started_at, ended_at, recording_s3_key VARCHAR, bytes_transferred BIGINT). audit_log (id UUID PK, actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, detail_json, cloudtrail_event_id, timestamp). break_glass_events (id UUID PK, on_call_engineer_id, target_system_id, justification TEXT, vault_lease_id, pagerduty_incident_id, auto_revoked_at, created_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for access_requests, approvals, target_systems, sessions, audit_log, break_glass_events. Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for active session WebSocket state and Vault lease_id -> request_id mapping for revocation lookup. S3 bucket (SSE-KMS with dedicated KMS key, versioning enabled) for session recording asciicast files. KMS key rotation annual (AWS managed).
data.consistency_notes: Approval action uses PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE on access_request row to prevent duplicate credential issuance on concurrent approval submissions. Vault lease creation wrapped in PostgreSQL transaction: lease_id written to access_requests.vault_lease_id and status set to approved atomically. If Vault call fails, transaction rolls back and request remains pending_approval.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in private subnets (no internet gateway). ALB internal (VPC-only). Three services: pam-api (2-4 tasks, Go backend), session-proxy (2 tasks with ALB sticky sessions), pam-frontend (2 tasks, Nginx serving React build). HashiCorp Vault cluster on ECS Fargate in a separate security group with restricted ingress from pam-api only. PostgreSQL on RDS Multi-AZ in private subnets. ElastiCache Redis in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: stages: lint (golangci-lint, eslint), test (Go test with 85% coverage gate, React Jest), sast (Semgrep for Go credential handling patterns, Snyk for dependency CVEs), build (Docker multi-stage, push ECR), security-review-gate (manual approval by Security team before staging deploy), deploy-staging, integration-tests (Playwright against staging), deploy-production (manual trigger only). All Docker images signed with Cosign.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service update failure: automatic rollback on task health check failure (3 consecutive failures). Manual rollback: update ECS service to previous task definition revision via GitLab pipeline manual trigger. Vault config changes: separate GitLab pipeline with mandatory security review gate before apply. Database migrations: Flyway with versioned scripts; no destructive migrations in production without DBA review.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on all ECS tasks. Structured JSON logs with request_id, user_id, access_request_id correlation. Key metrics: access_request_approval_duration_p95, break_glass_event_rate, session_recording_bytes_per_session, vault_credential_issuance_latency. PagerDuty: break_glass_event (critical, CISO page-out), vault_connectivity_failure (high, DevOps), pam_api_error_rate > 1% (high).

ISS-001.title: Approval state machine race condition allows duplicate credential issuance
ISS-001.detail: Two concurrent approvers could both submit approval actions within the same millisecond, both reading status=pending_approval, both triggering Vault credential issuance, resulting in two active leases for the same access request.

history.note: Identified in architect review
Partial fix: added application-level check but no DB lock
PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE on access_request row in approval transaction; duplicate approval returns 409

ISS-002.title: Frontend access grant notification uses polling causing 2-5 second lag
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 frontend polled GET /api/v1/access-requests/:id every 5 seconds to detect approval. In incident response scenarios, a 5-second delay in receiving credentials can be operationally critical.

history.note: Polling identified as latency issue
WebSocket hub added to backend but frontend still used polling
Frontend Zustand WebSocket store receives approval notification instantly; Tanstack Query cache invalidated on WebSocket event

ISS-003.title: Vault credential lease TTL fixed at 4 hours regardless of approved_duration
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 Vault credential issuance always set lease TTL to 4 hours. If a user was approved for 1-hour access, their Vault credential remained valid for 4 hours, violating the least-privilege principle and the SOX access duration control.

history.note: Fixed TTL in round 2 identified by security review
Vault lease TTL now set to access_request.duration_minutes * 60 at issuance

ISS-004.title: CloudTrail IAM role overly permissive (cloudtrail:*)
ISS-004.detail: Round 2 IAM role for CloudTrail event emission had cloudtrail:* Action. Per least-privilege, should be scoped to cloudtrail:PutEvents with a specific event bus ARN condition.

history.note: Identified in security review
IAM policy updated to cloudtrail:PutEvents with Condition: ArnEquals on custom event bus

focus_issues.title: Approval state machine race condition allows duplicate credential issuance
focus_issues.detail: Concurrent approvers can both trigger Vault credential issuance for the same access request.

focus_issues.title: Frontend access grant notification uses polling causing 2-5 second lag
focus_issues.detail: Frontend polls every 5 seconds for approval status; WebSocket push not wired to frontend Zustand store.

focus_issues.title: Vault credential lease TTL fixed at 4 hours regardless of approved_duration
focus_issues.detail: Vault lease always 4 hours regardless of 1h/2h/4h approved duration selection.

latest_recommendations: Use PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE in the approval transaction to prevent duplicate Vault credential issuance
Wire WebSocket approval notification to Tanstack Query cache invalidation in React frontend to eliminate polling
Set Vault lease TTL to access_request.duration_minutes * 60 at issuance time, not a fixed 4-hour value
Add SOX ITGC compliance evidence report endpoint to complete audit trail package

previous_plan.title: Bank PAM Application - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 + Gin PAM web app with Keycloak FIDO2/YubiKey auth, HashiCorp Vault dynamic PostgreSQL credentials, Go SSH proxy with asciicast session recording, and WebSocket hub (not yet wired to frontend). ISS-001, ISS-002, ISS-003 unresolved.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak hardware MFA. Vault dynamic credentials. CloudTrail PutEvents (IAM scope fixed in round 2). Break-glass CISO PagerDuty alert. Session recordings S3 SSE-KMS. Approval race condition unresolved.
previous_plan.api_design: REST: POST /api/v1/access-requests, POST /api/v1/access-requests/:id/approve, POST /api/v1/break-glass. WebSocket /ws/sessions hub (backend only, not consumed by frontend).
previous_plan.data_model: access_requests, approvals, target_systems, sessions, audit_log, break_glass_events in PostgreSQL.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 of a bank PAM web application. Three focus issues: ISS-001 (critical - approval race condition, needs SELECT FOR UPDATE in PostgreSQL transaction wrapping Vault call), ISS-002 (high - frontend polling replaced by WebSocket push wired to Tanstack Query), ISS-003 (high - Vault TTL must match approved_duration_minutes). ISS-004 was resolved in round 2. All four issues should be marked resolved in round 3. This is a strong quality target: the PAM domain requires very specific security control documentation (NIST 800-53, SOX ITGC, Vault lease management, CloudTrail). Plan must contain confidential data handling, compliance reporting, and real-time session monitoring specifics throughout.

fix_report.action_taken: Approval handler now wraps the entire approval action in a PostgreSQL transaction using SELECT FOR UPDATE on the access_request row: BEGIN; SELECT id, status FROM access_requests WHERE id=$1 FOR UPDATE; verify status=pending_approval (else return 409); INSERT INTO approvals ...; check approvals count >= target_system.requires_approver_count; if threshold met: call Vault Go SDK to issue dynamic credential with TTL=duration_minutes*60; UPDATE access_requests SET status=approved, vault_lease_id=..., expires_at=NOW()+duration; COMMIT. Concurrent second approver blocks on SELECT FOR UPDATE lock, reads status=approved after first approver commits, returns HTTP 409 Conflict with body {error: 'Approval already completed', approved_by: 'alice@bank.com'}.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent approval submissions are serialized by PostgreSQL row lock. Only one Vault credential lease is issued per access request. Duplicate credential issuance risk eliminated.

fix_report.action_taken: Frontend Zustand WebSocket store now subscribes to approval_granted and access_activated WebSocket message types. On receipt of approval_granted for the active user's pending request: queryClient.invalidateQueries(['access-request', requestId]) triggers immediate re-fetch, surfacing approved status and credential modal within <100ms of approval. The 5-second polling interval in the AccessRequestDetail component is removed; WebSocket is the sole notification mechanism with Tanstack Query as fallback on page refresh.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Access grant notification reaches requester within 100ms of approval action, reducing incident response latency from 2-5 seconds to sub-second.

fix_report.action_taken: Vault dynamic credential issuance now sets lease TTL dynamically: vaultClient.Write('database/creds/' + target_system.vault_role, map[string]interface{}{'ttl': strconv.Itoa(request.DurationMinutes * 60) + 's'}). The break-glass procedure retains a fixed 240-minute (4-hour) TTL. Vault max_ttl for the database role is set to 14400 seconds (4 hours) to enforce the hard upper bound. access_requests.expires_at is set to created_at + duration_minutes at approval time and displayed in the credential modal countdown timer.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Vault credential lease expiry matches the approved access duration exactly. 1-hour approved access results in credential valid for exactly 3600 seconds, satisfying SOX ITGC least-privilege access duration control.

target_output.title: Bank Privileged Access Management Application - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 + Gin backend, React 18 + Vite frontend PAM web application for 230 commercial bank IT operations users governing just-in-time production database access. JIT access requests go through a two-person approval workflow with PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE locking (ISS-001 resolved) to prevent duplicate HashiCorp Vault dynamic credential issuance. Approved credential notifications reach requesters via WebSocket push (ISS-002 resolved) with Tanstack Query cache invalidation in under 100ms. Vault lease TTL is set dynamically to match the approved access duration (1h/2h/4h) rather than a fixed 4 hours (ISS-003 resolved). Break-glass procedure issues a 4-hour Vault lease immediately, page-outs CISO via PagerDuty, and records a mandatory session via the Go SSH proxy with asciicast v2 recording to S3 SSE-KMS. CloudTrail PutEvents on all access state changes with IAM policy scoped to the custom event bus ARN. SOX ITGC compliance evidence report endpoint generates a signed PDF of access events for audit packages.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA (Vite build, Nginx on ECS Fargate) communicating over HTTPS to Go 1.22 Gin REST API (ECS Fargate, private VPC subnet). Go backend integrates with Keycloak 24 (OIDC/FIDO2 auth), HashiCorp Vault (ECS Fargate, separate security group) for dynamic PostgreSQL credential issuance, and Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for WebSocket session state. PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) stores access request lifecycle, approvals, audit log, and break-glass events. Session recordings stream through a separate Go SSH proxy service (ECS Fargate with ALB sticky sessions) to S3 SSE-KMS. WebSocket hub in the Go API pushes approval events and session state changes to connected React clients; Zustand store receives WebSocket messages and triggers Tanstack Query cache invalidation for zero-lag approval notification. All access state transitions emit CloudTrail PutEvents to a custom event bus. CloudTrail log integrity validation (SHA-256 digest) is enabled. Internal ALB only; no internet-facing components.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, GORM 2.0, HashiCorp Vault Go SDK v2, golang-jwt/jwt v5, gorilla/websocket. Auth: Keycloak 24 with FIDO2/YubiKey hardware tokens (Keycloak-x via ECS). Frontend: React 18, TypeScript 5.4, Vite 5.3, Tanstack Query v5, Zustand 4, React Hook Form 7, Radix UI. Infra: ECS Fargate, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3 SSE-KMS, HashiCorp Vault 1.16 on Fargate, AWS CloudTrail custom event bus. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Semgrep SAST, Snyk, Cosign image signing. Observability: Datadog APM, CloudTrail, PagerDuty Events API v2.
target_output.functional_feature_map: JIT Access Request: Requester selects target system (e.g., prod-db-01 PostgreSQL), justification text, and duration (1h/2h/4h). Request logged to access_requests (status: pending_approval), CloudTrail event emitted, approvers notified via WebSocket. Two-Person Approval: Approvers see pending requests in their approval scope. First approver action recorded; second approver action triggers SELECT FOR UPDATE Vault credential issuance; requester notified via WebSocket in <100ms. Credential Modal: Displays Vault-issued username, password, and connection string for 60 seconds only; countdown timer shows remaining access window. Session Recording: SSH proxy intercepts all database connections during active access window; records asciicast v2 stream to S3; recording accessible only to security_auditor and admin roles. Break-Glass Procedure: on_call engineer triggers break-glass with FIDO2 re-challenge; credentials issued immediately (4-hour Vault TTL); PagerDuty CISO alert fires within 30 seconds. SOX Compliance Report: security_auditor exports PDF of all access events (requests, approvals, sessions, break-glass) for specified date range, signed with app private key.
target_output.system_components: pam-api ECS Fargate service (Go Gin: REST API, WebSocket hub, Vault credential issuance, CloudTrail PutEvents, compliance report generator). session-proxy ECS Fargate service (Go SSH proxy with asciicast v2 recording to S3; ALB sticky sessions on session-id header). pam-frontend ECS Fargate service (Nginx serving React 18 Vite build). HashiCorp Vault ECS Fargate cluster (PostgreSQL secrets engine; restricted ingress from pam-api security group only; Vault Raft HA storage). Keycloak 24 ECS Fargate cluster (FIDO2/YubiKey authentication; LDAP federation to bank AD). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (access_requests, approvals, target_systems, sessions, audit_log, break_glass_events). ElastiCache Redis 7 (WebSocket session registry, Vault lease -> request_id mapping). S3 bucket SSE-KMS (session recording asciicast files, compliance report PDFs). AWS CloudTrail custom event bus (immutable audit, log integrity validation enabled). PagerDuty Events API v2 (break-glass and system alerts).
target_output.workflows: JIT Access Request and Approval Workflow: Requester POSTs /api/v1/access-requests with target_system_id, justification, duration_minutes. pam-api inserts access_request (status=pending_approval), emits CloudTrail event {action: ACCESS_REQUEST_CREATED}. WebSocket hub broadcasts approval_required message to all users in the target system's approval_routing_group. Approver 1 POSTs /api/v1/access-requests/:id/approve. Handler inserts approval record. Approver count still below threshold (1 of 2). Approver 2 concurrently POSTs /api/v1/access-requests/:id/approve. Handler begins PostgreSQL transaction: SELECT id, status FROM access_requests WHERE id=$1 FOR UPDATE - blocks if Approver 1's transaction is in progress. After Approver 1 commits: Approver 2 reads status. If now approved (race condition case), returns 409. Approver 2 is first to complete: transaction inserts approval, checks count=2 (threshold met), calls Vault Write database/creds/{vault_role} with ttl={duration_minutes*60}s, updates access_request (status=approved, vault_lease_id, expires_at=NOW()+INTERVAL), commits transaction. WebSocket hub broadcasts approval_granted to requester's WebSocket connection. Frontend Zustand WebSocket subscriber calls queryClient.invalidateQueries(['access-request', id]); React UI re-renders with credential modal displaying Vault credentials for 60 seconds. Break-Glass Emergency Workflow: on_call engineer submits break-glass form, triggers FIDO2 re-authentication challenge in browser. On FIDO2 success, POSTs /api/v1/break-glass with target_system_id and justification. pam-api issues Vault credential immediately (TTL=14400s fixed), inserts break_glass_event, emits CloudTrail event {action: BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVATED, severity: critical}. Calls PagerDuty Events API v2 POST /v2/enqueue with dedup_key=break_glass_{engineer_id}_{date}, severity=critical, routing_key=CISO_on_call_service_key. session-proxy is notified via Redis pub/sub to record the session. Vault TTL hard-expires credentials after 4 hours; no application-layer revocation cron needed.
target_output.data_model: access_requests (id UUID PK, requester_id UUID FK, target_system_id UUID FK, justification TEXT NOT NULL, duration_minutes INT CHECK (duration_minutes IN (60,120,240)), status ENUM pending_approval/approved/active/expired/revoked/failed NOT NULL DEFAULT pending_approval, vault_lease_id VARCHAR, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, activated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). approvals (id UUID PK, access_request_id UUID FK, approver_id UUID FK, action ENUM approved/rejected NOT NULL, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, comment TEXT). target_systems (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR NOT NULL, system_type ENUM postgresql/redis/ec2 NOT NULL, host VARCHAR NOT NULL, port INT NOT NULL, vault_role VARCHAR NOT NULL, requires_approver_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 2, approval_routing_group_id UUID FK). sessions (id UUID PK, access_request_id UUID FK NOT NULL, proxy_session_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, recording_s3_key VARCHAR, bytes_transferred BIGINT). audit_log (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID NOT NULL, action VARCHAR NOT NULL, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, detail_json JSONB, cloudtrail_event_id VARCHAR, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). break_glass_events (id UUID PK, on_call_engineer_id UUID FK, target_system_id UUID FK, justification TEXT, vault_lease_id VARCHAR, pagerduty_incident_id VARCHAR, fido2_assertion_id VARCHAR, auto_revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). Row-level security on audit_log: GRANT INSERT only; no UPDATE/DELETE to any application role.
target_output.api_design: REST (internal ALB, VPC-only): POST /api/v1/access-requests (create JIT request, requester role). POST /api/v1/access-requests/:id/approve (two-person approval; SELECT FOR UPDATE; approver role). POST /api/v1/access-requests/:id/revoke (early revocation; approver or admin; Vault lease revoke). GET /api/v1/access-requests (list with filters: status, requester, target_system, date range). POST /api/v1/break-glass (on_call role; FIDO2 step-up required; immediate credential issuance). GET /api/v1/sessions (security_auditor and admin only; active and historical). GET /api/v1/sessions/:id/recording (stream asciicast from S3; security_auditor and admin only). GET /api/v1/compliance/sox-report?start_date=&end_date= (generates signed PDF; security_auditor and admin). WebSocket /ws/sessions: authenticated with Keycloak JWT bearer in Upgrade header; messages: approval_required, approval_granted, access_activated, session_started, session_ended, break_glass_activated. All endpoints require Keycloak JWT; role checked per endpoint; 30-minute session timeout enforced by Keycloak token expiry.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak 24 FIDO2/YubiKey hardware MFA for all users; primary LDAP password + FIDO2 second factor. Break-glass requires step-up FIDO2 re-authentication. OIDC tokens 30-minute expiry; refresh requires hardware MFA after 8 hours idle. HashiCorp Vault dynamic PostgreSQL credentials: lease TTL = access_request.duration_minutes * 60 seconds. Vault max_ttl for database role = 14400 seconds (hard ceiling). Break-glass Vault TTL fixed at 14400 seconds (exact 4-hour expiry, no application-layer cron needed). Vault credential never stored in application database; passed directly to requester via WebSocket approval_granted message and credential modal (displayed 60 seconds only, then cleared from React state). Credentials not in sessionStorage, localStorage, or browser history. Session recordings encrypted at rest via S3 SSE-KMS; dedicated KMS key with annual rotation. CloudTrail PutEvents: IAM role with Action=cloudtrail:PutEvents and Condition ArnEquals on custom event bus ARN. CloudTrail log integrity validation (SHA-256 digest files) enabled on the custom event bus. audit_log table: row-level security INSERT only; break_glass_events INSERT only; no UPDATE/DELETE for any application service account. NIST 800-53 AC-2: access request lifecycle with approval workflow. AC-3: RBAC enforced in Go middleware (requester/approver/dba/on_call/security_auditor/admin). AU-2: CloudTrail events for all access state transitions. SOX ITGC: segregation of duties enforced (requester cannot approve own request; checked in approval handler). FFIEC: comprehensive audit trail, session recording, break-glass with CISO notification.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline stages: lint (golangci-lint strict mode, ESLint), test (Go test -race -coverprofile 85% gate, React Jest), sast (Semgrep rules for Go credential handling patterns and hardcoded secrets detection, Snyk for known CVEs in dependencies), build (Docker multi-stage: Go binary in distroless/static, React Vite build in Nginx alpine; images signed with Cosign), security-review-gate (manual approval by Security Team lead in GitLab environment), deploy-staging (ECS update via AWS CLI; Vault config apply via separate GitLab pipeline with mandatory security review), integration-tests (Playwright: JIT request happy path, approval workflow, break-glass flow against staging), deploy-production (manual trigger only; tagged release). ECS services: pam-api (min 2, max 4 tasks, CPU 60% scale-out), session-proxy (min 2 tasks, ALB sticky sessions on X-Proxy-Session-ID header, max 10 tasks on connection count). Vault HA: 3-node Raft cluster on ECS with Vault auto-unseal via AWS KMS. PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ failover ~60 seconds; pam-api uses connection pool with 30-second reconnect backoff. Secrets: Vault dynamic credentials for pam-api DB access (meta-circular design); AWS Secrets Manager for Keycloak admin credentials and PagerDuty routing key.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on pam-api and session-proxy tasks. Structured JSON logs: {request_id, user_id, access_request_id, target_system, action, vault_lease_id, duration_ms}. Domain metrics: access_request_approval_duration_p95 (target: <30 seconds), break_glass_event_rate (alert on >2/hour), vault_credential_issuance_latency_p99, session_recording_bytes_per_session, websocket_connected_clients. CloudTrail events are the authoritative audit trail; Datadog APM for operational health only. PagerDuty integrations: break_glass_activated (critical, CISO on-call via dedicated routing key), vault_connectivity_failure (high, DevOps on-call), pam_api_error_rate_p99_above_1pct (high). Weekly SOX compliance report auto-generated and emailed to security_auditor distribution list every Monday 07:00 UTC via Fargate scheduled task.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate (pam-api 2-4 tasks, session-proxy 2-10 tasks, frontend 2 tasks, Keycloak 2 tasks): ~$280/month at baseline. Vault ECS (3 tasks): ~$90/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large): ~$180/month. ElastiCache Redis (cache.r6g.large): ~$80/month. S3 + KMS for session recordings: ~$30/month (estimated 50GB/month at 230 users). Datadog APM (230 hosts/tasks): ~$300/month. PagerDuty: ~$60/month. Total: ~$1,020/month. Scaling: session-proxy scales to 10 tasks for 100 concurrent SSH sessions; Vault HA with 3 nodes handles 1,000 credential issuances/minute (well above PAM requirement).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks, SOX ITGC scope): Keycloak FIDO2 auth, access_requests + approvals workflow with SELECT FOR UPDATE locking, Vault dynamic PostgreSQL credentials with dynamic TTL, Go SSH session proxy with asciicast recording to S3, break-glass with CISO PagerDuty page-out, WebSocket approval notification, CloudTrail event emission, audit_log append-only table, basic compliance evidence export. Security review gate before production deployment. Phase 2 (8 weeks, FFIEC remediation scope): Server access (EC2 SSH and Windows RDP) session recording, SIEM integration (CloudTrail events forwarded to Splunk via Kinesis Firehose), compliance report PDF generation with digital signature, admin policy management UI for target system onboarding, SOX evidence report scheduling. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Mobile companion app (read-only access request status for on-call engineers), anomaly detection rules for unusual access patterns (e.g., break-glass outside business hours), Vault secret zero rotation automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint strict mode required in CI. No credentials in application logs (golangci-lint credential leak rule + Semgrep custom rule: no fmt.Sprintf with vault_lease_id). Vault SDK calls must use WithContext with 10-second timeout; no unbounded blocking on Vault API. PostgreSQL transactions with SELECT FOR UPDATE must use explicit BEGIN/COMMIT; GORM autocommit must be disabled for approval handler. React: Vault credentials displayed in modal must set a 60-second useEffect cleanup that clears the credential state; no credential in component props persisting after modal close. WebSocket reconnection: exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s); JWT re-validation on reconnect. Security gate checklist: every PR touching approval_handler.go or break_glass_handler.go requires Security Team review before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HashiCorp Vault downtime blocks all access request approvals. Mitigation: 3-node Vault HA Raft cluster; pam-api returns 503 with user-facing message 'Credential system temporarily unavailable; contact security operations' rather than silently failing. Break-glass retains access to operator-level emergency credentials outside Vault as a last resort. Risk: session-proxy scaling during major incident (30+ engineers requesting access simultaneously). Mitigation: session-proxy scales to 10 tasks via ECS; ALB connection draining prevents mid-session disruption. Risk: Keycloak FIDO2 hardware token failure. Mitigation: Keycloak account recovery procedure via security operations team; spare YubiKey provisioning for on-call engineers. Tradeoff: Vault on ECS Fargate vs HCP Vault (managed): ECS chosen for VPC-isolation requirement (no cloud-provider-managed control plane); complexity accepted. Tradeoff: Asciicast v2 format for session recording: human-readable replay via asciinema player; accepted over custom binary for easier forensic analysis without custom tooling.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Asciicast v2 selected over custom binary for SSH session recording: standard format with asciinema player support simplifies forensic replay for security auditors without custom tooling. Vault HA storage: Raft (integrated storage) selected over Consul backend to avoid an additional service dependency. Two-approver threshold: configurable per target_system.requires_approver_count, defaulting to 2; single-approver allowed for lower-criticality systems with explicit admin override and audit trail. SOX compliance report signing: app RSA private key stored in Vault PKI secrets engine; certificate issued per report and attached to PDF for authenticity verification.

metadata.notes: Round 3 bank PAM app; ISS-001 (approval race condition SELECT FOR UPDATE), ISS-002 (WebSocket push), ISS-003 (Vault dynamic TTL), ISS-004 (CloudTrail IAM) all resolved; strong quality with SOX ITGC, NIST 800-53, Vault, and session recording specifics throughout.

profile.domain: Banking Microservices Event Infrastructure

project_goal.value: Go SDK providing typed Kafka producer/consumer wrappers, dead-letter queue management, and Confluent Schema Registry integration for banking microservices teams
project_goal.rationale: Eliminate per-team reimplementation of event-driven messaging boilerplate across decomposed banking services

target_users.value: Internal Go engineering teams building banking microservices (payments, accounts, ledger, notifications)
target_users.rationale: SDK consumers are internal developers on decomposed service teams

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Go module, no hosted runtime required

capabilities.rationale: SDK wraps Kafka broker interactions and schema registry; no UI or auth surface

complexity_level.rationale: Typed generics, schema evolution, DLQ management, and backpressure handling require advanced Go design

risk_level.rationale: SDK misuse could cause message loss or schema conflicts in production banking pipelines

data_sensitivity.rationale: Event payloads may contain account references but SDK itself does not store data

external_exposure.rationale: SDK published to internal Go module proxy, not public registries

access_model.value: mTLS for Kafka broker authentication; module proxy access via internal network only
access_model.rationale: Broker-level mTLS required; module distribution controlled via internal proxy

feature_scope.value: Typed Kafka producer and consumer generics, schema registry client with auto-registration, dead-letter topic routing, consumer group lag monitoring, backpressure callback hooks, offset checkpoint management
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set covering message lifecycle from publish through DLQ handling

mvp_scope.value: Typed producer/consumer wrappers with schema registry auto-registration and DLQ routing
mvp_scope.rationale: Core messaging primitives needed before backpressure and offset management

security_baseline.value: mTLS client certificates for broker connections, payload encryption for account data fields, no secret storage in SDK config structs
security_baseline.rationale: Internal data governance policy mandates encryption of account-bearing event payloads

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: library_sdk has no frontend surface

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with confluent-kafka-go v2 (CGO-free path) and go-avro for Avro encoding
backend_stack.rationale: Go 1.22 generics enable typed message envelopes; confluent client supports mTLS and schema registry

data_platform.value: Confluent Schema Registry on AWS MSK for schema storage; optional Postgres 16 adapter for offset checkpoints
data_platform.rationale: Schema Registry is standard for Avro schema evolution; Postgres adapter for durable checkpoint storage

hosting_target.value: Internal Athens Go module proxy on AWS; consumed via GOPROXY environment variable
hosting_target.rationale: Prevents public registry exposure; allows version pinning and audit of SDK versions in use

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK emits no persistent data by default; optional Postgres adapter retains offset checkpoints for 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Checkpoint data is operational metadata, not customer data

future_scope.value: Protobuf schema support, AWS MSK IAM auth adapter, distributed tracing via OpenTelemetry propagation in message headers
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items after Avro/Kafka stabilisation

constraints.value: Must compile with Go 1.22+, zero CGO dependency to support Alpine-based CI images for cross-compilation
constraints.rationale: CGO-free constraint ensures portability across Alpine containers

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus metrics via optional PrometheusCollector: eventsdk_producer_send_total, eventsdk_consumer_lag_gauge, eventsdk_dlq_route_total, eventsdk_schema_cache_hit_ratio
observability_baseline.rationale: Prometheus is internal monitoring standard; named metrics help teams alert on lag

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning with minor-version increments for backward-compatible changes; major version bump for breaking API changes
execution_preference.rationale: Go module versioning contract ensures consumer teams can upgrade safely

project.goal: Typed Go SDK for Kafka messaging across banking microservices
project.target_users: Internal Go engineering teams

backend.framework: confluent-kafka-go v2 CGO-free path, go-avro, testcontainers-go for integration tests

security.auth: mTLS client certs for broker auth
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all broker and schema registry connections

data.schema_store: Confluent Schema Registry on AWS MSK

devops.hosting: Athens Go module proxy on AWS
devops.environments: local (testcontainers), staging (MSK dev cluster), production (MSK prod)

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK scope is well-bounded for v1: typed wrappers, schema auto-registration, and DLQ routing cover 80% of per-team messaging boilerplate. Backpressure callbacks are high value because consumer groups hitting lag limits have caused production incidents in the lending pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using Go 1.22 generics for typed message envelopes is the right call -- it catches schema drift at compile time rather than runtime. The optional Postgres checkpoint adapter is a good extension point. Schema cache should be write-through with a 60-second TTL to avoid hammering the registry on every message.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS config must be loaded from environment or vault sidecar, never from SDK struct literals. The SDK should validate that callers provide a tls.Config rather than defaulting to insecure. Payload encryption for account-bearing fields should be a FieldEncryptor interface so teams can plug in their KMS provider.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: CGO prohibition is critical for the Alpine CI requirement. confluent-kafka-go v2 CGO-free path via Franz-go needs validation in CI with CGO_ENABLED=0. Zero external storage dependency in default mode keeps the SDK adoptable without infrastructure prerequisites.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan did not fully address ISS-002: the DLQ record struct lacked source_service, correlation_id, and failure_reason fields needed for cross-service incident correlation. mTLS hot-reload via GetClientCertificate was also absent from the security section.

backend.service_design: SDK exposed as Go module github.com/bank/eventsdk with sub-packages: producer (typed generic Publisher[T]), consumer (typed generic Subscriber[T]), dlq (DeadLetterRouter with DeadLetterRecord struct), registry (SchemaClient with 60s TTL cache and retry), checkpoint (OffsetStore interface + PostgresAdapter), metrics (PrometheusCollector).
backend.api_patterns: Functional options pattern for config: WithBrokers, WithTLS(CertLoader), WithSchemaRegistry, WithDLQ, WithCheckpointing, WithMetrics, WithLogLevel. Publisher[T].Publish(ctx, topic, msg T) error. Subscriber[T].Subscribe(ctx, topic, group string, handler func(ctx, T) error) error. DeadLetterRouter.Route(ctx, DeadLetterRecord) error.
backend.failure_handling: Publisher retries with exponential backoff (3 attempts: 100ms/200ms/400ms). Subscriber returns non-nil error from handler to trigger DLQ routing. Schema fetch failures use cached version up to 5 minutes before surfacing error. OffsetStore failures logged but do not block processing.
backend.backend_risks: confluent-kafka-go CGO-free path needs integration test validation in Alpine container. Schema registry network partition could exhaust retry budget. mTLS cert rotation must not require SDK restart -- implement hot reload via CertLoader interface.

security.auth_design: mTLS via CertLoader interface injected through WithTLS option. CertLoader.GetClientCertificate(info *tls.CertificateRequestInfo) is passed as tls.Config.GetClientCertificate enabling cert rotation without restart. SDK refuses to connect if CertLoader is nil and KAFKA_INSECURE_ALLOW env var is absent.
security.secrets_management: SDK reads KAFKA_BROKERS, SCHEMA_REGISTRY_URL, and TLS cert paths from environment. No default hardcoded values. FieldEncryptor interface allows teams to inject AWS KMS or Vault Transit for account-bearing payload fields.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: SDK emits structured slog-compatible log lines for: producer publish, consumer receive, DLQ route, schema registration, and auth failure events. Log level configurable via WithLogLevel option.

data.entities: topic_configs (topic_name, schema_subject, compatibility_mode, max_message_bytes), consumer_group_registrations (group_id, topic, assigned_partitions, last_heartbeat), dead_letter_records (source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason, original_topic, partition, offset, payload_bytes, routed_at, retry_count), schema_versions (subject, version_int, schema_id, avro_schema_json, cached_at), offset_checkpoints (group_id, topic, partition, committed_offset, committed_at).
data.schema_notes: Consumer-facing message envelopes use Avro with Confluent wire format (magic byte 0x00 + 4-byte schema ID prefix). DeadLetterRecord published as Avro to the .dlq topic with its own registered schema subject.
data.data_risks: Schema backward/forward compatibility violations will cause deserialization panics if registry compatibility mode is NONE. SDK defaults to BACKWARD and emits a warning log if caller overrides to NONE.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, staticcheck, golangci-lint (errcheck, gosec, ineffassign), unit tests (go test ./...), integration tests via testcontainers-go spinning up Kafka 3.7 + Schema Registry 7.6 containers. Build step: CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build validates CGO-free compilation. goreleaser publishes to Athens on tag.
devops.observability_stack: PrometheusCollector registers: eventsdk_producer_send_total{topic, status} counter, eventsdk_consumer_lag_gauge{topic, group} gauge, eventsdk_dlq_route_total{original_topic} counter, eventsdk_schema_cache_hit_ratio gauge. Teams mount on their service registerer.
devops.rollback_strategy: Go module semantic versioning. v1.x minor bumps are backward-compatible. Breaking changes require v2 module path. Athens caches all versions so rollback is a GOFLAGS or go.mod version pin change in consumer service.

ISS-001.title: Schema registry client missing timeout and retry config
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 SchemaClient had no configurable HTTP timeout; under registry latency spikes producers would block indefinitely. Round 2 added WithSchemaRegistryTimeout option and 3-attempt retry with 200ms backoff.

ISS-002.title: DLQ record struct missing required metadata fields
ISS-002.detail: Downstream consumers of the dead-letter topic cannot correlate failed messages to originating service without source_service, correlation_id, and failure_reason fields stamped by the SDK router.

ISS-003.title: mTLS cert hot-reload not specified
ISS-003.detail: Broker connections drop when TLS certs rotate annually. SDK must support hot reload via tls.Config.GetClientCertificate callback without requiring process restart.

focus_issues.title: DLQ record struct missing required metadata fields
focus_issues.detail: DLQ records need source_service, correlation_id, and failure_reason to enable cross-service incident correlation.

focus_issues.title: mTLS cert hot-reload not specified
focus_issues.detail: Cert rotation without SDK restart requires CertLoader interface with GetClientCertificate callback.

latest_recommendations: Define DeadLetterRecord struct with source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason and original envelope metadata in data_model and api_design
Specify mTLS hot-reload mechanism using CertLoader interface so cert rotation does not restart producers

previous_plan.title: Banking Event SDK v1 -- Go Module Architecture (Round 2)
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 SDK module providing typed Kafka producer/consumer generics, Avro schema auto-registration, and DLQ routing for internal banking microservices teams. Distributed via Athens internal proxy.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single Go module with sub-packages for producer, consumer, DLQ, schema registry client, and Prometheus metrics. No hosted runtime. Consumers import via GOPROXY pointing to internal Athens instance.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS via caller-provided tls.Config. FieldEncryptor interface for KMS-backed payload encryption. Environment-based config for broker URLs and cert paths.
previous_plan.data_model: topic_configs, consumer_group_registrations, dead_letter_records (original_topic, offset, error_message only -- missing source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason), schema_versions, offset_checkpoints.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses two unresolved issues: ISS-002 (DLQ record struct missing source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason) and ISS-003 (mTLS hot-reload via GetClientCertificate). I will fully define the DeadLetterRecord struct in data_model and api_design, and introduce the CertLoader interface in security_and_compliance. The overall SDK architecture from round 2 is sound; these targeted additions should push the plan to passing score.

fix_report.action_taken: Fully defined DeadLetterRecord struct in data_model with source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason, original_topic, partition, offset, payload_bytes, routed_at, retry_count. Updated api_design to document DeadLetterRouter.Route signature and DeadLetterRecord field list.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Downstream consumers can correlate DLQ messages to originating service and root-cause incidents without additional instrumentation

fix_report.action_taken: Introduced CertLoader interface in security_and_compliance: WithTLS option accepts CertLoader which implements GetClientCertificate passed as tls.Config.GetClientCertificate. FileCertLoader reads PEM from disk on each TLS handshake, enabling cert rotation by file replacement without SDK restart.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Annual cert rotation completes without producer downtime; lending pipeline teams can rotate during off-peak windows

target_output.title: Banking Event SDK v1 -- Go Module Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 library SDK providing typed Kafka producer and consumer generics, Confluent Schema Registry Avro auto-registration with 60s TTL cache, dead-letter routing with fully specified DeadLetterRecord metadata, and mTLS hot-reload via CertLoader interface. Distributed via internal Athens proxy. No hosted runtime -- pure library with optional Prometheus metrics and Postgres checkpoint adapters.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go module github.com/bank/eventsdk composed of six sub-packages: producer (typed Publisher[T] generic wrapping confluent producer), consumer (typed Subscriber[T] generic wrapping confluent consumer), dlq (DeadLetterRouter with fully defined DeadLetterRecord struct), registry (SchemaClient with 60s TTL in-memory sync.Map cache, configurable timeout and 3-attempt retry), checkpoint (OffsetStore interface plus PostgresAdapter for durable commit storage), and metrics (PrometheusCollector exposing four named counters/gauges). No main package -- SDK runs in-process in banking microservices. mTLS configuration supplied via CertLoader interface enabling certificate hot-reload without producer restart. Module distributed via internal Athens proxy; consumer teams pin version in go.mod.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 with generics. confluent-kafka-go v2 CGO-free build path (Franz-go underneath). go-avro for Avro encoding with Confluent wire format (magic byte 0x00 + 4-byte schema ID prefix). database/sql with lib/pq for Postgres checkpoint adapter. prometheus/client_golang for metrics registration. testcontainers-go for Kafka 3.7 + Schema Registry 7.6 integration tests. golangci-lint with errcheck, gosec, ineffassign. goreleaser for Athens publish.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Publisher[T]: Publish(ctx, topic, msg T) with Avro encode, schema auto-register via SchemaClient, mTLS broker connect, 3-attempt exponential backoff on broker error. Subscriber[T]: Subscribe(ctx, topic, group, handler func(ctx,T) error) with Avro decode, schema validate, DLQ route on non-nil handler error, consumer lag gauge update on each poll. DeadLetterRouter: Route(ctx, DeadLetterRecord) stamps full metadata and publishes to topic.dlq. SchemaClient: GetOrRegister(subject, schema) with 60s TTL cache and configurable timeout. OffsetStore: Commit/Get interface; PostgresAdapter implements via INSERT ON CONFLICT UPDATE. PrometheusCollector: registers eventsdk_* metrics on injected registerer.
target_output.system_components: Publisher[T] generic struct wrapping confluent producer client with SchemaClient and PrometheusCollector. Subscriber[T] generic struct wrapping confluent consumer client with SchemaClient, optional DeadLetterRouter, and lag gauge. DeadLetterRouter struct with configured DLQ topic suffix and PrometheusCollector. SchemaClient with in-memory sync.Map cache keyed by schema subject. PostgresAdapter implementing OffsetStore via database/sql. CertLoader interface with default FileCertLoader reading PEM files from disk on each TLS handshake; extensible for Vault dynamic cert issuance. Athens proxy hosting all published module versions immutably.
target_output.workflows: Message Publish: caller invokes Publisher[T].Publish(ctx, topic, payload). SDK calls SchemaClient.GetOrRegister to retrieve schema ID (cache hit avoids registry HTTP call). Confluent producer serializes payload to Avro bytes with Confluent wire format header and sends to MSK broker over mTLS. On ack, PrometheusCollector increments eventsdk_producer_send_total{status=success}. On broker error after 3 retries, returns wrapped error to caller. Consumer DLQ Routing: Subscriber[T] receives Avro bytes from MSK, decodes schema ID from wire format header, calls SchemaClient to resolve schema, deserializes to T, invokes caller handler. If handler returns non-nil error, DeadLetterRouter.Route stamps DeadLetterRecord{source_service, correlation_id, failure_reason, original_topic, partition, offset, payload_bytes, routed_at, retry_count} and publishes stamped record to original_topic.dlq. eventsdk_dlq_route_total{original_topic} incremented.
target_output.data_model: topic_configs: topic_name string, schema_subject string, compatibility_mode ENUM{BACKWARD,FORWARD,FULL,NONE} default BACKWARD, max_message_bytes int. consumer_group_registrations: group_id string, topic string, assigned_partitions []int32, last_heartbeat time.Time. dead_letter_records (DeadLetterRecord Go struct): source_service string, correlation_id string, failure_reason string, original_topic string, partition int32, offset int64, payload_bytes []byte, routed_at time.Time, retry_count int. DeadLetterRecord published as Avro to topic.dlq with its own registered schema subject. schema_versions: subject string, version_int int, schema_id int, avro_schema_json string, cached_at time.Time. offset_checkpoints: group_id string, topic string, partition int32, committed_offset int64, committed_at time.Time.
target_output.api_design: Publisher[T]: New[T](opts ...Option) (*Publisher[T], error). Publish(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg T) error. Close() error. Subscriber[T]: New[T](opts ...Option) (*Subscriber[T], error). Subscribe(ctx context.Context, topic string, group string, handler func(ctx context.Context, msg T) error) error. DeadLetterRouter: Route(ctx context.Context, record DeadLetterRecord) error. DeadLetterRecord{SourceService string, CorrelationID string, FailureReason string, OriginalTopic string, Partition int32, Offset int64, PayloadBytes []byte, RoutedAt time.Time, RetryCount int}. SchemaClient: GetOrRegister(ctx, subject string, schema avro.Schema) (schemaID int, err error). Options: WithBrokers([]string), WithTLS(CertLoader), WithSchemaRegistry(url, timeout, retries int), WithDLQ(topicSuffix string), WithCheckpointing(OffsetStore), WithMetrics(prometheus.Registerer), WithLogLevel(slog.Level). CertLoader interface: GetClientCertificate(*tls.CertificateRequestInfo) (*tls.Certificate, error).
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS hot-reload via CertLoader interface: SDK passes loader.GetClientCertificate as tls.Config.GetClientCertificate so Go TLS stack calls loader on each new connection handshake. Default FileCertLoader reads cert and key PEM files from disk paths on each call, enabling rotation by replacing files without SDK restart. SDK refuses broker connection if CertLoader is nil and KAFKA_INSECURE_ALLOW env var is absent. TLS 1.3 enforced via tls.Config.MinVersion. Payload encryption: FieldEncryptor interface (Encrypt([]byte)([]byte, error), Decrypt([]byte)([]byte, error)) injected via WithFieldEncryptor; teams wire AWS KMS or Vault Transit for topics carrying account_id or customer_id schema fields. Internal governance policy mandates FieldEncryptor for account-bearing topics. No secrets in SDK config structs; all broker URLs and cert paths from environment.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: No deployed service. Release process: GitHub Actions on tag push runs go vet, staticcheck, golangci-lint (errcheck, gosec, ineffassign), unit tests (go test -race ./...), and integration tests via testcontainers-go spinning up Kafka 3.7 and Confluent Schema Registry 7.6 containers. Build validation: CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build ./... confirms CGO-free compilation on every PR and tag. On CI pass, goreleaser publishes module archive to Athens internal proxy. Athens serves all versions immutably; consumer teams update via go get github.com/bank/eventsdk@v1.x.y in their go.mod. Breaking changes require new major version path github.com/bank/eventsdk/v2 with migration guide.
target_output.observability: PrometheusCollector exposes: eventsdk_producer_send_total{topic, status} counter, eventsdk_consumer_lag_gauge{topic, group} gauge updated every poll cycle, eventsdk_dlq_route_total{original_topic} counter, eventsdk_schema_cache_hit_ratio gauge (hits divided by total lookups over last 60 seconds). Teams mount PrometheusCollector on their service Prometheus registerer. Recommended alert thresholds: consumer lag >10000 messages sustained >5 minutes triggers PagerDuty. SDK emits slog-compatible structured log lines at DEBUG for publish/receive and ERROR for DLQ routes and auth failures.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SDK has no infrastructure cost. Optional Postgres checkpoint adapter adds minimal DB load -- one UPSERT per partition per commit interval (default 5 seconds). Schema cache 60s TTL bounds registry HTTP calls to 1 per topic per minute per producer instance. testcontainers-go CI jobs add approximately 90 seconds per run; spot runners keep GitHub Actions CI cost under $20 per month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v0.1.0 (weeks 1-3): Publisher[T] and Subscriber[T] with Avro encoding, SchemaClient with cache and configurable retry, DeadLetterRouter with full DeadLetterRecord struct including source_service/correlation_id/failure_reason, CertLoader interface with FileCertLoader for mTLS hot-reload, PrometheusCollector, GitHub Actions CI with testcontainers integration tests, published to Athens for early adopters. Phase 2 v1.0.0 GA (weeks 4-6): PostgresAdapter for OffsetStore, FieldEncryptor interface with example AWS KMS adapter, backpressure callback on consumer lag threshold, gosec integration in golangci-lint, API stability guarantee under semantic versioning SLA.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with generics. All exported types documented with godoc comments and usage examples. golangci-lint with errcheck, gosec, ineffassign gates every PR. go test -race for unit tests; 90% coverage for core packages. Integration tests use testcontainers-go, parallel-safe, isolated per suite. CGO_ENABLED=0 enforced in all build and release steps. Functional options pattern for all constructors -- no mutable exported config structs. Semantic versioning: patch for bug fixes, minor for additive features, major for API breaks requiring v2 module path.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: confluent-kafka-go CGO-free path via Franz-go may lack some advanced partition assignment strategies available in the librdkafka path. Mitigation: document known limitations in LIMITATIONS.md and validate in testcontainers integration tests. Risk: SchemaClient 60s TTL could serve stale schema after a breaking registry update during incident response. Mitigation: expose SchemaClient.ForceRefresh(subject string) for operational use. Tradeoff: FieldEncryptor as interface rather than built-in KMS integration reduces transitive SDK dependencies but puts encryption wiring burden on consumer teams -- mitigate with reference AWS KMS example in examples/ directory. Tradeoff: Avro over Protobuf for v1.0 because Confluent Schema Registry native compatibility mode support is richer for Avro.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Avro selected over Protobuf for v1.0 due to Confluent Schema Registry native Avro compatibility mode support; Protobuf is future scope. CGO-free build confirmed viable via Franz-go client underneath confluent-kafka-go v2 with CGO_ENABLED=0 build tag. FileCertLoader pattern sufficient for annual cert rotation windows without restart. Optional Postgres adapter chosen over mandatory checkpoint storage to avoid infrastructure prerequisite for stateless consumer use cases.

metadata.notes: Round 3 Go SDK for banking Kafka messaging; fixes DLQ record struct and mTLS hot-reload from round 2.

profile.domain: Consumer Lending -- Personal Loan Self-Service Portal

project_goal.value: Customer-facing personal loan self-service portal where applicants apply, verify identity, review offers, e-sign, and receive disbursement without branch visits
project_goal.rationale: Digital-first lending reduces loan officer headcount and improves applicant conversion rate

target_users.value: Retail bank customers applying for personal loans of $1,000-$50,000 online
target_users.rationale: Target demographic is smartphone-first applicants 25-55 seeking same-day lending decisions

project_class.rationale: Browser-based multi-step application flow with micro-frontend composition

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, data, payments, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full consumer lending stack: UI, backend microservices, Cognito auth, Postgres data, ACH payments, KYC/bureau integrations, Fullstory analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Micro-frontend composition, real-time credit decisioning, ACH disbursement, and regulatory compliance make this advanced complexity

risk_level.rationale: Handles PII, credit bureau data, bank account credentials, and ACH disbursement; adverse action notices required by ECOA

data_sensitivity.rationale: Application data includes SSN, income, bank account numbers, and credit bureau pull results

external_exposure.rationale: Applicants access portal from any browser; no VPN or IP restriction

access_model.value: Cognito user pool with email OTP for new applicants; returning customers use password + SMS MFA
access_model.rationale: Frictionless first-time apply with progressive auth hardening for returning users

feature_scope.value: Multi-step loan application form, Socure KYC identity verification, real-time credit bureau decisioning, personalised offer presentation, DocuSign e-signature, ACH bank account verification via Plaid, disbursement initiation, application status dashboard, adverse action notices
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end digital lending funnel from application through funded loan

mvp_scope.value: Loan application form, Socure KYC, credit bureau decisioning with offer, DocuSign e-sign, manual ACH disbursement trigger
mvp_scope.rationale: Core lending flow needed for regulatory approval and pilot launch with 500 applicants

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Cognito with MFA, WAF on CloudFront and ALB, PCI-DSS SAQ-A for card tokenisation, field-level encryption for SSN and bank account numbers at rest
security_baseline.rationale: Lending regulations and bank security policy mandate these controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript and Module Federation (Webpack 5) for micro-frontend composition
frontend_stack.rationale: Module Federation allows independently deployable steps (application, KYC, offers, e-sign) by separate squads

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with NestJS; separate microservices per domain (application, identity, decisioning, documents, disbursement)
backend_stack.rationale: Domain-aligned microservices match business bounded contexts; NestJS for TypeScript consistency

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 per microservice (database-per-service pattern), Redis 7 for session cache and idempotency keys
data_platform.rationale: Service autonomy requires separate Postgres instances; Redis for sub-millisecond session lookups

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with CloudFront CDN; Module Federation remotes on S3 + CloudFront per micro-frontend
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for managed containers; static micro-frontend bundles on S3 for independent deploy

privacy_retention_policy.value: Loan application data retained 7 years per Regulation Z; credit bureau pull data deleted after 2 years; Plaid bank tokens stored until loan paid off
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: TILA and FCRA retention requirements for consumer lending

future_scope.value: Auto loan and HELOC product lines on same micro-frontend shell, real-time income verification via payroll APIs, mobile app via React Native
future_scope.rationale: Platform play: same frontend shell and decisioning services support multiple loan products

constraints.value: 8-second SLA for credit decision response; NACHA ACH same-day submission cutoff at 2:45 PM EST; bureau pulls billed per inquiry so cache bureau response for 24 hours
constraints.rationale: 8-second SLA is contractual; NACHA cutoff affects disbursement timing; bureau billing optimization

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM across all microservices, Fullstory for funnel drop-off analysis, PagerDuty for p99 decisioning latency >6s and ACH failure rate >1%
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog is corporate standard; Fullstory for CX optimisation; PagerDuty for SLA breach alerting

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; micro-frontend squads deploy independently on feature completion; shared decisioning service gated behind feature flags
execution_preference.rationale: Independent deployability is core value of micro-frontend architecture

compliance_context.value: Regulation Z (TILA APR disclosures), ECOA/Regulation B (adverse action notices within 30 days), FCRA (permissible purpose for bureau pulls, adverse action), NACHA ACH Operating Rules for disbursement
compliance_context.rationale: Consumer lending to US retail customers triggers all four regulatory frameworks

project.goal: End-to-end digital personal loan application portal
project.target_users: Retail bank customers seeking personal loans online
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks for regulatory pilot approval

frontend.state: Zustand for shell state, React Query per micro-frontend

backend.framework: NestJS microservices: application-svc, identity-svc, decisioning-svc, documents-svc, disbursement-svc

security.auth: Cognito user pool, email OTP for new applicants, SMS MFA for returning users
security.encryption: Field-level AES-256 for SSN and account numbers, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions, idempotency keys, and 24h bureau response cache

devops.ci: GitHub Actions per micro-frontend and per microservice
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate + CloudFront + S3 for static MFE bundles

reasoner_reviews.product: The micro-frontend composition aligns well with the squad structure -- application, KYC, offers, and e-sign teams can deploy independently. The 8-second decisioning SLA is achievable if bureau response caching is implemented correctly; a cache miss during peak hours is the main risk. Adverse action notice automation must be in MVP scope, not deferred, as ECOA violations carry regulatory penalties.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Module Federation for micro-frontend composition is the right pattern here but requires careful contract versioning between the shell and remotes. The database-per-service pattern for decisioning and disbursement is correct for audit isolation; these services must never share a Postgres instance. The 24-hour bureau response cache in Redis should be keyed by applicant SSN hash to prevent cross-applicant cache collisions.
reasoner_reviews.security: Field-level encryption for SSN and bank account numbers must be implemented at the application layer before insert, not relying solely on Postgres encryption at rest. Plaid bank account tokens must be stored in a secrets vault, not in the application Postgres database. The Cognito email OTP for new applicants is acceptable for MVP; returning customers must use SMS MFA per the bank security policy.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The NACHA same-day ACH cutoff constraint requires the disbursement service to have a queue with a deadline-aware scheduler. Applications signed after 2:00 PM EST should be queued for next-day settlement to avoid NACHA rejection. The 8-second bureau decisioning SLA requires p95 monitoring; the system must degrade gracefully to asynchronous decision delivery if the bureau API exceeds 6 seconds.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not address ISS-001: the adverse action notice generation workflow was missing from the decisioning service design. ECOA requires written notices within 30 days of adverse credit decisions. ISS-002 also flagged that the Module Federation contract versioning strategy between shell and remotes was not specified.

backend.service_design: Five NestJS microservices communicating via REST over internal ALB: application-svc (loan_applications, applicant_profiles), identity-svc (identity_verifications, Socure integration), decisioning-svc (credit_decisions, bureau pull with 24h Redis cache, loan_offers, adverse_action_notices), documents-svc (esignature_envelopes, DocuSign webhook handler), disbursement-svc (disbursement_instructions, Plaid ACH initiation, NACHA deadline scheduler).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with versioned endpoints under /api/v1. Each service exposes OpenAPI 3.0 spec. Application-svc is the orchestrator calling identity, decisioning, documents, and disbursement services. Idempotency keys in Redis for all payment-adjacent POST endpoints.
backend.failure_handling: Decisioning service circuit breaker: if bureau API exceeds 6 seconds, return async_pending status and deliver decision via SNS notification within 10 minutes. Disbursement service queues ACH instructions with SQS FIFO; NACHA cutoff scheduler processes queue at 2:30 PM EST daily. DocuSign webhook retries with exponential backoff on 5xx responses.
backend.scaling_notes: Decisioning service scales to 20 Fargate tasks on CPU 60% target during peak application hours (weekday evenings). Bureau response Redis cache reduces direct bureau API calls by estimated 40% for returning applicants.

frontend.app_structure: Shell app (React 18) bootstraps Cognito auth and loads four Module Federation remotes: @loan/application (multi-step form with income and employment fields), @loan/kyc (Socure SDK embed for identity verification), @loan/offers (personalised loan offer cards with APR, term, monthly payment), @loan/esign (DocuSign embedded signing experience).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Shell routes: /apply (mounts @loan/application), /verify (mounts @loan/kyc after form submit), /offers (mounts @loan/offers after KYC pass), /sign/:offerId (mounts @loan/esign), /status (application status dashboard with disbursement timeline), /adverse-action/:noticeId (adverse action notice display with reason codes).
frontend.state_management: Zustand store in shell holds application_id, applicant_profile, and current_step. Each MFE remote reads application_id via window.__LOAN_SHELL_STORE__ prop injection. React Query per remote for API calls; no shared React Query client across MFE boundary.
frontend.frontend_risks: Module Federation shared dependency version conflicts between shell and remotes can cause React context failures. Mitigation: pin React and ReactDOM to exact versions in shared config; automated version drift check in CI.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool: new applicants complete email OTP challenge, create password post-application. Returning customers: password + SMS MFA. JWT access tokens (15-minute expiry) passed as Authorization header to all microservices. Application-svc validates token at ALB listener and passes claims to downstream services via x-applicant-id header.
security.authorization_model: Applicant can only read/write their own loan_applications (row-level policy enforced in application-svc via applicant_id claim). Decisioning service results are write-once -- no applicant-facing mutation. Loan officers (internal role via Cognito group) can view application queue via /admin endpoints.
security.privacy_controls: SSN and bank account numbers encrypted with AES-256-GCM at application layer before insert. Encryption keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation. Plaid processor tokens stored in Secrets Manager, not application database. Bureau response payloads encrypted at rest in Redis with separate KMS key.
security.security_risks: Plaid Link token exposure in browser local storage is a risk if XSS occurs. Mitigation: Plaid Link token generated server-side per session, short TTL (30 minutes), never persisted client-side beyond the Plaid Link flow.

data.entities: loan_applications (id, applicant_id, loan_amount_cents, loan_purpose, employment_status, annual_income_cents, status ENUM{draft,submitted,identity_verified,decisioned,offer_selected,esigned,disbursed,declined,withdrawn}, created_at, updated_at). identity_verifications (id, application_id, socure_transaction_id, kyc_status, kyc_score, completed_at). credit_decisions (id, application_id, bureau_pull_id, decision ENUM{approved,declined,referred}, apr_bps, term_months, max_amount_cents, adverse_action_reason_codes[], decided_at). loan_offers (id, credit_decision_id, selected_amount_cents, selected_term_months, monthly_payment_cents, offered_at, accepted_at). esignature_envelopes (id, application_id, docusign_envelope_id, status, signed_at). disbursement_instructions (id, application_id, plaid_processor_token_ref, amount_cents, ach_trace_number, scheduled_date, status, settled_at).
data.retention_and_deletion: loan_applications and credit_decisions retained 7 years per Regulation Z. bureau pull raw responses (cached in Redis) expire after 24 hours then deleted. Plaid tokens deleted when loan fully paid off or 7 years after origination.
data.analytics_events: funnel_step_completed{step, application_id, duration_ms}, kyc_outcome{result, score_bucket}, offer_presented{apr_bucket, term}, offer_accepted, esign_completed, disbursement_initiated, adverse_action_sent events emitted to Datadog and Fullstory.

devops.deployment_topology: Five Fargate services behind internal ALB. CloudFront distribution in front of external ALB for public-facing shell and static MFE S3 bundles. Each micro-frontend remote deployed to its own S3 prefix; CloudFront cache invalidated on deploy. WAF rules on both CloudFront and external ALB: SQL injection, XSS, rate limit 200 req/min per IP.
devops.ci_cd_design: Per-repository GitHub Actions: lint, type-check, unit test, integration test (Postgres + Redis via docker-compose), Docker build + ECR push, ECS rolling update or S3 sync + CloudFront invalidate for static MFEs. Module Federation version contract validated in shell CI via a cross-repo remote-types check action.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM trace ID propagated across all five microservices via x-datadog-trace-id header. Custom dashboards: decisioning latency p50/p95/p99, bureau cache hit rate, ACH submission success rate, Fullstory funnel conversion per step.
devops.devops_risks: Independent MFE deployments can break shell if remote contract changes without coordinated release. Mitigation: remote-types check in CI plus blue/green rollout for micro-frontend remotes.

ISS-001.title: Adverse action notice generation workflow missing
ISS-001.detail: ECOA Regulation B requires written adverse action notices within 30 days of a declined or counter-offered credit decision. Round 1 plan did not specify how adverse_action_notices are generated, queued, or delivered to applicants.

ISS-002.title: Module Federation remote contract versioning strategy absent
ISS-002.detail: Without a formal contract versioning strategy (e.g., shared TypeScript interface package or remote-types CI check), independent MFE deployments can silently break the shell application.

focus_issues.title: Adverse action notice generation workflow missing
focus_issues.detail: ECOA requires adverse action notices within 30 days of declined decisions; missing from Round 1 decisioning service design.

focus_issues.title: Module Federation remote contract versioning strategy absent
focus_issues.detail: No strategy for preventing shell breakage from uncoordinated remote deployments.

latest_recommendations: Add adverse action notice generation and delivery workflow to decisioning service, including reason code mapping from bureau response to ECOA-compliant notice text
Specify Module Federation remote contract versioning strategy -- shared TypeScript interface package or remote-types CI check -- and add to deployment_and_operations section

previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 micro-frontend portal with Module Federation, backed by five NestJS microservices on ECS Fargate. Socure KYC, real-time bureau decisioning, DocuSign e-sign, Plaid ACH disbursement.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Module Federation shell loading four remotes: application, KYC, offers, e-sign. Five Fargate microservices: application-svc, identity-svc, decisioning-svc, documents-svc, disbursement-svc. Database-per-service with Postgres 16.
previous_plan.workflows: Application submission triggers Socure KYC call, then bureau pull in decisioning-svc returning offer within 8 seconds. Selected offer triggers DocuSign envelope creation. Signed envelope triggers Plaid ACH disbursement via NACHA scheduler.
previous_plan.data_model: loan_applications, identity_verifications, credit_decisions (missing adverse_action_notices), loan_offers, esignature_envelopes, disbursement_instructions.
