target_output.title: Bank Wholesale Deposit Rate Board -- Static Site Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A static informational website built with Hugo and Tailwind CSS, hosted on GCP Cloud Storage with Cloud CDN global edge caching, publishing wholesale deposit rates to corporate treasury clients. Treasury operations commits daily rate JSON files to GitHub; a GitHub Actions pipeline rebuilds and invalidates CDN cache within 3 minutes. FDIC rate disclosure requirements satisfied via Hugo base layout template. A Node.js 20 Express API on Cloud Run provides dynamic rate serving with Firestore as the backing database for real-time rate queries.
target_output.architecture_overview: Hugo static site generator builds rate card and archive pages from /data/rates/YYYY-MM-DD.json files. GitHub Actions triggers on push to main: hugo --minify, gsutil rsync to Cloud Storage bucket, Cloud CDN cache invalidation for /* completing in under 3 minutes. Cloud CDN delivers all pages from edge nodes globally with max-age=300 cache headers. Cloud Armor WAF limits 1,000 requests per minute per IP to prevent scraping abuse during rate volatility. FDIC disclaimer and publication timestamp rendered in Hugo base.html template, present on all pages. All rate data is pre-rendered as static JSON-fed Hugo templates at build time and served directly from Cloud CDN; institutional clients consume the published HTML and downloadable CSV rate files without any server-side runtime dependency.
target_output.technology_stack: Hugo 0.131 static site generator. Tailwind CSS 3.x with Hugo pipe for build-time CSS purging. GitHub Actions CI/CD. GCP: Cloud Storage (website hosting), Cloud CDN (global edge), Cloud Armor (WAF + rate limiting), Google-managed SSL certificate. Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymisation (gtag.js). Node.js 20 with Express framework on Cloud Run for dynamic rate API. Firestore for rate document storage. Google Cloud Monitoring + Uptime Check.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Current Rate Card: home page showing today's product tiers with rate_bps formatted as percentage, minimum deposit, term days, publication timestamp, and FDIC disclaimer. Historical Archive: /rates/YYYY-MM-DD/ pages auto-generated by Hugo from all files in /data/rates/ directory with same FDIC disclaimer in base layout. CSV Download: /download/ page with pre-generated CSV of all historical rates from data files. Treasury Publish Workflow: commit /data/rates/YYYY-MM-DD.json to main branch triggers GitHub Actions 3-minute rebuild cycle. Analytics: GA4 tracks page views, rate page engagement time, CSV download events.
target_output.system_components: Hugo build engine generating static HTML from /data/rates/ JSON files and layouts/. Cloud Storage bucket (rates.bank.com) hosting built site. Cloud CDN load balancer with SSL certificate and cache-control headers. Cloud Armor security policy with 1,000 req/min rate limit per IP. GitHub Actions workflow: hugo build, gsutil sync, CDN invalidation, on-failure Slack notification. Google Analytics 4 tag in Hugo base.html. Node.js 20 Express API on Cloud Run connected to Firestore for dynamic rate queries. Firestore rate_cards collection mirroring static rate JSON data.
target_output.workflows: Rate Publication: Treasury operations commits updated /data/rates/YYYY-MM-DD.json file (product_tiers array with rate_bps, term_days, minimum_deposit_usd, fdic_disclaimer_text, publication_date, published_by). GitHub Actions triggers hugo --minify (builds all pages including /rates/YYYY-MM-DD/ archive). gsutil -m rsync -r -d public/ gs://rates.bank.com/ syncs new and changed files. gcloud compute url-maps invalidate-cdn-cache path /* invalidates CDN (typically 60 seconds). If any step fails, on-failure step posts Slack message to #treasury-ops-alerts with commit SHA and error. Historical CSV Download: Client visits /download/, clicks CSV link pre-generated by Hugo from all /data/rates/ files. Hugo template iterates rate files and outputs CSV with date, product_name, term_days, rate_bps columns.
target_output.data_model: rate_cards: JSON file per date at /data/rates/YYYY-MM-DD.json -- {publication_date: string, published_by: string, product_tiers: [{product_name, term_days, rate_bps, minimum_deposit_usd, fdic_disclaimer_text}]}. rate_bps: integer (e.g. 525 = 5.25%) formatted in Hugo template as {{ div .rate_bps 100.0 | printf '%.2f' }}%. product_tiers: structured data iterated by Hugo layouts for rate card table rows. publication_dates: inferred from /data/rates/ filenames for archive page URL generation. historical_rate_archives: /rates/YYYY-MM-DD/ pages generated by Hugo range over .Site.Data.rates. Firestore rate_cards collection: documents keyed by YYYY-MM-DD mirroring JSON structure for Cloud Run API.
target_output.api_design: Static site has no API. All content served as pre-built HTML from Cloud CDN. The Node.js 20 Cloud Run service exposes: GET /api/v1/rates/current (returns today's rate_cards JSON from Firestore), GET /api/v1/rates/:date (historical rate by date from Firestore), GET /api/v1/rates?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD (range query). GA4 measurement protocol used for server-side event tracking of CSV downloads.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced: Cloud CDN load balancer redirects HTTP to HTTPS. Google-managed SSL certificate auto-renewed. HTTP security headers via Cloud CDN custom response headers: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; X-Frame-Options: DENY; Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000. Cloud Armor WAF: rate limit 1,000 req/min per IP. No user input forms. FDIC rate disclosure: disclaimer text in Hugo base.html footer template rendered on every page. GA4 IP anonymisation enabled per privacy policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main branch: step 1 hugo --minify (target <30 seconds), step 2 gsutil -m rsync -r -d public/ gs://rates.bank.com/ (parallel upload, target <30 seconds), step 3 gcloud compute url-maps invalidate-cdn-cache --path /* (target <60 seconds CDN propagation), on-failure step: curl Slack webhook with $GITHUB_SHA and $GITHUB_JOB. Total target: under 3 minutes. Cloud Storage bucket: storage class Standard, uniform bucket-level access, allUsers objectViewer IAM for public read. Cloud CDN: cache mode CACHE_ALL_STATIC, default TTL 300 seconds.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboard: Cloud CDN cache hit ratio (target >95%), Cloud CDN origin request rate, Cloud CDN 5xx error rate. Uptime Check: HTTPS GET https://rates.bank.com/ from us-central1 and europe-west1, 60-second interval, alert on 2 consecutive failures. Alert: CDN cache hit ratio <80% for 10 minutes triggers email to platform team. GitHub Actions build duration tracked in Actions metrics.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $10/month at typical traffic. Cloud Storage: <$1/month for static files (<50MB). Cloud CDN: egress ~$5/month at estimated 10GB/month during normal traffic; during rate volatility spike CDN absorbs traffic without origin cost increase. Cloud Armor: $5/month base policy fee. Google Analytics: free. Cloud Run Node.js API adds approximately $15/month at low request volume. Total approximately $25/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (week 1-2): Hugo site with rate card, archive pages, FDIC disclaimer in base layout, GitHub Actions CI/CD with Slack failure notification, Cloud Storage + Cloud CDN deployment, GA4 analytics. Phase 2 (week 3): CSV download generation from historical data files, Cloud Armor rate limiting, Google Cloud Monitoring dashboard and Uptime Check. Phase 3 (future): Cloud Run Node.js API with Firestore for dynamic rate query endpoints, rate alert email subscription service.
target_output.development_guidelines: Hugo templates use base.html layout inheritance so FDIC disclaimer and publication timestamp are never omitted. rate_bps stored as integers in JSON -- never as floats. Hugo build tested locally with hugo server before committing rate files. GitHub Actions workflow validated with act CLI locally. No JavaScript on rate pages except GA4 tag -- rate table rendered as plain HTML table by Hugo template.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Malformed rate JSON file fails Hugo build and CDN retains prior day rates without immediate treasury-ops awareness. Mitigation: Slack on-failure notification added in Round 2; recommend JSON schema validation step in GitHub Actions before hugo build. Risk: Cloud CDN 5-minute max-age means clients may see rates up to 5 minutes stale after CDN invalidation completes. Acceptable per 3-minute publication window constraint. Risk: Cloud Run API and Firestore introduce a server-side runtime for a declared static_website project, contradicting the contract. This architecture mismatch is an intentional flaw -- the Node.js API is unnecessary for a rate board with no dynamic queries and should be removed. Tradeoff: Hugo data-driven archive pages over a database-backed archive keeps hosting cost near-zero and eliminates all server-side availability risk during traffic spikes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Hugo base.html layout confirmed as the correct location for FDIC disclaimer to ensure presence on all generated pages. Slack webhook secret stored in GitHub Actions Secrets, not in workflow YAML. CDN max-age=300 accepted as compliant with 3-minute publication window. CSV download implemented as Hugo-generated static file rather than server-generated endpoint to maintain fully static hosting model.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: plan introduces a Node.js Cloud Run API and Firestore database that violate the static_website contract (backend_stack=none, data_platform=none). FDIC disclaimer and build notification issues fixed from round 1, but architecture mismatch introduced as training signal for contract violation detection.

profile.domain: Fintech Loan Servicing Migration

project_goal.value: CLI tool to migrate loan servicing data from legacy COBOL/VSAM core to PostgreSQL 16 with field mapping validation, dry-run mode, and S3 checkpoint-based rollback
project_goal.rationale: Core migration requirement for decommissioning COBOL core by Q4

target_users.value: Engineering migration operators and data integrity teams running supervised migration batches
target_users.rationale: CLI requires operator expertise; not end-user facing

project_class.rationale: Offline batch migration with no web UI; operator-driven execution

capabilities.rationale: No frontend needed; drives VSAM reads, field mapping, PostgreSQL writes, and checkpoint management

complexity_level.rationale: VSAM-to-relational field mapping, SOX controls, parallel batch processing with resumable checkpointing

risk_level.rationale: Zero-loss data transfer required by CFPB Regulation X; migration failures expose servicing violations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Loan records, payment histories, and escrow balances are regulated financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on air-gapped migration workstations; S3 access via IAM role only

access_model.value: IAM role-based access with operator MFA; no multi-user concurrent sessions
access_model.rationale: Single-operator execution model with IAM for S3 and CloudWatch access

feature_scope.value: Dry-run mode, live migration mode, field mapping validator, reconciliation report generator, S3 checkpoint writer/reader, rollback command, batch size configurability
feature_scope.rationale: All features required for safe zero-loss migration

mvp_scope.value: Dry-run mode with reconciliation report and live migration with S3 checkpointing; rollback via checkpoint restore
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first loan portfolio migration wave

security_baseline.value: IAM roles with least-privilege, TLS for PostgreSQL connection, S3 SSE-KMS for checkpoint files, no plaintext credentials on disk
security_baseline.rationale: SOX and financial data handling requirements

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI only; stdout/stderr with structured log output
frontend_stack.rationale: cli_tool class; no browser UI

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Cobra CLI framework; VSAM access via JDBC bridge; PostgreSQL 16 via pgx/v5
backend_stack.rationale: Go compile-to-binary suits ops distribution; Cobra for subcommand structure; pgx for high-throughput batch inserts

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (target) + legacy VSAM files via JDBC bridge on migration workstation
data_platform.rationale: VSAM read-only; PostgreSQL is the migration destination

hosting_target.value: Local execution on RHEL 8 migration workstations with S3 us-east-1 for checkpoint and report storage
hosting_target.rationale: Migration workstations have VSAM bridge access; S3 for durable state

privacy_retention_policy.value: Checkpoint files retained 90 days post-migration; reconciliation reports retained 7 years per SOX audit requirements
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX requires 7-year audit trail for financial data changes

future_scope.value: Auto-reconciliation against post-migration servicing reports; parallel multi-portfolio migration with SQS work queue
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after first portfolio migration validated

constraints.value: Must run on RHEL 8 migration workstations; binary distribution via internal Artifactory; 48-hour migration window maximum per portfolio
constraints.rationale: Infrastructure and SLA constraints for migration windows

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON logs to CloudWatch Logs via CloudWatch agent; progress metrics (records/sec, error count, checkpoint offset) to stdout
observability_baseline.rationale: Operator needs live progress visibility; CloudWatch for post-migration audit

execution_preference.value: Sequential batch processing with configurable batch size default 500; operator-confirmed mode transitions
execution_preference.rationale: Controlled execution reduces blast radius of mapping errors

compliance_context.value: SOX data integrity controls; CFPB Regulation X zero-loss mortgage servicing transfer requirements; internal change management approval gate before live migration
compliance_context.rationale: Financial data migration regulatory requirements

project.goal: Migrate COBOL/VSAM loan portfolios to PostgreSQL 16 with zero data loss
project.target_users: Engineering migration operators

backend.framework: Cobra CLI with pgx/v5 and JDBC bridge subprocess
backend.api: No HTTP API; subprocess and file I/O only

security.auth: IAM instance profile with MFA enforcement
security.encryption: S3 SSE-KMS for checkpoints, TLS for PostgreSQL

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 target; VSAM source via JDBC bridge

devops.ci: GitHub Actions producing RHEL 8 binary via cross-compilation

reasoner_reviews.product: Dry-run mode is the critical safety gate before any live migration run. The reconciliation report must express match rates per field group (borrower identity, balance fields, escrow) not just aggregate counts so operators can identify which field groups have mapping errors before committing. Previous plan lacked this breakdown.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The JDBC bridge introduces a JVM dependency on migration workstations. JDK 17 version must be documented in the operator runbook and verified in CI integration tests. The pgx/v5 SendBatch for batch inserts correctly reduces PostgreSQL round-trips from O(n) to O(n/batch_size), which is essential within the 48-hour migration window for large portfolios.
reasoner_reviews.security: IAM role scope is now correctly limited to the S3 checkpoint prefix and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents only. PostgreSQL connection uses a migration-scoped role with INSERT/UPDATE on target tables and SELECT on field_mappings only - no superuser rights. JDBC bridge credentials delivered via stdin pipe to avoid process list exposure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 48-hour window is achievable for portfolios up to 500K records at 500-record batches with current throughput. Async checkpoint flush every 10 batches bounds replay gap to 5000 records maximum on interruption, which is acceptable for the recovery time objective.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 improved IAM scoping and JDBC credential handling but did not resolve ISS-001: the ReconciliationBuilder output struct was not updated to include field-group match rates. Round 3 must deliver the updated struct, the reconciliation_reports schema change, the JSON schema publication, and an integration test asserting the field-group keys are present.

backend.service_design: Cobra root command 'loanmigrate' with subcommands: validate-mapping, dry-run, migrate, rollback, status. Each subcommand loads a YAML config specifying VSAM dataset names, field mapping rules, PostgreSQL DSN secret ARN, batch size, and S3 checkpoint prefix.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API. JDBC bridge invoked as subprocess via os/exec with credentials piped via stdin; stdout parsed as newline-delimited JSON loan records. PostgreSQL writes use pgx/v5 SendBatch for batch insert of loan_records, payment_histories, escrow_accounts in a single round-trip per batch.
backend.business_modules: FieldMapper applies 47 declarative YAML rules (rename, numeric-cast, date-format, derive, default). ValidationEngine runs 12 domain rules per record (balance non-negative, payment chronological order, escrow balance matches disbursement sum). ReconciliationBuilder accumulates per-field-group counts (borrower_identity, balance_fields, escrow_fields) and emits field_group_match_rates_json.
backend.failure_handling: Per-batch atomic PostgreSQL transaction; on error the batch rolls back and the failed record is written to dead-letter CSV with field values and rule violation codes. Migration continues to next batch. Dead-letter review required before operator accepts migration as complete.
backend.background_jobs: Async checkpoint goroutine buffers run state in memory and flushes to S3 every 10 batches or on SIGTERM. Bounds replay gap to 5000 records maximum on unexpected interruption.

security.auth_design: No user login. Binary authenticates to AWS via instance profile IAM role. PostgreSQL via env-var DSN sourced from Secrets Manager at startup; held in memory, never written to disk or logs.
security.secrets_management: Secrets Manager stores PostgreSQL DSN and JDBC bridge credentials. CLI fetches at startup, holds in process memory only. JDBC credentials piped via stdin to subprocess; not in environment variables or process arguments.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every migrated record ID written to structured audit log (record_id, source_offset, target_id, timestamp, operator_run_id). Audit log shipped to CloudWatch Logs and written as immutable gzip to S3 at run completion. Retained 7 years per SOX policy.
security.security_risks: VSAM field widths could allow numeric overflow during cast; FieldMapper pre-validates all numeric casts before insert rather than relying on PostgreSQL constraint errors. Checkpoint files encrypted SSE-KMS; no plaintext financial data persisted on workstation disk.

data.entities: loan_records (id UUID, legacy_loan_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, borrower_profile_id UUID FK, principal_balance_cents BIGINT, interest_rate_bps INT, origination_date DATE, maturity_date DATE, loan_status VARCHAR, migrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_histories (id UUID, loan_id UUID FK, payment_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, payment_type VARCHAR, legacy_transaction_id VARCHAR UNIQUE). escrow_accounts (id UUID, loan_id UUID FK, escrow_balance_cents BIGINT, disbursement_schedule_json JSONB, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). borrower_profiles (id UUID, legacy_borrower_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_encrypted BYTEA, ssn_hash VARCHAR, address_json JSONB). migration_checkpoints (run_id UUID, last_committed_offset BIGINT, batch_number INT, s3_key VARCHAR). field_mappings (id UUID, source_field VARCHAR, target_field VARCHAR, transform_rule VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN). reconciliation_reports (run_id UUID, entity_type VARCHAR, total_records INT, matched_records INT, field_group_match_rates_json JSONB NOT NULL, dead_letter_count INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.schema_notes: All PII fields encrypted at rest via pgcrypto with KMS-managed key. legacy_loan_id and legacy_borrower_id retained as immutable cross-references for post-migration SOX audit. field_group_match_rates_json schema published to docs/schemas/reconciliation-report.json as stable contract.
data.data_risks: VSAM numeric field widths differ from PostgreSQL BIGINT precision; overflow caught in FieldMapper before insert. Duplicate legacy_loan_id across VSAM datasets possible due to historical data splits; deduplication logic required before upsert to loan_records.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds RHEL 8 binary via GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build. Binary and SHA-256 manifest published to internal Artifactory. Operators download via curl with checksum verification before each migration run.
devops.ci_cd_design: CI pipeline: golangci-lint, unit tests (FieldMapper and ValidationEngine at 90% coverage gate), integration test against testcontainers PostgreSQL 16 with 500-record VSAM fixture asserting field-group keys present in dry-run output, binary build, Artifactory publish.
devops.rollback_strategy: 'loanmigrate rollback --run-id <id>' reads last committed checkpoint from S3, issues targeted DELETE on target tables for records written after last checkpoint offset, reports records removed. Rollback tested in staging with injected process kill at batch 50.
devops.devops_risks: JDBC bridge JVM version mismatch between CI and RHEL 8 workstation could produce different numeric parsing behavior. Pinned JDK 17 in operator runbook; CI integration test runs JDBC bridge in same JDK version.

ISS-001.title: Reconciliation report lacks per-field-group match rate breakdown
ISS-001.detail: Report only emits total matched/total counts. SOX audit tools require match rates broken out by field groups: borrower_identity, balance_fields, escrow_fields. Round 2 plan acknowledged the issue but did not update the ReconciliationBuilder output struct or reconciliation_reports schema.

ISS-002.detail: Resolved in round 2 by scoping IAM role to S3 checkpoint prefix path only.

Policy scoped to checkpoint prefix and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents.

ISS-003.title: JDBC bridge credentials exposed via environment variable
ISS-003.detail: Resolved in round 2 by piping credentials via stdin to subprocess.

fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved; downstream SOX audit tools can parse field-group match rates without schema negotiation

target_output.title: Loan Servicing COBOL Migration CLI - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Cobra CLI for engineering operators migrating loan portfolios from COBOL/VSAM to PostgreSQL 16. Features dry-run mode with field-group-level reconciliation reporting (borrower_identity, balance_fields, escrow_fields match rates), live batch migration with async S3 SSE-KMS checkpointing, and checkpoint-based rollback. SOX audit controls maintained via immutable CloudWatch Logs and S3-archived audit files per run. Round 3 resolves ISS-001 by delivering structured field-group match rates in both the dry-run stdout output and the reconciliation_reports database schema.
target_output.architecture_overview: Operator-driven CLI with five Cobra subcommands executing on RHEL 8 migration workstations. 'validate-mapping' parses field mapping YAML and checks type compatibility against both VSAM field widths and PostgreSQL column definitions before any data read. 'dry-run' invokes the JDBC bridge subprocess, applies FieldMapper transforms (47 rules), runs ValidationEngine (12 domain rules), and emits a structured JSON reconciliation report to stdout with per-entity and per-field-group match rates. 'migrate' executes live batches via pgx/v5 SendBatch; an async checkpoint goroutine flushes run state to S3 with SSE-KMS every 10 batches bounding replay gap to 5000 records. 'rollback' reads the last committed S3 checkpoint and issues targeted DELETEs on target tables for records beyond that offset. 'status' reports the current checkpoint offset and error count for a given run_id. IAM instance profile authenticates to AWS; PostgreSQL credentials sourced from Secrets Manager at startup.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 cross-compiled to RHEL 8 binary (GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64). Cobra for subcommand parsing; Viper for YAML config loading. pgx/v5 with SendBatch for batch PostgreSQL inserts. IBM JDBC bridge invoked via os/exec with stdin pipe for credential delivery. AWS SDK v2 for S3 SSE-KMS checkpoint storage and Secrets Manager credential retrieval. CloudWatch Logs SDK for structured log shipping. zerolog for structured JSON logging. testcontainers-go for PostgreSQL integration tests. Binary and SHA-256 manifest published to Artifactory via GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: validate-mapping: parses field_mapping YAML, checks source VSAM field widths against PostgreSQL column types, reports incompatibilities before any data read. dry-run: reads full VSAM dataset, applies FieldMapper and ValidationEngine, emits JSON reconciliation report with per-entity and per-field-group match rates to stdout. migrate: live batch migration with progress bar (records/sec, error count, checkpoint offset) to stdout and structured logs to CloudWatch; supports --resume flag for checkpoint-based continuation. rollback: reads S3 checkpoint, deletes post-checkpoint records from target tables, reports records removed. status: fetches last checkpoint for a run_id and reports offset, batch number, and error count.
target_output.system_components: Cobra CLI entrypoint parsing subcommands and flags. FieldMapper module: applies 47 YAML-declared rules (rename, numeric-cast, date-format, derive, default) to raw VSAM record structs. ValidationEngine: 12 business rules (balance non-negative, payment chronological order, escrow balance matches disbursement sum, interest rate in valid range). ReconciliationBuilder: aggregates match counts per field group (borrower_identity: name, SSN hash, DOB; balance_fields: principal_balance_cents, interest_rate_bps, outstanding_fees_cents; escrow_fields: escrow_balance_cents, disbursement_schedule) and emits field_group_match_rates_json per the published schema. CheckpointManager: async goroutine buffering state in memory and flushing to S3 every 10 batches or on SIGTERM. Dead-letter writer: appends failing records to CSV with field values and rule violation codes for operator review.
target_output.workflows: Dry-Run Validation Workflow: Operator runs 'loanmigrate dry-run --config migration.yaml --portfolio PORTFOLIO-A --output-file report.json'. JDBC bridge subprocess reads all VSAM loan records for the portfolio. FieldMapper applies 47 mapping rules. ValidationEngine runs 12 rules per record. ReconciliationBuilder accumulates counts per field group. Output JSON: {total_records: 10000, matched_records: 9955, field_group_match_rates: {borrower_identity: {total: 10000, matched: 9980, match_pct: 99.8}, balance_fields: {total: 10000, matched: 9955, match_pct: 99.6}, escrow_fields: {total: 8200, matched: 8180, match_pct: 99.8}}, dead_letter_count: 45, rule_violation_breakdown: {balance_negative: 12, escrow_mismatch: 33}}. Operator reviews field-group rates, confirms all above 99% threshold, obtains change management approval before proceeding to live migration. Live Migration Workflow: Operator runs 'loanmigrate migrate --config migration.yaml --portfolio PORTFOLIO-A --batch-size 500'. Tool reads 500-record VSAM batches. FieldMapper transforms. pgx SendBatch inserts loan_records, payment_histories, escrow_accounts in single PostgreSQL round-trip per batch. CheckpointManager goroutine flushes run state to S3 SSE-KMS every 10 batches. Dead-letter records written to CSV. On completion, audit log gzipped to S3. On interruption, operator resumes with --resume --run-id flag.
target_output.data_model: loan_records (id UUID PK, legacy_loan_id VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE, borrower_profile_id UUID FK, principal_balance_cents BIGINT, interest_rate_bps INT, origination_date DATE, maturity_date DATE, loan_status VARCHAR, migrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_histories (id UUID PK, loan_id UUID FK, payment_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, payment_type VARCHAR, legacy_transaction_id VARCHAR UNIQUE). escrow_accounts (id UUID PK, loan_id UUID FK, escrow_balance_cents BIGINT, disbursement_schedule_json JSONB, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). borrower_profiles (id UUID PK, legacy_borrower_id VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE, name_encrypted BYTEA, ssn_hash VARCHAR, address_json JSONB, migrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). migration_checkpoints (run_id UUID PK, last_committed_offset BIGINT, batch_number INT, portfolio_id VARCHAR, s3_key VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). field_mappings (id UUID PK, source_field VARCHAR, target_field VARCHAR, transform_rule VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN). reconciliation_reports (run_id UUID PK, portfolio_id VARCHAR, entity_type VARCHAR, total_records INT, matched_records INT, field_group_match_rates_json JSONB NOT NULL, dead_letter_count INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Stable JSON schema for field_group_match_rates_json published at docs/schemas/reconciliation-report.json with version field for future evolution.
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API. Command interface: 'loanmigrate validate-mapping --config <yaml>' checks VSAM-to-PostgreSQL type compatibility. 'loanmigrate dry-run --config <yaml> --portfolio <id> [--output-file report.json]' produces field-group reconciliation report. 'loanmigrate migrate --config <yaml> --portfolio <id> --batch-size <n> [--resume] [--run-id <id>]' executes live migration. 'loanmigrate rollback --run-id <id>' deletes post-checkpoint records. 'loanmigrate status --run-id <id>' reports current offset. Config YAML schema: vsam_datasets[], field_mapping_rules[], target_dsn_secret_arn, s3_checkpoint_prefix, cloudwatch_log_group, batch_size.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM instance profile role grants S3 PutObject/GetObject/ListBucket on arn:aws:s3:::migration-checkpoints/PORTFOLIO-A/* and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents on the designated log group only; no wildcard S3 permissions. PostgreSQL connection uses migration-scoped role with INSERT/UPDATE on loan_records, payment_histories, escrow_accounts, borrower_profiles; SELECT on field_mappings; no DDL rights. Credentials from Secrets Manager at startup; held in memory only; never written to disk or logs. JDBC credentials piped via stdin; not in environment variables or process args. S3 checkpoint and audit files use SSE-KMS with migration-dedicated KMS key. Reconciliation reports include SOX audit metadata: operator_run_id, workstation_hostname (os.Hostname()), start_time, end_time. CloudWatch structured logs retained 7 years per SOX policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: golangci-lint, go test with 90% coverage gate on FieldMapper, ValidationEngine, and ReconciliationBuilder, integration test against testcontainers PostgreSQL 16 with 500-record VSAM fixture asserting field-group breakdown keys present in dry-run JSON output, cross-compile RHEL 8 binary with ldflags version injection, compute SHA-256, publish to Artifactory. Release notes document JDK 17 requirement. Operator distribution: curl download from Artifactory with checksum verification. Pre-migration runbook: (1) verify JDK 17 installed, (2) confirm IAM role attached and MFA active, (3) run validate-mapping, (4) run dry-run and review field-group match rates in reconciliation report, (5) obtain change management approval with dual-operator sign-off, (6) run migrate. Environments: staging uses 10K-record synthetic portfolio with anonymized borrower data; production requires CAB approval ticket reference in migration config.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON logs to CloudWatch Logs via CloudWatch agent on RHEL 8 workstation. Per-batch stdout progress line: {batch_number, records_processed, records_failed, current_rate_rps, checkpoint_offset, elapsed_seconds}. CloudWatch metric filters extract migration_records_per_second, migration_error_rate, checkpoint_lag_batches. Post-run CloudWatch Logs Insights query validates total migrated_at count in loan_records matches VSAM source record count. Reconciliation report JSON written to --output-file for ingestion by SOX audit tooling pipeline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud compute cost; runs on existing RHEL 8 migration workstations. S3 checkpoint JSON files under 1 MB per portfolio; audit log gzip under 50 MB per run. CloudWatch Logs ingestion approximately 100 MB per migration run. KMS API calls one per S3 PutObject. Future parallel multi-portfolio migration via SQS work queue would require checkpoint namespace partitioning by portfolio_id to prevent cross-portfolio offset collision.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (complete in rounds 1-2): validate-mapping, dry-run, live migrate with S3 checkpointing, rollback, IAM least-privilege scoping, JDBC credential security via stdin pipe. Phase 2 - Round 3 delivery: ReconciliationBuilder field-group match rate output struct, reconciliation_reports JSONB schema update, JSON schema publication at docs/schemas/reconciliation-report.json, integration test for field-group keys. Phase 3 (future): parallel multi-portfolio migration with SQS work queue, auto-reconciliation against post-migration servicing platform daily reports, Prometheus metrics endpoint for migration operations dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforced in CI. FieldMapper, ValidationEngine, and ReconciliationBuilder unit tests at 90% coverage minimum. Integration tests use testcontainers-go for PostgreSQL 16 with a VSAM fixture simulator. ReconciliationBuilder output struct is a stable public contract; any field addition or removal requires a semver minor or major bump and a schema_version increment in reconciliation_reports. No fmt.Println in library code; all output via zerolog structured logger or explicit os.Stdout writes in the CLI presentation layer. Binary versioned with ldflags -X main.Version=$(git describe --tags). SHA-256 manifest required for every Artifactory release.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: VSAM numeric field overflow during cast to BIGINT for unusual historical loan amounts coded outside standard COBOL PIC 9 widths. Mitigation: FieldMapper pre-validates all numeric casts with explicit overflow check; dead-letters on overflow rather than silent truncation. Risk: JDBC bridge JVM version mismatch between CI environment and RHEL 8 workstation producing different decimal parsing behavior for interest rates. Mitigation: JDK 17 pinned in operator runbook; CI integration test runs JDBC bridge in same JDK version as production workstations. Tradeoff: async checkpoint flush every 10 batches over per-batch flush. Chosen for throughput; performance testing showed per-batch S3 writes added 40ms latency per batch, which would extend a 500K-record migration by 5+ hours.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Reconciliation report JSON schema published to docs/schemas/reconciliation-report.json as stable contract for SOX audit tool integration. JDBC bridge credential delivery confirmed as stdin pipe; environment variable approach abandoned. Checkpoint granularity set at 10-batch async flush after performance testing. JDK 17 version pinned in operator runbook and verified in CI integration test JDBC bridge execution environment.

metadata.notes: Round 3 COBOL/VSAM loan migration CLI; ISS-001 resolved with field-group reconciliation report schema; SOX and CFPB Regulation X compliant.

profile.domain: Fintech Payment Rails SDK

project_goal.value: TypeScript/Node.js SDK providing a unified interface to ACH, wire transfer, RTP, and FedNow payment rails with ISO 20022 message formatting, idempotency, and status polling so consuming services avoid per-rail implementation
project_goal.rationale: Multiple internal services each reimplementing ACH and wire logic with divergent idempotency handling; SDK unifies this

target_users.value: Internal backend engineering teams building payment initiation and reconciliation services
target_users.rationale: SDK is a developer tool for internal consumers; not end-user facing

project_class.rationale: Distributed as an npm package to internal services; no hosted platform or UI

capabilities.rationale: SDK wraps payment rail gateways; no frontend, no auth service, no AI

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-rail ISO 20022 message formatting, idempotency key management, exponential backoff polling, and return code handling across NACHA, Fedwire, and FedNow rule sets

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect message formatting or duplicate submission could result in erroneous fund transfers with regulatory and financial exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Handles payment amounts, account routing numbers, and settlement records

external_exposure.rationale: Published to internal npm registry; not exposed publicly

access_model.value: OAuth 2.0 client credentials per rail; consuming services provide per-rail credential config at SDK initialization
access_model.rationale: Each payment rail gateway requires separate OAuth client credential grant

feature_scope.value: send() for payment initiation per rail, status polling with exponential backoff, return and reversal event emission, idempotency key deduplication via Redis, rail-specific ISO 20022 message builders, settlement record hydration
feature_scope.rationale: Core SDK API surface covering all four rails

mvp_scope.value: ACH and RTP rails with send(), status polling, and return event emission; idempotency via Redis
mvp_scope.rationale: ACH and RTP cover 90% of current internal payment volume

security_baseline.value: OAuth 2.0 client credentials with token rotation; no credential storage in SDK; TLS 1.3 to all rail gateways; idempotency key TTL to prevent replay
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS data handling requirements for card-linked funding

frontend_stack.value: N/A - Node.js SDK package only; no browser bundle
frontend_stack.rationale: library_sdk class; no browser UI or frontend bundle

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with TypeScript 5.4; strict mode; published to internal npm registry
backend_stack.rationale: TypeScript strict mode ensures consumer type safety; Node.js 20 LTS for production stability

data_platform.value: Redis 7 for idempotency key storage and status event cache; no persistent database owned by the SDK
data_platform.rationale: SDK is stateless except for idempotency; Redis TTL handles key expiry

hosting_target.value: Internal npm registry (Artifactory); consumers run the SDK in their own service containers
hosting_target.rationale: Library package; not a hosted service

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK does not persist payment data; idempotency keys in Redis TTL 72 hours; consumers own retention of payment_instructions and settlement_records
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK is a passthrough; data ownership and retention belongs to consuming services

future_scope.value: SWIFT cross-border rail support; webhook delivery for status events; circuit breaker per rail with health dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap after ACH/RTP adoption validated

constraints.value: Must not bundle any persistent storage; must export full TypeScript types; semver strictly enforced; breaking changes require major version bump
constraints.rationale: Consumer services depend on stable types and no surprise storage dependencies

observability_baseline.value: SDK emits OpenTelemetry spans per rail call; consumers wire their own Datadog APM exporter; structured error objects include rail, error_code, and trace_id
observability_baseline.rationale: SDK must be transparent to consumer APM without owning the telemetry backend

execution_preference.value: Async/await throughout; no callbacks; EventEmitter for status events; TypeScript strict mode enforced in CI
execution_preference.rationale: Modern async patterns required for consuming service compatibility

compliance_context.value: NACHA Operating Rules for ACH debit/credit entries; Fedwire and FedNow service rules for real-time gross settlement; PCI-DSS data handling for card-linked funding source account data
compliance_context.rationale: All four payment rails carry independent regulatory obligations

project.goal: Unified TypeScript SDK for ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow payment rails
project.target_users: Internal backend engineering teams
project.timeline: Q2 internal release with ACH and RTP; Q3 wire and FedNow

backend.framework: TypeScript 5.4 strict mode; no framework; pure library
backend.api: Exported TypeScript functions and classes; EventEmitter for status events

security.auth: OAuth 2.0 client credentials per rail; token lifecycle managed by SDK
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 to all rail gateways; idempotency key TTL prevents replay
security.compliance: NACHA, Fedwire rules, FedNow rules, PCI-DSS

data.cache: Redis 7 for idempotency key storage and deduplication

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with TypeScript strict compile, Jest unit tests, npm publish to Artifactory

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK must be strictly additive across minor versions. Consuming services cannot absorb breaking changes in the send() function signature without coordinated releases. The return event emission API introduced in round 1 used an opaque error object rather than a typed PaymentReturnedEvent; this is the ISS-001 blocker that must be resolved in round 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round 1 plan used a generic Error subclass for return events, which means consuming services must do runtime instanceof checks with no TypeScript narrowing benefit. The fix is a typed PaymentReturnedEvent exported interface with discriminated union on event_type. The EventEmitter approach is sound but the event payload type must be exported explicitly in the package index.
reasoner_reviews.security: Token rotation for OAuth client credentials was specified in round 1 but the implementation detail was left as 'refresh before expiry'. The SDK must proactively refresh tokens when the TTL falls below 60 seconds and queue in-flight calls during the refresh window rather than letting them fail with 401. This is critical for RTP where sub-second latency is expected.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Redis TTL of 72 hours for idempotency keys matches NACHA same-day settlement window. For RTP and FedNow where final status is typically returned within 10 seconds, a shorter TTL of 1 hour is sufficient and reduces Redis memory footprint for high-volume consumers.
reasoner_reviews.critic: ISS-001 from round 1 (untyped return event payload) was acknowledged in the round 1 plan narrative but the exported types in the package index did not include PaymentReturnedEvent. Round 2 must export the typed event interface and update the EventEmitter generic typing to PaymentEventEmitter<PaymentReturnedEvent>.

backend.service_design: SDK exports a RailClient class instantiated with per-rail config (rail type, OAuth credentials, Redis client, optional OTel tracer). Single send(instruction: PaymentInstruction): Promise<PaymentResult> method handles all four rails by delegating to rail-specific MessageBuilder and GatewayClient implementations.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP server. Public API surface: RailClient class, send() method, PaymentInstruction interface, PaymentResult interface, PaymentReturnedEvent interface (typed discriminated union), poll() for manual status queries, on('payment.returned', handler) EventEmitter subscription. All interfaces exported from package index.
backend.business_modules: ACHMessageBuilder: formats NACHA CTX entries with addenda records for corporate credits. RTPMessageBuilder: formats ISO 20022 pain.001 XML for RTP gateway submission. FedNowMessageBuilder: formats ISO 20022 pacs.008 for FedNow credit transfer. WireMessageBuilder: formats Fedwire funds transfer messages. IdempotencyService: checks Redis for existing idempotency key before submission; stores result on success. StatusPoller: exponential backoff polling (100ms, 200ms, 400ms... cap 30s) for rail gateways that return async status.
backend.failure_handling: Typed error hierarchy: PaymentRailError (base), PaymentDuplicateError (idempotency hit), PaymentReturnedError (NACHA return code), PaymentTimeoutError (polling cap exceeded), PaymentValidationError (pre-submission rule check). All error types exported from package index for consumer instanceof checks with TypeScript narrowing.
backend.scaling_notes: SDK is stateless per call except for Redis idempotency. High-volume consumers should share a single RailClient instance per rail to reuse the OAuth token cache and Redis connection pool.

security.auth_design: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow per rail. SDK manages token lifecycle: fetches token at first use, caches in memory with expiry, proactively refreshes when TTL < 60 seconds, queues in-flight calls during 200ms refresh window rather than returning 401.
security.secrets_management: Consuming services supply credentials via RailClient constructor config; SDK never reads from environment variables or config files. Credentials held in memory; never logged or included in OTel span attributes.
security.abuse_prevention: Idempotency key (UUID v4 generated per send() call or supplied by consumer) stored in Redis with 72-hour TTL for ACH/wire, 1-hour TTL for RTP/FedNow. Duplicate key returns cached PaymentResult immediately without re-submitting to gateway.
security.security_risks: Card-linked funding source account numbers must not be included in OTel span attributes; SDK masks account_number to last 4 digits in all telemetry. NACHA file submissions must use TLS 1.3; SDK enforces minimum TLS version in gateway HTTP clients.

data.entities: PaymentInstruction (rail, amount_cents, currency, source_account, destination_account, idempotency_key, metadata). PaymentResult (payment_id, rail, status, submitted_at, gateway_reference, idempotency_key). PaymentReturnedEvent (event_type: 'payment.returned', payment_id, rail, return_code, return_reason, returned_at). StatusEvent (payment_id, rail, status, event_type, timestamp). ReturnCode (NACHA R-codes: R01-R29; FedNow reject codes; Fedwire error codes). SettlementRecord (payment_id, rail, settled_amount_cents, settlement_date, gateway_settlement_id).
data.schema_notes: SDK does not own a database schema. PaymentInstruction and all other types are TypeScript interfaces exported from the package. IdempotencyService serializes PaymentResult to Redis JSON with TTL. Consumers are responsible for persisting PaymentResult and SettlementRecord to their own storage.
data.data_risks: NACHA return codes are rail-specific; a generic return_code string field would lose typing. Discriminated union on rail field allows consumers to narrow to NACHA-specific R-codes vs FedNow reject codes with TypeScript type guards.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions publishes to internal Artifactory npm registry on every semver tag. Separate preview channel for pre-release versions. Changelog generated from conventional commits.
devops.ci_cd_design: CI: tsc --strict compile, ESLint with @typescript-eslint/strict ruleset, Jest unit tests at 85% coverage gate (MessageBuilders and IdempotencyService), Jest integration tests against mocked gateway fixtures and Redis testcontainer, npm pack and publish to Artifactory on tag.
devops.devops_risks: Major version bumps require consumer migration guides. Automated breaking change detection via @arethetypeswrong/cli ensures exported types do not regress. Redis dependency version must be documented in peer dependencies.

ISS-001.title: PaymentReturnedEvent payload is untyped; not exported from package index
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 emitted return events using a generic EventEmitter with an opaque object payload. Consuming services cannot narrow the event type with TypeScript without runtime instanceof checks. PaymentReturnedEvent interface must be exported from the package index with discriminated union on event_type.

history.note: Auditor flagged: EventEmitter<any> loses all TypeScript benefit for return event consumers.

ISS-002.title: OAuth token refresh races concurrent calls on expiry
ISS-002.detail: If multiple concurrent send() calls hit the OAuth token expiry boundary simultaneously, all will attempt a token refresh. Need a single-flight refresh mutex to coalesce concurrent refreshes into one HTTP call.

history.note: Security reviewer noted: concurrent 401s under load would cause thundering herd on OAuth endpoint.

focus_issues.title: PaymentReturnedEvent payload is untyped; not exported from package index
focus_issues.detail: Define and export PaymentReturnedEvent interface with discriminated union on event_type and rail-specific return_code union types. Update EventEmitter typing to PaymentEventEmitter.

focus_issues.title: OAuth token refresh races concurrent calls on expiry
focus_issues.detail: Implement single-flight refresh mutex using a pending Promise stored in memory; concurrent calls await the same refresh Promise rather than each issuing a new HTTP call.

latest_recommendations: Export PaymentReturnedEvent as a typed discriminated union interface from package index
Implement single-flight OAuth token refresh mutex
Add rail-specific return code union types (NACHA R-codes, FedNow reject codes) to exported types
Update EventEmitter typing to generic PaymentEventEmitter
