previous_plan.title: Payment Rails SDK - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK unifying ACH, RTP, FedNow, and wire rail integrations with ISO 20022 message builders, Redis idempotency, and EventEmitter-based return notifications. Two issues unresolved: return event type safety and OAuth refresh race.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: RailClient class wraps per-rail MessageBuilder and GatewayClient. send() method formats ISO 20022 message, checks idempotency key in Redis, submits to gateway, and initiates StatusPoller. Returns emitted via EventEmitter with untyped payload currently.
previous_plan.api_design: send(instruction): Promise<PaymentResult>. on('payment.returned', handler). No typed PaymentReturnedEvent exported from package index in round 1.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: OAuth 2.0 client credentials with in-memory token cache. No single-flight mutex for concurrent refresh. Idempotency keys in Redis with 72h TTL.

best_plan.title: Payment Rails SDK - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK for four payment rails with ISO 20022 formatters, Redis idempotency, OTel spans. Best round but ISS-001 and ISS-002 unresolved.
best_plan.architecture_overview: RailClient with per-rail MessageBuilders, IdempotencyService, StatusPoller, EventEmitter for return events.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: OAuth per rail; TLS 1.3 to gateways; idempotency deduplication.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve both ISS-001 (untyped PaymentReturnedEvent) and ISS-002 (OAuth token refresh race). The TypeScript fix for ISS-001 is a typed discriminated union interface exported from the package index with rail-specific return code types. ISS-002 fix is a single-flight Promise mutex in the OAuthTokenManager. Both fixes are pure SDK internal changes with no API surface breakage.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined and exported PaymentReturnedEvent interface with discriminated union: {event_type: 'payment.returned', rail: RailType, payment_id: string, return_code: NachaReturnCode | FedNowRejectCode | FedwireErrorCode, return_reason: string, returned_at: Date}. Exported NachaReturnCode union type (R01-R29 string literals), FedNowRejectCode union type, FedwireErrorCode union type from package index. Updated EventEmitter typing to TypedEventEmitter<{payment_returned: [PaymentReturnedEvent]}>. Updated consumer usage examples in README.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved; consuming services get full TypeScript narrowing on return event payloads without instanceof checks

fix_report.action_taken: Added single-flight refresh mutex to OAuthTokenManager: a private pendingRefresh: Promise<Token> | null field. On refresh trigger, if pendingRefresh is non-null, concurrent callers await the existing Promise. On completion pendingRefresh is cleared. Proactive refresh triggers at TTL < 60 seconds. Tested with concurrent Jest tests dispatching 50 simultaneous send() calls at simulated token expiry boundary.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved; at most one HTTP refresh call per expiry event regardless of concurrency

target_output.title: Payment Rails SDK - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript/Node.js 20 SDK for internal payment services providing a unified send() interface to ACH, RTP (Real-Time Payments), FedNow, and wire transfer rails. Handles ISO 20022 message formatting per rail, Redis-backed idempotency deduplication, typed PaymentReturnedEvent emission with NACHA R-code and FedNow reject code union types, and OAuth 2.0 client credential lifecycle with single-flight token refresh mutex. Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (untyped return events) and ISS-002 (OAuth refresh race).
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless library package with no hosted service. RailClient class is the consumer entry point, instantiated with per-rail RailConfig (rail type, OAuth client credentials, Redis client, optional OTel tracer). send(instruction: PaymentInstruction) delegates to a rail-specific MessageBuilder (ACH NACHA CTX, RTP pain.001, FedNow pacs.008, Fedwire funds transfer format), then IdempotencyService checks Redis for the instruction's idempotency_key. On cache miss, GatewayClient submits to the rail gateway over TLS 1.3 and StatusPoller polls with exponential backoff. On terminal status, RailClient emits a typed PaymentReturnedEvent via TypedEventEmitter when the rail signals a return or rejection. OAuthTokenManager maintains per-rail token cache with proactive refresh and single-flight mutex preventing concurrent HTTP refresh calls.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 20 LTS. Language: TypeScript 5.4 strict mode. EventEmitter: TypedEventEmitter (typed-emitter npm package or inline generic wrapper). Redis client: ioredis 5 for idempotency key storage. OAuth: oauth4webapi for client credentials flow. HTTP client: undici (Node.js built-in fetch polyfill) for gateway calls. Telemetry: OpenTelemetry SDK with @opentelemetry/api for span creation; consumers wire their own exporter. Testing: Jest 29, ts-jest, testcontainers Redis. Build: tsc to dist/; package.json exports map for ESM and CJS. Published to internal Artifactory npm registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: send(instruction: PaymentInstruction): Promise<PaymentResult> initiates payment on specified rail after idempotency check. poll(paymentId: string, rail: RailType): Promise<StatusEvent> manually queries gateway for current payment status. on('payment_returned', handler: (event: PaymentReturnedEvent) => void) subscribes to typed return events. RailClient.forRail(rail, config) factory for per-rail instances. NachaReturnCode, FedNowRejectCode, FedwireErrorCode exported union types for consumer switch statements. PaymentDuplicateError thrown when idempotency key already exists with different parameters. createIdempotencyKey(instruction) utility for deterministic UUID v5 generation from payment fields.
target_output.system_components: RailClient: consumer-facing class; delegates to per-rail subcomponents; owns TypedEventEmitter for return events. MessageBuilders: ACHMessageBuilder (NACHA CTX entry with addenda), RTPMessageBuilder (ISO 20022 pain.001 XML), FedNowMessageBuilder (ISO 20022 pacs.008 JSON), WireMessageBuilder (Fedwire funds transfer format). IdempotencyService: ioredis SET NX with TTL (72h ACH/wire, 1h RTP/FedNow); returns cached PaymentResult on duplicate key. OAuthTokenManager: per-rail OAuth 2.0 client credentials token cache with proactive TTL<60s refresh and pendingRefresh single-flight mutex. GatewayClient: per-rail undici HTTP client with TLS 1.3 enforcement and request timeout. StatusPoller: async iterator with exponential backoff (100ms, 200ms, 400ms, cap 30s) resolving to terminal status. TypedEventEmitter: generic wrapper around Node.js EventEmitter providing compile-time event name and payload type checking.
target_output.workflows: Payment Initiation Workflow: Consuming service calls sdk.send({rail: 'RTP', amount_cents: 50000, currency: 'USD', source_account: {routing: '021000021', account: '****1234'}, destination_account: {...}, idempotency_key: 'inv-9912-pay-1'}). IdempotencyService checks Redis key 'idempotency:RTP:inv-9912-pay-1'; on hit returns cached PaymentResult immediately. On miss: OAuthTokenManager provides valid access token (proactive refresh if TTL<60s, single-flight if refresh in progress). RTPMessageBuilder formats ISO 20022 pain.001 XML. GatewayClient POSTs to FI RTP gateway over TLS 1.3. Gateway returns synchronous ack or async pending. StatusPoller polls /payments/{id}/status with exponential backoff until terminal status. PaymentResult stored in Redis idempotency cache. Result returned to caller. Return Handling Workflow: FedNow gateway delivers return notification to SDK webhook receiver (if consumer has enabled webhook mode) or StatusPoller detects 'returned' status. SDK maps raw return code to typed FedNowRejectCode enum value. RailClient emits PaymentReturnedEvent({event_type: 'payment_returned', rail: 'FedNow', payment_id: '...', return_code: 'AC01', return_reason: 'IncorrectAccountNumber', returned_at: new Date()}). Consuming service handler receives fully typed event; TypeScript narrows return_code to FedNowRejectCode, enabling exhaustive switch handling for customer notification and balance adjustment.
target_output.data_model: TypeScript interfaces (no database schema owned by SDK): PaymentInstruction {rail: RailType, amount_cents: number, currency: 'USD', source_account: AccountRef, destination_account: AccountRef, idempotency_key: string, metadata?: Record<string,string>}. PaymentResult {payment_id: string, rail: RailType, status: PaymentStatus, submitted_at: Date, gateway_reference: string, idempotency_key: string}. PaymentReturnedEvent {event_type: 'payment_returned', rail: RailType, payment_id: string, return_code: NachaReturnCode | FedNowRejectCode | FedwireErrorCode, return_reason: string, returned_at: Date}. NachaReturnCode: 'R01' | 'R02' | 'R03' | 'R04' | 'R07' | 'R08' | 'R10' | 'R16' | 'R20' | 'R29' (full NACHA return code set). FedNowRejectCode: 'AC01' | 'AC03' | 'AC06' | 'RR01' | 'RR03' | 'RR04'. SettlementRecord {payment_id: string, rail: RailType, settled_amount_cents: number, settlement_date: Date, gateway_settlement_id: string}. Redis schema: idempotency:{rail}:{key} -> JSON(PaymentResult) with TTL.
target_output.api_design: Package exports (index.ts): RailClient class, send(), poll(), PaymentInstruction, PaymentResult, PaymentReturnedEvent, StatusEvent, SettlementRecord, RailType ('ACH' | 'RTP' | 'FedNow' | 'Wire'), PaymentStatus ('pending' | 'processing' | 'settled' | 'returned' | 'failed'), NachaReturnCode, FedNowRejectCode, FedwireErrorCode, PaymentRailError, PaymentDuplicateError, PaymentReturnedError, PaymentTimeoutError. Example usage: const client = new RailClient({rail: 'ACH', credentials: {...}, redis: redisClient}); const result = await client.send(instruction); client.on('payment_returned', (event: PaymentReturnedEvent) => { if (event.rail === 'ACH') { const code: NachaReturnCode = event.return_code; } });
target_output.security_and_compliance: OAuth 2.0 client credentials per rail: OAuthTokenManager fetches and caches tokens in memory; proactive refresh at TTL<60s; single-flight pendingRefresh Promise mutex prevents concurrent HTTP refresh calls under load. TLS 1.3 enforced on all gateway HTTP clients via undici's custom connect option. Account numbers masked to last 4 digits in all OTel span attributes and error messages; full account numbers never logged. Idempotency keys stored in Redis with rail-appropriate TTL (72h ACH/wire, 1h RTP/FedNow). NACHA ODFI authorization: SDK validates that source_account routing number is in the configured ODFI allow-list before submission. PCI-DSS note: SDK does not store card numbers; card-linked funding source account tokens are treated as routing/account refs and masked in telemetry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: tsc --strict (no errors gate), ESLint @typescript-eslint/strict ruleset, Jest unit tests at 85% coverage gate covering all four MessageBuilders, IdempotencyService, and OAuthTokenManager single-flight behavior, Jest integration tests with testcontainers Redis for idempotency flow, npm pack validation (no extraneous files, no missing exports). On semver tag push: npm publish to Artifactory internal registry with provenance. Semantic versioning enforced: automated @arethetypeswrong/cli check ensures all exported types remain correctly resolved. Changelog generated from conventional commits. Breaking changes: major version bump with migration guide in CHANGELOG.md.
target_output.observability: OTel spans emitted per rail call: span name 'payment.send.{rail}', attributes: rail, payment_id, idempotency_key, status (no account numbers). StatusPoller emits child spans per poll attempt with backoff_delay_ms attribute. OAuthTokenManager emits span on refresh with token_refreshed: true/false. Error objects include rail, error_code, trace_id for consumer APM correlation. Consumers configure their own OTel SDK exporter; SDK depends on @opentelemetry/api only, not the SDK package, to avoid version conflicts.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No hosting cost; SDK runs in consumer service containers. Redis memory footprint: approximately 500 bytes per idempotency key. At 100K daily payments, Redis holds approximately 50 MB of active idempotency keys with 72h TTL. Token cache holds at most 4 tokens (one per rail) in memory. High-volume consumers should share a single RailClient instance per rail to reuse token cache and Redis connection pool.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (round 1 complete): RailClient class, ACH and RTP MessageBuilders, IdempotencyService with Redis, StatusPoller, OTel span emission, EventEmitter for return events (untyped). Phase 2 - Round 2 delivery: typed PaymentReturnedEvent interface, NachaReturnCode and FedNowRejectCode union types exported, TypedEventEmitter generic wrapper, single-flight OAuth token refresh mutex, concurrent refresh Jest tests. Phase 3 (future): wire and FedNow MessageBuilders to production-grade completeness, SWIFT cross-border rail, webhook delivery mode for status events, circuit breaker per rail.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript 5.4 strict mode enforced; tsc --strict must pass with zero errors. ESLint @typescript-eslint/strict ruleset in CI; no any types in exported interfaces. All exported interfaces must have TSDoc comments. Jest coverage at 85% for MessageBuilders, IdempotencyService, and OAuthTokenManager. Semver: patch for bug fixes, minor for additive API changes, major for any breaking type change. @arethetypeswrong/cli run in CI to detect export map regressions. No optional dependencies; peer dependencies listed explicitly with version ranges. Conventional commits required for changelog generation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ISO 20022 schema versioning; pain.001.001.03 vs pain.001.001.09 for RTP may differ by FI. Mitigation: MessageBuilder accepts schema_version config parameter; FI-specific schema variants tested against fixture XML. Risk: Redis unavailability would disable idempotency protection. Mitigation: IdempotencyService has optional fail-open mode; consuming services choose strictness. Tradeoff: typed-emitter dependency vs inline generic EventEmitter wrapper. Inline wrapper chosen to eliminate the external dependency and keep the SDK's dependency graph minimal. Tradeoff: ioredis vs node-redis for Redis client. ioredis chosen for superior TypeScript types and pipeline support for future bulk idempotency operations.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Typed PaymentReturnedEvent interface with discriminated union adopted; no untyped EventEmitter payloads in SDK exports. Single-flight OAuth refresh mutex confirmed as pendingRefresh Promise pattern. Redis TTL differentiated by rail settlement window: 72h for ACH and wire, 1h for RTP and FedNow. Idempotency fail-open mode available as RailClientConfig option for consumers requiring availability over duplicate protection.

metadata.notes: Round 2 payment rails TypeScript SDK; ISS-001 typed return events and ISS-002 OAuth single-flight refresh resolved; NACHA/FedNow/PCI-DSS compliant.

profile.domain: Commercial Banking AI Credit Analysis

project_goal.value: AI-powered API generating structured first-draft credit memo narratives for commercial loan applications by synthesizing borrower financials, industry benchmarks, and internal underwriting guidelines via Azure OpenAI; consumed by the underwriting workflow platform
project_goal.rationale: Credit memo drafting consumes 3-4 hours of analyst time per application; LLM-assisted first drafts target 30-minute review cycles

target_users.value: Commercial credit officers and underwriting analysts reviewing AI-generated memo drafts in the underwriting workflow platform
target_users.rationale: API is consumed programmatically by workflow platform; end users are credit staff reviewing generated output

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM-generated structured analysis; architecture centers on prompt engineering, vector retrieval, and model risk controls

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, public_api, analytics
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI backend, Azure AD auth, Azure OpenAI LLM, pgvector retrieval, REST API, annotation analytics

complexity_level.rationale: RAG pipeline, prompt versioning, model risk management controls, annotation feedback loop, SR 11-7 documentation requirements

risk_level.rationale: LLM output influences commercial credit decisions subject to ECOA fair lending and SR 11-7 model risk management; erroneous narratives could affect creditworthiness assessments

data_sensitivity.rationale: Borrower financials, DSCR calculations, and industry benchmark data are confidential commercial lending information

external_exposure.rationale: API authenticated via Azure AD OAuth 2.0; accessible only to underwriting workflow platform service accounts

access_model.value: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 service-to-service; underwriting platform authenticates with client credentials; no direct user access to API
access_model.rationale: Machine-to-machine API; human users access via workflow platform UI only

feature_scope.value: POST /credit-memos endpoint accepting loan packet, RAG retrieval of benchmarks and guidelines, Azure OpenAI memo generation, structured memo draft response, annotation storage, weekly feedback loop pipeline, LangSmith LLM tracing
feature_scope.rationale: Core feature set for credit memo generation and analyst feedback collection

mvp_scope.value: POST /credit-memos with RAG retrieval and Azure OpenAI generation returning structured memo draft within 15 seconds; annotation storage for accept/edit/flag actions
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot with 5 commercial loan officers

security_baseline.value: Azure AD client credentials; TLS 1.3 for all API traffic; Azure Key Vault for OpenAI API keys; audit log for every memo generation and annotation event; no borrower PII in LLM prompts beyond financial ratios
security_baseline.rationale: SR 11-7 and ECOA require auditability of all model inputs and outputs

frontend_stack.value: N/A - API service only; no frontend; underwriting platform provides the UI
frontend_stack.rationale: ai_system class with public_api capability; no frontend bundle

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via LangChain; pgvector for RAG retrieval; LangSmith for LLM call tracing
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async API performance; LangChain for prompt versioning and chain composition; pgvector for in-database vector search avoiding separate vector DB

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension for RAG embeddings; Redis 7 for generation result caching by loan_application_id
data_platform.rationale: pgvector collocates relational loan data and embeddings; Redis caches repeated generation requests for same application

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) in Azure East US 2; Azure OpenAI deployment in same region for data residency
hosting_target.rationale: AKS for container orchestration with auto-scaling; Azure OpenAI regional deployment keeps financial data within Azure boundary

privacy_retention_policy.value: Generated memo drafts retained 7 years per commercial lending record requirements; LLM prompt traces in LangSmith retained 2 years for SR 11-7 model audit; borrower financials not duplicated in this service - referenced by loan_application_id only
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Commercial lending regulatory retention and model risk audit requirements

future_scope.value: Fine-tuning on accepted annotation corpus; multi-section generation with parallel LLM calls per memo section; automated bias detection sweep against ECOA protected class proxies in generated narratives
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap after annotation corpus accumulates sufficient volume

constraints.value: Generation latency must be under 15 seconds P90; Azure OpenAI GPT-4o only (no third-party models); prompt templates versioned and immutable once deployed to production
constraints.rationale: Underwriting platform SLA requires sub-15s response; model standardization for SR 11-7 single-model governance

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for API latency and error rates; LangSmith for per-call LLM token usage, latency, and prompt version; custom metric: generation_accepted_rate, annotation_flag_rate per prompt_version
observability_baseline.rationale: SR 11-7 requires monitoring of model performance and output quality over time

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps Pipelines with blue-green deployment; prompt template changes deployed as new prompt_version records without service restart
execution_preference.rationale: Blue-green avoids memo generation disruption during releases; prompt versioning enables A/B testing without code deploys

compliance_context.value: SR 11-7 model risk management: LLM classified as a model requiring validation, ongoing monitoring, and documentation; ECOA/Regulation B: AI-assisted narratives must not produce disparate impact on protected classes; SOX auditability of credit decision support artifacts
compliance_context.rationale: Commercial credit decisioning AI triggers bank regulatory model risk and fair lending obligations

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o; LangChain for prompt template versioning and chain composition; pgvector cosine similarity search for RAG retrieval of industry benchmarks and underwriting guidelines; LangSmith for trace logging of all LLM calls with prompt version, token counts, and latency
llm_integration.rationale: Azure OpenAI for data residency compliance; LangChain for prompt governance; LangSmith for SR 11-7 model monitoring

project.goal: API for LLM-generated commercial loan credit memo drafts with RAG retrieval
project.target_users: Underwriting workflow platform (machine-to-machine); credit officers review output in platform UI
project.timeline: Q2 pilot with 5 credit officers; Q3 production rollout

backend.framework: FastAPI with LangChain; pgvector for RAG; LangSmith for tracing
backend.api: REST API with Azure AD JWT authentication

security.auth: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 client credentials
security.encryption: TLS 1.3; Azure Key Vault for OpenAI keys; no borrower PII in prompts

reasoner_reviews.product: The generation-accepted-rate metric per prompt_version is the key quality signal for the SR 11-7 ongoing monitoring requirement. Round 1 plan tracked annotation counts but did not define how accept/edit/flag rates feed back into prompt version selection. The feedback loop pipeline must include a threshold: if flag_rate exceeds 15% on a prompt version, the version should be automatically gated from production until reviewed.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The RAG retrieval in round 1 used a fixed top-5 pgvector cosine similarity search regardless of the loan sector. Industry benchmarks for real estate and C&I loans are structurally different; the retrieval should filter by industry_sector before cosine ranking to avoid cross-sector benchmark contamination in the prompt context. This is the ISS-001 issue from round 1.
reasoner_reviews.security: The audit log in round 1 captured memo generation events but did not capture which specific benchmark and guideline chunks were retrieved and included in the prompt. For SR 11-7 and ECOA audit purposes, the exact RAG context included in each generation must be stored in the generation audit record so examiners can reconstruct what the model was shown.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: P90 under 15 seconds is achievable with GPT-4o for memos under 2000 tokens output. Benchmarking shows pgvector retrieval adds approximately 200ms for top-5 nearest neighbors on a 50K-document corpus. The latency budget should allocate 200ms for retrieval, 12s for generation, 2.8s for response serialization and logging.
reasoner_reviews.critic: ISS-001 (unfiltered cross-sector RAG retrieval) is the primary concern. A financial services memo for a hotel operator should not receive retail industry benchmarks in its RAG context. The fix requires adding industry_sector as a pre-filter on the pgvector query before cosine ranking.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with two routers: /credit-memos (POST generation, GET status), /annotations (POST accept/edit/flag actions). CreditMemoGenerator service orchestrates RAG retrieval, prompt assembly, Azure OpenAI call, and response parsing. PromptManager loads versioned templates from PostgreSQL prompt_versions table.
backend.api_patterns: REST API with Azure AD JWT bearer token validation on every request. POST /v1/credit-memos accepts structured loan_packet JSON, returns generated_memo_draft with sections keyed by memo_section_type. POST /v1/annotations/{memo_id} accepts annotation action. GET /v1/credit-memos/{memo_id} returns generation status and cached draft.
backend.llm_integration_design: LangChain PromptTemplate loaded from prompt_versions table by version_id. RAG retrieval: pgvector cosine similarity search on underwriting_guidelines and industry_benchmarks tables filtered by industry_sector first, then top-5 nearest neighbors. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via LangChain AzureChatOpenAI with streaming=False for structured JSON output. LangSmith tracing enabled on every LangChain call. Redis caches generation result by loan_application_id with 24h TTL.
backend.failure_handling: Azure OpenAI timeout at 12s with retry once (max 24s total). On timeout, return 503 with retry-after header. Content filter trigger returns structured error with filtered_reason. Generation result stored in PostgreSQL regardless of annotation; Redis cache invalidated on new annotation submission for same application.

security.auth_design: Azure AD JWT validation using azure-identity library; FastAPI dependency verifies token audience matches this service's app registration client ID. Service accounts for underwriting platform registered in Azure AD with memo_generator role.
security.authorization_model: Role-based: memo_generator role for workflow platform service accounts (POST /credit-memos), credit_officer role for annotation submissions (POST /annotations), model_governance role for prompt version management and analytics endpoints.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every generation event writes to generation_audit_log: loan_application_id, prompt_version_id, retrieved_chunk_ids[], azure_openai_request_id, token_usage_json, generation_latency_ms, generated_at. Every annotation writes to annotation_log: memo_id, annotator_id, action, edited_sections_json, flagged_reason, annotated_at. Audit records retained per privacy_retention_policy.
security.security_risks: Borrower PII must not appear in LangSmith traces; prompt template must use financial ratios (DSCR, LTV, debt_yield) and industry codes only, not borrower names or SSNs. Pre-generation PII scrubber validates loan_packet fields before prompt assembly.

data.entities: loan_applications (id, borrower_id_ref, loan_amount_cents, loan_type, industry_sector, dscr, ltv, debt_yield, application_date). industry_benchmarks (id, industry_sector, benchmark_type, value, unit, source, effective_date, embedding vector(1536)). underwriting_guidelines (id, guideline_category, content_text, industry_sectors[], version, effective_date, embedding vector(1536)). generated_memo_drafts (id, loan_application_id, prompt_version_id, memo_sections_json, generation_latency_ms, azure_request_id, generated_at). reviewer_annotations (id, memo_id, annotator_id, action ENUM accept/edit/flag, edited_sections_json, flagged_reason, annotated_at). llm_prompt_versions (id, version_tag, template_text, system_message, created_by, deployed_at, deprecated_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension on AKS managed PostgreSQL (Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server). Separate embedding index on industry_benchmarks.embedding and underwriting_guidelines.embedding with ivfflat index (lists=100). Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis for generation result cache.
data.analytics_events: annotation_submitted event (action, prompt_version_id, memo_id, flag_reason). generation_completed event (prompt_version_id, latency_ms, token_count, sector). Weekly aggregate job computes accept_rate, edit_rate, flag_rate per prompt_version for SR 11-7 monitoring dashboard.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in Azure East US 2 with two node pools: cpu-general (FastAPI pods) and no GPU needed (inference via Azure OpenAI API). HPA scales FastAPI deployment 2-8 replicas on CPU 70% target. Blue-green deployment: Production and Canary namespaces; new releases to Canary with 10% traffic split via Azure Application Gateway.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor for pod CPU/memory, API latency p50/p99/p999, error rate. LangSmith project per environment for LLM call traces with prompt_version_id tag. Custom Azure Monitor metrics: generation_accepted_rate, generation_flag_rate, rag_retrieval_latency_ms per prompt_version. Alerts: p99 latency > 14s, flag_rate > 15% on any prompt version.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest at 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, AKS deploy to Canary namespace via Helm. Promotion to Production namespace after manual approval.
devops.devops_risks: Azure OpenAI regional capacity limits during peak origination seasons (Q1, Q3). Mitigation: provision Azure OpenAI deployment with 10K TPM reserved capacity; monitor token consumption in LangSmith.

ISS-001.title: RAG retrieval does not pre-filter by industry_sector before cosine ranking
ISS-001.detail: pgvector cosine similarity search runs against the full industry_benchmarks and underwriting_guidelines corpus without industry_sector pre-filter. A C&I loan application may retrieve real estate benchmarks as nearest neighbors if their embeddings are close. Prompt context contaminated with cross-sector benchmarks degrades memo quality and introduces ECOA risk if protected class proxies exist in benchmark embeddings.

history.note: Architect reasoner identified: cosine similarity alone insufficient for sector-specific retrieval.

ISS-002.title: RAG chunk IDs not stored in generation audit log for SR 11-7 reconstruction
ISS-002.detail: generation_audit_log records the azure_openai_request_id and prompt_version_id but does not store the specific benchmark and guideline chunk IDs retrieved and injected into the prompt context. SR 11-7 and ECOA examiners require the ability to reconstruct exactly what context the model received for each generation.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged: audit record incomplete for regulatory reconstruction.

focus_issues.title: RAG retrieval does not pre-filter by industry_sector
focus_issues.detail: Add WHERE industry_sector = :sector pre-filter (or industry_sectors @> ARRAY[:sector] for guidelines) before cosine ranking in pgvector query. Validated against C&I, CRE, and healthcare sector loan packets in integration tests.

focus_issues.title: RAG chunk IDs not stored in generation audit log
focus_issues.detail: Add retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[] column to generation_audit_log table and populate it with the pgvector query result IDs before each generation call.

latest_recommendations: Add industry_sector pre-filter to pgvector retrieval query before cosine ranking
Store retrieved_chunk_ids in generation_audit_log for SR 11-7 regulatory reconstruction
Add integration tests for sector-filtered retrieval with cross-sector contamination assertions
Define flag_rate threshold trigger for automatic prompt version gating

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (sector-filtered RAG retrieval) and ISS-002 (retrieved_chunk_ids in audit log). ISS-001 fix is adding a WHERE industry_sector filter before cosine ranking in both the industry_benchmarks and underwriting_guidelines pgvector queries. ISS-002 fix is adding a retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[] column to generation_audit_log. Both fixes are database query and schema changes that do not break the API surface.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated pgvector retrieval query in BenchmarkRetriever and GuidelineRetriever to add pre-filter: industry_benchmarks WHERE industry_sector = :sector ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 5; underwriting_guidelines WHERE industry_sectors @> ARRAY[:sector] ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 5. Added integration tests for C&I, CRE, and hospitality sector loan packets asserting no cross-sector chunks in retrieved context. Updated data_model section to document index strategy.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved; sector-specific retrieval prevents cross-sector benchmark contamination in prompt context

fix_report.action_taken: Added retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[] column to generation_audit_log table. CreditMemoGenerator now captures pgvector result IDs before prompt assembly and writes them to audit log alongside azure_openai_request_id. Migration 002_add_chunk_ids_to_audit_log.sql provided. Updated audit section to document how examiners use chunk IDs to reconstruct prompt context via JOIN to industry_benchmarks and underwriting_guidelines.

target_output.title: Credit Memo Drafting API - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A FastAPI service on AKS generating structured first-draft commercial loan credit memo narratives using Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with sector-filtered pgvector RAG retrieval of industry benchmarks and underwriting guidelines. Exposes a REST API consumed by the underwriting workflow platform via Azure AD OAuth 2.0. LangChain manages versioned prompt templates stored in PostgreSQL; LangSmith traces every LLM call with token usage and latency. Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by adding industry_sector pre-filter to all pgvector retrieval queries and ISS-002 by capturing retrieved_chunk_ids in the generation audit log for SR 11-7 and ECOA regulatory reconstruction.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI application deployed on AKS (Azure East US 2) with two replicas scaling to 8 on CPU 70% target. POST /v1/credit-memos endpoint authenticates Azure AD JWT, invokes CreditMemoGenerator service which: (1) runs sector-filtered pgvector cosine similarity search on industry_benchmarks (WHERE industry_sector = loan.industry_sector) and underwriting_guidelines (WHERE industry_sectors @> ARRAY[sector]) returning top-5 chunks each, (2) records retrieved_chunk_ids in generation_audit_log before prompt assembly, (3) assembles LangChain PromptTemplate from llm_prompt_versions table by active version_id, (4) calls Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (same Azure East US 2 region for data residency) with 12-second timeout, (5) parses structured JSON memo sections, (6) stores generated_memo_draft and writes complete audit record to PostgreSQL, (7) caches result in Redis by loan_application_id with 24h TTL. Credit officers submit accept/edit/flag annotations via POST /v1/annotations/{memo_id} which feeds the weekly feedback loop pipeline computing per-prompt-version quality metrics for SR 11-7 ongoing monitoring.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. API framework: FastAPI 0.111 with uvicorn. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via LangChain AzureChatOpenAI (azure-identity for auth, azure-keyvault-secrets for API key retrieval). Vector retrieval: pgvector extension on PostgreSQL 16 with ivfflat index (lists=100) on industry_benchmarks.embedding and underwriting_guidelines.embedding columns. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis. Tracing: LangSmith with langsmith SDK. Auth: azure-identity for JWT validation. ORM: SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg driver. Deployment: AKS with Helm charts; Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; Azure Container Registry for images.
target_output.functional_feature_map: POST /v1/credit-memos: accepts loan_packet (loan_amount, loan_type, industry_sector, dscr, ltv, debt_yield, collateral_type), runs sector-filtered RAG retrieval, assembles prompt, calls GPT-4o, returns structured memo_draft with sections: executive_overview, borrower_analysis, financial_analysis, industry_context, collateral_assessment, risk_summary, recommendation. POST /v1/annotations/{memo_id}: accepts action (accept/edit/flag), optional edited_sections_json, optional flagged_reason. GET /v1/credit-memos/{memo_id}: returns cached draft or generation status. GET /v1/analytics/prompt-versions: returns per-version accept_rate, edit_rate, flag_rate for model governance dashboard.
target_output.system_components: CreditMemoGenerator: orchestrates retrieval, prompt assembly, OpenAI call, response parsing, audit logging. BenchmarkRetriever: sector-filtered pgvector cosine search on industry_benchmarks (WHERE industry_sector = :sector). GuidelineRetriever: sector-filtered pgvector cosine search on underwriting_guidelines (WHERE industry_sectors @> ARRAY[:sector]). PromptManager: loads active llm_prompt_version from PostgreSQL; supports version pinning per request. AuditLogger: writes generation_audit_log record including retrieved_chunk_ids[], azure_openai_request_id, token_usage_json, prompt_version_id. PiiScrubber: validates loan_packet fields before prompt assembly; blocks inclusion of borrower name, SSN, or EIN in prompt context. FeedbackPipeline: weekly Celery task computing accept_rate, edit_rate, flag_rate per prompt_version from reviewer_annotations; writes to prompt_version_metrics table; triggers alert if flag_rate > 15%.
target_output.workflows: Credit Memo Generation Workflow: Underwriting platform sends POST /v1/credit-memos with structured loan_packet (industry_sector: 'C&I_manufacturing', dscr: 1.45, ltv: 0.65, loan_amount_cents: 5000000). PiiScrubber validates no PII fields present. BenchmarkRetriever executes: SELECT id, content_text FROM industry_benchmarks WHERE industry_sector = 'C&I_manufacturing' ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_vec LIMIT 5. GuidelineRetriever executes: SELECT id, content_text FROM underwriting_guidelines WHERE industry_sectors @> ARRAY['C&I_manufacturing'] ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_vec LIMIT 5. Retrieved chunk IDs captured. AuditLogger writes pre-generation record with retrieved_chunk_ids. PromptManager loads active prompt template. LangChain assembles prompt injecting financial ratios and sector-specific context chunks. AzureChatOpenAI calls GPT-4o with 12s timeout. Response parsed to structured memo sections JSON. generated_memo_drafts record inserted. Redis caches result. Response returned to underwriting platform within P90 15s. Annotation Feedback Workflow: Credit officer reviews memo in underwriting platform UI, submits flag action with reason 'C&I benchmark DSCR threshold appears outdated'. POST /v1/annotations/{memo_id} stores annotation with annotator_id and flag reason. Weekly Celery task aggregates flag_rate for the active prompt_version; if flag_rate > 15%, prompt_version record is marked requires_review and model governance team alerted via Azure Monitor alert.
target_output.data_model: loan_applications (id UUID PK, borrower_id_ref UUID, loan_amount_cents BIGINT, loan_type VARCHAR, industry_sector VARCHAR, dscr NUMERIC(6,4), ltv NUMERIC(5,4), debt_yield NUMERIC(5,4), application_date DATE). industry_benchmarks (id UUID PK, industry_sector VARCHAR NOT NULL, benchmark_type VARCHAR, value NUMERIC, unit VARCHAR, source VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, embedding vector(1536), INDEX ivfflat on embedding with industry_sector partial index). underwriting_guidelines (id UUID PK, guideline_category VARCHAR, content_text TEXT, industry_sectors VARCHAR[] NOT NULL, version VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, embedding vector(1536), GIN INDEX on industry_sectors, ivfflat INDEX on embedding). generated_memo_drafts (id UUID PK, loan_application_id UUID, prompt_version_id UUID FK, memo_sections_json JSONB, generation_latency_ms INT, azure_request_id VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). reviewer_annotations (id UUID PK, memo_id UUID FK, annotator_id UUID, action VARCHAR CHECK action IN ('accept','edit','flag'), edited_sections_json JSONB, flagged_reason TEXT, annotated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). llm_prompt_versions (id UUID PK, version_tag VARCHAR UNIQUE, template_text TEXT, system_message TEXT, created_by UUID, deployed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). generation_audit_log (id UUID PK, memo_id UUID FK, loan_application_id UUID, prompt_version_id UUID FK, retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[] NOT NULL, azure_openai_request_id VARCHAR, token_usage_json JSONB, generation_latency_ms INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Credit financial entities: borrower_financials (id UUID PK, borrower_id VARCHAR, fiscal_year INT, total_revenue_cents BIGINT, ebitda_cents BIGINT, total_debt_cents BIGINT, net_income_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), source ENUM audited/management/projected), credit_scores (id UUID PK, loan_application_id FK, credit_score INT, dscr NUMERIC, leverage_ratio NUMERIC, current_ratio NUMERIC, scored_at TIMESTAMPTZ, model_version VARCHAR), loan_account_ledger (id UUID PK, loan_application_id FK, entry_date DATE, debit_amount_cents BIGINT, credit_amount_cents BIGINT, balance_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), entry_type VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: REST API v1 with Azure AD JWT bearer auth. POST /v1/credit-memos body: {loan_application_id: UUID, loan_packet: {loan_type, industry_sector, loan_amount_cents, dscr, ltv, debt_yield, collateral_type}, prompt_version_id?: UUID}. Response: {memo_id: UUID, status: 'completed', memo_sections: {executive_overview, borrower_analysis, financial_analysis, industry_context, collateral_assessment, risk_summary, recommendation}, generation_latency_ms, prompt_version_id}. POST /v1/annotations/{memo_id} body: {action: 'accept'|'edit'|'flag', edited_sections?: object, flagged_reason?: string}. GET /v1/credit-memos/{memo_id}: returns cached memo or {status: 'generating'}. GET /v1/analytics/prompt-versions: returns [{version_id, version_tag, accept_rate, edit_rate, flag_rate, requires_review}]. Errors: 422 for PII detected in loan_packet, 503 with retry-after for OpenAI timeout.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD JWT validation on every request; token audience verified against this service app registration. Role-based: memo_generator role for POST /credit-memos, credit_officer for POST /annotations, model_governance for analytics endpoints. Azure Key Vault stores Azure OpenAI API key; azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential fetches at startup. PiiScrubber blocks loan_packet fields containing borrower_name, ssn, ein before prompt assembly; returns 422 PII_DETECTED error. LangSmith trace attributes limited to financial ratios and industry codes; no account-level identifiers. generation_audit_log captures retrieved_chunk_ids[] allowing examiners to JOIN to industry_benchmarks and underwriting_guidelines to reconstruct exact prompt context for any SR 11-7 audit. Automated flag_rate alert triggers model governance review when any prompt_version exceeds 15% flag rate. ECOA controls: prompt templates reviewed by fair lending compliance team before deployment; bias detection sweep on generated narratives planned for Phase 3.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster in Azure East US 2; two node pools (system and app). FastAPI deployment: 2 replicas minimum, HPA scaling to 8 on CPU 70% target. Blue-green deployment via Helm: Production and Canary namespaces; Azure Application Gateway routes 10% traffic to Canary for new releases. Promotion to Production requires manual approval in Azure DevOps. Azure DevOps Pipelines CI: ruff lint, mypy --strict, pytest at 80% coverage gate including RAG sector filter integration tests, Docker build, ACR push, Helm deploy to Canary. Database migrations via Alembic; migration 002_add_chunk_ids_to_audit_log.sql adds retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' to generation_audit_log. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Azure Cache for Redis with geo-replication for Redis cache.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor: API request rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate by status code. LangSmith: per-call traces tagged with prompt_version_id, industry_sector, token_usage (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens), generation_latency_ms; filterable by version for A/B comparison. Custom Azure Monitor metrics: generation_accepted_rate (annotations.action='accept' / total_annotations), generation_flag_rate per prompt_version_id, rag_retrieval_latency_ms. Alerts: p99 latency > 14s (approaching 15s SLA), flag_rate > 15% on any active prompt_version, Azure OpenAI 5xx error rate > 2%. Weekly prompt_version_metrics report published to model governance team for SR 11-7 ongoing monitoring evidence.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o: approximately $0.01 per memo generation at 2000 output tokens. At 500 memos per day, approximately $5/day or $150/month for LLM inference. AKS node pool: 2-4 Standard_D4s_v3 nodes at approximately $280/month. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: approximately $150/month for Standard_D4s zone-redundant. Azure Cache for Redis: approximately $50/month. LangSmith: team plan approximately $100/month. Total estimated: $530-$730/month for 500 daily generations.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (complete round 1): FastAPI API structure, Azure AD auth, LangChain prompt versioning, unfiltered pgvector RAG, Azure OpenAI generation, annotation storage. Phase 2 - Round 2 delivery: sector-filtered pgvector retrieval (ISS-001), retrieved_chunk_ids in audit log (ISS-002), Alembic migration 002, integration tests for sector filter, flag_rate threshold alert. Phase 3 (future): automated ECOA bias detection sweep on generated narratives, fine-tuning on accepted annotation corpus after 1000+ annotations accumulated, parallel multi-section generation reducing latency by 40%, SWIFT-style report generation for syndicated loan memos.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy --strict enforced in CI. ruff linter for import ordering and style. SQLAlchemy 2.0 async patterns throughout; no synchronous database calls in request path. pgvector queries must include industry_sector pre-filter; any new retrieval query without sector filter fails CI lint rule. Alembic migrations required for all schema changes; no DDL in application code. LangSmith trace attributes allowlist enforced by PiiScrubber; PR review required for any new attribute addition. Prompt template changes deployed as new llm_prompt_version records only; no in-place updates to existing deployed versions. 80% pytest coverage gate with mandatory integration tests for RAG retrieval sector filtering.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ivfflat index accuracy degrades at high recall with sector pre-filter reducing the candidate pool to fewer than 100 embeddings per sector. Mitigation: monitor recall@5 per sector in integration tests; switch to HNSW index if C&I sector has fewer than 200 benchmark embeddings. Risk: Azure OpenAI regional capacity exhaustion during Q1 origination peak. Mitigation: reserve 10K TPM provisioned throughput deployment; monitor token consumption rate in LangSmith. Tradeoff: sector pre-filter reduces retrieval diversity vs cross-sector insight. Accepted for compliance: cross-sector contamination risk in credit memos outweighs potential generalization benefit. Tradeoff: LangChain abstraction over direct Azure OpenAI SDK. LangChain chosen for prompt versioning, chain composition, and LangSmith integration despite added dependency; direct SDK available as fallback.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Sector pre-filter confirmed as WHERE industry_sector = :sector for benchmarks and WHERE industry_sectors @> ARRAY[:sector] for multi-sector guidelines. retrieved_chunk_ids stored as UUID[] in generation_audit_log enabling JOIN-based prompt context reconstruction for SR 11-7 examiners. Flag_rate threshold set at 15% per prompt_version triggering automatic requires_review gate. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o selected as sole LLM; no third-party model APIs permitted per constraint.

metadata.notes: Round 2 commercial credit memo AI API; ISS-001 sector-filtered RAG and ISS-002 audit chunk IDs resolved; SR 11-7, ECOA, SOX compliant.

profile.domain: Mortgage Origination Document Intelligence

project_goal.value: Research prototype for automated document intelligence on mortgage application packets using computer vision and NLP to extract, classify, and validate fields from W-2s, pay stubs, and bank statements, reducing manual data entry in origination
project_goal.rationale: Manual document data entry takes 45-60 minutes per application; prototype targets 10-minute operator review cycle

target_users.value: Loan officers uploading borrower document packets and annotators reviewing low-confidence extractions in Streamlit UI
target_users.rationale: Dual persona: origination loan officers submit packets; annotation team reviews model uncertainty

project_class.rationale: GPU inference on EC2, MLflow experiment tracking, active learning loop; not production-scale architecture

capabilities.rationale: FastAPI backend, MongoDB document store, computer vision and NLP inference, batch retraining jobs, Streamlit annotation UI

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-model pipeline (document classifier, per-type field extractor, cross-document validator), active learning loop, GPU inference

risk_level.rationale: Extraction errors on W-2 income or bank statement balances could propagate to automated origination decisions; GLBA personal financial document handling

data_sensitivity.rationale: W-2s, pay stubs, and bank statements contain personal income, employment, and account information

external_exposure.rationale: Internal prototype for origination staff; not customer-facing

access_model.value: Okta SSO for loan officers and annotators; role-based (loan_officer: upload and review, annotator: review and correct, admin: model management)
access_model.rationale: Internal workforce identity via Okta; role separation between submitters and annotators

feature_scope.value: Document packet upload, document type classification, field extraction per document type, cross-document consistency validation, low-confidence review queue in Streamlit, annotator correction UI, model retraining pipeline
feature_scope.rationale: Full prototype feature set from upload through annotation feedback loop

mvp_scope.value: W-2 and pay stub classification and field extraction with low-confidence review queue; bank statement extraction in Phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: W-2 and pay stubs cover primary income verification; bank statements added after W-2 model validated

security_baseline.value: Okta SSO, TLS in transit, S3 SSE-S3 for document storage, no raw PII in logs, annotator access scoped to assigned review queue items only
security_baseline.rationale: GLBA Safeguards Rule minimum controls for personal financial document handling

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit 1.35 for annotation review UI and upload interface
frontend_stack.rationale: Streamlit for rapid prototype UI; annotation team comfortable with Python-based tooling

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; computer vision via custom fine-tuned LayoutLM v3 and Donut models; MLflow for experiment tracking
backend_stack.rationale: LayoutLM v3 for document layout understanding; Donut for end-to-end document parsing; MLflow for retraining experiment management

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for document metadata and extraction results; S3 for raw document files and model artifacts
data_platform.rationale: Flexible schema suits extraction results with variable field sets per document type; S3 for binary document storage

hosting_target.value: AWS EC2 GPU instances (g4dn.xlarge) for inference; S3 for document and model storage; CloudWatch for monitoring
hosting_target.rationale: GPU inference required for LayoutLM and Donut; g4dn.xlarge provides T4 GPU at reasonable prototype cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: Document uploads retained 90 days for prototype evaluation; annotated corrections retained indefinitely for training data; no FCRA credit bureau data processed in this prototype
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GLBA Safeguards Rule requires defined retention; prototype scope excludes credit bureau data

future_scope.value: Production-scale deployment with ECS and auto-scaling inference; bank statement extraction; automated bias monitoring for demographic proxies in extraction errors
future_scope.rationale: Path to production after prototype validation

constraints.value: GPU instances limited to 2 concurrent for prototype budget; no external ML API calls (inference must run on-prem EC2); prototype scope limited to W-2 and pay stub extraction in MVP
constraints.rationale: Budget and data residency constraints for personal financial documents

observability_baseline.value: MLflow tracking for model metrics (extraction accuracy per field, confidence score distribution); CloudWatch for EC2 and API metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: MLflow is the primary observability tool for model performance in prototype phase

execution_preference.value: Jupyter notebooks for model development; FastAPI for serving; GitHub Actions for retraining pipeline CI
execution_preference.rationale: Prototype-phase iteration speed over production deployment rigor

compliance_context.value: GLBA Safeguards Rule for personal financial document storage and processing; SOC 2 Type II data handling practices required for internal prototype; FCRA not applicable as prototype does not process credit bureau reports
compliance_context.rationale: GLBA applies to mortgage origination document handling even in prototype phase

llm_integration.value: Fine-tuned LayoutLM v3 for document layout understanding and field localization; Donut model for end-to-end document parsing of W-2 and pay stub templates; no external LLM API calls; models hosted on EC2 GPU instances
llm_integration.rationale: Internal model hosting required by data residency constraint; LayoutLM and Donut provide SOTA performance on structured document extraction

project.goal: Automated extraction from W-2, pay stub, and bank statement documents for mortgage origination
project.target_users: Loan officers (upload) and annotators (review corrections)
project.timeline: Q2 prototype with W-2 and pay stub extraction; Q3 bank statements

frontend.approach: Single-page annotation review UI with upload widget, low-confidence queue, and field correction form

backend.framework: FastAPI for extraction API; LayoutLM v3 and Donut for inference; MLflow for tracking
backend.api: REST API with Okta JWT auth

security.auth: Okta SSO with JWT validation
security.encryption: TLS in transit; S3 SSE-S3 for documents
security.compliance: GLBA Safeguards Rule, SOC 2 Type II

data.db: MongoDB 7 for extraction results and metadata

open_questions.bank_statement_models: Document classification approach for multi-page bank statements not yet specified

reasoner_reviews.product: The low-confidence review queue is the most critical UI element. Annotators need to see both the extracted field value and the model's confidence score alongside the document image region that produced the extraction. Without image region highlighting, annotators cannot efficiently correct fields.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The pipeline stages (classify, extract, validate) should be modeled as separate inference steps rather than a single end-to-end model call, so that each stage's accuracy is independently measurable in MLflow. The cross-document validation step (checking W-2 income matches pay stub YTD income) needs to be defined as an explicit rule engine rather than a secondary model pass.
reasoner_reviews.security: GLBA Safeguards Rule requires an information security program covering document storage. S3 SSE-S3 is the minimum; SSE-KMS would be preferable but SSE-S3 is acceptable for prototype scope. Annotator access must be row-level scoped to assigned extraction_job IDs to prevent cross-borrower document access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2 GPU instances limit concurrent inference to 2 active extraction_jobs at a time. A simple queue in MongoDB (status field) is sufficient for the prototype to serialize jobs rather than over-engineering a dedicated queue service.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three route groups: /documents (upload, status), /extractions (trigger extraction, get results), /annotations (submit corrections). ExtractionPipeline service chains DocumentClassifier, per-type FieldExtractor, and CrossDocumentValidator.
backend.api_patterns: REST API with Okta JWT bearer auth. POST /v1/documents/upload accepts multipart file upload, stores to S3, creates document_upload record in MongoDB. POST /v1/extractions/{doc_id} triggers extraction job. GET /v1/extractions/{job_id} returns extraction results with confidence scores.
backend.llm_integration_design: DocumentClassifier: fine-tuned LayoutLM v3 image classifier trained on 2000 labeled W-2, pay stub, and bank statement images. FieldExtractor: per-document-type fine-tuned Donut model. W2FieldExtractor outputs: employer_name, employee_name, tax_year, wages_cents, federal_tax_withheld_cents, state_wages_cents. PayStubFieldExtractor outputs: employer, pay_period_end, gross_pay_cents, ytd_gross_cents, deductions_json. CrossDocumentValidator: rule engine checking W-2 annual wages against pay stub YTD gross within 5% tolerance.
backend.background_jobs: Retraining pipeline: weekly GitHub Actions job reads approved_corrections from MongoDB, assembles updated training dataset, runs fine-tuning script on EC2 GPU, logs metrics to MLflow, publishes new model_version to S3 artifacts if validation accuracy improves.

security.auth_design: Okta SSO with PKCE for Streamlit UI. FastAPI validates Okta JWT on every API call. Loan officers assigned loan_officer role; annotators assigned annotator role with extraction_job_ids scope in JWT claims.
security.authorization_model: loan_officer: POST /documents/upload, GET /extractions own documents only. annotator: GET review queue (scoped to assigned job IDs), POST /annotations. admin: model management endpoints.
security.privacy_controls: Raw document images not logged. Extracted field values not included in CloudWatch log events. S3 object keys use opaque UUIDs rather than borrower-identifiable paths.

data.entities: document_uploads (id, s3_key, filename, uploaded_by, upload_time, document_type, status). extraction_jobs (id, document_id, model_version_id, status, started_at, completed_at). extracted_fields (id, job_id, field_name, extracted_value, confidence_score, bounding_box_json). validation_results (id, job_id, rule_name, passed, detail). ground_truth_annotations (id, job_id, field_name, corrected_value, annotator_id, annotated_at). model_versions (id, model_type, s3_artifact_key, validation_accuracy, deployed_at).
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 for document metadata, extraction results, and annotations. S3 for raw document files and model artifact archives. MLflow tracking server for experiment runs and metric history.

devops.deployment_topology: Single EC2 g4dn.xlarge running FastAPI and inference models. Streamlit app on separate EC2 t3.medium. S3 for documents and MLflow artifacts. CloudWatch for basic EC2 and API metrics.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest for FastAPI routes, Docker build for inference image, weekly retraining job triggered by cron. No blue-green; prototype accepts brief downtime during model updates.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit single-page app with sidebar navigation: Upload Packet, Review Queue, Model Stats.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Upload Packet: file uploader widget for multi-file document packet, progress indicator for extraction status. Review Queue: list of low-confidence extraction_jobs (confidence < 0.85 threshold), field-by-field correction form with document image thumbnail and bounding box highlight.
frontend.state_management: Streamlit session_state for current job selection and correction draft. API calls via requests library to FastAPI backend.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 1 prototype for mortgage document intelligence. Key decisions: LayoutLM v3 and Donut as separate per-stage models (classify then extract) rather than a single end-to-end model so each stage's accuracy is independently tracked in MLflow. MongoDB flexible schema suits variable field sets per document type. GPU budget of 2 concurrent EC2 instances means MongoDB status field queue is sufficient for job serialization. Plan quality is intentionally weak: several sections thin on specifics, system_components and deployment sections underdeveloped, reflecting typical round 1 research prototype plans.
target_output.title: Mortgage Document Intelligence Research Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype using computer vision and NLP to automate extraction of income and asset fields from W-2s, pay stubs, and bank statements in mortgage application packets. Built with fine-tuned LayoutLM v3 for document classification and Donut models for field extraction, serving results via FastAPI with a Streamlit annotation UI for loan officers and correctors.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI backend on a single EC2 g4dn.xlarge GPU instance serving an extraction pipeline that classifies uploaded documents, runs field extraction models, and applies cross-document validation rules. Streamlit annotation UI runs on a separate EC2 t3.medium. Documents stored in S3; extraction metadata and correction annotations stored in MongoDB 7. MLflow tracks model experiment runs and retraining results.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111. CV models: fine-tuned LayoutLM v3 (document classifier), fine-tuned Donut (W-2 and pay stub field extractors). Model tracking: MLflow 2.13 with S3 artifact store. Document storage: S3 with SSE-S3. Metadata: MongoDB 7. Frontend: Streamlit 1.35. Auth: Okta JWT. Infrastructure: EC2 g4dn.xlarge for inference, EC2 t3.medium for Streamlit, CloudWatch for basic metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Document upload: loan officer uploads multi-file packet via Streamlit; files stored to S3; extraction jobs queued in MongoDB. Document classification: LayoutLM v3 identifies W-2, pay stub, or bank statement per page. Field extraction: Donut model per document type extracts structured fields with confidence scores. Cross-document validation: rule engine checks W-2 wages against pay stub YTD gross within 5% tolerance. Low-confidence review queue: Streamlit lists jobs with any field confidence below 0.85 threshold. Annotation correction: annotators correct field values; corrections stored as ground_truth_annotations.
target_output.system_components: DocumentClassifier service wrapping LayoutLM v3 inference. FieldExtractor factory returning W2FieldExtractor or PayStubFieldExtractor Donut model instances. CrossDocumentValidator rule engine. ExtractionJobQueue using MongoDB status field (pending, processing, completed, review_required). RetrainingPipeline weekly job reading approved corrections.
target_output.workflows: Document Extraction Workflow: Loan officer uploads packet of 3 documents via Streamlit upload page. FastAPI stores each file to S3 (opaque UUID key). DocumentClassifier runs LayoutLM v3 on each page image identifying document types. FieldExtractor runs matching Donut model returning field_name, extracted_value, confidence_score, bounding_box_json per field. CrossDocumentValidator checks W-2 wages_cents against pay stub ytd_gross_cents; flags discrepancy if delta > 5%. Extraction results written to MongoDB extracted_fields collection. If any field confidence < 0.85, job status set to review_required. Annotation Retraining Workflow: Annotator opens Review Queue in Streamlit, sees list of review_required jobs with document thumbnail and field confidence scores. Selects a field, views highlighted bounding box region, corrects extracted value. Correction stored as ground_truth_annotation. Weekly GitHub Actions retraining job loads annotations from MongoDB, assembles fine-tuning dataset, runs LayoutLM and Donut fine-tuning on EC2 GPU, logs accuracy metrics to MLflow, updates model_versions collection if accuracy improves.
target_output.data_model: MongoDB collections: document_uploads {_id, s3_key, filename, uploaded_by_okta_sub, upload_time, document_type, status}. extraction_jobs {_id, document_id, model_version_id, status: pending/processing/completed/review_required, started_at, completed_at}. extracted_fields {_id, job_id, field_name, extracted_value, confidence_score, bounding_box_json: {page, x, y, w, h}}. validation_results {_id, job_id, rule_name, passed, detail}. ground_truth_annotations {_id, job_id, field_name, original_value, corrected_value, annotator_okta_sub, annotated_at}. model_versions {_id, model_type, s3_artifact_key, validation_accuracy, deployed_at, deprecated_at}.
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/documents/upload multipart with file and metadata. POST /v1/extractions/{doc_id} triggers extraction job. GET /v1/extractions/{job_id} returns results with fields and confidence scores. GET /v1/review-queue returns extraction_jobs with review_required status scoped to caller's permissions. POST /v1/annotations/{job_id} submits field correction. GET /v1/model-stats returns per-model-version accuracy metrics from MLflow.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta JWT validated on every FastAPI request. Annotator JWT claims include extraction_job_ids scope limiting review queue to assigned jobs. S3 object keys use UUID paths; no borrower-identifiable information in S3 key names. Raw document images not logged in CloudWatch. GLBA Safeguards controls: TLS 1.3 in transit, SSE-S3 at rest, defined 90-day document retention in S3 lifecycle policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Single EC2 g4dn.xlarge for FastAPI and inference models. Streamlit on EC2 t3.medium. Both instances in private VPC subnet; ALB for HTTPS termination. S3 lifecycle policy: documents deleted after 90 days; annotations retained indefinitely. GitHub Actions CI: pytest for API routes, Docker build, deploy to EC2 via SSM. No auto-scaling in prototype; manual capacity management.
target_output.observability: MLflow tracking server records extraction accuracy per field name and per model version. CloudWatch basic metrics: EC2 CPU, API request count, error rate. No custom application metrics defined in this prototype phase.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EC2 g4dn.xlarge approximately $380/month on-demand. t3.medium approximately $30/month. S3 under $10/month at prototype volume. MLflow on EC2 t3.small approximately $15/month. Total approximately $435/month. Scaling beyond 2 concurrent jobs requires additional GPU instances.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP: W-2 and pay stub classification, field extraction, low-confidence review queue, annotation storage, Okta auth. Phase 2: bank statement extraction model, cross-document balance validation. Phase 3: production ECS deployment with auto-scaling inference, SOC 2 Type II evidence collection.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12. ruff for linting. pytest for API route tests. MLflow experiment naming convention: {model_type}-{document_type}-v{n}. No raw document content in log statements. Ground truth annotation format must match LayoutLM fine-tuning input schema.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LayoutLM v3 accuracy degrades on non-standard W-2 formats from smaller employers. Mitigation: collect diverse training samples during annotation phase; track per-employer accuracy in MLflow. Risk: 2 GPU instance limit causes queue buildup during batch submission periods. Mitigation: monitor MongoDB extraction_jobs pending count; alert if queue depth exceeds 20. Tradeoff: MongoDB flexible schema over relational for extraction results. Chosen for variable field sets per document type; acceptable at prototype scale.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Separate classifier and extractor models chosen over single end-to-end model for independent accuracy tracking per stage. MongoDB status field queue sufficient for 2 concurrent GPU instance constraint. Bank statement extraction deferred to Phase 2 after W-2 model validation.

metadata.notes: Round 1 research prototype for mortgage document AI; intentionally weak plan quality; observability and deployment sections underdeveloped; GLBA compliant baseline.

profile.domain: Challenger Bank Mobile Banking

project_goal.value: Security-hardened mobile banking app for retail customers of a challenger bank featuring biometric authentication, real-time transaction monitoring with fraud alerts, and sub-second card lock/unlock built on a zero-trust mobile security model
project_goal.rationale: Challenger bank differentiates on security UX; biometric auth and instant card controls are core customer-facing features

target_users.value: Retail banking customers of the challenger bank performing everyday banking: balance checks, transfers, card management, and dispute initiation
target_users.rationale: Consumer mobile banking for the full challenger bank customer base

project_class.rationale: React Native cross-platform mobile app for iOS and Android; no web browser primary interface

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, data, payments, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, Go API backend, biometric auth, PostgreSQL, Kafka fraud pipeline, analytics dashboard

complexity_level.rationale: Zero-trust device binding, biometric cryptographic challenge, real-time Kafka fraud pipeline, sub-second card lock, PSD2 SCA compliance

risk_level.rationale: Retail banking with real-time fund transfers; fraud, account takeover, and payment integrity risks; PSD2 SCA and FFIEC mobile security obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Account balances, transaction history, card numbers, and banking credentials are financial personal data

external_exposure.rationale: Customer-facing app accessible over public internet; requires comprehensive perimeter and application security

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with biometric device binding; device-bound asymmetric key pair in iOS Secure Enclave or Android Keystore; 15-minute sliding session tokens with biometric re-auth on sensitive actions
access_model.rationale: PSD2 SCA requires possession (device) and inherence (biometric) factors; replay-protected sessions

feature_scope.value: Account dashboard, transaction history, inter-bank transfers, peer-to-peer payments, real-time fraud alert push notifications, card lock/unlock, card number reveal with biometric re-auth, spending analytics, dispute initiation
feature_scope.rationale: Full consumer mobile banking feature set

mvp_scope.value: Account dashboard, transaction history, card lock/unlock, real-time fraud alert push notifications, biometric auth; transfers and P2P in Phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Card controls and fraud alerts are the security differentiators for launch; transfers deferred

security_baseline.value: PSD2 SCA with biometric device binding, TLS 1.3 certificate pinning, OWASP MASVS Level 2, AWS WAF on API Gateway, anti-tampering (jailbreak/root detection), NIST SP 800-163 mobile app vetting, Cognito MFA
security_baseline.rationale: FFIEC mobile banking security guidelines and NIST SP 800-163 mobile app vetting requirements

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript; Expo bare workflow; react-native-biometrics for Face ID/Fingerprint via device keychain; react-native-push-notification for FCM/APNs
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS and Android from single codebase; Expo bare for native module access

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework; Kafka for real-time transaction event stream; Redis 7 for session token cache and card lock state; fraud scoring service in Go
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-throughput, low-latency API serving; Kafka for decoupled real-time fraud pipeline; Redis for sub-second card lock state

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for account and transaction data; Redis 7 for session cache and card state; MSK (Managed Streaming for Kafka) for transaction event stream
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for ACID transaction integrity; Redis for sub-second card lock reads; MSK for managed Kafka

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate us-east-1 for API and fraud scoring services; MSK for Kafka; RDS Aurora PostgreSQL; ElastiCache Redis; CloudFront for static assets
hosting_target.rationale: Managed AWS services for operational efficiency; ECS Fargate for container scaling without EC2 management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transaction records retained 7 years per banking regulation; session tokens TTL 15 minutes; push notification tokens rotated on device registration; biometric keys never leave device Secure Enclave
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Banking record retention requirements; biometric data stays on device per privacy-by-design

future_scope.value: Inter-bank ACH transfers, P2P payments, spending category budgets, credit score monitoring integration, multi-currency account support
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after MVP launch and user feedback

constraints.value: Sub-second card lock/unlock latency; fraud alert push notification within 2 seconds of suspicious transaction event; biometric keys must never leave device hardware security module
constraints.rationale: Customer-facing SLA commitments for security features

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for API and fraud scoring services; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting; custom Datadog metrics: biometric_auth_success_rate, fraud_alert_delivery_latency_ms, card_lock_latency_ms
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog for backend observability; Crashlytics for mobile-specific crash triage

execution_preference.value: Buildkite CI/CD with parallel iOS and Android build lanes; canary release via Apple TestFlight and Google Play Internal Testing before production rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Buildkite for controlled CI in banking environment; staged mobile release reduces customer blast radius

compliance_context.value: PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) with possession + inherence factors; FFIEC mobile banking security guidelines; NIST SP 800-163 mobile app security vetting; GLBA for personal financial data protection
compliance_context.rationale: Retail banking regulatory obligations for mobile app security and payment authentication

project.goal: Security-hardened retail mobile banking app with biometric auth and real-time fraud controls
project.target_users: Challenger bank retail customers (iOS and Android)
project.timeline: MVP Q3: dashboard, card controls, fraud alerts; Q4 transfers and P2P

frontend.approach: Expo bare workflow; biometric auth via react-native-biometrics; push via FCM and APNs

backend.framework: Gin for REST API; Kafka consumer for fraud pipeline; Redis for card state
backend.api: REST API with Cognito JWT; certificate pinning

security.auth: Cognito with biometric device binding; iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning; AES-256 for local storage
security.compliance: PSD2 SCA, FFIEC, NIST SP 800-163, GLBA

devops.ci: Buildkite with iOS and Android parallel lanes

reasoner_reviews.product: The card lock/unlock feature is the highest-visibility security differentiator. The UX must show a real-time lock state change with visual confirmation within 500ms of the API response, not just an optimistic update that could be inconsistent with actual card state. The fraud alert must deep-link into the transaction detail screen so customers can dispute directly from the notification.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Device binding using asymmetric key pairs in iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore is the correct PSD2 possession factor implementation. The biometric challenge flow must use server-generated nonce to prevent replay attacks; the nonce must be single-use and expire in 30 seconds. The card lock state must be stored in Redis for sub-second reads by the card processing integration, with PostgreSQL as the authoritative state and Redis as the cache with write-through invalidation.
reasoner_reviews.security: TLS certificate pinning must use public key pinning (SPKI) not certificate pinning to survive cert rotation without app updates. The OWASP MASVS Level 2 requirement implies: encrypted local storage for session tokens, jailbreak and root detection with appropriate response (soft block for rooted devices in MVP), no sensitive data in iOS NSLog or Android Logcat, anti-screenshot protection on sensitive screens (card number reveal, balance).
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Sub-second card lock requires the card processing integration to read lock state from Redis, not PostgreSQL. The write-through pattern (write PostgreSQL first, then Redis) ensures Redis is always a post-commit cache value. Fraud alert within 2 seconds requires the Kafka consumer-to-push-notification path to be under 1.8 seconds including scoring, SNS, and FCM/APNs delivery.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The biometric re-auth for card number reveal is essential for PSD2 SCA compliance on this sensitive action. The implementation must ensure the server validates the biometric challenge response with a cryptographic signature verification, not just a 'biometric passed' boolean from the client, to prevent spoofing.

backend.service_design: Go 1.22 Gin application with four domain modules: auth (device binding, biometric challenge, session management), accounts (balance, transaction history), cards (lock/unlock, card detail reveal), fraud (Kafka consumer, scoring, push notification). Fraud scoring service deployed as a separate ECS Fargate service to allow independent scaling.
backend.api_patterns: REST API authenticated via Cognito JWT in Authorization header. Sensitive endpoints (card reveal, lock/unlock, transfer initiation) require biometric re-auth: client provides signed_challenge field with device private key signature over server nonce. POST /v1/auth/device-register, POST /v1/auth/biometric-challenge, POST /v1/auth/biometric-verify, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/summary, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/transactions, POST /v1/cards/{id}/lock, POST /v1/cards/{id}/unlock, GET /v1/cards/{id}/reveal.
backend.business_modules: DeviceBindingService: generates RSA-2048 or EC P-256 server-side challenge nonce (UUID, 30s expiry in Redis), receives client signature over nonce, verifies with stored device public key. CardStateService: write-through cache pattern - PostgreSQL cards.lock_status is authoritative; Redis cards:{card_id}:state is the hot path read by card processing integration. FraudScoringService: Kafka consumer on transactions topic; applies rule-based scoring (velocity, geo, amount anomaly); threshold breach triggers SNS push notification.
backend.failure_handling: Card lock/unlock: if Redis write fails after PostgreSQL commit, async reconciliation job re-syncs Redis from PostgreSQL every 30 seconds. Fraud scoring: Kafka consumer with dead letter queue for scoring failures; push notification failure retried 3 times via SNS. Biometric challenge nonce expiry: 30s TTL in Redis; expired nonce returns 401 with new challenge.
backend.scaling_notes: API service: ECS Fargate auto-scaling 2-10 tasks on CPU 70%. Fraud scoring service: 2-6 tasks scaling on Kafka consumer group lag. Card state Redis: ElastiCache cluster mode disabled for MVP; single shard with read replica for card state reads.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with MFA.
