Device registration: client generates EC P-256 key pair in iOS Secure Enclave or Android Keystore; public key sent to POST /v1/auth/device-register and stored in device_registrations table. Biometric challenge flow: POST /v1/auth/biometric-challenge returns server_nonce (UUID, 30s Redis TTL); client signs nonce with device private key using biometric-gated key access; POST /v1/auth/biometric-verify verifies signature with stored public key; issues session JWT (15-minute sliding expiry).
security.authorization_model: Cognito JWT claims: customer_id, device_id. Gin middleware verifies token, extracts customer_id. All account and card endpoints verify resource ownership: accounts.customer_id must match JWT customer_id. Card reveal and lock/unlock additionally require fresh biometric signature in request body.
security.abuse_prevention: AWS WAF on ALB: rate limit 20 req/s per IP, block OWASP Top 10 patterns. Biometric challenge nonce single-use: Redis DEL on first verification attempt. Device registration limited to 3 registered devices per customer. Jailbreak/root detection in React Native app: block card reveal and session initiation on compromised devices.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every biometric auth event logged to auth_audit_log (customer_id, device_id, action, outcome, ip_address, timestamp). Every card lock/unlock logged to card_event_log. Fraud alert events logged to fraud_event_log with kafka_offset for audit trail. Datadog log pipeline strips card numbers and account numbers from all log lines.
security.security_risks: Certificate pinning using SPKI hash (not leaf cert) to survive cert rotation without app update. Anti-screenshot flag on card reveal screen and balance screen via React Native WindowManager FLAG_SECURE (Android) and UITextField secureTextEntry equivalent patterns (iOS).

data.entities: customer_accounts (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, account_type VARCHAR, balance_cents BIGINT, currency VARCHAR, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transactions (id UUID PK, account_id UUID FK, amount_cents BIGINT, direction VARCHAR CHECK(credit/debit), merchant_name VARCHAR, merchant_category_code VARCHAR, transaction_date TIMESTAMPTZ, kafka_offset BIGINT, fraud_score NUMERIC(5,4)). cards (id UUID PK, account_id UUID FK, card_last4 VARCHAR, network VARCHAR, lock_status VARCHAR CHECK(locked/unlocked), lock_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). device_registrations (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, device_public_key_pem TEXT, device_platform VARCHAR, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ). biometric_bindings (id UUID PK, device_id UUID FK, binding_type VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). fraud_alerts (id UUID PK, transaction_id UUID FK, customer_id UUID FK, fraud_score NUMERIC(5,4), alert_type VARCHAR, push_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, customer_action VARCHAR). push_notification_tokens (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, device_id UUID FK, token TEXT, platform VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for account, transaction, card, and device data. ElastiCache Redis 7 for: session tokens (TTL 15min), card lock state (write-through, no TTL), biometric challenge nonces (TTL 30s). MSK Kafka: transactions topic with 12 partitions keyed by account_id for ordering within account.
data.analytics_events: transaction_created event on Kafka: consumed by fraud scoring service and analytics aggregation service. card_locked/card_unlocked events on card-events topic for operations dashboard. fraud_alert_delivered event with latency_ms for SLA monitoring.
data.data_risks: Kafka consumer offset commit must occur after successful fraud scoring and push notification dispatch to ensure no transaction is silently skipped. If scoring fails, offset is not committed and the transaction is reprocessed after dead-letter handling.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a and us-east-1b (multi-AZ). API service: 2 AZs, 2-10 tasks. Fraud scoring service: 2 AZs, 2-6 tasks. Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with automated failover. ElastiCache Redis single-shard with read replica. MSK 3-broker cluster with 3 AZs. ALB with WAF in front of API service. CloudFront for static assets.
devops.environments: dev: single AZ, reduced instance sizes, MSK single broker. staging: multi-AZ mirror of production topology. production: multi-AZ with auto-scaling. Mobile: TestFlight (iOS) and Play Internal Testing (Android) as pre-production canary channels.
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite: parallel lanes for iOS (Xcode build, unit tests, TestFlight upload) and Android (Gradle build, unit tests, Play upload). Backend lane: Go lint (golangci-lint), go test at 85% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Canary: 10% traffic weight on new ECS task version via ALB weighted target groups before 100% promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task. Custom Datadog metrics: card_lock_latency_ms (target: p99 < 500ms), fraud_alert_delivery_latency_ms (target: p99 < 2000ms), biometric_auth_success_rate. Firebase Crashlytics for iOS and Android crash aggregation with stack traces. PagerDuty on-call for: card_lock_latency p99 > 1s, fraud_alert_latency p99 > 2s, auth error rate > 2%, Kafka consumer lag > 1000 messages.
devops.devops_risks: MSK Kafka broker failure could delay fraud alert delivery. Mitigation: 3-AZ broker replication factor 3, min_insync_replicas 2. ECS rolling update could briefly route traffic to mixed API versions during deployment; use version headers on all API responses for client compatibility tracking.

frontend.app_structure: React Native 0.74 Expo bare workflow. Navigation: React Navigation v6 with bottom tab navigator (Dashboard, Cards, Payments, Profile) and modal stack for biometric prompts and fraud alert detail.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard screen: account balance, recent transactions list, fraud alert banner if active alerts pending. Transaction Detail: merchant, amount, fraud score indicator, dispute button. Card Management: card lock toggle (optimistic update with server confirmation), card number reveal behind biometric re-auth. Fraud Alert Modal: deep-linked from push notification, shows transaction detail, dispute and confirm-not-fraud actions. Biometric Auth Modal: triggered for sensitive actions; shows device biometric prompt via react-native-biometrics; sends signed_challenge to backend.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state (account balance, transactions, card status). Zustand for local UI state (active fraud alerts, biometric challenge nonce). Card lock state uses optimistic update pattern: toggle immediately in UI, revert on API error response.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Certificate pinning via react-native-ssl-pinning (SPKI hash). Screenshot prevention via react-native-flag-secure (Android FLAG_SECURE) and custom iOS UIWindow override for balance and card reveal screens. Jailbreak and root detection via react-native-jail-monkey; card reveal and card unlock blocked on compromised devices. Session token stored in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore; never in AsyncStorage.
frontend.performance_notes: Transaction list virtualized via FlashList for smooth scroll performance at 2-year history depth. Balance and card state fetched with react-query staleTime: 30s to reduce redundant API calls. Push notification cold-start optimization: deep link pre-fetches fraud alert data before screen render.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 1 for a security-hardened challenger bank mobile app. Core architectural decisions: biometric device binding using device HSM key pairs (not just Cognito MFA) for PSD2 SCA compliance; Redis write-through for card lock state to achieve sub-second lock latency; Kafka-to-SNS fraud alert pipeline targeting under 2 seconds end-to-end; certificate SPKI pinning for cert rotation survivability. Strong plan quality with all sections richly domain-specific.
target_output.title: Challenger Bank Security-Hardened Mobile Banking App
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native 0.74 mobile banking app for iOS and Android implementing a zero-trust security model for a challenger bank. Core security features: biometric device binding using iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore asymmetric key pairs for PSD2 SCA compliance, real-time Kafka-driven fraud scoring pipeline delivering push alerts within 2 seconds of suspicious transactions, and sub-second card lock/unlock via Redis write-through cache. Backend is Go 1.22 Gin on ECS Fargate with Aurora PostgreSQL 16 and MSK Kafka. Buildkite CI/CD with TestFlight and Play Internal Testing canary releases.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native client communicates over TLS 1.3 with SPKI certificate pinning to a Go Gin REST API on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF. Cognito user pool handles identity; device-bound biometric auth adds the PSD2 SCA possession and inherence factors using EC P-256 key pairs generated in iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore. The server issues a cryptographic nonce challenge (30s Redis TTL); the client signs the nonce with the device-bound private key; the server verifies the signature against the stored public key before issuing session JWTs with 15-minute sliding expiry. Card state is stored authoritatively in Aurora PostgreSQL with write-through to ElastiCache Redis for sub-second card lock reads by the card processing integration. Transaction events published to MSK Kafka trigger a separate ECS Fargate fraud scoring service; scoring results above threshold trigger SNS push notifications delivered via FCM and APNs within 2 seconds. Datadog APM monitors card_lock_latency_ms (p99 target 500ms) and fraud_alert_delivery_latency_ms (p99 target 2000ms).
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript, Expo bare workflow, React Navigation v6, react-native-biometrics (Secure Enclave/Keystore), react-native-ssl-pinning (SPKI), react-native-flag-secure, FlashList, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin, Cognito JWT auth, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL, go-redis/v9, confluent-kafka-go. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1 multi-AZ, ALB with WAF, Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, MSK Kafka 3-broker 3-AZ. CI/CD: Buildkite (iOS Xcode + Android Gradle lanes), ECR, ECS rolling deploy with weighted canary. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Firebase Crashlytics, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Account Dashboard: real-time balance with 30s react-query staleTime, recent transactions with FlashList virtualization, active fraud alert banner with deep-link to dispute. Card Management: card lock toggle with optimistic UI update confirmed by Redis-backed API response, card number reveal gated by biometric re-auth generating fresh server challenge nonce. Fraud Alert Pipeline: Kafka transaction event consumed by FraudScoringService, velocity/geo/amount-anomaly rule evaluation, SNS push to FCM/APNs within 2 seconds of trigger, deep-link notification opening dispute flow. Biometric Auth Flow: server nonce generation, device-side biometric prompt accessing HSM key, client-side RSA signature, server-side signature verification, session JWT issuance. Spending Analytics: transaction categorization by merchant_category_code, monthly spend-by-category aggregation served from pre-computed PostgreSQL materialized view.
target_output.system_components: AuthService (Go): Cognito JWT validation middleware, device public key storage, biometric challenge nonce generation and verification. AccountService (Go): balance read, paginated transaction history with cursor pagination on transaction_date and id. CardStateService (Go): write-through lock/unlock to PostgreSQL then Redis; Redis key cards:{card_id}:state holds lock_status string. FraudScoringService (Go, separate ECS service): Kafka confluent consumer on transactions topic, velocity check (5 transactions in 1 minute same merchant), geo anomaly (transaction location vs last login IP), amount anomaly (3x 30-day average). PushNotificationService (Go): SNS topic publish with FCM and APNs platform applications. Analytics aggregation job: nightly Lambda computing merchant_category_code spend buckets into account_monthly_analytics table. React Native client with biometric challenge module, SPKI-pinned HTTP client, Secure Enclave/Keystore key management wrapper.
target_output.workflows: Biometric Login Workflow: Customer opens app. Client calls POST /v1/auth/biometric-challenge. Server generates UUID nonce, stores in Redis with 30s TTL, returns {nonce, challenge_id}. Client invokes react-native-biometrics.createSignature(nonce) which prompts Face ID or Fingerprint, accesses device-bound EC P-256 private key in Secure Enclave or Keystore, returns base64 ECDSA signature. Client calls POST /v1/auth/biometric-verify {challenge_id, signed_challenge}. Server fetches nonce from Redis, verifies ECDSA signature against stored device public key, DELetes nonce from Redis (single-use), issues Cognito JWT with 15-minute sliding expiry. Client stores JWT in iOS Keychain or Android Keystore. Real-Time Fraud Alert Workflow: Transaction event published to MSK transactions topic (keyed by account_id). FraudScoringService Kafka consumer receives event within 200ms. Scoring rules evaluated: velocity check (5 txns/60s same MCC), geo anomaly (IP geolocation delta > 500km from last auth), amount anomaly (amount > 3x 30-day median). If score > threshold: FraudScoringService publishes alert to fraud-events topic; PushNotificationService consumes fraud-events, calls SNS publish to customer push_notification_token platform endpoint. FCM or APNs delivers push within total 2 seconds of transaction event. Push notification payload includes deep link to transaction_detail/{txn_id}. Customer taps notification, app opens to TransactionDetail screen with Dispute and Confirm-Not-Fraud actions.
target_output.data_model: customer_accounts (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, account_type VARCHAR CHECK('checking','savings'), balance_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'USD', status VARCHAR CHECK('active','frozen','closed'), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transactions (id UUID PK, account_id UUID FK, amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, direction VARCHAR CHECK('credit','debit'), merchant_name VARCHAR, merchant_category_code CHAR(4), transaction_date TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, kafka_offset BIGINT, fraud_score NUMERIC(5,4), INDEX(account_id, transaction_date DESC)). cards (id UUID PK, account_id UUID FK, card_last4 CHAR(4), network VARCHAR, lock_status VARCHAR CHECK('locked','unlocked') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'unlocked', lock_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). device_registrations (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, device_public_key_pem TEXT NOT NULL, device_platform VARCHAR CHECK('ios','android'), registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ). biometric_bindings (id UUID PK, device_id UUID FK, binding_type VARCHAR CHECK('face_id','fingerprint','touch_id'), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). fraud_alerts (id UUID PK, transaction_id UUID FK, customer_id UUID FK, fraud_score NUMERIC(5,4), alert_type VARCHAR, push_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, customer_action VARCHAR). push_notification_tokens (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID FK, device_id UUID FK, token TEXT NOT NULL, platform VARCHAR CHECK('ios','android'), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Redis keys: session:{jwt_id} -> customer_id (TTL 15min), cards:{card_id}:state -> lock_status (no TTL, write-through), auth:nonce:{challenge_id} -> nonce (TTL 30s).
target_output.api_design: REST API v1 with Cognito JWT bearer auth. POST /v1/auth/device-register body: {device_public_key_pem, device_platform}. POST /v1/auth/biometric-challenge returns {challenge_id, nonce, expires_at}. POST /v1/auth/biometric-verify body: {challenge_id, signed_challenge} returns {access_token, expires_at}. GET /v1/accounts/{id}/summary returns {balance_cents, currency, status}. GET /v1/accounts/{id}/transactions?cursor=&limit=20 returns paginated transactions list. POST /v1/cards/{id}/lock body: {signed_challenge} (biometric re-auth required) returns {lock_status, updated_at}. POST /v1/cards/{id}/unlock body: {signed_challenge} returns {lock_status, updated_at}. GET /v1/cards/{id}/reveal body: {signed_challenge} returns {card_number_masked, expiry, cvv_masked} with fresh biometric session. POST /v1/disputes body: {transaction_id, reason} initiates dispute. GET /v1/analytics/spending?period=30d returns per-MCC spend buckets.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PSD2 SCA: possession factor via device-bound EC P-256 key pair in iOS Secure Enclave or Android Keystore; inherence factor via biometric gate on private key access (Face ID or Fingerprint). Server-side cryptographic verification of ECDSA signature prevents client-side biometric spoofing. TLS 1.3 with SPKI certificate pinning using react-native-ssl-pinning; hash of SubjectPublicKeyInfo hardcoded in app, surviving cert rotation without app update. OWASP MASVS Level 2: encrypted local storage (JWT in Keychain/Keystore, never AsyncStorage), jailbreak/root detection blocking card reveal and lock on compromised devices, anti-screenshot on balance and card screens. AWS WAF: OWASP Core Rule Set, rate limit 20 req/s per IP on auth endpoints, bot control. Biometric challenge nonce: single-use (Redis DEL on first verification), 30s TTL prevents replay. Session JWT: 15-minute sliding expiry, RS256 signed by Cognito, customer_id and device_id claims validated on every request. Card number reveal: requires fresh biometric re-auth; never cached in React Query; response not logged. Audit logs: auth_audit_log, card_event_log, fraud_event_log; Datadog log pipeline strips card numbers and account numbers via regex scrubbing rules.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate multi-AZ (us-east-1a, us-east-1b): API service 2-10 tasks auto-scaling CPU 70%, Fraud scoring service 2-6 tasks scaling on MSK consumer group lag. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with automated failover (typically 20-40s). ElastiCache Redis single-shard with read replica (read replica for balance reads; primary for card state writes). MSK: 3-broker cluster across 3 AZs, replication factor 3, min_insync_replicas 2. ALB with WAF in front of API service. Buildkite CI: iOS lane (Xcode 15 build, XCTest unit tests, TestFlight upload via fastlane), Android lane (Gradle build, JUnit tests, Play Internal upload), backend lane (golangci-lint, go test 85% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update with 10% canary weight on ALB). Database migrations via golang-migrate, applied in CI before ECS deploy. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Cognito app client secret and database DSN; 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on ECS sidecars: trace per API request with customer_id tag (hashed). Custom Datadog metrics: card_lock_latency_ms (histogram, p99 target 500ms), fraud_alert_delivery_latency_ms (histogram, p99 target 2000ms), biometric_auth_success_rate (counter, target >99.5%), kafka_consumer_lag (gauge, FraudScoringService consumer group). Firebase Crashlytics: iOS and Android crash aggregation with symbolicated stack traces; alerts on new crash types affecting >0.1% of sessions. PagerDuty escalation: card_lock p99 >1s, fraud_alert p99 >2.5s, auth error rate >2%, MSK consumer lag >1000 messages, Aurora failover event. Structured JSON logs via zerolog on all Go services; Datadog log pipeline with card/account number scrubbing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate API service: 2 tasks baseline at approximately $120/month; scales to 10 at $600/month peak. Fraud scoring service: 2 tasks at $60/month. Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ: approximately $350/month (db.r6g.large). ElastiCache Redis: approximately $80/month. MSK Kafka 3-broker: approximately $240/month. Datadog: approximately $200/month for 4 hosts and custom metrics. Firebase Crashlytics: free tier covers prototype volume. Estimated total: $1050-$1650/month depending on traffic. At 100K MAU (mobile active users), ECS scales to 4-6 tasks at mid-range of estimate.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Q3, 10 weeks): Cognito setup with device binding flow, biometric challenge API, account dashboard, transaction history, card lock/unlock, fraud alert pipeline (Kafka consumer, scoring rules, SNS push), Streamlit-style monitoring dashboard for ops team, Buildkite CI with iOS and Android lanes, TestFlight and Play Internal Testing canary channels. Phase 2 (Q4, 8 weeks): ACH inter-bank transfers with NACHA formatting, P2P payment initiation, spending analytics with category budget alerts, spending history export. Phase 3 (future): multi-currency account support, credit score monitoring integration, international card controls, automated fraud model retraining.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforced in CI. 85% go test coverage gate for auth, card, and fraud scoring modules. React Native TypeScript strict mode; ESLint @typescript-eslint/strict. All API responses must not include full card numbers; card number reveal endpoint is the only exception and must use biometric re-auth on every call. Biometric challenge nonce must be verified server-side with cryptographic signature check; no 'biometric_passed: true' boolean accepted from client. Redis write for card lock state must occur after successful PostgreSQL COMMIT; not before. Kafka consumer offset commit must occur after push notification dispatch confirmation, not on message receipt. PR review required for any change to auth, card reveal, or fraud scoring code paths.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Biometric key unavailability when customer replaces device before re-registration. Mitigation: fallback Cognito username/password auth with MFA for device re-enrollment; old device registration revoked on successful new device enrollment. Risk: MSK Kafka broker failure delays fraud alert delivery beyond 2-second SLA. Mitigation: 3-AZ replication factor 3, min_insync_replicas 2; PagerDuty alert on consumer group lag. Risk: SPKI pinning with wrong hash blocks all API traffic if mis-deployed. Mitigation: staged Buildkite deployment with 10% canary on production ALB; rollback automated on elevated 4xx/5xx rate. Tradeoff: device-bound key pairs over Cognito software MFA. Higher UX friction for device re-enrollment; chosen because PSD2 SCA requires hardware-backed possession factor for banking-grade authentication. Tradeoff: write-through Redis cache for card lock state over direct PostgreSQL reads. Redis adds a cache coherence responsibility (reconciliation job) but delivers the sub-second card lock latency SLA that PostgreSQL read latency cannot reliably match.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SPKI pinning chosen over leaf certificate pinning to allow cert rotation without emergency app update. Device-bound EC P-256 key pairs chosen over RSA-2048 for better performance in Secure Enclave key generation. Redis write-through pattern confirmed for card lock state; 30-second reconciliation job ensures Redis is never stale beyond that window. MSK min_insync_replicas set to 2 (not 3) to allow continued operation during single broker maintenance with acceptable durability tradeoff. Fraud scoring uses rule-based engine in MVP; ML-based model deferred to Phase 3 after labeled fraud data accumulates.

metadata.notes: Round 1 challenger bank mobile app; biometric device binding, Kafka fraud pipeline, sub-second card lock; PSD2 SCA, FFIEC, NIST SP 800-163, GLBA compliant.

profile.domain: Regulatory Reporting - CCAR Data Pipeline

project_goal.value: Ingest daily loan and deposit data from multiple core banking systems, transform to CCAR Y-14 reporting schemas, validate with 200+ DQ assertions, and deliver submission-ready packages to the Federal Reserve portal
project_goal.rationale: Core regulatory mandate under Dodd-Frank Section 165

target_users.value: Capital reporting team, risk officers, and Federal Reserve examiners
target_users.rationale: Internal staff submit; Fed examiners consume delivered data

project_class.rationale: Batch ETL with validation gates and external regulatory submission

capabilities.rationale: No bespoke UI required; pipeline with dbt Cloud monitoring layer

complexity_level.rationale: 200+ quality rules, multi-source ingestion, partner-facing mTLS submission

risk_level.rationale: Regulatory submission errors carry material financial penalties from the Fed

data_sensitivity.rationale: Loan and deposit portfolio data classified financial under internal policy

external_exposure.rationale: Submission packages delivered to Federal Reserve portal via mTLS

access_model.value: IAM role-based access for MWAA; Snowflake RBAC with three-tier roles; Fed portal restricted to two named submitters with MFA
access_model.rationale: Minimise blast radius on regulatory submission credentials

feature_scope.value: Multi-source SFTP and REST ingestion, dbt transformation layer, 200+ DQ assertions, reconciliation report with variance tiering, Fed portal submission, lineage tracking, exception management queue
feature_scope.rationale: All features required for CCAR cycle automation

mvp_scope.value: Y-14M loan schedule and Y-14Q deposit schedule pipelines with automated DQ gating and manual submission trigger
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers highest-risk schedules for first CCAR submission cycle

security_baseline.value: AES-256 at rest in Snowflake and S3, TLS 1.3 in transit, mTLS to Fed portal with ACM-managed client cert, IAM least-privilege, no long-lived credentials
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing regulatory submission requires mTLS per Fed DDP requirements

frontend_stack.value: None - pipeline only; dbt Cloud UI for operator monitoring of run status and test results
frontend_stack.rationale: No bespoke frontend needed; dbt Cloud provides sufficient pipeline visibility

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 on MWAA; dbt Core 1.8 with dbt-snowflake adapter
backend_stack.rationale: MWAA managed Airflow reduces ops burden; dbt provides SQL-layer transformation with built-in lineage

data_platform.value: Snowflake Enterprise (raw/staging/reporting schemas); S3 for source file landing zone and Glacier archival
data_platform.rationale: Three-layer Snowflake enforces lineage; S3 Glacier provides durable source archival for 7-year retention

hosting_target.value: AWS MWAA in us-east-1; Snowflake Enterprise Business Critical in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Data residency requirement and Fed portal connectivity from us-east-1

privacy_retention_policy.value: Source extracts retained 7 years in S3 Glacier; Snowflake reporting tables retained 10 years per CCAR record-keeping rules
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Fed Y-14 instructions require 10-year retention of supporting data

future_scope.value: Automated Fed DDP API submission, DFAST stress testing pipeline, real-time DQ monitoring dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 automation reduces manual submission risk and adds DFAST capability

constraints.value: Submission deadline T+1 by 09:00 ET; pipeline must complete within 6-hour window starting 02:00 ET; zero tolerance for submission errors
constraints.rationale: Fed penalties for late or erroneous CCAR submissions

observability_baseline.value: Datadog pipeline monitors, dbt Cloud quality test results, Airflow task-level alerting, PagerDuty for DQ gate failures and file-arrival SLA breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Capital reporting team needs immediate visibility of any pipeline failure before T+1 deadline

execution_preference.value: Nightly automated run with human-in-the-loop approval gate before Fed submission
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory submissions require named approver sign-off for defensibility

compliance_context.value: CCAR/Dodd-Frank Section 165, DFAST stress testing requirements, Federal Reserve Y-14 reporting instructions, SOX Section 404 data integrity controls
compliance_context.rationale: Governing regulatory framework for all CCAR submissions

project.goal: Automate CCAR Y-14 data pipeline from core banking ingestion through transformation, DQ validation, and Fed portal delivery
project.target_users: Capital reporting team, risk officers

backend.framework: Apache Airflow 2.9 on MWAA, dbt Core 1.8
backend.api: Internal Airflow REST API for DAG trigger and status

security.auth: IAM roles + Snowflake RBAC + MFA for Fed portal submitters
security.transport: TLS 1.3 in transit, mTLS with ACM client cert to Federal Reserve portal

data.storage: S3 Standard landing zone, S3 Glacier for 7-year source archival

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for dbt model testing and DAG linting

reasoner_reviews.product: The Y-14M loan and Y-14Q deposit schedules correctly scope the MVP as the highest-risk CCAR schedules. The human-in-the-loop approval gate before submission is essential - automated Fed submissions without named sign-off create regulatory defensibility gaps. The reconciliation step against the independent risk system is a critical control that must be fully specified before any submission cycle goes live.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Three-layer Snowflake architecture with dbt transformations is the correct pattern for CCAR - it provides a full audit trail from source extract to submission package. The T+1 09:00 deadline with a 6-hour window means ingestion must start by 02:00 ET; the Airflow schedule must build in 30-minute buffer for source system SLA delays. A file-arrival sensor with a circuit-breaker pattern is essential - a truncated extract reaching dbt models will produce silently incorrect Y-14 figures.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS to the Federal Reserve DDP portal is non-negotiable; client certificate rotation must be automated 30 days before expiry via a Lambda trigger. Snowflake Business Critical tier is required for network policy enforcement restricting connections to MWAA NAT gateway IPs only. submission_packages must be append-only with no DELETE privilege on the reporting role.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 6-hour processing window is achievable for current data volumes but should be benchmarked in UAT with production data clones. dbt --threads 8 on a Snowflake XL warehouse should complete Y-14 model runs within 2 hours, leaving buffer for the reconciliation and approval steps. The T+1 09:00 hard deadline leaves no room for unresolved exceptions - the exception management SLA must be enforced.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan lacked specificity on the reconciliation variance threshold framework and the exception management resolution path. These are compliance-critical gaps that would leave operations with no defined process when variances or DQ failures occur. Both must be fully specified in this revision with concrete data model changes.

backend.service_design: Five Airflow DAGs: ccar_ingestion_dag (SFTP pull, REST fetch, S3 staging, checksum validation), ccar_transform_dag (dbt run --select ccar_y14 --threads 8), ccar_validate_dag (dbt test --select ccar_y14 with severity gating), ccar_reconcile_dag (variance computation against risk system, threshold classification), ccar_submit_dag (manual trigger, XML packaging, mTLS POST to Fed DDP)
backend.business_modules: Source adapters for core banking SFTP, loan origination REST API, deposit system REST API. dbt layer: raw schema (typed source tables), staging schema (cast/coalesced per Y-14 field specs), reporting schema (Y-14M and Y-14Q schedule tables). Reconciliation Lambda computes exposure totals and queries risk system API.
backend.failure_handling: File-arrival sensor with 30-minute timeout then PagerDuty P1. SHA-256 checksum validation before dbt ingestion; mismatched files quarantined. DQ gate task fails DAG if any critical-severity dbt test fails. Reconciliation critical variance blocks ccar_submit_dag trigger.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly S3 lifecycle Lambda moves raw files older than 90 days to Glacier. Weekly dbt manifest snapshot job archives manifest.json and run_results.json to S3 for lineage audit trail.

security.auth_design: MWAA execution IAM role scoped to specific S3 buckets and Secrets Manager paths. Snowflake connection via key-pair auth; private key in Secrets Manager with quarterly rotation. Fed portal mTLS uses ACM-managed client certificate with Lambda-triggered renewal 30 days before expiry.
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for all credentials: Snowflake private key, SFTP passwords, risk system API key, Fed portal client cert private key. No secrets in DAG code or environment variables. Rotation policies enforced via Secrets Manager rotation functions.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Airflow task logs shipped to CloudWatch with run_id correlation. Snowflake query history retained 365 days. All submission events written to submission_packages with submitter identity, package hash, and Fed acknowledgement code. Exception override approvals recorded in exception_overrides with dual-approver identity.

data.entities: source_system_extracts (id UUID, source_name, file_path_s3, checksum_sha256, received_at, row_count, status ENUM received/validated/failed). ccar_report_tables (schedule_code ENUM Y-14M/Y-14Q, reporting_period DATE, row_count, dq_pass_count, dq_fail_count, generated_at). data_quality_checks (id, model_name, test_name, severity ENUM critical/warn, result ENUM pass/fail, run_at). validation_exceptions (id, run_id, schedule_code, field_name, source_value, expected_range, severity, raised_at, resolved_by, resolved_at). exception_overrides (id, exception_id FK, approver_1_id, approver_2_id, justification TEXT, approved_at). reconciliation_thresholds (id, schedule_code, field_name, immaterial_pct DECIMAL, material_pct DECIMAL, critical_pct DECIMAL). reconciliation_results (id, run_id, schedule_code, field_name, pipeline_value, risk_system_value, variance_pct, severity ENUM immaterial/material/critical). reconciliation_approvals (id, run_id, approver_id, role, approved_at, notes TEXT). submission_packages (id, reporting_period, schedule_codes JSONB, s3_key, submitted_at, submitter_id, fed_ack_code). lineage_records (id, run_id, source_extract_id, dbt_run_id, output_table, dbt_manifest_s3_key, created_at).
data.schema_notes: dbt sources defined for each core banking system; staging models apply CAST and COALESCE per Y-14 field specifications; reporting models join staging layers and enforce Fed column ordering
data.retention_and_deletion: Raw S3 files: 90 days hot then Glacier Deep Archive for 7 years. Snowflake reporting tables: 10 years. Submission packages: permanent S3 archive. submission_packages and reconciliation_approvals are append-only with no DELETE privilege.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions runs dbt compile, dbt test (dev Snowflake database), and DAG unit tests with pytest on PR. Merge to main syncs DAG files to MWAA S3 bucket via aws s3 sync. Snowflake schemas deployed via dbt CLI in CI.
devops.environments: dev (MWAA Small, Snowflake dev database), uat (MWAA Medium, Snowflake clone of production data for volume benchmarking), production (MWAA Large 10 workers, Snowflake Enterprise Business Critical with network policy)
devops.observability_stack: Datadog monitors: MWAA task p95 duration, S3 file arrival by 02:30 ET (P1), dbt critical test failure count (P1), reconciliation critical variance (P1). dbt Cloud quality dashboard shows per-model test pass rates over rolling 30 submission cycles.
devops.rollback_strategy: Snowflake time-travel restores reporting tables to pre-run state within 24-hour window via UNDROP TABLE or AT(TIMESTAMP). Reprocessing from S3 source archive available up to 7 years. Fed submission rollback requires documented runbook with Fed DDP support contact.

ISS-001.title: Reconciliation workflow underspecified - variance thresholds and submission-blocking criteria absent
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stated a reconciliation report is generated but did not define acceptable variance thresholds, which field-level variances block submission, or the escalation path when variances are material.

history.note: Identified by critic reviewer in Round 1 audit

ISS-002.title: Exception management process for DQ failures not defined
ISS-002.detail: When dbt tests flag DQ failures, Round 1 plan did not specify routing rules, reviewer assignments, SLA for resolution, or whether exceptions can be overridden to permit partial submission.

history.note: Identified by critic reviewer in Round 1 audit

Specify exception management workflow with routing rules, schedule-owner queues, 4-hour SLA for critical DQ items, and dual-approver override policy

previous_plan.title: CCAR Y-14 Regulatory Data Pipeline - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Automated CCAR pipeline ingesting from three core banking sources via SFTP and REST, transforming via dbt on Snowflake, executing 200+ DQ assertions, and delivering to the Federal Reserve portal with human approval gate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: MWAA Airflow orchestrates sequential DAGs: ingestion from SFTP/REST to S3, dbt transformation in Snowflake, DQ assertion gate, reconciliation report generation, and manual submission trigger to Fed portal via mTLS.
previous_plan.workflows: Ingestion DAG pulls SFTP files and REST payloads, stages to S3, computes checksums. dbt DAG runs Y-14 models. DQ DAG fails on critical assertion count > 0. Submission DAG awaits manual approval. Reconciliation report generated but threshold logic and escalation path not specified.
previous_plan.data_model: source_system_extracts, ccar_report_tables, data_quality_checks, submission_packages defined. reconciliation_thresholds, reconciliation_results, reconciliation_approvals, exception_overrides absent from schema.

target_output.thinking_summary: This Round 2 revision must address two compliance-critical gaps: the missing reconciliation variance threshold framework (ISS-001) and the absent exception management workflow (ISS-002). I need to add reconciliation_thresholds, reconciliation_results, reconciliation_approvals, and exception_overrides to the data model, define variance severity tiers with concrete percentage thresholds, specify the submission-blocking logic, and design the exception routing workflow with schedule-owner queues and dual-approver override. The Round 1 ingestion and transformation architecture was sound and should be preserved.

fix_report.action_taken: Added reconciliation_thresholds table with per-schedule field tolerance tiers: immaterial (<0.01%), material (0.01-0.1%), critical (>0.1%). Defined submission-blocking logic: any critical variance blocks ccar_submit_dag trigger; material variances require CFO sign-off recorded in reconciliation_approvals table. Added reconciliation_results and reconciliation_approvals to data model. Documented full reconciliation workflow in workflows section including escalation path.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Capital reporting team has a clear, auditable reconciliation process with concrete variance thresholds; every submission decision is traceable to a named approver; Fed examiners can reconstruct variance classification for any cycle.

fix_report.action_taken: Designed exception_items workflow: dbt critical test failures auto-routed to schedule_owner SQS queue with raised_at timestamp. Material exceptions trigger PagerDuty alert to schedule owner with 4-hour SLA. Dual-approver override for partial submission recorded in exception_overrides table; single-approver override attempts rejected by application logic. Added exception_overrides to data model. Documented exception routing in workflows and risks_and_tradeoffs sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every DQ exception has a defined resolution path with SLA enforcement; dual-approver override ensures no single person can force a submission with unresolved critical failures, satisfying internal audit requirements.

target_output.title: CCAR Y-14 Regulatory Data Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A CCAR-compliant Airflow pipeline on AWS MWAA ingesting daily loan and deposit data from three core banking systems, transforming via dbt on Snowflake against 200+ Y-14 assertions, applying reconciliation variance controls with immaterial/material/critical threshold tiers, routing DQ exceptions to schedule-owner queues with dual-approver override, and delivering submission packages to the Federal Reserve DDP portal via mTLS with named approver sign-off.
target_output.architecture_overview: Five sequential Airflow DAGs on MWAA orchestrate the CCAR flow: ccar_ingestion_dag pulls SFTP extracts and REST payloads from core banking, loan origination, and deposit systems into S3 with SHA-256 checksum validation before staging. ccar_transform_dag runs dbt promoting data through raw-to-staging-to-reporting layers yielding Y-14M and Y-14Q schedule tables. ccar_validate_dag executes 200+ dbt assertions gated by severity; any critical failure immediately fails the DAG and routes the exception to the schedule_owner SQS queue. ccar_reconcile_dag compares pipeline exposure totals against the independent risk system API, classifies variances against thresholds in reconciliation_thresholds, writes results to reconciliation_results, and blocks or escalates per variance severity. ccar_submit_dag is manually triggered only after reconciliation_approvals and exception_overrides records confirm all issues resolved; it packages Y-14 tables to Fed XML schema and submits via mTLS POST to the Federal Reserve DDP endpoint.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA Large (10 workers, 3 schedulers). Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 with dbt-snowflake adapter, 45 models across raw/staging/reporting schemas, dbt test suite with 200+ assertions. Data platform: Snowflake Enterprise Business Critical in us-east-1, multi-cluster XL warehouse for parallel dbt runs. Storage: S3 Standard landing zone (90-day hot), S3 Glacier Deep Archive (7-year source retention). Security: AWS Secrets Manager for credentials, ACM for Fed portal mTLS certificates with Lambda auto-renewal 30 days before expiry. CI/CD: GitHub Actions for dbt tests and DAG unit tests. Monitoring: Datadog APM and dashboards, PagerDuty escalation, dbt Cloud quality metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Multi-source ingestion from core banking SFTP (loan portfolio extracts), loan origination REST API (Y-14M origination fields), and deposit system REST API (Y-14Q balance fields). dbt transformation normalizing raw data to Fed Y-14 column specifications with CAST rules and null-fill per reporting instructions. 200+ dbt schema and custom macro tests for Y-14 field-level validation covering not_null, accepted_values, range checks, and cross-field consistency. Reconciliation engine comparing pipeline totals against risk system with variance tiering (immaterial/material/critical per reconciliation_thresholds). Exception management with schedule-owner SQS routing, 4-hour SLA for critical items, and dual-approver override. Submission packaging and mTLS delivery to Fed DDP. SOX 404 lineage capture from source extract to submission package.
target_output.system_components: MWAA Airflow cluster (3 schedulers, 10 workers, auto-scaling to 20 at peak). S3 landing bucket (versioning enabled, 90-day lifecycle to Glacier). Snowflake raw/staging/reporting schemas with Business Critical network policy. Reconciliation Lambda (computes exposure totals, queries risk system API, writes reconciliation_results). Exception router (SQS queue per schedule owner, Lambda consumer sends PagerDuty alerts). Submission packager (Python task building Fed XML from Y-14M/Y-14Q reporting tables). ACM-managed mTLS client certificate. AWS Secrets Manager. CloudWatch Logs aggregator. Datadog monitors and PagerDuty integration.
target_output.workflows: Ingestion: MWAA file-arrival sensor polls S3 at 02:00 ET; missing files by 02:30 trigger PagerDuty P1. On arrival SHA-256 checksum validated against manifest; mismatched files quarantined to s3://ccar-quarantine. Valid files registered in source_system_extracts with row_count and status=validated. Transform: ccar_transform_dag triggers on ingestion DAG success; dbt run --select ccar_y14 --threads 8 promotes raw to staging to reporting; dbt artifacts archived to S3 for lineage. DQ Gate: ccar_validate_dag runs dbt test; critical-severity failures write to validation_exceptions with schedule_code, field_name, source_value, expected_range; exception router Lambda delivers SQS message to schedule_owner queue with 4-hour SLA; non-critical warnings logged but do not block. Exception Override: if schedule owner cannot resolve within SLA, dual-approver override signed in exception_overrides table (two distinct approver IDs required; API rejects single-approver attempts). Reconciliation: ccar_reconcile_dag computes total_loans_usd, total_deposits_usd, total_commitments_usd per schedule and fetches matching totals from risk system API; variances classified per reconciliation_thresholds (immaterial <0.01%: log only; material 0.01-0.1%: senior analyst sign-off in reconciliation_approvals; critical >0.1%: DAG fails, CFO sign-off required). Submission: ccar_submit_dag validates reconciliation_approvals and exception_overrides present for current reporting_period; packages Y-14M and Y-14Q to Fed XML schema; submits mTLS POST to Fed DDP; Fed acknowledgement code stored in submission_packages.
target_output.data_model: source_system_extracts (id UUID PK, source_name VARCHAR, file_path_s3, checksum_sha256, received_at TIMESTAMP, row_count INT, status ENUM received/validated/failed). ccar_report_tables (schedule_code ENUM Y-14M/Y-14Q, reporting_period DATE, row_count INT, dq_pass_count INT, dq_fail_count INT, generated_at TIMESTAMP). data_quality_checks (id UUID PK, model_name, test_name, severity ENUM critical/warn, result ENUM pass/fail, run_at TIMESTAMP). validation_exceptions (id UUID PK, run_id, schedule_code, field_name, source_value, expected_range, severity ENUM critical/warn, raised_at TIMESTAMP, resolved_by VARCHAR, resolved_at TIMESTAMP, override_justification TEXT). exception_overrides (id UUID PK, exception_id FK, approver_1_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, approver_2_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, justification TEXT, approved_at TIMESTAMP). reconciliation_thresholds (id UUID PK, schedule_code, field_name, immaterial_pct DECIMAL(10,4), material_pct DECIMAL(10,4), critical_pct DECIMAL(10,4)). reconciliation_results (id UUID PK, run_id, schedule_code, field_name, pipeline_value DECIMAL(20,2), risk_system_value DECIMAL(20,2), variance_pct DECIMAL(10,4), severity ENUM immaterial/material/critical, created_at TIMESTAMP). reconciliation_approvals (id UUID PK, run_id, approver_id VARCHAR, role VARCHAR, approved_at TIMESTAMP, notes TEXT). submission_packages (id UUID PK, reporting_period DATE, schedule_codes JSONB, s3_key, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, submitter_id VARCHAR, fed_ack_code VARCHAR, fed_response_payload JSONB). lineage_records (id UUID PK, run_id, source_extract_id FK, dbt_run_id VARCHAR, output_table VARCHAR, dbt_manifest_s3_key VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP). All tables in Snowflake reporting schema; submission_packages and reconciliation_approvals append-only with no DELETE privilege granted to transformer role. Regulatory financial entities: capital_positions (id UUID PK, report_run_id FK, legal_entity VARCHAR, reporting_date DATE, cet1_capital_cents BIGINT, tier1_capital_cents BIGINT, total_capital_cents BIGINT, rwa_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3)), stress_test_scenarios (id UUID PK, report_run_id FK, scenario_name VARCHAR, loan_loss_rate NUMERIC, net_revenue_cents BIGINT, pre_provision_net_revenue_cents BIGINT, stressed_capital_ratio NUMERIC, currency CHAR(3)), ledger_balances_snapshot (id UUID PK, report_run_id FK, account_type VARCHAR, balance_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), as_of_date DATE).
target_output.api_design: Internal Airflow REST API: POST /api/v1/dags/ccar_submit_dag/dagRuns (trigger submission; validates reconciliation_approvals exist for reporting_period), GET /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns/{run_id} (status polling), GET /api/v1/dags/ccar_validate_dag/dagRuns/{run_id}/taskInstances (DQ results). Internal reconciliation service: GET /internal/reconciliation/{reporting_period}/results (variance report by schedule and field), POST /internal/reconciliation/{reporting_period}/approve (approver sign-off; validates role and approver_id). Exception override API: POST /internal/exceptions/{exception_id}/override (body: approver_1_id, approver_2_id, justification; rejects if approver_1_id == approver_2_id). External: POST https://reporting.federalreserve.gov/ddp/submit (mTLS, Fed XML schema, returns ack_code).
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS to Federal Reserve DDP portal using ACM-managed client certificate; Lambda CloudWatch Event monitors expiry and triggers ACM renewal 30 days before expiry with Secrets Manager rotation of the private key reference. IAM execution roles for MWAA scoped to specific S3 bucket ARNs and Secrets Manager path prefixes; no wildcard S3 permissions. Snowflake Business Critical network policy restricts connections to MWAA NAT gateway IP only; all other source IPs blocked at network policy level. Snowflake query history retained 365 days for SOX 404 forensic audit capability. submission_packages and reconciliation_approvals tables are append-only; transformer role has no DELETE or UPDATE privilege on these tables. Dual-approver override enforced at API layer with identity validation; single-approver requests return HTTP 422. CCAR, DFAST, and SOX 404 controls documented in annual compliance runbook with attestation sign-off by capital reporting director.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: dbt compile on dev Snowflake database, dbt test --select ccar_y14 (fails PR on critical assertion failures), DAG unit tests with pytest (minimum 85% coverage on ingestion and reconciliation modules), ruff linting. Merge to main triggers S3 sync of DAG files to MWAA DAG bucket. Snowflake schemas deployed via dbt CLI run in CI against dev then promoted to uat and prod via environment-scoped dbt profiles. MWAA environments: dev (Small, single worker), uat (Medium, 3 workers, Snowflake production data clone for volume benchmarking), prod (Large, 10 workers auto-scaling to 20, Business Critical Snowflake). Production schedule: ccar_ingestion_dag at 02:00 ET daily; downstream DAGs chain on task success. Rollback: Snowflake time-travel via UNDROP TABLE or SELECT ... AT(TIMESTAMP =>) restores reporting tables to pre-run state within 24-hour window. Source reprocessing available from S3 archive for up to 7 years. Fed submission errors follow documented runbook with Fed DDP support escalation path and manual CSV fallback.
target_output.observability: Datadog monitors: MWAA ccar_ingestion_dag task p95 duration > 90 min (warning) / > 150 min (critical); S3 file arrival CloudWatch event by 02:30 ET missing (P1 PagerDuty); dbt ccar_validate_dag critical test failure count > 0 (P1 PagerDuty); ccar_reconcile_dag critical variance detected in reconciliation_results (P1 PagerDuty). dbt Cloud quality dashboard showing per-model test pass rates, critical failure trends, and row count anomalies over rolling 30 submission cycles. CloudWatch structured logs for all Airflow task output with run_id and reporting_period correlation. Submission audit Datadog dashboard showing submitter identity, package hash, Fed ack code, end-to-end pipeline latency, and variance classification breakdown per submission cycle.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA Large environment approximately $2,800/month; Snowflake Business Critical XL warehouse approximately $4,200/month at current Y-14 data volumes (estimated 50M rows across loan and deposit schedules). S3 Standard and Glacier storage approximately $180/month. Total approximately $7,200/month. Cost controls: Snowflake warehouses auto-suspend after 5 minutes idle; MWAA workers scale to zero outside 02:00-10:00 ET daily window; S3 Intelligent-Tiering for intermediate dbt artifacts. Y-14 data volumes are regulatory-defined and stable year-over-year; vertical Snowflake warehouse scaling (XL to 2XL) is the primary lever if dbt run time approaches 4-hour budget.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Y-14M loan schedule and Y-14Q deposit schedule ingestion from two primary sources, dbt raw-to-reporting models with 120 critical DQ assertions, reconciliation engine with variance classification (immaterial/material/critical), reconciliation_thresholds and reconciliation_approvals tables, exception management SQS queue with PagerDuty alerts, dual-approver override workflow, manual submission trigger with mTLS, SOX lineage records, MWAA dev/uat/prod environments, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Third source system (deposit REST API), remaining 80+ DQ assertions, reconciliation_thresholds management interface for capital reporting team, ACM cert auto-renewal Lambda, dbt Cloud quality dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Fed DDP API automated submission replacing manual trigger pending Fed onboarding approval, DFAST scenario pipeline scaffolding, real-time DQ monitoring dashboard, Glacier lifecycle automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: dbt model naming: raw_{source}_{entity}, stg_ccar_{entity}, rpt_y14m_{schedule} / rpt_y14q_{schedule}. All dbt models require schema.yml with not_null and accepted_values tests on every Fed-required Y-14 field. Python DAG code tested with pytest; 85% line coverage minimum on ingestion and reconciliation modules. GitHub branch protection requires 1 senior data engineer review and green CI. Secrets never in DAG code or environment variables; always AWS Secrets Manager. Snowflake role hierarchy: sysadmin > ccar_transformer (dbt run) > ccar_reader (reporting queries); no direct production data modifications outside scheduled dbt runs. All schema changes require ADR approval before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Fed DDP portal outage on T+1 submission day. Mitigation: documented manual CSV fallback path with Fed DDP support SLA; ccar_submit_dag implements retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts before alerting. Risk: Snowflake warehouse saturation delaying dbt run beyond 4-hour window. Mitigation: auto-scale multi-cluster warehouse as secondary lever; benchmark in UAT with production data clone. Risk: Source system file delivery after 02:30 ET SLA. Mitigation: PagerDuty P1 fires at 02:30; source system on-call contacts are in runbook; late-start DAG can still meet T+1 09:00 if ingestion starts by 04:00. Tradeoff: human-in-the-loop submission gate over full automation increases operational burden but provides regulatory defensibility and satisfies risk committee. Tradeoff: dbt Core over dbt Cloud for transformation - reduces licensing cost but requires GitHub Actions to run dbt tests rather than using dbt Cloud scheduler.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Snowflake Business Critical required for network policy enforcement restricting partner-facing submission data. Confirmed dual-approver model for exception overrides over single CFO sign-off to satisfy internal audit requirements. 0.01% variance threshold for material classification aligned with Fed Y-14 materiality guidance per capital reporting director. Fed DDP API automated submission deferred to Phase 2 pending Fed DDP onboarding process completion.

metadata.notes: CCAR Y-14 regulatory data pipeline Round 2 fully resolves reconciliation variance threshold framework (ISS-001) and exception management workflow (ISS-002).

profile.domain: Agricultural Lending - Field Loan Officer Mobile App

project_goal.value: Enable field loan officers to access farm borrower financials, crop production history, and collateral valuations offline during rural farm visits with encrypted local storage and reliable sync when connectivity restores
project_goal.rationale: Field officers frequently operate in areas with no cellular coverage

target_users.value: Agricultural loan officers conducting on-site farm visits and their branch supervisors reviewing completed assessments
target_users.rationale: Primary field users identified in discovery interviews

project_class.rationale: Offline-first Flutter app with sync to central lending platform API

capabilities.rationale: Full offline data access with sync, auth, and reporting requires all five capabilities

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution and encrypted local storage is architecturally complex

risk_level.rationale: Financial data on mobile devices with offline capability requires strong device-bound encryption and GLBA controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm borrower financials, loan files, and collateral valuations are NPI under GLBA

external_exposure.rationale: App requires Cognito MFA authentication; no public endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with loan_officer and branch_supervisor roles; loan officers access only their assigned borrower portfolio; supervisors access full branch portfolio
access_model.rationale: Portfolio-scoped access minimises NPI exposure per GLBA Safeguards Rule

feature_scope.value: Borrower portfolio pre-fetch for offline access, crop production history viewer, collateral appraisal entry, field visit notes with photo capture, covenant check questionnaire, sync queue management, branch supervisor review dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full set of field workflows required for loan officer efficiency

mvp_scope.value: Offline borrower profile and loan file access, field visit note capture with photo upload, covenant check form, sync on connectivity restore
mvp_scope.rationale: Core offline capture workflow for Q2 pilot with 20 loan officers

security_baseline.value: Device-bound AES-256 SQLite encryption via Drift, TLS 1.3 to API, Cognito MFA, 30-minute inactivity session timeout, remote wipe capability via MDM
security_baseline.rationale: GLBA Safeguards Rule requires encryption of NPI on mobile devices and remote wipe

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3.22 with Dart; Drift for local SQLite ORM; Riverpod for state management
frontend_stack.rationale: Flutter cross-platform (iOS and Android) with Drift providing encrypted SQLite and Riverpod for reactive offline-first state

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI on ECS Fargate; PostgreSQL 16 central lending database
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async for sync API; Fargate for managed container hosting

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ (central); SQLite via Drift (device-local, encrypted at rest)
data_platform.rationale: Central PostgreSQL is source of truth; local SQLite with device-bound key for offline access

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-west-2; RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ us-west-2
hosting_target.rationale: Farm portfolio concentrated in western US; data residency in us-west-2

privacy_retention_policy.value: Loan files retained 7 years after loan close per OCC requirements; field notes and photos retained 5 years; local device data purged after 30-day inactivity or MDM remote wipe
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OCC agricultural lending handbook and GLBA retention obligations

future_scope.value: Automated DSCR and liquidity ratio computation from uploaded financials, integration with USDA FSA loan data feeds, AI-assisted crop yield risk scoring
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items pending pilot feedback

constraints.value: App must function fully offline for 8-hour field visits with no connectivity; iOS and Android support; MDM enrollment required before app installation
constraints.rationale: Rural farm visits regularly exceed cellular coverage areas

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting and mobile error tracking; AWS CloudWatch for API and sync endpoint metrics; sync success/failure rates tracked per loan officer
observability_baseline.rationale: Field teams need rapid diagnosis of sync failures to prevent data loss

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with TestFlight and Firebase App Distribution for beta distribution to pilot loan officers
execution_preference.rationale: Rapid iteration with field officer feedback during pilot

compliance_context.value: GLBA Safeguards Rule (NPI protection on mobile devices), Farm Service Agency lending guidelines, OCC agricultural lending handbook, Regulation B adverse action documentation
compliance_context.rationale: Agricultural financial data on mobile devices triggers GLBA and OCC requirements

project.goal: Offline-first mobile app for agricultural loan officers to capture field assessment data and sync to central lending platform
project.target_users: Agricultural loan officers (iOS/Android), branch supervisors

backend.framework: FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy

security.auth: Cognito user pools with MFA
security.device_encryption: AES-256 device-bound SQLite key via Flutter Secure Storage

data.sync: Server-wins conflict resolution for financial data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Flutter test and Python pytest

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first scope is correctly prioritised - rural farm visits with no connectivity are the core use case. The MVP covenant check form and field visit note capture address the most time-sensitive field workflows. Photo capture for collateral documentation is high-value for credit quality but adds complexity around sync queue management and S3 upload retry logic.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Drift encrypted SQLite is the right choice for local storage - it provides typed ORM, migration support, and AES-256 encryption with a device-bound key. The server-wins conflict resolution policy for financial data is appropriate; however, field visit notes and photos captured offline should use a client-wins merge strategy since they are net-new records created on device. These two conflict resolution strategies must be explicitly separated in the sync engine design.
reasoner_reviews.security: Device-bound encryption key stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore via Flutter Secure Storage is the correct pattern - it prevents key extraction even if the device storage is imaged. MDM remote wipe must be tested to confirm it purges both the Drift database and the Keychain/Keystore key. 30-minute session timeout must lock the app and require Cognito re-auth, not just an app-level PIN.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 8-hour offline duration requires pre-fetching the full borrower portfolio at the start of the field day. Pre-fetch volume for a portfolio of 50 borrowers including financials and production history could exceed 100MB - test pre-fetch latency on 4G and ensure delta pre-fetch (only changed records since last sync) is implemented from the start.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan improved sync conflict resolution but left the pre-fetch delta strategy underspecified and did not address the MDM remote wipe key purge test requirement. Both gaps persist into Round 3.

backend.service_design: FastAPI service with modules: auth (Cognito JWT validation), portfolio (borrower pre-fetch with delta filtering), sync (upload field notes, photos, covenant checks with idempotency keys), covenants (covenant check results storage), admin (supervisor review endpoints)
backend.api_patterns: REST with JWT bearer auth; idempotency keys on sync upload endpoints; ETag-based delta pre-fetch (If-None-Match header on portfolio endpoints); presigned S3 URLs for photo uploads
backend.failure_handling: Sync upload retries with exponential backoff (max 5 attempts, jitter). Photo upload failures queued separately with persistent retry. API circuit breaker for central PostgreSQL connections.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter feature modules: auth (Cognito login/MFA), portfolio (offline borrower list and profiles), visit (field visit note capture, photo picker), covenants (check questionnaire), sync (queue status and manual trigger), supervisor (branch review screen)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login with MFA -> Portfolio pre-fetch on day start -> Borrower detail (offline) -> Field visit capture -> Covenant check form -> Sync queue view -> Supervisor review dashboard
frontend.state_management: Riverpod providers: AuthNotifier (Cognito session), PortfolioRepository (Drift + API), SyncQueueNotifier (pending uploads), ConnectivityNotifier (dart:io network status)
frontend.performance_notes: Delta pre-fetch using ETags reduces pre-fetch payload from 100MB to <5MB on subsequent days. Drift lazy loading for borrower financials (load on demand, not on portfolio list render). Photo thumbnails generated at capture, full resolution queued for S3 upload.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with TOTP MFA; Cognito JWT validated on every FastAPI request via python-jose. Flutter Secure Storage persists refresh token in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore. 30-minute inactivity timer fires Cognito sign-out and clears in-memory session; Drift database remains encrypted at rest.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: loan_officer role accesses only farm_borrowers where assigned_officer_id = token.sub. branch_supervisor role accesses all farm_borrowers in branch. FastAPI dependency injects scoped query filter per role.
security.privacy_controls: Drift database encrypted with device-bound AES-256 key; key stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore. MDM Jamf Pro can push remote wipe command purging app data including Keychain/Keystore entry. Photos stored in app-sandboxed directory, never in device Camera Roll.

data.entities: farm_borrowers (id UUID PK, borrower_name, farm_address, assigned_officer_id FK, fsn VARCHAR, entity_type ENUM individual/partnership/corporation, created_at). loan_files (id UUID PK, borrower_id FK, loan_number, loan_type ENUM operating/real_estate/equipment, principal_amount, maturity_date, status ENUM current/watch/substandard). crop_production_records (id UUID PK, borrower_id FK, crop_year INT, commodity VARCHAR, acres_planted INT, yield_bu_per_acre DECIMAL, revenue_usd DECIMAL). collateral_appraisals (id UUID PK, loan_id FK, appraisal_date, appraiser_name, appraised_value_usd DECIMAL, collateral_type ENUM real_estate/equipment/crops, report_s3_key). field_visit_notes (id UUID PK, loan_id FK, officer_id FK, visited_at TIMESTAMP, observations TEXT, risk_flags JSONB, sync_status ENUM pending/synced). collateral_photos (id UUID PK, visit_note_id FK, s3_key, captured_at TIMESTAMP, sync_status ENUM pending/uploading/synced). covenant_checks (id UUID PK, loan_id FK, check_date DATE, dscr_actual DECIMAL, current_ratio DECIMAL, pass_fail ENUM pass/fail/waived, officer_notes TEXT, sync_status ENUM pending/synced). sync_queue_items (id UUID PK, entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID, operation ENUM create/update, created_at TIMESTAMP, retry_count INT, last_attempt_at TIMESTAMP, sync_status ENUM pending/in_flight/synced/failed).
data.schema_notes: Drift local schema mirrors central PostgreSQL schema; sync_status field on mutable entities tracks upload state; server-wins merge applied to loan_files and covenant_checks; client-wins for field_visit_notes and collateral_photos as net-new records
data.consistency_notes: Central PostgreSQL is source of truth for loan_files and covenant_checks; field-captured data (field_visit_notes, photos, covenant_checks) are immutable once synced

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions: Flutter unit tests, widget tests, FastAPI pytest (90% coverage gate), docker build and push ECR, ECS rolling update. TestFlight for iOS beta, Firebase App Distribution for Android beta. Production: ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks, RDS Multi-AZ, ALB with WAF.
devops.environments: dev (ECS 1 task, RDS single-AZ), staging (ECS 2 tasks, RDS Multi-AZ, 5 pilot officers), production (ECS 2-4 tasks, RDS Multi-AZ, autoscaling on CPU 60%)
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with previous task definition retained. RDS automated backups with 7-day retention and point-in-time recovery. Drift migration rollback via versioned migration files.

ISS-001.title: Delta pre-fetch strategy underspecified - full portfolio re-download on each pre-fetch
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan introduced ETag-based delta pre-fetch mention but did not specify how the server computes the ETag (per-borrower or per-portfolio), how the Flutter app stores per-borrower ETags, or what the fallback is when ETag cache is stale after a loan update.

history.note: Pre-fetch volume concern flagged by architect_reasoner
ETag mentioned but not specified

ISS-002.title: MDM remote wipe Keychain/Keystore purge not confirmed in security design
ISS-002.detail: Security section states MDM can push remote wipe but does not confirm the Jamf Pro wipe command purges the iOS Keychain and Android Keystore entries holding the Drift encryption key, nor does it specify a test procedure to verify this before production deployment.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer in Round 2 audit

ISS-003.title: Server-wins vs client-wins conflict resolution boundary not explicitly coded in sync engine
ISS-003.detail: Resolved in Round 2 - sync engine now applies server-wins to loan_files/covenant_checks and client-wins to field_visit_notes/collateral_photos.

history.note: Architect_reasoner flagged two strategies needed
Sync engine entity-type routing implemented

focus_issues.title: Delta pre-fetch strategy underspecified - full portfolio re-download on each pre-fetch
focus_issues.detail: ETag-based delta pre-fetch mentioned in Round 2 but ETag computation, per-borrower storage in Drift, and stale-cache fallback not specified.

focus_issues.title: MDM remote wipe Keychain/Keystore purge not confirmed in security design
focus_issues.detail: Jamf Pro wipe command effect on iOS Keychain and Android Keystore Drift encryption key not confirmed; no test procedure specified.

latest_recommendations: Specify ETag computation as a per-borrower updated_at hash; store per-borrower ETags in a Drift etag_cache table; define full-portfolio fallback when cache miss exceeds 10% of portfolio
Confirm Jamf Pro full-device wipe purges iOS Keychain and Android Keystore; add a dedicated pre-production MDM wipe test procedure to the security checklist
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Agricultural Lending Field Officer App - Round 2

convergence_state.reason: Two issues remain from Round 2; Round 3 score improvement expected with full specification

previous_audits.summary: Good offline sync architecture with server-wins/client-wins boundary now specified, but delta pre-fetch remains too vague to implement and MDM key purge verification is absent.
strengths: Drift encrypted SQLite with device-bound Keychain/Keystore key is architecturally correct for GLBA mobile NPI
Server-wins vs client-wins conflict resolution boundary clearly separated by entity type
Cognito MFA with 30-minute inactivity lock satisfies GLBA Safeguards Rule session controls
concerns: ETag delta pre-fetch strategy lacks implementation detail - how ETags are stored and invalidated on the client is unspecified
MDM remote wipe purging the encryption key is stated as a requirement but not confirmed with a test procedure

Add MDM wipe verification test: enroll test device, populate Drift database, execute Jamf Pro full-device wipe, attempt Drift open on fresh install, confirm AES key absent from Keychain/Keystore
