previous_plan.title: Agricultural Lending Field Officer App - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Offline-first Flutter app for agricultural loan officers with Drift encrypted SQLite, Cognito MFA, ETag-based delta pre-fetch (underspecified), and dual conflict resolution strategies (server-wins for financials, client-wins for field notes). Syncs to FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL central store.
previous_plan.workflows: Portfolio pre-fetch on day-start using ETag headers; offline borrower access; field visit note and photo capture to sync queue; covenant check form completion; sync on connectivity restore with entity-type routing for conflict resolution.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Device-bound AES-256 Drift encryption; Flutter Secure Storage in iOS Keychain/Android Keystore; Cognito MFA with 30-minute inactivity lock; Jamf Pro MDM remote wipe stated but not confirmed for Keychain/Keystore purge.
previous_plan.data_model: farm_borrowers, loan_files, crop_production_records, collateral_appraisals, field_visit_notes, collateral_photos, covenant_checks, sync_queue_items defined. etag_cache table absent.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must fully resolve the delta pre-fetch ETag specification (ISS-001) and the MDM key purge confirmation (ISS-002). For ISS-001 I need to define ETag computation as a per-borrower hash of relevant updated_at timestamps, specify the Drift etag_cache table, define the stale-cache fallback threshold, and document the API If-None-Match header flow. For ISS-002 I need to specify the Jamf Pro full-device wipe behavior on iOS Keychain and Android Keystore and add a mandatory pre-production verification test procedure. The rest of the Round 2 architecture was sound.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined per-borrower ETag as SHA-256 hash of MAX(updated_at) across farm_borrowers, loan_files, crop_production_records, and collateral_appraisals for that borrower_id. Added etag_cache (borrower_id UUID PK, etag VARCHAR, cached_at TIMESTAMP) to data model. Pre-fetch API endpoint accepts If-None-Match header per borrower; returns 304 Not Modified when ETag matches, 200 with delta payload when changed. Flutter PortfolioRepository fetches per-borrower ETags from etag_cache, sends batch If-None-Match requests, updates only changed borrowers. Full-portfolio fallback triggered when cache miss rate exceeds 10% of portfolio (e.g., first install or after 30-day device inactivity purge). Documented complete ETag flow in workflows section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Subsequent-day pre-fetch reduced from ~100MB full portfolio download to <5MB delta payload for a 50-borrower portfolio with typical daily change rate of 5%; 8-hour offline compliance reliably achievable within 4G pre-fetch window.

fix_report.action_taken: Confirmed Jamf Pro full-device wipe (not selective wipe) purges iOS Keychain and Android Keystore, removing the Drift AES-256 encryption key. Added mandatory pre-production MDM wipe verification test procedure to security_and_compliance section: (1) enroll test device in Jamf Pro, (2) install app and complete login, (3) verify Drift database populates with borrower data, (4) execute Jamf Pro remote wipe from MDM console, (5) re-enroll device, (6) attempt Drift database open without login - confirm database is inaccessible due to missing Keychain/Keystore key, (7) document test result in security sign-off checklist before production deployment. Also clarified that selective app wipe only removes app sandbox but does NOT purge the Keychain/Keystore entry on iOS; full-device wipe is required for complete key destruction.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GLBA Safeguards Rule requirement for remote destruction of NPI on lost/stolen devices is confirmed operational; security team has a repeatable test procedure for MDM wipe verification at each major release.

target_output.title: Agricultural Lending Field Officer Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first Flutter app for agricultural loan officers enabling 8-hour field visits with no connectivity. Farm borrower financials, loan files, crop production history, and collateral data pre-fetched to device-encrypted Drift SQLite with per-borrower ETag delta strategy. Field visit notes, covenant checks, and collateral photos captured offline and synced to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate with entity-type-aware conflict resolution. GLBA-compliant with Cognito MFA, 30-minute session locks, Jamf Pro MDM with confirmed full-device wipe key destruction, and portfolio-scoped RBAC.
target_output.architecture_overview: Offline-first architecture with a Dart/Flutter client storing all borrower portfolio data in a Drift AES-256-encrypted SQLite database keyed by a device-bound secret in iOS Keychain/Android Keystore. On field-day start the PortfolioRepository executes a per-borrower ETag delta pre-fetch against the FastAPI sync API, downloading only borrowers whose upstream records changed since last cache. During offline field visits the app reads from and writes to the local Drift database. On connectivity restore the SyncQueueNotifier uploads pending field_visit_notes, collateral_photos (via S3 presigned URLs), and covenant_checks using idempotency keys; server-wins conflict resolution applied to loan_files and covenant_checks; client-wins for field visit notes and photos as net-new records. A FastAPI service on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ serves as the central source of truth for all financial data.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22 (Dart), Drift 2.x (encrypted SQLite ORM, AES-256), Riverpod 2.x (state management), Flutter Secure Storage 9.x (iOS Keychain/Android Keystore), Cognito Hosted UI with TOTP MFA, connectivity_plus for network state, http for REST calls. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy 2.x, PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, S3 for collateral photo storage, Cognito JWT validation via python-jose. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks, ALB with WAF, RDS Multi-AZ us-west-2, S3 bucket with SSE-S3, Jamf Pro MDM for iOS/Android fleet management, CloudWatch, Sentry mobile SDK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Portfolio pre-fetch with ETag delta strategy (etag_cache per borrower, If-None-Match batch requests, full-portfolio fallback at >10% miss rate). Offline borrower detail view showing farm_borrowers profile, loan_files summary, crop_production_records 3-year history, collateral_appraisals. Field visit note capture with free-text observations, risk_flags tagging, and collateral photo attachment. Covenant check questionnaire recording DSCR actual, current ratio, pass/fail determination, and officer notes. Sync queue dashboard showing pending_count, in_flight items, last_sync_at, and manual re-trigger. Branch supervisor review screen showing recent field visit notes and covenant check results across assigned officers. MDM-enforced remote wipe with verified Keychain/Keystore key destruction.
target_output.system_components: Flutter mobile app (iOS 16+ and Android 13+). Drift encrypted SQLite local database with etag_cache, sync_queue_items, and full borrower entity schema. Riverpod state layer with AsyncNotifier providers. FastAPI sync service on ECS Fargate (portfolio pre-fetch, sync upload, covenant storage, supervisor API). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (central source of truth). S3 bucket for collateral photos (SSE-S3, lifecycle to Glacier after 5 years). Cognito user pool with TOTP MFA. Jamf Pro MDM for remote wipe. Sentry mobile SDK for crash reporting. CloudWatch for API metrics.
target_output.workflows: Portfolio Pre-fetch (Day Start): PortfolioRepository reads all borrower_id entries from Drift; constructs batch GET /portfolio/pre-fetch request with If-None-Match headers per borrower_id (ETags from etag_cache). Server computes ETag for each borrower as SHA-256(MAX(updated_at) across farm_borrowers JOIN loan_files JOIN crop_production_records WHERE borrower_id). Returns 304 for unchanged borrowers, 200 with delta payload for changed. Client updates Drift records and refreshes etag_cache. If cache miss rate > 10% (first install or stale cache) triggers full portfolio download. Full pre-fetch for 50-borrower portfolio: ~100MB; delta pre-fetch typical day: <5MB. Field Visit Capture: Loan officer selects borrower from offline list, opens field visit note form, records observations and risk_flags, attaches photos via device camera (stored in app sandbox). Covenant check form records DSCR actual, current ratio, pass/fail. All entries written to Drift with sync_status=pending. Sync on Connectivity Restore: SyncQueueNotifier monitors connectivity_plus stream; on transition to connected state, iterates sync_queue_items ordered by created_at. For loan_files and covenant_checks: POST /sync/upload with server-wins merge (server version wins on conflict). For field_visit_notes and collateral_photos: POST /sync/upload with client-wins (new records, no conflict possible). Photo upload uses S3 presigned URL returned by API; retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff on upload failure. Idempotency key (entity_id + officer_id + created_at hash) prevents duplicate uploads on retry.
target_output.data_model: farm_borrowers (id UUID PK, borrower_name VARCHAR, farm_address TEXT, assigned_officer_id UUID FK, fsn VARCHAR, entity_type ENUM individual/partnership/corporation, updated_at TIMESTAMP). loan_files (id UUID PK, borrower_id UUID FK, loan_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, loan_type ENUM operating/real_estate/equipment, principal_amount DECIMAL(15,2), maturity_date DATE, status ENUM current/watch/substandard, updated_at TIMESTAMP). crop_production_records (id UUID PK, borrower_id UUID FK, crop_year INT, commodity VARCHAR, acres_planted INT, yield_bu_per_acre DECIMAL(8,2), revenue_usd DECIMAL(12,2), updated_at TIMESTAMP). collateral_appraisals (id UUID PK, loan_id UUID FK, appraisal_date DATE, appraiser_name VARCHAR, appraised_value_usd DECIMAL(15,2), collateral_type ENUM real_estate/equipment/crops, report_s3_key VARCHAR, updated_at TIMESTAMP). field_visit_notes (id UUID PK, loan_id UUID FK, officer_id UUID FK, visited_at TIMESTAMP, observations TEXT, risk_flags JSONB, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed). collateral_photos (id UUID PK, visit_note_id UUID FK, s3_key VARCHAR, captured_at TIMESTAMP, sync_status ENUM pending/uploading/synced/failed). covenant_checks (id UUID PK, loan_id UUID FK, check_date DATE, dscr_actual DECIMAL(6,3), current_ratio DECIMAL(6,3), pass_fail ENUM pass/fail/waived, officer_notes TEXT, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed). sync_queue_items (id UUID PK, entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID, operation ENUM create/update, created_at TIMESTAMP, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0, last_attempt_at TIMESTAMP, sync_status ENUM pending/in_flight/synced/failed). etag_cache (borrower_id UUID PK, etag VARCHAR(64), cached_at TIMESTAMP). All tables exist in both Drift local schema and PostgreSQL central schema; etag_cache and sync_queue_items are Drift-only. Agricultural lending financial entities: loan_accounts (id UUID PK, farm_borrower_id FK, loan_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, loan_type ENUM operating/equipment/real_estate, principal_amount_cents BIGINT, outstanding_balance_cents BIGINT, interest_rate_bps INT, currency CHAR(3), maturity_date DATE, status ENUM current/delinquent/default), payment_transactions (id UUID PK, loan_account_id FK, payment_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), payment_type ENUM principal/interest/partial, method ENUM check/ach/cash, posted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), credit_analyses (id UUID PK, farm_borrower_id FK, analyst_id UUID, credit_score INT, debt_service_coverage_ratio NUMERIC, ltv_ratio NUMERIC, decision ENUM approved/declined/modified, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/portfolio/pre-fetch?borrower_ids=uuid1,uuid2 with If-None-Match header per borrower_id (JSON map); returns 200 with changed borrower payloads or 304 per unchanged borrower. GET /api/v1/portfolio/{borrower_id} (full borrower with loan_files, crop_production_records, collateral_appraisals; fallback for full pre-fetch). POST /api/v1/sync/upload (body: entity_type, entity_id, payload, idempotency_key; returns 201 created or 409 conflict with server_version). POST /api/v1/sync/photo-url (returns S3 presigned PUT URL for collateral photo upload). GET /api/v1/supervisor/visits?officer_ids=[] (branch supervisor review). All endpoints require Cognito JWT Bearer; portfolio endpoints apply RBAC filter (loan_officer: assigned borrowers only; branch_supervisor: full branch). Rate limit: 200 req/min per user.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Device-bound encryption: Drift AES-256 SQLite key generated on first install and stored in iOS Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly) and Android Keystore (HARDWARE_BACKED when available). This key is never transmitted off-device. MDM remote wipe: Jamf Pro full-device wipe command purges iOS Keychain and Android Keystore, permanently destroying the Drift encryption key rendering the local database inaccessible. IMPORTANT: Selective app wipe (Jamf Pro 'Remove Managed App') removes the app sandbox but does NOT purge the iOS Keychain entry - full-device wipe is required. Pre-production MDM wipe test procedure (mandatory before production deployment): (1) enroll test device in Jamf Pro dev group, (2) install app, complete Cognito login, pre-fetch 5 test borrowers, (3) verify Drift database is populated, (4) execute Jamf Pro full-device wipe from console, (5) re-enroll device, fresh install app without login, (6) attempt Drift database open - must fail with encryption key not found error, (7) document result in security sign-off checklist. Cognito session: 30-minute inactivity timer triggers Cognito signOut() and clears in-memory session token; Drift database remains encrypted at rest. RBAC enforced at FastAPI dependency layer. TLS 1.3 for all API traffic. Photos stored in app-sandboxed directory, not in device Camera Roll. GLBA Safeguards Rule, OCC agricultural lending handbook, and Regulation B documented in compliance runbook.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Flutter analyze, flutter test (unit and widget tests), FastAPI pytest (90% coverage gate), docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling update. Mobile distribution: TestFlight (iOS) and Firebase App Distribution (Android) for pilot beta. Production ECS: 2-4 Fargate tasks, auto-scaling on CPU 60%, ALB with WAF. RDS Multi-AZ with 7-day automated backups and point-in-time recovery. S3 collateral photos with SSE-S3 and Glacier lifecycle after 5 years. Drift schema migrations managed via versioned Drift migration files; server-side PostgreSQL migrations via Alembic in CI. Jamf Pro device policies enforced at MDM enrollment; app distribution via Jamf Pro Self Service for authorized loan officers.
target_output.observability: Sentry mobile SDK captures Flutter crashes with Drift query context, sync queue depth at time of crash, and device OS version. CloudWatch monitors FastAPI: sync upload p95 latency, pre-fetch p95 latency, error rate by endpoint. Custom CloudWatch metrics: sync_success_rate per officer_id (alerts when below 95% over 1-hour window), photo_upload_retry_count (P2 alert when retries > 3 for a single item). Structured logs from FastAPI with correlation_id, officer_id, borrower_id, and sync_operation for end-to-end field visit tracing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks approximately $180/month. RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ approximately $140/month. S3 for photos with Glacier lifecycle approximately $60/month at 200 officers capturing 10 photos per visit. Total approximately $400/month for 200-officer production fleet. Scaling levers: ECS auto-scaling on CPU; RDS read replica if supervisor analytics queries impact sync API latency. Flutter app size target <50MB download; delta pre-fetch minimises daily data usage to <5MB per officer.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito MFA login, portfolio pre-fetch (full download) with ETag delta strategy, offline borrower profile and loan file access, field visit note capture, covenant check form, sync queue with server-wins/client-wins routing, collateral photo capture and S3 upload, etag_cache implementation, MDM wipe test procedure completed, TestFlight and Firebase beta with 20 pilot officers. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Crop production history viewer, collateral appraisal entry, branch supervisor review dashboard, delta pre-fetch optimised with batch If-None-Match, offline-available covenant history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): DSCR and liquidity ratio auto-computation, push notifications for supervisor follow-ups, sync conflict resolution audit log, App Store and Google Play production release.
target_output.development_guidelines: Dart/Flutter: feature modules under lib/features/ with domain/application/presentation layers. All Drift queries defined in typed DAO classes; no raw SQL strings outside DAOs. Riverpod providers co-located with feature module. Flutter unit tests cover all Drift DAO operations and sync queue logic; widget tests for visit capture and covenant check forms. FastAPI: async endpoints only; SQLAlchemy async sessions. Alembic migration required for every schema change. No borrower data in application logs; log borrower_id only. PR requires 1 senior mobile or backend engineer review depending on change scope.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: iOS Keychain key not purged by selective app wipe. Mitigation: documented and tested - full-device wipe required; loan officer MDM policy enforces full-device wipe for lost devices. Risk: Pre-fetch payload exceeds device storage on large portfolios (>200 borrowers). Mitigation: delta pre-fetch reduces ongoing payload; portfolio assignment limits loan officers to max 100 active borrowers. Risk: Sync conflict on covenant_checks if supervisor updates central record while officer is offline. Mitigation: server-wins policy; officer's offline entry is overwritten with server version and officer notified. Tradeoff: Drift local schema maintains full copy of borrower financials rather than on-demand fetch - increases pre-fetch complexity but enables 8-hour offline operation. Tradeoff: Server-wins for financial data over manual merge UI - reduces UX complexity but means offline officer edits to loan_files are discarded on conflict.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed full-device wipe (not selective) required for GLBA-compliant key destruction on lost devices; documented in Jamf Pro device policy. Confirmed ETags computed server-side as SHA-256 of borrower-scoped updated_at MAX timestamps; no client-side ETag computation. Confirmed portfolio size cap of 100 active borrowers per loan officer to bound pre-fetch payload.

metadata.notes: Agricultural lending field officer mobile app Round 3 resolves delta pre-fetch ETag specification (ISS-001) and MDM key purge confirmation (ISS-002).

profile.domain: SME Business Banking - Digital Payments and Cash Flow Portal

project_goal.value: Provide small business owners with a scalable digital banking portal to manage accounts, initiate domestic and international wire transfers, view 30-day rolling cash flow forecasts, and manage payroll funding batches, designed to support growth from 10,000 to 500,000 business accounts
project_goal.rationale: Core business banking portal replacing legacy branch-only wire initiation workflow

target_users.value: Small and medium-sized business owners and their designated co-signatories; bank operations staff via admin panel
target_users.rationale: Business owners are primary; co-signatories required for dual-approval wire workflow

project_class.rationale: Next.js frontend with Spring Boot backend, admin panel, real-time balance updates, and payment processing

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, data, payments, realtime, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full portal capabilities including wire payments, forecasting, real-time balances, and bank staff admin

complexity_level.rationale: 50x account growth from 10k to 500k requires sharded PostgreSQL and CockroachDB for global distribution

risk_level.rationale: Wire payments and payroll funding involve large financial transfers requiring BSA/AML, OFAC screening, and Reg E compliance

data_sensitivity.rationale: Business accounts, wire instructions, payroll batches, and beneficiary records are financial data under BSA/Reg E

external_exposure.rationale: Business banking portal publicly accessible via internet; WAF and DDoS protection required

access_model.value: Auth0 MFA with RBAC: business_owner (full account access), co_signatory (approve wires and payroll), read_only (view balances and statements), bank_admin (operations panel)
access_model.rationale: Wire dual-approval requires co_signatory role; bank_admin role for operations team access to business accounts

feature_scope.value: Account dashboard with real-time balance, transaction history, domestic and international wire initiation with dual-approval, OFAC beneficiary screening, payroll batch funding, 30-day cash flow forecast, account statements, bank admin panel for account management and wire review
feature_scope.rationale: Full SME banking portal feature set

mvp_scope.value: Account dashboard, domestic wire initiation with dual-approval, OFAC screening, transaction history, account statements
mvp_scope.rationale: Core wire payment workflow is the primary driver; cash flow forecast and payroll deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Auth0 MFA mandatory for all roles, TLS 1.3, WAF (Google Cloud Armor) on GKE ingress, OFAC screening on all beneficiary records, wire transaction signing with audit trail, PCI-DSS for any card data, no card PANs in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: Public internet portal with wire payments requires comprehensive security controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS; React Query for server state; next-auth v5 for Auth0 integration
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js SSR for SEO and initial load performance; next-auth v5 for Auth0 OIDC integration

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.3; Spring Security for RBAC; Spring Data JPA with CockroachDB for distributed accounts
backend_stack.rationale: Java Spring Boot is the bank's standard backend platform; CockroachDB for multi-region account distribution

data_platform.value: CockroachDB (business_accounts, wire_instructions, beneficiary_records) for global distribution; PostgreSQL 16 sharded (transactions, payroll_funding_batches); Redis 7 Cluster for session cache and real-time balance cache
data_platform.rationale: CockroachDB for accounts requiring geo-distributed consistency; PostgreSQL sharded for high-volume transaction append

hosting_target.value: GCP GKE Autopilot (us-central1, us-east1 multi-region); CockroachDB Dedicated Cloud on GCP; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot for managed Kubernetes; CockroachDB Dedicated for multi-region accounts; Cloud SQL for transactional data

privacy_retention_policy.value: Wire transaction records retained 7 years for BSA/AML record-keeping; OFAC screening results retained 5 years; payroll batch records 7 years; account statements 7 years; session logs 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: BSA requires 5-7 year retention of all financial transaction records and AML screening results

future_scope.value: International wire expansion (SWIFT MT103, SEPA), payroll funding API for payroll providers, AI-powered cash flow anomaly detection, multi-currency account support
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2-3 international expansion and API monetisation roadmap

constraints.value: Must support 500,000 concurrent business accounts at full scale; wire initiation to Fedwire must complete acknowledgement within 30 seconds; OFAC screening response within 5 seconds
constraints.rationale: SLA commitments to business customers and regulatory requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing, structured JSON logs, PagerDuty alerts for payment failures, Datadog custom metrics for wire throughput and OFAC screening latency
observability_baseline.rationale: Payment operations require real-time visibility of wire pipeline and OFAC screening performance

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; CircleCI for CI/CD; blue-green deployments for zero-downtime wire processing
execution_preference.rationale: Wire payment availability SLA requires zero-downtime deployments

compliance_context.value: Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) and FinCEN AML program, OFAC sanctions screening (SDN list), Regulation E electronic fund transfers, FinCEN Customer Due Diligence (CDD) and beneficial ownership requirements for business accounts
compliance_context.rationale: SME wire payment portal subject to full BSA/AML, OFAC, and Reg E regulatory framework

project.goal: Scalable SME digital banking portal with wire payments, OFAC screening, and cash flow analytics
project.target_users: Small business owners, co-signatories, bank admin staff

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.3 with Spring Security
backend.payment: Fedwire API for domestic wires, SWIFT MT103 deferred to Phase 3

security.auth: Auth0 MFA mandatory
security.screening: OFAC SDN list screening on beneficiaries and wire originators
security.waf: Google Cloud Armor on GKE ingress

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope of domestic wire with dual-approval and OFAC screening correctly prioritises the highest-risk and highest-value feature. The dual-approval workflow via co-signatory push notification is essential for BSA compliance and fraud prevention. Cash flow forecast is deferred correctly - it provides value but is not on the critical path for regulatory compliance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The CockroachDB + PostgreSQL sharding split is the right architectural decision: CockroachDB for business_accounts and wire_instructions requiring SERIALIZABLE transactions with geo-distribution, PostgreSQL sharded for high-volume transaction append. The Spring Boot wire service must implement idempotency keys on Fedwire submission to prevent duplicate wire submissions on retry. The dual-approval workflow state machine (pending_approval -> approved -> submitted_to_fedwire -> acknowledged) must be stored in CockroachDB with optimistic locking to prevent concurrent approval race conditions.
reasoner_reviews.security: OFAC screening must run on beneficiary records at creation time and again at wire submission time - beneficiaries are not perpetually clean after initial screening. Auth0 MFA is correctly mandatory. Google Cloud Armor must have rate limiting rules specific to wire initiation endpoints (stricter than general API rate limits). Wire audit trail must capture initiator identity, co-signatory identity, OFAC screening result reference, Fedwire submission reference, and acknowledgement code in a single immutable record.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 30-second Fedwire acknowledgement SLA requires the wire service to return a synchronous response to the frontend. A fire-and-forget async submission pattern would require polling which adds latency uncertainty. Spring WebFlux reactive endpoints are worth evaluating for the wire submission path to handle concurrent wire submissions without thread blocking. OFAC 5-second screening SLA is achievable with a dedicated screening service caching the SDN list in Redis.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan underspecified the wire dual-approval state machine transitions and did not address the OFAC re-screening requirement at wire submission time. The beneficiary management workflow also lacked detail on how beneficiaries are verified before being available for wire use.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot microservices (deployed as separate GKE Deployments): account-service (balance queries, transaction history, CockroachDB reads), wire-service (wire initiation, dual-approval state machine, Fedwire submission, CockroachDB writes), ofac-service (SDN list cache in Redis, screening logic, result persistence), payroll-service (batch funding, PostgreSQL sharded writes), forecast-service (30-day projection computation from transaction history), admin-service (bank staff operations, account management)
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.1 spec per service. Wire initiation: POST /api/v1/wires with idempotency_key header. Dual approval: POST /api/v1/wires/{id}/approve (co_signatory JWT required). Fedwire submission: synchronous call via wire-service with 30-second timeout. OFAC screening: POST /api/v1/beneficiaries/{id}/screen returns ScreeningResult within 5 seconds.
backend.business_modules: WireStateMachine: states DRAFT -> PENDING_COSIGNATORY_APPROVAL -> OFAC_SCREENING -> SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE -> FEDWIRE_ACKNOWLEDGED -> COMPLETED / REJECTED. OFACScreeningEngine: loads SDN OFAC list into Redis sorted set on startup and daily refresh; fuzzy name matching (Levenshtein distance < 2) against beneficiary name; country code lookup. BeneficiaryVerificationService: validates routing number via Fed routing directory, validates account number format before adding to beneficiary_records.
backend.failure_handling: Fedwire submission timeout: wire transitions to SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE state; background Spring Scheduler job polls Fedwire acknowledgement endpoint every 60 seconds for up to 4 hours. OFAC service unavailable: wire blocked with OFAC_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE status; operations team alerted; no wire proceeds without positive OFAC screening. CockroachDB transaction retry on serialization error (CockroachDB recommends retry loop for SQLSTATE 40001).

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with route groups: (banking) for authenticated business owner flows, (admin) for bank staff flows, (auth) for Auth0 callbacks. Server Components for account dashboard and transaction history (SSR for initial load). Client Components for wire initiation form, real-time balance ticker, and approval notifications.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard: account balance, recent transactions, pending wire approvals badge, cash flow forecast thumbnail. Wire Initiation: beneficiary selector, amount/currency/memo, dual-approval confirmation screen, OFAC screening status indicator, Fedwire submission progress. Co-signatory approval: push notification -> approval screen with wire details, approve/reject buttons. Payroll Funding: batch upload CSV, funding amount validation, batch status tracker. Admin Panel: account search, wire review queue, AML alert queue.
frontend.state_management: React Query: account balance (30s revalidation), transaction history (on-demand), wire status (10s polling while PENDING/SUBMITTED). Zustand: wire draft state, beneficiary selection, notification badge count. Server-Sent Events for real-time balance updates pushed from account-service.

security.auth_design: Auth0 OIDC with TOTP MFA mandatory for all roles. next-auth v5 handles Auth0 callback, stores JWT in httpOnly cookie, passes to Spring Boot services as Bearer token. Auth0 custom claims include user role and business_account_id for RBAC enforcement.
security.authorization_model: Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize: wire approval endpoint restricted to co_signatory and business_owner roles; admin endpoints restricted to bank_admin role. Business account isolation: all data queries filter by business_account_id claim from JWT.
security.abuse_prevention: Google Cloud Armor rate limits: wire initiation 10 req/min per business_account_id; OFAC beneficiary creation 20 req/min per account; login 5 attempts per 5 minutes. Anomaly detection: wire amounts >3 standard deviations from account's 90-day average trigger AML review queue.

data.entities: business_accounts (id UUID PK, legal_name, ein VARCHAR, account_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, routing_number VARCHAR, balance_usd DECIMAL(20,2), account_status ENUM active/frozen/closed, kyb_verified_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP) - CockroachDB. wire_instructions (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, beneficiary_id FK, amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), currency ENUM USD/EUR, memo VARCHAR, initiator_user_id, cosignatory_user_id, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/ofac_screening/submitted/acknowledged/completed/rejected, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, fedwire_reference VARCHAR, fedwire_ack_code VARCHAR, ofac_screening_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMP, approved_at TIMESTAMP, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP) - CockroachDB. beneficiary_records (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, beneficiary_name, bank_name, routing_number, account_number_encrypted, address JSONB, last_ofac_screened_at TIMESTAMP, ofac_status ENUM clear/flagged/pending, created_at TIMESTAMP) - CockroachDB. account_transactions (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, transaction_type ENUM wire_debit/wire_credit/payroll_debit/fee, amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), balance_after DECIMAL(20,2), reference_id, settled_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL sharded by business_account_id mod 16. payroll_funding_batches (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, batch_date DATE, total_amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), employee_count INT, status ENUM pending/processing/completed/failed, ach_batch_id VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. cash_flow_forecasts (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, forecast_period_start DATE, forecast_period_end DATE, projected_inflows JSONB, projected_outflows JSONB, net_cash_position DECIMAL(20,2), generated_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. ofac_screening_results (id UUID PK, entity_id UUID, entity_type ENUM beneficiary/wire_originator, screened_name VARCHAR, matched_sdn_id VARCHAR NULL, match_score DECIMAL(5,4), screening_status ENUM clear/matched/review_required, screened_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. account_signatories (id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, user_id VARCHAR, role ENUM business_owner/co_signatory/read_only, added_at TIMESTAMP, active BOOL) - CockroachDB.
data.storage_design: CockroachDB Dedicated 3-region (us-central1, us-east1, us-west1): business_accounts, wire_instructions, beneficiary_records, account_signatories with PARTITION BY RANGE on business_account_id for geo-locality. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL sharded (16 shards by business_account_id mod 16): account_transactions, payroll_funding_batches, cash_flow_forecasts. Redis 7 Cluster: balance cache key balance:{business_account_id} with 30s TTL, OFAC SDN list (sorted set by name), session tokens.
data.retention_and_deletion: wire_instructions and ofac_screening_results: 7 years (BSA). account_transactions: 7 years. payroll_funding_batches: 7 years. cash_flow_forecasts: 2 years. session logs: 90 days. Archive via GCS lifecycle for records beyond 1 year.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot multi-region (us-central1 primary, us-east1 secondary). Services: account-service (3 replicas), wire-service (5 replicas, HPA CPU 60%), ofac-service (3 replicas, dedicated Redis SDN cache), payroll-service (2 replicas), forecast-service (2 replicas), next-js-frontend (4 replicas). Blue-green deployment for wire-service via GKE traffic splitting. Cloud Load Balancing with Google Cloud Armor WAF.
devops.ci_cd_design: CircleCI pipeline: Maven test (Spring Boot), Jest/Cypress (Next.js), Docker build, GCR push, GKE rolling deploy (blue-green for wire-service). Staging environment: GKE Autopilot us-central1 with CockroachDB dev cluster and Fedwire simulator.
devops.cost_controls: GKE Autopilot scales to zero outside business hours for non-critical services. CockroachDB Dedicated right-sized at 3 nodes per region; scale before 50k accounts milestone. PostgreSQL Cloud SQL db.n2-standard-4 with read replica for forecast queries.

ISS-001.title: OFAC re-screening at wire submission time not specified - beneficiaries screened only at creation
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan screens beneficiaries at creation and stores ofac_status but does not re-screen at wire submission time. A beneficiary added to the OFAC SDN list after initial screening could receive a wire payment without detection.

history.note: Security reviewer identified OFAC screening gap

ISS-002.title: Wire dual-approval state machine race condition - concurrent approvals not addressed
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan described dual-approval workflow but did not specify how concurrent approval attempts by two signatories are handled. Without optimistic locking on the wire_instructions record, two co-signatories could both read PENDING_APPROVAL state and both transition to APPROVED simultaneously.

history.note: Architect_reasoner flagged concurrency risk in approval state machine

ISS-003.title: Beneficiary verification workflow underspecified before wire use
ISS-003.detail: Beneficiaries can be added to beneficiary_records and immediately used for wire initiation without any verification step. Round 1 plan did not specify routing number validation, account verification, or cooling-off period before first wire use.

history.note: Critic reviewer flagged beneficiary onboarding gap

focus_issues.title: OFAC re-screening at wire submission time not specified
focus_issues.detail: Beneficiaries screened only at creation; OFAC SDN list changes between creation and wire submission are not caught.

focus_issues.title: Wire dual-approval state machine race condition not addressed
focus_issues.detail: Concurrent approval attempts by two signatories without optimistic locking can cause double-approval race condition.

latest_recommendations: Add OFAC re-screening step in WireStateMachine OFAC_SCREENING state: re-screen beneficiary and wire originator at submission time using current SDN list; wire blocked if match found even if beneficiary previously marked clear

Add optimistic locking version column to wire_instructions; approval endpoint uses CockroachDB UPDATE WHERE status='pending_approval' AND version=N; if 0 rows updated, return 409 Conflict (already approved or rejected)
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: SME Business Banking Portal - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved; ISS-003 medium severity deferred to Round 3

previous_audits.summary: Well-structured portal architecture with correct CockroachDB/PostgreSQL data split but two high-severity compliance and concurrency issues in the wire payment flow undermine regulatory readiness.
strengths: CockroachDB for distributed account data with PostgreSQL sharding for transaction volume is architecturally sound
Wire dual-approval workflow with co-signatory push notification is correctly designed at a high level
Google Cloud Armor rate limiting on wire initiation endpoints is a good fraud prevention control
concerns: OFAC screening only at beneficiary creation creates a gap for newly-sanctioned entities
Dual-approval state machine lacks concurrency control - race condition possible on simultaneous approval attempts
blocking_issues: OFAC screening gap is a regulatory compliance blocker - cannot process wires without point-in-time screening at submission
Approval race condition is a financial risk blocker - could result in double-approval allowing wires to proceed without proper authorisation
recommendations: Add OFAC_SCREENING state in WireStateMachine that re-screens beneficiary and originator at wire submission time; block if any match found
Add optimistic lock version column to wire_instructions; approval CAS UPDATE returns 409 if version mismatch detected

issue_updates.title: OFAC re-screening at wire submission absent
issue_updates.detail: Beneficiary screened at creation only; no re-screening at wire submission time

issue_updates.title: Wire approval race condition in state machine
issue_updates.detail: No optimistic locking on wire_instructions approval transition

previous_plan.title: SME Business Banking Portal - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 frontend with Spring Boot 3.3 backend on GKE Autopilot. CockroachDB for distributed business accounts and wire instructions. PostgreSQL 16 sharded for transactions. Auth0 MFA. Wire dual-approval with co-signatory push notification. OFAC screening at beneficiary creation. Fedwire domestic wire submission.
previous_plan.workflows: Wire initiation: business_owner creates wire draft -> co_signatory approves -> OFAC screening (at creation only - gap identified) -> Fedwire submission -> acknowledgement. Beneficiary creation: add beneficiary record, OFAC screen, store result - no re-screening at wire time.
previous_plan.data_model: business_accounts, wire_instructions (no version column for optimistic lock), beneficiary_records, account_transactions, account_signatories defined. ofac_screening_results present but linked only to beneficiary creation, not wire submission.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve two high-severity issues: OFAC re-screening at wire submission time (ISS-001) and the dual-approval race condition (ISS-002). For ISS-001 I need to add an explicit OFAC_SCREENING state in the WireStateMachine that re-screens both the beneficiary and wire originator against the current SDN list at submission time, and add a wire-level ofac_screening_result_id FK. For ISS-002 I need to add an optimistic lock version column to wire_instructions and use a CAS (compare-and-swap) UPDATE on the approval state transition. ISS-003 (beneficiary verification) is medium severity and should be addressed with a routing number validation step and 24-hour cooling-off period for first wire use. The underlying architecture, CockroachDB/PostgreSQL data split, and multi-service GKE design are sound.

fix_report.action_taken: Added OFAC_SCREENING as an explicit state in WireStateMachine between PENDING_COSIGNATORY_APPROVAL and SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE. On transition to OFAC_SCREENING, wire-service calls ofac-service POST /screen with both beneficiary_id and originator_user_id. ofac-service re-screens beneficiary name and originator name against current Redis SDN cache (refreshed daily from OFAC SDN XML feed). If any match found (match_score >= 0.85 Levenshtein threshold), wire transitions to OFAC_BLOCKED status, new ofac_screening_result created with MATCHED status, AML alert queued in bank_admin panel. If clear, wire transitions to SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE. Added wire_ofac_screening_id FK to wire_instructions table referencing the submission-time screening result. Updated workflows section with complete OFAC_SCREENING state transition.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every wire submission re-screens beneficiary and originator against the current SDN list at submission time; newly-sanctioned entities added to the OFAC list after initial beneficiary creation are caught before wire reaches Fedwire; bank satisfies OFAC regulatory requirement for point-in-time screening.

fix_report.action_taken: Added version INT column to wire_instructions table. Approval endpoint in wire-service executes CockroachDB UPDATE wire_instructions SET status='pending_ofac_screening', cosignatory_user_id=?, approved_at=NOW(), version=version+1 WHERE id=? AND status='pending_cosignatory_approval' AND version=?; if rows_updated=0, returns HTTP 409 Conflict with body {error: 'wire_already_processed', current_status: <actual status>}. Frontend displays user-friendly message on 409: 'This wire has already been approved or rejected by another signatory.' Version column also used on OFAC_SCREENING -> SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE transition and on Fedwire acknowledgement update to prevent concurrent state corruption. Updated data_model and api_design sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent approval attempts by two co-signatories result in exactly one successful approval; the second returns 409 Conflict with clear user feedback; no race condition can double-approve a wire or advance it to Fedwire submission without exactly one signatory approval.

target_output.title: SME Business Banking Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A scalable SME business banking portal built on Next.js 14 and Spring Boot 3.3 on GCP GKE Autopilot, designed to grow from 10,000 to 500,000 business accounts. Domestic wire initiation with co-signatory dual-approval uses an optimistic-locked state machine (DRAFT -> PENDING_APPROVAL -> OFAC_SCREENING -> SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE -> ACKNOWLEDGED) with point-in-time OFAC SDN re-screening at submission. CockroachDB Dedicated stores business accounts and wire instructions across three regions; PostgreSQL 16 sharded handles high-volume transaction append. Auth0 MFA mandatory for all roles with Google Cloud Armor WAF on public internet endpoints.
target_output.architecture_overview: Six Spring Boot microservices run as GKE Deployments: account-service (balance reads, transaction history from PostgreSQL shards), wire-service (WireStateMachine orchestration, CockroachDB writes, Fedwire HTTP client), ofac-service (SDN list in Redis Cluster, submission-time screening), payroll-service (ACH batch funding, PostgreSQL writes), forecast-service (30-day projection from transaction history), admin-service (bank operations and AML alert queue). Next.js 14 App Router frontend renders dashboard and wire initiation as Server Components for fast initial load; wire approval notifications use Server-Sent Events from account-service. Cloud Load Balancing with Google Cloud Armor WAF fronts all public traffic. CockroachDB Dedicated spans us-central1, us-east1, us-west1 with PARTITION BY geo-locality for business_accounts and wire_instructions. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL with 16 shards routes transactions by business_account_id mod 16. Redis 7 Cluster caches real-time balances (30s TTL), OFAC SDN sorted set, and session tokens.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router, TypeScript), Tailwind CSS, React Query 5.x, Zustand 4.x, next-auth v5 (Auth0 OIDC), @tanstack/react-table for transaction history. Backend: Java 21 (Virtual Threads for reactive concurrency), Spring Boot 3.3, Spring Security 6.x (Auth0 JWT validation), Spring Data JPA (CockroachDB via PostgreSQL dialect), Hibernate 6.x, Spring Scheduler for Fedwire polling. Databases: CockroachDB Dedicated v23.2 (3 regions), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (16 shards), Redis 7 Cluster on Memorystore. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot (us-central1 primary, us-east1 secondary), Cloud Load Balancing, Google Cloud Armor, Cloud Armor WAF rules, GCS for statement archival, CircleCI for CI/CD.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Account Dashboard: real-time balance via Redis cache with SSE push for updates, 30-transaction recent history, pending wire approvals badge count, cash flow forecast thumbnail (Phase 2). Wire Initiation: beneficiary selector (verified beneficiaries only, 24-hour cooling-off for new beneficiaries), amount/memo/currency form with BSA threshold indicator (>$10,000 shown), dual-approval request sent to co-signatory push notification, OFAC_SCREENING state indicator with 5-second SLA, Fedwire submission progress bar, acknowledgement reference display. Co-signatory Approval: push notification -> wire detail review screen with initiator name, beneficiary details, amount, OFAC pre-check status; Approve/Reject with confirmation modal. Payroll Funding: CSV batch upload, funding amount validation against account balance, ACH batch submission via payroll-service, batch status tracker. Admin Panel: account search by EIN or account number, wire review queue for AML alerts, OFAC match queue for manual review. BSA CTR and SAR filing placeholders in admin panel.
target_output.system_components: Next.js frontend (GKE 4 replicas). account-service (GKE 3 replicas, SSE balance stream, CockroachDB + PostgreSQL shard reads). wire-service (GKE 5 replicas, HPA CPU 60%, WireStateMachine, CockroachDB writes, Fedwire HTTP client, Spring Scheduler for acknowledgement polling). ofac-service (GKE 3 replicas, Redis SDN sorted set, Levenshtein screening, submission-time re-screening). payroll-service (GKE 2 replicas, ACH batch formatting, PostgreSQL writes). forecast-service (GKE 2 replicas, PostgreSQL transaction aggregation). admin-service (GKE 2 replicas, AML alert queue, OFAC match review queue). CockroachDB Dedicated (3-region, 3 nodes/region). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL (16 shards, db.n2-standard-4 + read replica). Redis 7 Cluster (Memorystore 3-node). Google Cloud Armor WAF. Auth0 OIDC tenant. Fedwire HTTP API endpoint.
target_output.workflows: Wire Initiation: business_owner submits POST /api/v1/wires with beneficiary_id, amount_usd, memo, idempotency_key. wire-service verifies beneficiary in beneficiary_records (status=verified, cooling_off_expires_at < NOW()). Creates wire_instructions record in CockroachDB with status=DRAFT, version=1, idempotency_key (UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate creates). Transitions to PENDING_COSIGNATORY_APPROVAL; pushes notification to co_signatory users for this business_account_id via account-service SSE. Co-signatory Approval: co-signatory opens wire detail, POSTs /api/v1/wires/{id}/approve with JWT. wire-service executes CockroachDB UPDATE SET status='OFAC_SCREENING', cosignatory_user_id=?, approved_at=NOW(), version=version+1 WHERE id=? AND status='PENDING_COSIGNATORY_APPROVAL' AND version=1. If 0 rows_updated: returns 409 Conflict. OFAC Screening: wire-service sends POST /internal/ofac/screen?entity_ids=beneficiary_id,originator_user_id to ofac-service. ofac-service performs Levenshtein fuzzy match on beneficiary_name and originator full name against Redis SDN sorted set (refreshed daily from OFAC SDN XML). If match_score >= 0.85: creates ofac_screening_result with MATCHED status; updates wire to OFAC_BLOCKED; sends AML alert to admin-service queue; returns BLOCKED to wire-service which returns HTTP 422 to frontend. If clear: creates ofac_screening_result with CLEAR status; updates wire_ofac_screening_id FK; transitions wire to SUBMITTED_TO_FEDWIRE. Fedwire Submission: wire-service POSTs to Fedwire API (synchronous HTTP, 30-second timeout). On success: stores fedwire_reference, fedwire_ack_code, transitions wire to ACKNOWLEDGED, updates account balance in CockroachDB, appends account_transactions debit record to PostgreSQL shard. On timeout: Spring Scheduler polls Fedwire acknowledgement endpoint every 60 seconds for up to 4 hours. On rejection: wire transitions to REJECTED with rejection_code.
target_output.data_model: business_accounts (CockroachDB): id UUID PK, legal_name VARCHAR, ein VARCHAR UNIQUE, account_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, routing_number VARCHAR, balance_usd DECIMAL(20,2), account_status ENUM active/frozen/closed, kyb_verified_at TIMESTAMP. wire_instructions (CockroachDB, optimistic lock): id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, beneficiary_id FK, amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), currency ENUM USD/EUR, memo VARCHAR(140), initiator_user_id VARCHAR, cosignatory_user_id VARCHAR NULL, status ENUM draft/pending_cosignatory_approval/ofac_screening/ofac_blocked/submitted_to_fedwire/fedwire_acknowledged/completed/rejected, version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, fedwire_reference VARCHAR NULL, fedwire_ack_code VARCHAR NULL, wire_ofac_screening_id UUID FK NULL (submission-time OFAC result), rejection_code VARCHAR NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP, approved_at TIMESTAMP NULL, submitted_at TIMESTAMP NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMP NULL. beneficiary_records (CockroachDB): id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, beneficiary_name VARCHAR, bank_name VARCHAR, routing_number VARCHAR, account_number_encrypted VARCHAR, address JSONB, verification_status ENUM pending/verified/rejected, routing_verified_at TIMESTAMP NULL, cooling_off_expires_at TIMESTAMP NULL, last_ofac_screened_at TIMESTAMP, ofac_status ENUM clear/flagged/pending, created_at TIMESTAMP. account_transactions (PostgreSQL sharded by business_account_id mod 16): id UUID PK, business_account_id, transaction_type ENUM wire_debit/wire_credit/payroll_debit/fee, amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), balance_after DECIMAL(20,2), reference_id UUID, settled_at TIMESTAMP. ofac_screening_results (PostgreSQL): id UUID PK, entity_id UUID, entity_type ENUM beneficiary/wire_originator/wire_submission, screened_name VARCHAR, matched_sdn_id VARCHAR NULL, match_score DECIMAL(5,4), screening_status ENUM clear/matched/review_required, screened_at TIMESTAMP. payroll_funding_batches (PostgreSQL): id UUID, business_account_id FK, batch_date DATE, total_amount_usd DECIMAL(20,2), employee_count INT, status ENUM pending/processing/completed/failed, ach_batch_id VARCHAR. account_signatories (CockroachDB): id UUID PK, business_account_id FK, user_id VARCHAR, role ENUM business_owner/co_signatory/read_only, active BOOL, added_at TIMESTAMP.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/wires (create wire draft, Idempotency-Key header required, returns WireResponse with status=DRAFT). POST /api/v1/wires/{id}/approve (co-signatory JWT required; CAS UPDATE with optimistic lock; 409 if version mismatch). GET /api/v1/wires/{id}/status (poll wire state during submission). GET /api/v1/wires?status=pending_approval (co-signatory approval queue). POST /api/v1/beneficiaries (create beneficiary, triggers OFAC screening and routing number validation, sets cooling_off_expires_at = NOW() + 24h). GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/balance (Redis cache with 30s TTL, fallback to CockroachDB). GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/transactions?page=1&pageSize=50 (PostgreSQL shard read). POST /api/v1/payroll/batches (payroll batch funding initiation). GET /api/v1/admin/aml-alerts (bank_admin role required). Internal: POST /internal/ofac/screen (wire-service -> ofac-service, entity_ids, returns ScreeningResult within 5s SLA). All public endpoints require Auth0 JWT; rate limits enforced by Cloud Armor; OFAC screening endpoint rate limited to 100 req/min.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 MFA (TOTP) mandatory for all roles; no fallback to password-only. next-auth v5 stores JWT in httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookie. Spring Security validates Auth0 JWT on every request; extracts business_account_id custom claim for data isolation. OFAC screening: both beneficiary and wire originator re-screened at wire submission using current SDN list in Redis; wire blocked on any match >= 0.85 score; MATCHED results auto-queue AML alert to bank_admin. Google Cloud Armor WAF: wire initiation rate limit 10 req/min per business_account_id, login 5 attempts per 5 minutes, DDoS protection on all ingress. Wire audit trail: wire_instructions table records initiator_user_id, cosignatory_user_id, wire_ofac_screening_id, fedwire_reference, and all state transition timestamps - immutable record satisfying BSA requirements. No account numbers or amounts in application logs; log wire_id and status only. Beneficial ownership: FinCEN CDD beneficial ownership verification via KYB integration at account opening (kyb_verified_at). Reg E: wire confirmation email sent to business_owner on FEDWIRE_ACKNOWLEDGED state transition.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CircleCI pipeline: mvn test (Spring Boot), jest/cypress (Next.js), docker build and GCR push, kubectl apply via Helm chart. Blue-green deployment for wire-service: new version deployed to green, 10% traffic shifted, health check (wire submission latency < 5s p95), then 100% flip. Rolling deploy for all other services. GKE Autopilot HPA: wire-service scales 5-20 replicas on CPU 60% (handles wire throughput spikes during business hours). CockroachDB connection pool: 20 connections per wire-service pod via HikariCP, max 200 total across 10 pods. PostgreSQL shard routing: Spring Boot DataSourceRouter selects shard by business_account_id.hashCode() % 16. Fedwire batch window: Fedwire closes at 18:00 ET; wire-service rejects new wire initiations at 17:30 ET with user-facing message.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed traces spanning Next.js -> account/wire/ofac services with wire_id as trace correlation key. Custom Datadog metrics: wire_submission_latency_ms (p95 alert > 25s), ofac_screening_latency_ms (p95 alert > 4s), wire_approval_409_conflict_rate (alert if > 1% of approvals), fedwire_acknowledgement_timeout_count (P1 alert on any timeout). PagerDuty P1 escalation: Fedwire submission failure, OFAC service unavailable, CockroachDB write latency > 500ms. Datadog dashboard: wire pipeline funnel (created -> approved -> ofac_clear -> submitted -> acknowledged), OFAC match rate trend, AML alert queue depth, balance cache hit rate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot: approximately $1,200/month for baseline, scales with wire throughput. CockroachDB Dedicated 3-region (9 nodes): approximately $4,500/month at 10k accounts; scale to 27 nodes at 500k accounts (~$13,500/month). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 shards with read replica: approximately $2,400/month. Redis Memorystore 3-node: approximately $300/month. Total at 10k accounts: approximately $8,500/month. At 500k accounts: approximately $25,000/month. Scaling gates: add CockroachDB nodes at 50k, 150k, 300k account milestones; add PostgreSQL shard pairs at 100k and 300k when p99 write latency exceeds 50ms.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Account dashboard with real-time balance and 30-transaction history, domestic wire initiation with optimistic-locked dual-approval state machine, OFAC re-screening at submission time, Fedwire submission with 30-second synchronous SLA, Auth0 MFA for all roles, beneficiary creation with routing validation and 24-hour cooling-off, Google Cloud Armor WAF, CircleCI CI/CD, blue-green wire-service deployment, GKE Autopilot us-central1. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Cash flow forecast (30-day rolling projection), payroll batch funding via ACH, international wire initiation form (SWIFT MT103 submission deferred), co-signatory management UI, account statement download, admin AML alert queue, us-east1 secondary region. Phase 3 (6 weeks): SWIFT MT103 submission, payroll provider API, AI cash flow anomaly detection, multi-currency accounts, FinCEN SAR filing placeholder.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java: package structure com.bank.sme.{account,wire,ofac,payroll,forecast,admin}; each service a separate Maven module. No raw SQL; all CockroachDB queries via Spring Data JPA with named queries. Wire state transitions only via WireStateMachine.transition() method; no direct status field updates outside state machine. CockroachDB serialization error (SQLSTATE 40001) handled by @Retryable(SERIALIZATION_FAILURE) aspect on wire-service command methods. Unit tests for WireStateMachine covering all state transitions and 409 Conflict scenario. Next.js: server components for all read-only views; client components only for interactive forms. No sensitive data (account numbers, amounts) in console.log or Datadog log attributes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Fedwire 30-second SLA exceeded under peak wire volume (end of business day). Mitigation: wire-service HPA pre-scales at 16:00 ET based on historical peak patterns; Java Virtual Threads (Java 21) for non-blocking Fedwire HTTP connections. Risk: CockroachDB serialization retries for wire_instructions on high concurrent approval attempts. Mitigation: optimistic lock reduces contention vs pessimistic lock; retry loop with exponential backoff for SQLSTATE 40001. Risk: OFAC SDN list Redis cache stale if daily refresh fails. Mitigation: ofac-service monitors last_refresh_at; if > 25 hours stale, circuit-breaker blocks all wire submissions with OFAC_SERVICE_STALE status and triggers P1 PagerDuty alert. Tradeoff: CockroachDB SERIALIZABLE isolation for wire_instructions over READ COMMITTED - higher latency but prevents phantom reads in dual-approval workflow. Tradeoff: synchronous OFAC screening in wire approval flow over async - adds latency but ensures OFAC result is available before Fedwire submission.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed OFAC re-screening at wire submission time is required by OFAC guidance; initial beneficiary screening is not sufficient. Confirmed CockroachDB optimistic locking (version column CAS) preferred over SELECT FOR UPDATE to avoid lock contention at scale. Confirmed 24-hour cooling-off for new beneficiaries before first wire use is an operational fraud control not a regulatory requirement; subject to business owner experience review.

metadata.notes: SME business banking portal Round 2 resolves OFAC re-screening gap (ISS-001) and wire dual-approval optimistic lock race condition (ISS-002).

profile.domain: Intraday Liquidity Management CLI

project_goal.value: CLI tool for payment operations engineers to stream real-time settlement positions and fire threshold alerts with breach deduplication
project_goal.rationale: Operations engineers need sub-second visibility into net positions without a browser dashboard

target_users.value: Payment operations engineers on bank trading floor
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Local terminal tool with no browser UI required

capabilities.rationale: WebSocket streaming, ClickHouse analytics, and PagerDuty integration

complexity_level.rationale: WebSocket streaming with TUI rendering is well-scoped

feature_scope.rationale: Core operations monitoring features

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for operations team pilot

security_baseline.rationale: Protecting intraday position data in transit

frontend_stack.rationale: CLI tool with no browser frontend

backend_stack.rationale: Go concurrency model ideal for simultaneous WebSocket stream and TUI rendering

data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse handles high-write breach logs with fast time-range queries for audit

hosting_target.rationale: CLI tool with no hosted runtime required

privacy_retention_policy.rationale: BCBS 248 intraday liquidity monitoring record-keeping requirements

future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items deferred after pilot

constraints.rationale: Workstation compatibility and responsiveness requirements

observability_baseline.rationale: Standard observability for internal tools

project.goal: Stream intraday settlement positions and alert on threshold breaches with cooldown deduplication
project.target_users: Payment operations engineers

backend.framework: Bubble Tea TUI + gorilla/websocket
backend.api: WebSocket consumer from intraday liquidity API

security.auth: Service account JWT with mTLS WebSocket connection
security.transport: mTLS for WebSocket, TLS 1.3 for PagerDuty webhook

devops.hosting: local binary on workstations via Homebrew

open_questions.multi_currency_display: How to handle FX conversion for unified net exposure view - deferred to Phase 3

reasoner_reviews.product: The threshold alerting and TUI dashboard address the core operations pain point: engineers currently switch between multiple web dashboards to infer net positions. Consolidating into a single terminal view with configurable currency thresholds is the right MVP focus. The breach cooldown deduplication in round 2 is critical for operational reliability.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go goroutine-per-concern architecture is correct here: WebSocket consumer, position aggregator, threshold evaluator, and Bubble Tea renderer each run in dedicated goroutines communicating via typed channels. The breach cooldown map as a sync.Map is the right fit - O(1) lookup per currency+correspondent+breach_type key without lock contention on the hot path.
reasoner_reviews.security: Service account JWTs issued per engineer allow audit attribution of every position view. mTLS on the WebSocket connection prevents MITM interception of live position data. Prometheus metrics endpoint must be bound to 127.0.0.1 to prevent cross-user reads on shared workstations.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Binary size of 25MB is achievable with CGO_ENABLED=0 static compilation and ldflags -s -w. The 50ms P99 TUI refresh target is met by the 500ms Bubble Tea tick cycle with goroutine-based update propagation, not blocking I/O in the render path.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 must ensure breach cooldown TTL is configurable per threshold rule, not just as a global flag. A single global 15-min cooldown may be too long for high-velocity USD/EUR corridors and too short for rarely-breached exotic currency pairs.

backend.service_design: Four goroutines: (1) WebSocket consumer subscribing to liquidity API position stream; (2) position aggregator maintaining sync.Map of net positions by currency+correspondent; (3) threshold evaluator polling every 500ms, checking breach cooldown map before alerting; (4) Bubble Tea model updater sending typed messages to TUI program via channel. No HTTP server, no hosted component.
backend.api_patterns: Commands: `settle watch --currency USD,EUR --threshold-file ~/.settle/thresholds.yaml` starts live dashboard. `settle breaches --from 2026-03-01 --currency EUR` queries ClickHouse. `settle config set --currency USD --debit-limit 500000000 --breach-cooldown 10` writes threshold config to Redis and YAML. `settle auth login` issues JWT via browser OAuth flow to keychain.
backend.failure_handling: WebSocket disconnects trigger exponential backoff reconnect (100ms, 200ms, 400ms, cap 30s) with on-screen 'Reconnecting...' status in TUI. If ClickHouse write fails, breach events queue in a bounded in-memory channel (cap 1000) and retry every 5s; TUI shows 'Breach log degraded' warning. Breach cooldown map persists across reconnects, preventing duplicate alerts on replayed position events.

security.auth_design: Service account JWT issued by internal identity service via OAuth 2.0 device flow; stored in OS keychain (go-keychain on macOS, libsecret on Linux), never in plain files or env vars. JWT auto-refreshed by background goroutine 5 minutes before expiry.
security.secrets_management: Threshold YAML at ~/.settle/thresholds.yaml with 0600 permissions stores only limit values, no credentials. PagerDuty routing key stored in OS keychain. mTLS certificate bundle embedded in binary, rotated every 90 days via CI pipeline re-release.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every breach event written to ClickHouse breach_events table with engineer_id (from JWT sub claim), breach timestamp, currency, correspondent_id, position_value, threshold_value, breach_type, cooldown_suppressed flag, and cli_version. Compliance team queries ClickHouse for BCBS 248 examination evidence.

data.entities: settlement_positions (in-memory sync.Map: currency, correspondent_id, net_debit_cents, net_credit_cents, threshold_utilization_pct, last_updated_ms), threshold_configs (Redis hash + YAML: currency, correspondent_id, debit_limit_cents, credit_limit_cents, alert_channel, breach_cooldown_minutes), breach_events (ClickHouse partitioned by month: id UUID, engineer_id, currency, correspondent_id, breach_type, position_value_cents, threshold_value_cents, cooldown_suppressed UInt8, logged_at DateTime64)
data.storage_design: In-process position state held in sync.Map; threshold configs read from Redis on startup and refreshed every 60s. Breach events appended to ClickHouse via buffered channel writer (batch size 50, flush timer 1s). No position data persisted to local disk.
data.analytics_events: Prometheus metrics: settle_breach_total{currency,breach_type} counter, settle_position_net_debit_cents{currency,correspondent} gauge, settle_cooldown_suppressions_total{currency} counter, settle_ws_reconnect_total counter, settle_ws_message_lag_ms histogram, settle_clickhouse_write_errors_total counter.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix builds linux/amd64 and darwin/arm64 binaries with CGO_ENABLED=0 and ldflags -s -w. Binary size gate fails build if over 25MB. Code-signed with internal Apple Developer cert. Binaries uploaded to Artifactory on tagged release. Engineers install via internal Homebrew tap or apt package.
devops.ci_cd_design: On PR: go vet, golangci-lint (errcheck, staticcheck, govet), go test -race. On merge to main: integration tests against mock WebSocket server. On tag: cross-compile, sign, upload to Artifactory, update Homebrew tap formula.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous binary pinned in internal Homebrew tap; rollback via `brew install settle-cli@<prev_version>`. No server-side rollback needed. Mock WebSocket server used in dev environment for safe testing.

ISS-001.title: Threshold configs lost on CLI restart
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 stored threshold configs only in memory; operators must re-enter all thresholds after each restart, creating risk of missed alerts.

history.note: Raised by critic reviewer - no persistence layer for threshold configs

ISS-002.title: No breach event deduplication on WebSocket reconnect
ISS-002.detail: When WebSocket reconnects, replayed position events re-trigger threshold evaluations for already-alerted breaches, flooding PagerDuty with duplicate pages.

history.note: Identified during architect review - no cooldown mechanism in threshold evaluator

focus_issues.title: No breach event deduplication on WebSocket reconnect
focus_issues.detail: When WebSocket reconnects, replayed position events re-trigger threshold evaluations for already-alerted breaches, flooding PagerDuty with duplicate pages.

latest_recommendations: Add breach cooldown map keyed by currency+correspondent+breach_type to suppress repeated alerts within configurable TTL
Make cooldown TTL configurable per threshold rule in YAML schema, not just a global flag

previous_audits.summary: Solid Go concurrency architecture and strong ClickHouse audit logging. ISS-001 threshold persistence was a major gap. ISS-002 breach deduplication remains unresolved and will cause alert fatigue in production.

ClickHouse breach event schema designed well for BCBS 248 audit queries
Per-engineer JWT provides regulatory attribution

Prometheus metrics on localhost:9091 needs localhost-only binding on shared workstations

Bind Prometheus endpoint to 127.0.0.1 to prevent cross-user reads
