previous_plan.title: Settlement Position Monitor CLI - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 CLI using Bubble Tea TUI to stream intraday settlement positions via WebSocket. Threshold alerts fire via PagerDuty webhook. Breach events logged to ClickHouse for BCBS 248 compliance. Threshold configs stored in memory only (ISS-001 gap).
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single Go binary: WebSocket consumer goroutine, in-memory position aggregator (sync.Map), threshold evaluator (no cooldown map in round 1), Bubble Tea TUI renderer. Breach events written to ClickHouse.
previous_plan.workflows: WebSocket consumer receives position delta events, updates sync.Map of net positions. Threshold evaluator compares against in-memory configs (not persisted). On breach, fires PagerDuty webhook directly with no deduplication - reconnect storms cause duplicate pages.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS WebSocket connection, per-engineer service account JWT stored in OS keychain. Breach events logged with engineer_id to ClickHouse. BCBS 248 record-keeping. Prometheus metrics on localhost:9091 (no address binding restriction).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 key fix: ISS-002 breach deduplication. Adding sync.Map cooldown map keyed by currency+correspondent+breach_type with per-rule configurable TTL (default 15 min). Threshold evaluator checks cooldown before alerting; cooldown persists across WebSocket reconnects preventing duplicate PagerDuty pages on replayed events. Also binding Prometheus to 127.0.0.1. ISS-001 (threshold persistence) already resolved via Redis+YAML. CLI tool - no FrontendAgent subplan, no browser components.

fix_report.action_taken: Added breach cooldown map (sync.Map keyed by currency+correspondent+breach_type string) to threshold evaluator goroutine. Before firing PagerDuty webhook, evaluator checks if cooldown entry exists and has not expired. Per-rule cooldown_minutes field added to threshold YAML schema. Cooldown map survives WebSocket reconnects so replayed position events do not re-trigger resolved breaches. Suppressed events still written to ClickHouse with cooldown_suppressed=1 for audit.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero duplicate PagerDuty alerts on WebSocket reconnect; alert fatigue eliminated; suppressed breach events auditable in ClickHouse

target_output.title: Settlement Position Monitor CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 CLI for payment operations engineers that streams intraday net settlement positions from the bank's liquidity API via WebSocket, renders a live Bubble Tea TUI dashboard showing net debit/credit positions by currency pair and correspondent refreshed every 500ms, and fires threshold alerts with per-rule breach cooldown deduplication. Breach events are immutably logged to ClickHouse for Basel III BCBS 248 audit trail. Threshold configs persist across restarts via Redis and local YAML fallback.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single statically compiled Go binary with four concurrent goroutines communicating via typed channels: a WebSocket consumer goroutine subscribing to the intraday liquidity API position stream with mTLS; an in-process position aggregator maintaining a sync.Map of SettlementPosition structs by currency+correspondent key; a threshold evaluator goroutine polling every 500ms that compares net positions against Redis-cached threshold configs and checks a breach cooldown sync.Map before firing PagerDuty webhooks; and a Bubble Tea TUI renderer updating the position grid at 500ms intervals with delta-color highlighting. Breach events flow through a buffered channel to a ClickHouse batch writer. No HTTP server, no hosted deployment, no browser component.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22, statically compiled (CGO_ENABLED=0, ldflags -s -w). TUI: Bubble Tea 0.25 with lipgloss styling for color coding. WebSocket: gorilla/websocket 1.5 with mTLS dialer. CLI flags: cobra + pflag. Config: Viper 1.18 reading ~/.settle/thresholds.yaml. Auth: golang-jwt/jwt stored via go-keychain (macOS) / libsecret (Linux). Breach log: ClickHouse Go driver v2. Threshold cache: go-redis v9 against Redis 7. Metrics: Prometheus client_golang bound to 127.0.0.1:9091.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Position Dashboard: Bubble Tea table showing currency pair, correspondent name, net debit position (cents), net credit position, threshold utilization %, and movement delta indicator updated every 500ms. Threshold Alert Engine: evaluator goroutine fires terminal bell + red status row + PagerDuty Events API v2 call on breach, suppressed per per-rule cooldown TTL. Breach cooldown_suppressed=1 events still written to ClickHouse. Config Commands: `settle config set` writes threshold YAML and pushes to Redis; `settle config show` displays active thresholds with cooldown TTLs. Breach History: `settle breaches --from --to --currency` queries ClickHouse and renders tabular results.
target_output.system_components: WebSocket Consumer Goroutine (gorilla/websocket mTLS dialer, exponential backoff reconnect 100ms-30s cap, emits PositionDelta messages to aggregator channel). Position Aggregator (sync.Map[string]SettlementPosition, updated on each delta). Threshold Evaluator Goroutine (500ms tick, reads threshold configs from Redis, checks breach cooldown sync.Map[string]time.Time before alerting, writes to ClickHouse channel). Breach Cooldown Map (sync.Map keyed by currency+correspondent+breach_type, TTL per threshold rule, consulted before every PagerDuty call). ClickHouse Writer (buffered channel cap 1000, batch writes 50 rows or 1s flush). Prometheus Endpoint (127.0.0.1:9091, not 0.0.0.0).
target_output.workflows: Live Monitoring: Operator runs `settle watch --currency USD,EUR`; CLI loads threshold YAML, reads JWT from keychain, dials WebSocket with mTLS. Consumer goroutine streams PositionDelta events; aggregator updates net positions. Threshold evaluator polls every 500ms: for each currency+correspondent pair, if net debit exceeds debit_limit, evaluator checks cooldown map - if entry absent or expired, writes breach event to ClickHouse channel with cooldown_suppressed=0, fires PagerDuty webhook, renders TUI breach row in red, sets cooldown map entry with per-rule TTL. If cooldown active, writes to ClickHouse with cooldown_suppressed=1, no PagerDuty call. Reconnect Handling: on disconnect, consumer enters exponential backoff; TUI shows Reconnecting status. On reconnect, replayed position events pass through evaluator; cooldown map prevents re-alerting already-known breaches. Breach Query: operator runs `settle breaches --from 2026-03-20 --currency EUR`; CLI queries ClickHouse breach_events table and renders results to stdout.
target_output.data_model: settlement_positions (in-process sync.Map: key=string(currency+correspondent_id), value=SettlementPosition{currency string, correspondent_id string, correspondent_name string, net_debit_cents int64, net_credit_cents int64, threshold_utilization_pct float64, last_updated_ms int64}). threshold_configs (Redis hash + YAML: currency string, correspondent_id string, debit_limit_cents int64, credit_limit_cents int64, alert_channel string, breach_cooldown_minutes int default 15). breach_events (ClickHouse partitioned toYYYYMM(logged_at): id UUID, engineer_id String, cli_version String, currency LowCardinality(String), correspondent_id String, breach_type Enum8('debit'=1,'credit'=2), position_value_cents Int64, threshold_value_cents Int64, cooldown_suppressed UInt8, logged_at DateTime64(3)).
target_output.api_design: External API consumed: WebSocket wss://liquidity-api.internal/v2/positions/stream - server pushes PositionDelta JSON {currency, correspondent_id, net_debit_cents, net_credit_cents, sequence_number, event_time_ms}. CLI sends heartbeat ping every 10s. PagerDuty Events API v2: POST https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue with {routing_key, event_action:'trigger', payload:{summary, source:'settle-cli', severity:'error', custom_details:{currency, correspondent, position_value, threshold_value, breach_type}}}. CLI commands: `settle watch [--currency] [--threshold-file]`, `settle breaches [--from] [--to] [--currency]`, `settle config set [flags]`, `settle config show`, `settle auth login`, `settle version`.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS: WebSocket dialer uses client cert bundle embedded in binary, rotated every 90 days via CI pipeline re-release. JWT: per-engineer service account token issued by internal identity service, stored in OS keychain (go-keychain/libsecret), refreshed 5 min before expiry by background goroutine. Prometheus metrics bound to 127.0.0.1:9091 to prevent cross-user reads on shared workstations. No position data written to local disk in cleartext. Threshold YAML stores only limit values, no credentials. Breach events written with engineer_id for BCBS 248 regulatory attribution. Cooldown-suppressed events logged in ClickHouse for audit completeness.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Build: GitHub Actions matrix builds linux/amd64 and darwin/arm64 with CGO_ENABLED=0. Binary size gate: CI fails if binary exceeds 25MB (checked with `ls -l`). Code-signed with internal Apple Developer cert for macOS notarization. Tagged releases upload to internal Artifactory repository. Distribution: internal Homebrew tap for macOS (`brew install settle-cli`); apt package for Ubuntu workstations. Dev environment uses `settle watch --mock` flag pointing to local mock WebSocket server (Go httptest). Rollback: `brew install settle-cli@<prev_version>` or `apt install settle-cli=<prev_version>`. No server-side deployment or rollback needed.
target_output.observability: Prometheus on 127.0.0.1:9091/metrics: settle_breach_total{currency,breach_type} counter, settle_cooldown_suppressions_total{currency} counter, settle_position_net_debit_cents{currency,correspondent} gauge, settle_ws_reconnect_total counter, settle_ws_message_lag_ms histogram (buckets 1,5,10,50,100,500ms), settle_clickhouse_write_errors_total counter. Structured JSON logs to ~/.settle/logs/settle.log: each breach event, cooldown suppression, reconnect, JWT refresh, and config reload logged with timestamp, correlation_id, and engineer_id. Log rotation daily, 30-day local retention.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No hosting cost - CLI runs on existing workstation hardware. ClickHouse is shared infrastructure; breach events volume under 500 rows per engineer per day, negligible cluster impact. Redis used for threshold config hot-read only from local CLI, no sustained connection pool. Binary distribution via Artifactory has no per-download cost. Total incremental infrastructure cost is near zero.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): WebSocket consumer goroutine, in-process position aggregator, Bubble Tea TUI with currency/correspondent grid, JWT auth via keychain, mTLS WebSocket dialer, threshold YAML plus Redis persistence, breach event logging to ClickHouse, CI binary build and Homebrew tap, Prometheus metrics on 127.0.0.1. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Breach cooldown deduplication map with per-rule TTL (ISS-002 fix), PagerDuty Events API v2 integration, `settle breaches` ClickHouse query command, `settle config set` Redis write command. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Multi-currency FX conversion for unified net exposure view, session replay mode for post-incident analysis, SWIFT gpi tracker integration for cross-border position attribution.
target_output.development_guidelines: All commands implemented as Cobra subcommands with `--help` flag validation before any network calls. Goroutines never share mutable state without sync.Map or buffered channel - no global variables with mutex. Unit tests cover threshold evaluator cooldown logic with table-driven cases (breach fired, cooldown suppressed, expired cooldown re-fires). Integration tests use Go httptest mock WebSocket server. golangci-lint enforced: errcheck, staticcheck, govet. Binary size gate in CI. All breach cooldown TTL values must be positive integers; invalid YAML values fail fast at startup with clear error message.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse unavailability causes breach event loss. Mitigation: bounded in-memory queue (cap 1000 events) with 5s retry; TUI shows 'Breach log degraded' warning; events lost only if queue fills during extended outage. Risk: JWT expiry mid-session causes WebSocket auth rejection. Mitigation: background goroutine refreshes JWT 5 min before expiry with three retries before alerting operator. Tradeoff: per-rule cooldown TTL may suppress a genuine second breach of the same corridor. Accepted: all suppressed events written to ClickHouse with cooldown_suppressed=1 for audit review. Tradeoff: mTLS cert embedded in binary requires 90-day re-release cadence. Mitigation: CI automation handles cert rotation and binary re-release with no manual steps.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Multi-currency FX conversion for unified net exposure view deferred to Phase 3 pending treasury confirming reference rate source. PagerDuty routing key: single key with breach metadata in payload, ops team uses PagerDuty service rules to route by currency. ClickHouse credentials: IAM role-based access using engineer workstation identity, no shared service credentials.

metadata.notes: Round 2 Go CLI for intraday settlement position monitoring. ISS-002 breach cooldown deduplication resolved. No FrontendAgent subplan - cli_tool project class.

profile.domain: Retail Banking ML Infrastructure

project_goal.value: Shared ML infrastructure platform for credit risk, fraud detection, and AML model development and deployment at a retail bank, providing GPU orchestration, model registry, feature store, and experiment tracking as managed services for internal data science teams.
project_goal.rationale: Data science teams duplicating infrastructure effort across model domains; centralizing reduces time-to-production from 12 weeks to 3 weeks.

target_users.value: Internal data scientists, ML engineers, and model risk managers across credit, fraud, and AML teams.
target_users.rationale: Three primary consumer groups with distinct access patterns: developers submitting pipelines, risk managers reviewing model governance, ops monitoring serving endpoints.

project_class.rationale: Shared compute and storage infrastructure, not an end-user application.

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline orchestration, feature store, model registry, GPU batch training, and analytics dashboards cover the full capability surface.

complexity_level.rationale: GPU cluster management, multi-tenant isolation, feature store serving at sub-10ms latency, and model governance workflows exceed advanced complexity.

risk_level.rationale: Models on this platform directly influence credit decisions and fraud blocking; platform failures cascade to production model serving.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Feature store holds PII-adjacent customer transaction features and credit bureau data used in model training.

external_exposure.rationale: Platform accessed exclusively via internal VPN and Google IAP; no public endpoints.

access_model.value: Google IAP with Workload Identity for GKE service accounts; data scientist roles via IAP-protected Kubeflow UI; model risk team access via governance portal with MFA enforced.
access_model.rationale: Workload Identity eliminates long-lived service account keys; IAP provides identity-aware perimeter.

feature_scope.value: GPU cluster provisioning with quota management, Kubeflow Pipeline orchestration, MLflow experiment tracking, Feast feature store on Redis and Snowflake, model registry with lineage tracking, Vertex AI endpoint deployment with traffic splitting, governance portal for model risk approval workflows, cost attribution dashboards per team.
feature_scope.rationale: Full platform scope aligned with reducing data science toil across all three model domains.

mvp_scope.value: Kubeflow pipeline submission with GPU scheduling, MLflow experiment tracking, model registry with basic lineage, Vertex AI deployment for one credit risk model, IAP-based access control.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates end-to-end pipeline for one domain before onboarding fraud and AML teams.

security_baseline.value: Google IAP for all web interfaces, Workload Identity for GKE pods, CMEK encryption for Snowflake and GCS training data, network policies isolating team namespaces in GKE, audit logging of all model registry operations and data access to Cloud Audit Logs.
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data sensitivity and SR 11-7 model governance requirements mandate comprehensive access and audit controls.

frontend_stack.value: No customer-facing frontend. Internal Kubeflow Pipelines UI and MLflow UI served via IAP. Governance portal built with Streamlit for model risk reviewers.
frontend_stack.rationale: Internal tooling UIs are sufficient; no investment in custom frontend justified for MVP.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with KFP (Kubeflow Pipelines SDK) for pipeline definitions, FastAPI for internal platform API, Argo Workflows for pipeline execution, Vertex AI for managed serving endpoints.
backend_stack.rationale: KFP and Argo are the GKE-native pipeline orchestration stack; FastAPI exposes platform management APIs consumed by CLI and governance portal.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for platform metadata, Feast feature store with Redis 7 for online serving and Snowflake for offline training data, GCS for model artifact storage.
data_platform.rationale: Feast with Redis handles sub-10ms online feature retrieval; Snowflake provides governed access to historical transaction data for training.

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot for pipeline workloads and platform services, Vertex AI for model serving endpoints, Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL, GCS for artifacts.
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot removes node pool management overhead; Vertex AI provides managed GPU serving with autoscaling.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Training dataset snapshots retained 3 years for model reproducibility; model artifacts retained 7 years per SOX requirement; pipeline run logs retained 1 year; experiment metadata retained for model lifetime.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SR 11-7 requires model lifecycle documentation; SOX mandates multi-year retention for models influencing financial reporting.

future_scope.value: Real-time feature pipelines via Pub/Sub, automated model retraining triggers on data drift detection, external model validation partner access via private VPC peering.
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 priorities after MVP onboards all three model domains.

constraints.value: All compute must remain in GCP us-central1 for data residency; no training data may leave GCP tenant boundary; GPU budget capped at $80K/month; existing Snowflake contract must be reused.
constraints.rationale: Data residency required for FFIEC compliance; budget set by CFO approval.

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for GPU utilization, pipeline queue depth, and feature store latency; Prometheus metrics from Kubeflow and Argo; MLflow tracking for model metrics; PagerDuty alerts for GPU quota exhaustion, Vertex endpoint error rate >1%, and pipeline failure spikes.
observability_baseline.rationale: GPU cost and pipeline reliability are the two highest operational concerns.

execution_preference.value: Platform Engineering team owns infrastructure; model teams consume via self-service CLI and APIs; 2-week sprint cadence with quarterly platform capability reviews.
execution_preference.rationale: Self-service model reduces platform team bottleneck; sprint cadence aligns with bank engineering standard.

compliance_context.value: SR 11-7 model risk management guidelines requiring pre-deployment model validation, ongoing monitoring, and model inventory; SOX IT general controls for models used in net interest margin and provision calculations; internal model governance framework with mandatory risk team sign-off before production promotion.
compliance_context.rationale: Regulatory and internal governance requirements mandate governance portal integration and full audit trail for all model lifecycle events.

project.goal: Provide shared ML infrastructure for credit risk, fraud, and AML model teams to reduce time-to-production and standardize governance
project.target_users: Data scientists, ML engineers, model risk managers
project.timeline: MVP in Q2, full platform GA in Q3

backend.framework: KFP SDK, FastAPI for platform API, Argo Workflows for execution
backend.api: Internal REST API for pipeline submission, model registry, and governance actions

security.auth: Google IAP + Workload Identity for all service access
security.encryption: CMEK for Snowflake and GCS, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.compliance: SR 11-7, SOX IT general controls, internal model governance framework

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL for metadata
data.artifact_store: GCS with versioned buckets per model domain

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for platform code, Argo CD for GitOps deployment
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with namespace isolation

constraints.data_residency: No training data outside GCP tenant

open_questions.drift_detection: Threshold calibration for automated retraining triggers deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform addresses a genuine bottleneck: data science teams spending 60-70% of effort on infrastructure rather than model development. The MVP scope is correctly bounded to one model domain. Feature store with online and offline paths is a non-negotiable requirement for fraud and credit risk models. Governance portal for model risk sign-off is essential before any model touches production credit decisions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: KFP on GKE Autopilot with Argo Workflows for execution is the right stack for this scale. The split between Feast online (Redis) and offline (Snowflake) is correct for latency requirements. Key risk in the prior plan was insufficient namespace isolation between credit, fraud, and AML teams — shared GPU nodes with lax network policies could allow cross-team feature data leakage. Vertex AI for serving eliminates custom inference server management. The model registry must store training data version SHA256 hashes for full lineage reproducibility.
reasoner_reviews.security: SR 11-7 compliance requires that the governance portal captures model risk team sign-off with immutable audit records. The prior plan lacked explicit immutability controls on the audit log — Cloud Audit Logs are append-only by design but the PostgreSQL audit table needed a BEFORE DELETE trigger. Workload Identity is correctly specified; CMEK scoping to model-domain-level GCS buckets must be explicit. Network policies must prevent pods in the fraud namespace from querying feature definitions owned by the credit namespace via Feast.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GPU budget of $80K/month is tight for three model domains with concurrent training runs. Priority queuing in Argo Workflows must favor production retraining over experimental runs. Cost attribution per team via GCP labels is required for charge-back. Snowflake reuse eliminates offline feature store cost but the Feast Snowflake connector must be validated against the bank's enterprise account tier.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan's phasing underestimated Feast deployment complexity. A single Feast deployment with shared Redis raises cross-team key collision risks if feature namespacing is not enforced at the Feast project level. The Streamlit governance portal is acceptable for MVP but will not scale to 50+ concurrent model reviewers; this must be documented as a Phase 2 replacement candidate in risks.

backend.service_design: FastAPI platform API service exposing /pipelines, /models, /features, /governance, /costs endpoints. KFP SDK pipeline compiler runs as a validation step before Argo submission. Separate metadata service handles model registry writes and lineage graph construction using PostgreSQL with adjacency list for version dependencies.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful internal API with OpenAPI 3.0. Pipeline submission via POST /pipelines/runs with KFP pipeline YAML payload. Model registry via POST /models with artifact GCS URI and training data version hash. Governance actions via PATCH /models/{id}/status with approver identity from IAP header.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly cost attribution job aggregates GPU node usage by GCP label and writes charge-back records to PostgreSQL. Weekly model drift check job pulls prediction logs from BigQuery and computes PSI against training distribution, publishing results to MLflow experiment tracker.
backend.failure_handling: Argo Workflows retries failed pipeline steps up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Vertex AI endpoint failures trigger automatic traffic rollback to previous model version. FastAPI platform API returns structured error responses with pipeline run IDs for debugging.

security.auth_design: Google IAP authenticates all web interface access (Kubeflow UI, MLflow UI, governance portal). GKE Workload Identity binds each namespace service account to a dedicated GCP service account with least-privilege IAM roles. Data scientists authenticate to FastAPI CLI via gcloud application-default credentials.
security.authorization_model: Namespace-level RBAC in GKE: credit, fraud, and AML namespaces each have dedicated service accounts. Feast project-level isolation prevents cross-domain feature access. IAM conditions on GCS buckets restrict model artifact reads to the owning team service account. Governance portal restricts PATCH /models/{id}/status to model_risk_reviewer IAM group.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs capture all GCS object reads/writes, Vertex AI endpoint creation, and IAM policy changes. PostgreSQL model_audit_log uses a BEFORE DELETE trigger to raise exception, enforcing append-only semantics. DELETE and UPDATE privileges revoked from platform_api_role. MLflow experiment deletion disabled via server configuration flag.
security.security_risks: Cross-namespace Feast feature key collision if namespacing not enforced at Feast project level. CMEK key rotation disrupting active training jobs if rotation window overlaps with GCS reads. Jupyter notebook egress risk — network policy required to block pod egress except to approved GCP endpoints.

data.entities: model_registry_entries (id, name, version, domain ENUM credit/fraud/aml, artifact_gcs_uri, training_data_version_hash, status ENUM candidate/validated/production/retired, registered_by, registered_at). feature_definitions (id, feast_project CHECK IN bank-credit/bank-fraud/bank-aml, feature_name, entity_type, dtype, source_snowflake_table, owner_team, created_at). pipeline_runs (id, domain, pipeline_name, submitter, argo_workflow_id, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed, gpu_count, started_at, completed_at, mlflow_experiment_id). gpu_cluster_jobs (id, pipeline_run_id, gpu_type, gpu_count, cost_usd, gcp_labels JSONB). model_audit_log (id, model_id, event_type ENUM registered/validated/promoted/retired, actor_email, actor_role, governance_comment, timestamp) — append-only via BEFORE DELETE trigger.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL for all platform metadata with PgBouncer pooling. GCS versioned buckets per domain (gs://bank-ml-credit/, gs://bank-ml-fraud/, gs://bank-ml-aml/) with CMEK per bucket. Feast online store on Memorystore Redis 7 Cluster with key prefix isolation per Feast project. Feast offline store on Snowflake with dedicated ml_credit, ml_fraud, ml_aml schemas.
data.data_risks: Training data snapshot growth in GCS; implement lifecycle policy to archive snapshots older than 90 days to Nearline. Redis memory exhaustion if feature store cardinality grows beyond capacity estimate; set maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru and alert at 75% utilization.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 with four namespaces: platform (Argo controller, Feast server, FastAPI, MLflow), credit, fraud, aml. Vertex AI endpoints in us-central1. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with private IP only. Memorystore Redis in same VPC. Artifact Registry for pipeline component images.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type-check, pytest for pipeline components, Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Argo CD sync trigger. Model promotion pipeline separate from platform CI: requires passing validation suite and governance portal approval before Argo CD deploys updated Vertex AI endpoint configuration.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Argo Workflows, Kubeflow metadata server, and FastAPI metrics. Grafana dashboards: GPU utilization heatmap by namespace, pipeline queue depth, Feast online latency p99, Vertex endpoint error rate. MLflow tracks per-experiment AUC, F1, PSI drift scores. PagerDuty: GPU quota >90%, Vertex error rate >1%, pipeline failure rate >20% in 1h.
devops.devops_risks: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency (60-90s) may delay urgent fraud model retraining; mitigate with pre-warmed reserved node pool for production retraining priority class. Argo CD sync conflicts if multiple teams push pipeline definitions simultaneously; enforce branch protection and team-scoped directories in infrastructure repo.

ISS-001.title: Feast feature namespace collision risk between credit and fraud teams
ISS-001.detail: Prior plan used a single shared Feast deployment without explicit project-level isolation. Credit and fraud teams registering features with the same entity type (customer_id) to the same Redis keyspace risk overwriting each other's online feature values, corrupting model inputs at serving time.

history.note: Identified in architect_reasoner and security reviews; plan mentioned Feast but did not specify project-level namespace enforcement or Redis key prefix strategy.

ISS-002.title: Missing immutability controls on PostgreSQL model audit log
ISS-002.detail: The model_audit_log table in PostgreSQL can be deleted or updated by any service account with write access to the Cloud SQL instance, undermining SR 11-7 audit trail requirements. Cloud Audit Logs capture GCP-level events but do not replace application-level immutability on the model lifecycle table.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer; prior plan did not include BEFORE DELETE trigger or privilege revocation on audit table.

ISS-003.title: GPU cost attribution lacks per-team charge-back mechanism
ISS-003.detail: Original plan did not define how GPU node costs would be attributed across credit, fraud, and AML teams. Without GCP label enforcement and nightly aggregation jobs, cost reporting would be manual and inaccurate.

history.note: Raised in constraints review.
Backend subplan now includes nightly cost attribution job with GCP label aggregation writing charge-back records to PostgreSQL.

focus_issues.title: Feast feature namespace collision risk between credit and fraud teams
focus_issues.detail: Prior plan used a single shared Feast deployment without explicit project-level isolation, risking Redis key collisions across model domains.

focus_issues.title: Missing immutability controls on PostgreSQL model audit log
focus_issues.detail: model_audit_log table lacks BEFORE DELETE trigger and privilege revocation, undermining SR 11-7 tamper-evident audit trail requirement.

latest_recommendations: Enforce Feast project-level isolation with one Feast project per model domain (bank-credit, bank-fraud, bank-aml) and separate Redis key prefixes per project.
Add BEFORE DELETE trigger on model_audit_log table and revoke DELETE privilege from all application service accounts.
Include GKE NetworkPolicy manifests blocking cross-namespace Feast queries.
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Retail Bank ML Infrastructure Platform v1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved; score 7.1 below passing threshold of 8.0.

previous_audits.summary: Solid infrastructure foundation with correct technology choices, but two high-severity gaps in Feast isolation and audit log immutability block SR 11-7 compliance sign-off. GPU cost attribution addressed in specialist plans but not surfaced in main plan sections.
strengths: Correct selection of GKE Autopilot with Argo Workflows for pipeline orchestration at this scale.
Feast with Redis/Snowflake split correctly addresses online vs offline latency requirements.
Vertex AI serving with traffic splitting appropriate for canary deployment of credit risk models.
Workload Identity specification eliminates long-lived key risks.
concerns: Feast deployment does not specify project-level isolation; shared Redis keyspace will cause key collisions when credit and fraud teams register same-entity features.
model_audit_log in PostgreSQL has no immutability guarantee; SR 11-7 requires tamper-evident audit records.
Streamlit governance portal scalability risk not acknowledged in risks section.
blocking_issues: Feast namespace isolation must be specified before platform can onboard multiple model teams concurrently.
Audit log immutability controls required for SR 11-7 compliance sign-off.
recommendations: Define one Feast project per model domain with Redis key prefix isolation enforced at Feast configuration level.
Add BEFORE DELETE trigger on model_audit_log and revoke DELETE/UPDATE on id and timestamp columns from application roles.
Add NetworkPolicy manifest blocking cross-namespace GKE pod communication to Feast server.
Acknowledge Streamlit governance portal replacement as Phase 3 item in risks section.

issue_updates.title: Feast feature namespace collision risk
issue_updates.detail: Plan mentioned Feast but did not specify project-level namespacing or Redis key prefix strategy.

issue_updates.title: Missing audit log immutability controls
issue_updates.detail: model_audit_log table had no trigger or privilege controls preventing deletion of audit records.

previous_plan.title: Retail Bank ML Infrastructure Platform v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A shared ML infrastructure platform on GCP providing Kubeflow Pipelines orchestration, Feast feature store, MLflow experiment tracking, model registry, and Vertex AI serving for credit risk, fraud, and AML model teams. Platform eliminates per-team infrastructure toil and establishes a governed path from experiment to production model deployment.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster hosts Kubeflow Pipelines, Argo Workflows, and FastAPI platform API. Feast connects to Redis for online serving and Snowflake for offline training data access. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL stores pipeline metadata and model registry. Vertex AI hosts production model serving endpoints. All access controlled via Google IAP and Workload Identity.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Google IAP protects all web interfaces. Workload Identity assigned per namespace service account. CMEK encryption on GCS artifact buckets and Snowflake. Cloud Audit Logs capture GCP-level events. SR 11-7 governance portal built with Streamlit for model risk sign-off. Audit log stored in PostgreSQL model_audit_log table without immutability controls.
previous_plan.data_model: model_registry_entries, feature_definitions, pipeline_runs, gpu_cluster_jobs. No explicit immutability controls on audit tables. Feast deployed as single instance serving all teams from shared Redis cluster without project-level key isolation.

target_output.thinking_summary: This round-2 revision must resolve two high-severity issues: Feast project-level namespace isolation to prevent Redis key collisions across credit, fraud, and AML teams, and PostgreSQL audit log immutability via BEFORE DELETE trigger and privilege revocation. The Snowflake-backed offline store and Vertex AI serving architecture from round 1 is sound and retained. SR 11-7 compliance requires both fixes before the platform can be signed off by model risk management. The security section explicitly addresses both issues, the data model adds trigger definitions and privilege revocations, and Streamlit scalability is documented as a risk.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined three separate Feast projects (bank-credit, bank-fraud, bank-aml) each with a dedicated Redis key prefix (credit:, fraud:, aml:) enforced in Feast feature_store.yaml. Added GKE NetworkPolicy blocking pods in one team namespace from reaching Feast server pod in platform namespace via other team ports. Updated data_model to add feast_project CHECK constraint and security_and_compliance to describe per-project isolation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-team Redis key collisions eliminated; credit and fraud teams can register customer_id-based features independently without online feature value overwrite risk.

fix_report.action_taken: Added BEFORE DELETE trigger on model_audit_log that raises exception unconditionally. Revoked DELETE and UPDATE privileges on model_audit_log from platform_api_role. Added row-level security policy restricting INSERT to platform_api_role only. Documented these controls explicitly in security_and_compliance and data_model sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: model_audit_log is append-only at the database level, satisfying SR 11-7 tamper-evident audit trail requirement and unblocking compliance sign-off.

target_output.title: Retail Bank ML Infrastructure Platform — Governed Multi-Tenant Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale shared ML infrastructure platform on GCP for a retail bank's credit risk, fraud detection, and AML model teams, built on GKE Autopilot with Kubeflow Pipelines and Argo Workflows orchestration and Vertex AI serving. The platform provides multi-tenant isolation via GKE namespace RBAC and Feast project-level separation across three model domains, a tamper-evident model audit log enforced at the PostgreSQL trigger layer, and an SR 11-7-compliant governance portal for model risk sign-off before any model reaches production decision systems. This revision resolves the Feast namespace collision and audit log immutability gaps from round 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 partitioned into four namespaces: platform (Argo Workflows controller, FastAPI platform API, Feast serving pods, MLflow server), credit, fraud, and aml. Each team namespace runs pipeline executor pods bound to domain-scoped Workload Identity service accounts. Feast serves online features from three isolated Redis key prefixes (credit:, fraud:, aml:) within a shared Memorystore Redis 7 Cluster, with each Feast project enforcing prefix isolation via feature_store.yaml. Snowflake provides offline feature retrieval via dedicated ml_credit, ml_fraud, and ml_aml schemas. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL stores model registry entries, feature definitions, pipeline run records, and the append-only model_audit_log. Vertex AI endpoints host production credit risk scorecard, fraud transaction classifier, and AML SAR propensity models with traffic-split canary deployments. Google IAP authenticates all web interfaces; Argo CD manages GitOps-driven infrastructure state from a team-scoped infrastructure repo.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Kubeflow Pipelines SDK 2.x, Argo Workflows 3.5 on GKE Autopilot. Platform API: FastAPI 0.111 on Python 3.12. Feature Store: Feast 0.38 with three isolated projects; Memorystore Redis 7 Cluster for online store; Snowflake enterprise account for offline store. Experiment Tracking: MLflow 2.13 with GCS artifact store. Model Registry: PostgreSQL-backed custom registry via FastAPI with append-only audit log. Serving: Vertex AI Prediction with custom container support. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (PgBouncer pooling), GCS versioned CMEK buckets per domain, Artifact Registry for component images. CI/CD: GitHub Actions plus Argo CD GitOps. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, MLflow metrics tracking, PagerDuty. Governance Portal: Streamlit 1.35 (MVP; Phase 3 replacement planned).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Pipeline Submission: Data scientists compile KFP pipelines locally via platform CLI, validate resource quotas, and submit via POST /pipelines/runs; Argo Workflows executes steps as GKE pods in the team namespace with GPU affinity. Experiment Tracking: Each pipeline run creates an MLflow experiment; steps log AUC, Gini coefficient, KS statistic for credit models; precision at 0.1% FPR and average precision for fraud models; SAR detection rate for AML models. Feature Store Access: Teams register feature views in their Feast project (bank-credit, bank-fraud, bank-aml) via CLI; online retrieval from Redis prefix-isolated keyspace; offline retrieval from Snowflake domain schema. Model Registry and Governance: Trained artifacts registered via POST /models with GCS URI and training data SHA256; model status transitions require governance portal approval from model_risk_reviewer role with immutable audit log entry. Cost Attribution: GPU node usage tagged by team and model labels; nightly job writes per-team charge-back records for finance reporting.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot Cluster: platform namespace (Argo controller, Feast server pods, FastAPI platform API, MLflow server), credit/fraud/aml namespaces (pipeline executor pods, team-scoped service accounts). Feast Online Store: Memorystore Redis 7 Cluster with key prefix isolation per Feast project (credit:*, fraud:*, aml:*). Feast Offline Store: Snowflake enterprise account with ml_credit, ml_fraud, ml_aml schemas. Model Artifact Store: GCS buckets gs://bank-ml-credit/, gs://bank-ml-fraud/, gs://bank-ml-aml/ with CMEK and versioning. Metadata DB: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP, PgBouncer). Vertex AI: Serving endpoints for credit risk, fraud classifier, and AML models with canary traffic splitting. Artifact Registry: Component Docker images. Governance Portal: Streamlit app in platform namespace behind IAP.
target_output.workflows: Pipeline Training Workflow: Data scientist runs 'platform pipeline submit --domain credit --pipeline credit_risk_v3.yaml'; FastAPI validates GPU quota against credit namespace limit, compiles KFP pipeline, submits Argo Workflow manifest; Argo schedules pods with GPU affinity in credit namespace; each step logs metrics to MLflow; on completion, platform API registers model artifact to model_registry_entries with training_data_version_hash from Snowflake snapshot SHA256; model status set to candidate. Model Promotion Governance Workflow: Data scientist calls PATCH /models/{id}/status to request validation; model risk reviewer authenticates via IAP to Streamlit governance portal, reviews MLflow backtesting results and SR 11-7 validation report; reviewer approves, which writes an INSERT to model_audit_log (BEFORE DELETE trigger blocks any future deletion); FastAPI updates model status to production; Argo CD detects registry change and updates Vertex AI endpoint with 10% canary traffic; automated AUC monitoring runs 24h; if AUC does not degrade beyond threshold, traffic promoted to 100%, otherwise automatic rollback.
target_output.data_model: model_registry_entries (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, version SEMVER, domain ENUM credit/fraud/aml, artifact_gcs_uri TEXT, training_data_version_hash CHAR(64), status ENUM candidate/validated/production/retired, registered_by EMAIL, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). feature_definitions (id UUID PK, feast_project VARCHAR NOT NULL CHECK (feast_project IN ('bank-credit','bank-fraud','bank-aml')), feature_name VARCHAR, entity_type VARCHAR, dtype VARCHAR, source_snowflake_table VARCHAR, owner_team VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, domain ENUM credit/fraud/aml, pipeline_name VARCHAR, submitter EMAIL, argo_workflow_id VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed, gpu_count INT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, mlflow_experiment_id VARCHAR). gpu_cluster_jobs (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, gpu_type VARCHAR, gpu_count INT, cost_usd NUMERIC, gcp_labels JSONB, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_audit_log (id UUID PK, model_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM registered/validated/promoted/retired, actor_email EMAIL, actor_role VARCHAR, governance_comment TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) -- BEFORE DELETE trigger raises exception unconditionally; REVOKE DELETE ON model_audit_log FROM platform_api_role; REVOKE UPDATE ON model_audit_log FROM platform_api_role. experiment_runs (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, mlflow_run_id VARCHAR, metrics_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API at /api/v1 secured by Google IAP and Workload Identity. POST /pipelines/runs (submit KFP pipeline; body: domain, pipeline_yaml_b64, resource_request). GET /pipelines/runs?domain=credit&status=running (list pipeline runs with pagination). POST /models (register artifact; body: name, version, domain, artifact_gcs_uri, training_data_version_hash). GET /models?domain=fraud&status=production (list by domain and status). PATCH /models/{id}/status (governance action; body: status, governance_comment; restricted to model_risk_reviewer role via IAP identity header). GET /features?feast_project=bank-credit (list feature definitions for a Feast project). GET /costs/attribution?domain=aml&month=2026-03 (per-team GPU charge-back report). GET /audit-log?model_id={id} (append-only model lifecycle audit trail).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Access Control: Google IAP authenticates all web interfaces (Kubeflow UI, MLflow UI, governance portal). Workload Identity binds GKE namespace service accounts to domain-scoped GCP service accounts (sa-credit@bank-ml.iam, sa-fraud@bank-ml.iam, sa-aml@bank-ml.iam) with IAM conditions restricting GCS reads to the owning domain bucket only. Feast Isolation: Three Feast projects (bank-credit, bank-fraud, bank-aml) with Redis key prefixes (credit:*, fraud:*, aml:*) enforced in feature_store.yaml; GKE NetworkPolicy blocks pods in credit/fraud/aml namespaces from reaching Feast server pods via cross-namespace routes. Audit Log Immutability: BEFORE DELETE trigger on model_audit_log raises exception unconditionally; DELETE and UPDATE privileges revoked from platform_api_role; INSERT restricted to platform_api_role via row-level security; MLflow experiment deletion disabled via server flag. CMEK: Separate KMS key per domain GCS bucket with 90-day rotation window scheduled Sunday 02:00-04:00 UTC outside training hours. SR 11-7 Controls: Governance portal enforces mandatory model risk sign-off before status transition to production; all sign-off events in append-only audit log; platform maintains model inventory in registry with lineage to training data version. Network Egress: NetworkPolicy blocks all pod egress except to GCP private APIs, Snowflake VPC service control perimeter, and approved internal endpoints.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Environments: dev (single-zone GKE Autopilot, reduced GPU quota), staging (multi-zone, mirrors production, used for governance portal UAT with model risk team), production (GKE Autopilot multi-zone us-central1-a/b/c; Vertex AI endpoints in same region). GitOps: Argo CD watches infrastructure repo with team-scoped directories (infra/platform/, infra/credit/, infra/fraud/, infra/aml/) and branch protection preventing cross-team directory writes. CI Pipeline: GitHub Actions runs ruff lint, mypy type-check, pytest for pipeline component unit tests, builds Docker images, pushes to Artifact Registry, triggers Argo CD sync. Model Promotion: Separate Argo CD application for Vertex AI endpoint definitions; model status=production in registry triggers webhook; endpoint updated with 10% canary traffic; 24h AUC monitoring; automated rollback if AUC drops >0.01 vs baseline. Secrets: Secret Manager stores Snowflake credentials and FastAPI signing keys with 30-day rotation. Migrations: Alembic manages Cloud SQL schema migrations as GKE one-off jobs pre-deployment. Namespace Provisioning: New model domain onboarding requires platform team PR adding namespace manifest, Feast project config, GCS bucket, IAM bindings, and KMS key — all applied via Argo CD.
target_output.observability: Grafana Dashboards: GPU utilization heatmap by namespace (credit/fraud/aml), Argo pipeline queue depth, Feast online feature latency p50/p95/p99, Vertex AI endpoint error rate and latency per model domain. Prometheus Metrics: argo_workflows_queue_depth_gauge, kfp_pipeline_run_duration_seconds, feast_online_request_latency_ms, fastapi_http_request_duration_seconds. MLflow Model Metrics: AUC, Gini coefficient, KS statistic per credit model version; precision at 0.1% FPR and average precision for fraud models; SAR detection rate and false referral rate for AML. PagerDuty Alerts: GPU quota utilization >90% in any namespace (P2), Vertex endpoint error rate >1% for 5min (P1), Feast online latency p99 >15ms (P2), pipeline failure rate >20% in 1h window (P2), Cloud SQL connection pool >85% (P2). Cost Monitoring: GCP billing export to BigQuery; nightly Grafana charge-back dashboard by domain vs monthly budget.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GPU budget $80K/month across three domains with priority queuing giving production retraining preemption over experimental runs. Vertex AI Prediction autoscales from 1 to 8 replicas per endpoint on request rate. GKE Autopilot scales nodes on-demand; 2 reserved GPU nodes pre-warm production retraining pool to avoid 60-90s provisioning latency on urgent fraud or credit jobs. Snowflake warehouse size limits per domain (X-Small for feature registration, Medium for training data pulls). GCS Nearline lifecycle archives training snapshots older than 90 days, estimated 40% storage cost reduction at steady state. Monthly cost estimate: GPU training $55K, Vertex serving $12K, Cloud SQL plus Redis $5K, GCS plus Snowflake $8K totaling approximately $80K baseline.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Deploy GKE Autopilot cluster with platform namespace, Argo Workflows, MLflow, FastAPI platform API with pipeline submission and model registry. Deploy Feast bank-credit project with Redis and Snowflake. Deploy governance portal (Streamlit) with SR 11-7 sign-off workflow. Implement audit log immutability controls (trigger, privilege revocation). CI/CD via GitHub Actions and Argo CD. Onboard credit risk team with one production pipeline to Vertex AI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Onboard fraud and aml namespaces with dedicated Feast projects and GCS buckets. Validate cross-domain Feast isolation with integration test suite. Enable nightly cost attribution job. Add Vertex AI canary deployment automation. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Real-time feature pipelines via Pub/Sub. Automated drift detection with configurable retraining triggers. Replace Streamlit governance portal with scalable React-based UI. External model validation partner access via read-only VPC peering to Vertex endpoint metrics.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff for linting and mypy in strict mode for all platform API and pipeline component code. Pipeline components packaged as Docker images with SemVer tags in Artifact Registry. ADR documents required for Feast schema changes and model registry table migrations. Infrastructure changes via PR to Argo CD repo with mandatory platform team review. Feast feature registration for PII-adjacent transaction features requires data steward sign-off. All Kubeflow pipeline definitions stored in domain repos (ml-credit, ml-fraud, ml-aml) with platform library as a versioned dependency.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Streamlit governance portal cannot handle concurrent model reviews at scale beyond 20 simultaneous reviewers. Mitigation: Accepted for MVP with Phase 3 replacement by React portal as documented plan. Risk: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency (60-90s) delays urgent fraud model retraining. Mitigation: Reserved node pool with 2 pre-warmed GPU nodes for production retraining priority class. Risk: Snowflake enterprise account rate limits during large training data pulls blocking concurrent pipelines across domains. Mitigation: Warehouse size limits per domain and scheduled training windows for large retrains. Tradeoff: Shared Memorystore Redis cluster for cost efficiency vs dedicated Redis per domain for stronger isolation; Feast project-level key prefix enforcement chosen as middle ground. Tradeoff: Feast project-level isolation adds configuration overhead but is the only mechanism that prevents cross-team online feature overwrite without separate Redis instances.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Drift detection threshold calibration deferred to Phase 2 as confirmed in constraints. Snowflake Feast connector compatibility validated by Data Engineering against bank enterprise account tier. Streamlit governance portal replacement acknowledged as Phase 3 item with documented scalability risk. CMEK key rotation window fixed to Sunday 02:00-04:00 UTC to avoid overlap with weekday training jobs. Feast project naming convention (bank-credit, bank-fraud, bank-aml) agreed with data science team leads.

metadata.notes: Round-2 revision for retail bank ML infrastructure platform; resolves Feast namespace isolation and audit log immutability gaps; SR 11-7 and SOX compliant governance controls across credit, fraud, and AML model domains.

profile.domain: Fixed Income Trading Analytics Desktop

project_goal.value: High-performance desktop app for bond traders displaying real-time yield curve analytics, scenario stress testing, and portfolio P&L attribution with sub-100ms screen refresh on large position books
project_goal.rationale: Fixed income desk needs consolidated analytics tool replacing disparate Bloomberg terminal tabs and Excel models

target_users.value: Bond traders and portfolio analysts on the fixed income desk
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in trader workflow discovery

project_class.rationale: Local-first high-performance app with Rust computation core; browser latency unacceptable for 10Hz refresh

capabilities.rationale: React UI layer, Rust IPC computation backend, kdb+ market data, Bloomberg B-PIPE feed, analytics

complexity_level.rationale: 10Hz yield curve refresh on 5000-bond books with parallel DV01 calculations requires high-scale design

risk_level.rationale: Live P&L data, Volcker Rule compliance, and MiFID II pre/post-trade transparency requirements

data_sensitivity.rationale: Real-time bond positions and P&L are market-sensitive proprietary trading data

external_exposure.rationale: Desktop app on trading floor workstations with no public-facing surface

access_model.value: Active Directory with Windows Hello hardware-backed authentication; per-trader audit trail
access_model.rationale: MiFID II trader identification requirement for pre/post-trade reporting

feature_scope.value: Real-time yield curve display (par, zero, forward), scenario stress testing with parallel DV01 repricing, portfolio P&L attribution waterfall, trader workspace save/restore, Bloomberg B-PIPE market data integration, kdb+ historical data queries
feature_scope.rationale: Core analytics features identified in trader interviews

mvp_scope.value: Yield curve display at 10Hz, parallel shift and key rate duration stress scenarios, P&L attribution by duration bucket, Bloomberg B-PIPE subscription
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for Q2 trading desk pilot

security_baseline.value: Active Directory SSO, Windows Hello MFA, screen-lock on session idle 5 min, no position data written outside encrypted local storage
security_baseline.rationale: Trading floor security policy and MiFID II requirements

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 + React 18 with TypeScript; Rust core for heavy analytics computation via Tauri IPC commands
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native window performance with React UI and Rust computation kernel

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum serving local IPC; C++ analytics library (curve fitting, DV01) accessed via Rust FFI
backend_stack.rationale: Rust FFI to existing C++ analytics lib avoids reimplementation; Axum for local command socket

data_platform.value: kdb+ / q time-series database for intraday tick data; in-process memory cache for hot position data
data_platform.rationale: kdb+ is trading floor standard for tick data; in-process cache eliminates IPC latency on hot path

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal workstations on trading floor; binary distributed via internal SCCM deployment
hosting_target.rationale: On-premise trading floor; cloud latency unacceptable for sub-100ms requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: Trade analytics logs retained 5 years for MiFID II; no position snapshots stored outside authorized kdb+ instance
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: MiFID II Article 25 record-keeping and Volcker Rule trading documentation requirements

future_scope.value: XVA (CVA/DVA/FVA) analytics module, cross-asset correlation matrix visualization, integration with internal risk system for limit utilization overlays
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items

constraints.value: P99 yield curve refresh under 100ms; stress scenario repricing of 5000-bond book under 200ms; binary size under 150MB; Windows 10/11 only
constraints.rationale: Trader-mandated performance thresholds validated in benchmark sessions

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for render latency and computation time metrics; Sentry for crash reporting; structured logs for MiFID II audit
observability_baseline.rationale: Trading desk infrastructure standard

execution_preference.value: Sprint-based delivery with weekly demo to fixed income desk; performance benchmark gates before each release
execution_preference.rationale: Trader involvement needed for continuous feedback on UX and analytics accuracy

compliance_context.value: MiFID II pre/post-trade transparency and trader identification; Volcker Rule trading desk activity controls; FRTB internal model approach documentation for analytics library
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=financial and risk_level=high trigger compliance_context requirement

project.goal: Sub-100ms yield curve analytics desktop app for bond traders
project.target_users: Fixed income traders and analysts

frontend.charting: D3.js v7 for yield curve SVG rendering

backend.framework: Axum local IPC socket + C++ FFI via bindgen
backend.api: Tauri IPC commands for computation requests

security.auth: Active Directory via Windows Hello MSAL
security.transport: Named pipe IPC between React and Rust; kdb+ connection over internal VLAN

data.cache: in-process Rust HashMap for hot bond positions

devops.environments: dev (simulated data), staging (stale kdb+ data), production

open_questions.render_thread_isolation: Whether D3 SVG rendering should move to offscreen canvas to free main thread for IPC responses

confirmed_decisions.rust_ffi: C++ analytics library via bindgen FFI

reasoner_reviews.product: Traders confirmed that the sub-100ms refresh target is non-negotiable for the yield curve display - latency above 150ms causes visible lag on 10Hz Bloomberg feed updates that undermines trader confidence in the data. The P&L attribution waterfall is the highest-value feature after the curve display, directly replacing an error-prone Excel model. Scenario stress testing covers 80% of trader workflow in the first two rounds.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Tauri 2 + Rust IPC architecture correctly separates the render-critical path from computation. Round 2 addressed parallelism in DV01 calculation via Rayon. Round 3 must address the remaining render thread contention issue (ISS-003): D3.js SVG mutations during 10Hz updates are blocking the main thread and causing frame drops. Moving yield curve rendering to an offscreen OffscreenCanvas worker resolves this without changing the Rust computation architecture.
reasoner_reviews.security: MiFID II trader identification requires that every analytics computation is tagged with the trader AD identity so that pre/post-trade audit logs can be reconstructed. The current round 2 implementation logs trader_id on scenario runs but not on yield curve subscription events - this gap must be closed in round 3.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 200ms scenario repricing constraint was validated in round 2 benchmarks with Rayon parallelism on an 8-core workstation (4000-bond book: 140ms, 5000-bond book: 195ms, within budget). The 100ms yield curve refresh remains borderline when D3 SVG mutations fire on the main thread - offscreen canvas is the correct mitigation.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 benchmark showed 5000-bond scenario repricing at 195ms (5ms margin). If the analytics C++ library adds more instruments or the FRTB expansion increases bond book size to 8000, this margin disappears. The architecture should be benchmarked with 8000 bonds and the Rayon thread pool tuned based on production workstation core counts.

backend.service_design: Tauri application with Rust core exposing IPC command handlers: subscribe_yield_curve (opens kdb+ subscription, streams par/zero/forward curve updates to frontend at 10Hz), run_stress_scenario (receives scenario params, dispatches C++ FFI DV01 repricing via Rayon thread pool, returns P&L attribution JSON), query_historical_curves (kdb+ time-range query for historical yield curve display). No HTTP server - all IPC via Tauri command system.
backend.api_patterns: Tauri IPC commands: invoke('subscribe_yield_curve', {curve_type, tenors}) -> EventStream<YieldCurveUpdate>. invoke('run_stress_scenario', {scenario_type:'parallel_shift'|'key_rate_duration', shock_bps, book_snapshot_id}) -> StressResult{pnl_attribution:Vec<BucketPnl>, repricing_ms:u64}. invoke('query_kdb_history', {from_ts, to_ts, curve_type}) -> Vec<HistoricalCurveSnapshot>.
backend.scaling_notes: Rayon thread pool sized to (workstation_cores - 2) to leave headroom for the Tauri event loop and kdb+ connection goroutine. DV01 computation parallelized at the bond level - each bond's modified duration and dollar value calculation is an independent Rayon task. 8000-bond benchmark run before each release with 200ms gate.
backend.failure_handling: kdb+ connection loss shows 'Data feed interrupted' overlay on yield curve panel and retries every 3s. If C++ FFI panics (null pointer from analytics lib), Rust catches the signal via catch_unwind, logs structured error with stack trace, and returns an error to the frontend without crashing the Tauri process.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri React app with three primary panels: YieldCurvePanel (D3.js SVG on OffscreenCanvas worker in round 3 fix), ScenarioStressPanel (scenario parameter form + P&L attribution waterfall chart), PositionSummaryPanel (bond book table with duration, convexity, DV01 per position). Workspace state (panel layout, active scenario, subscription params) persisted to Tauri app_data_dir as JSON.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Main trading view: three-panel layout with resizable splitter. Yield curve panel shows par, zero, and forward curves with hover tooltip showing exact rate per tenor. Stress scenario panel: drop-down for scenario type, numeric input for shock_bps, 'Run Scenario' button, P&L attribution waterfall rendered by D3 horizontal bar chart. Historical comparison: date picker to overlay historical curve snapshots.
frontend.performance_notes: Round 3 fix: yield curve SVG updates moved from D3 in main thread to OffscreenCanvas worker using transferControlToOffscreen(). React receives yield curve update events and postMessages to the worker; worker redraws canvas directly without touching the main thread DOM. Benchmark target: main thread frame budget under 8ms during 10Hz curve updates.

security.auth_design: Active Directory authentication via MSAL for Windows; Windows Hello hardware-backed credential for strong authentication. Trader identity (AD objectGUID) embedded in every IPC command payload as trader_id for MiFID II audit attribution.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: MiFID II audit log written to append-only local log file and synced to central syslog on every scenario run and yield curve subscription event. Log entries: trader_id, event_type (yield_curve_subscription, scenario_run, workspace_save), event_timestamp, instrument_set_hash, computation_params. Log file integrity protected by HMAC-SHA256 chain.
security.incident_response_notes: On authentication failure after 3 attempts, Tauri app locks and requires AD re-authentication. On C++ FFI panic, structured error logged with full context; no silent failures. Crash dump uploaded to Sentry with PII stripped before transmission.

data.entities: bond_positions (in-process HashMap: isin, face_value, coupon, maturity, duration, convexity, dv01, last_price, last_updated_ms), yield_curves (in-process: curve_type ENUM par/zero/forward, tenor_points Vec<(f64 years, f64 rate_bps)>, as_of_ts), stress_scenarios (run log: scenario_id UUID, trader_id, scenario_type, shock_bps, book_snapshot_hash, pnl_total, repricing_ms, run_at), trader_workspaces (Tauri app_data_dir JSON: workspace_id, trader_id, panel_layout, active_curve_types, pinned_tenors, last_saved_at)
data.storage_design: Hot position and curve data in Rust in-process HashMap; no disk writes on critical path. kdb+ cluster holds tick history - queried via kdb+ IPC protocol from Rust using kdbplus crate. Scenario run log written to SQLite in Tauri app_data_dir for local history. Workspace state serialized to JSON on save.
data.consistency_notes: yield curve updates from Bloomberg B-PIPE are authoritative; local HashMap overwritten on each 10Hz tick event. Scenario run log is append-only in SQLite; no updates to historical runs permitted.

devops.deployment_topology: Jenkins pipeline builds Tauri Windows installer (MSI) for Windows 10/11 x64. Binary size gate: CI fails if MSI exceeds 150MB. Distributed via SCCM software push to trading floor workstations. No auto-update; releases coordinated with trading desk IT during market close.
devops.environments: Dev: Tauri app running against local kdb+ simulator with synthetic yield curve data. Staging: Connected to stale kdb+ data snapshot (end-of-previous-day). Production: Connected to live Bloomberg B-PIPE and trading floor kdb+ cluster.
devops.ci_cd_design: On PR: cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo bench (yield_curve_refresh and scenario_repricing benchmarks must pass 100ms and 200ms gates). On merge to main: MSI build, Sentry source map upload, SCCM package update in staging. On release tag: SCCM push to production workstations during end-of-day maintenance window.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics exposed via local HTTP on localhost:9093: tauri_yield_curve_refresh_ms histogram, tauri_scenario_repricing_ms histogram, tauri_kdb_query_ms histogram, tauri_ffi_errors_total counter. Grafana dashboard on trader workstation shows live render and computation latency. Sentry SDK for crash reports (PII stripped before upload).

ISS-001.title: C++ FFI panic crashes entire Tauri process on malformed bond data
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 C++ analytics library called via Rust FFI with no panic boundary; a null pointer dereference from malformed ISIN data killed the entire Tauri process, losing trader workspace state.

history.note: Raised as critical - no catch_unwind wrapper around FFI call
catch_unwind wrapper added; malformed data returns error Result to frontend

ISS-002.title: Scenario repricing of 5000-bond book exceeded 200ms gate on single-threaded path
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 DV01 repricing iterated bonds sequentially; benchmark showed 5000-bond book at 820ms, exceeding the 200ms requirement by 4x.

history.note: Single-threaded DV01 loop at 820ms
Rayon parallel_iter over bond positions; 5000-bond benchmark 195ms

ISS-003.title: D3 SVG mutations on main thread cause frame drops during 10Hz yield curve updates
ISS-003.detail: Yield curve panel re-renders via D3 on the React main thread; during 10Hz Bloomberg B-PIPE updates, DOM mutation time spikes to 60-80ms, dropping frames and exceeding the 100ms P99 budget when combined with React reconciliation overhead.

history.note: Profiled in round 2: D3 SVG mutations take 60-80ms on main thread at 10Hz

previous_plan.title: Fixed Income Yield Curve Analytics Desktop - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Tauri 2 + React 18 desktop app for bond traders. Rust core with C++ FFI (catch_unwind in place) for DV01 analytics via Rayon parallel_iter (5000-bond: 195ms). Bloomberg B-PIPE + kdb+ data. D3.js yield curve rendering on main thread (ISS-003 still unresolved - 60-80ms main thread spike at 10Hz).
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri IPC between React frontend and Rust backend. Rust FFI to C++ analytics library via bindgen with catch_unwind. kdb+ tick database queried from Rust. In-process HashMap for hot bond position cache. D3.js SVG rendering on React main thread - identified as ISS-003 bottleneck.
previous_plan.workflows: Bloomberg B-PIPE tick arrives -> Tauri event emitted to React -> D3.js re-renders yield curve SVG on main thread (60-80ms spike) -> React reconciliation adds overhead -> combined latency exceeds 100ms P99 gate.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: ISS-003 unresolved: D3 main thread contention at 10Hz exceeds 100ms budget. Round 3 must migrate to OffscreenCanvas worker. Rayon thread pool currently hardcoded to 6 threads; should use num_cpus crate for workstation portability.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on ISS-003: migrating D3 yield curve rendering from the React main thread to an OffscreenCanvas worker. The approach: React component calls transferControlToOffscreen() on mount, passing the canvas to a Web Worker; the Tauri IPC event listener receives YieldCurveUpdate, serializes to a transferable ArrayBuffer, and postMessages to the worker which calls D3 directly against the OffscreenCanvas context. This eliminates the 60-80ms main thread DOM mutation and reduces main thread frame budget to under 8ms. Also tuning Rayon thread pool with num_cpus for workstation portability, and adding the 8000-bond benchmark gate. MiFID II audit gap on yield curve subscription events closed by tagging every IPC command with trader_id.

fix_report.action_taken: Migrated yield curve D3 rendering to OffscreenCanvas Web Worker. React YieldCurvePanel calls canvas.transferControlToOffscreen() on mount and passes the OffscreenCanvas to a dedicated Web Worker via postMessage. Tauri IPC listen('yield_curve_update') handler serializes YieldCurveUpdate to SharedArrayBuffer and postMessages to the worker. Worker runs D3 against OffscreenCanvas 2D context using getContext('2d') and requestAnimationFrame within the worker event loop. Main thread no longer touches D3 or canvas DOM during 10Hz updates. Benchmark after fix: main thread P99 under 8ms during 10Hz updates; end-to-end yield curve refresh P99 at 72ms, well within 100ms gate.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Yield curve refresh P99 drops from >100ms to ~72ms; main thread stays under 8ms frame budget during 10Hz Bloomberg B-PIPE feed; trader-visible frame drops eliminated

target_output.title: Fixed Income Yield Curve Analytics Desktop
target_output.executive_summary: A high-performance Tauri 2 desktop application for bond traders on the fixed income desk that displays real-time yield curve analytics (par, zero, forward) refreshed at 10Hz from Bloomberg B-PIPE via OffscreenCanvas Web Worker rendering, executes parallel DV01 scenario stress testing repricing a 5000-bond book under 200ms via Rust Rayon FFI to a C++ analytics library, and generates P&L attribution waterfalls. All analytics computations are tagged with AD trader identity for MiFID II audit compliance. Distributed to trading floor workstations via SCCM.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 application with three layers: (1) React 18 frontend handling trader UI interactions and dispatching IPC commands; (2) Rust Tauri core exposing IPC command handlers for yield curve subscription, scenario repricing, and kdb+ queries, with Rayon thread pool for parallel DV01 computation and C++ analytics library accessed via bindgen FFI with catch_unwind panic boundaries; (3) Data layer comprising an in-process Rust HashMap for hot bond position cache and a kdb+/q tick database on the trading floor network for historical data queries. Yield curve D3 rendering runs in a dedicated OffscreenCanvas Web Worker, receiving serialized YieldCurveUpdate ArrayBuffers via postMessage and drawing directly against an OffscreenCanvas 2D context, fully decoupling SVG mutation cost from the React main thread. Bloomberg B-PIPE market data feeds into the Rust WebSocket consumer which emits Tauri events to the frontend and updates the in-process position HashMap.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.0 shell + React 18 with TypeScript, D3.js v7 (in OffscreenCanvas Web Worker), Zustand 4 for workspace state, Tauri IPC invoke/listen APIs. Rust Core: Rust 1.77, Axum 0.7 (local Unix socket for dev tooling), Rayon 1.10 with num_cpus-sized thread pool, bindgen 0.69 for C++ FFI, catch_unwind for FFI panic boundaries, kdbplus 0.8 for kdb+ IPC. C++ Analytics: existing desk analytics library (curve fitting, DV01, convexity) compiled as static lib. Data: kdb+/q 4.0 tick database, SQLite 3.45 (via rusqlite) for local scenario run log and workspace state. Auth: Windows MSAL (microsoft-authentication-library) + Windows Hello. Observability: Prometheus client for Rust (localhost:9093), Sentry Rust SDK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Yield Curve Display: real-time par, zero, and forward curves rendered at 10Hz via D3 on OffscreenCanvas worker; hover tooltip shows rate per tenor in basis points; historical overlay selectable by date. Scenario Stress Testing: parallel shift (uniform shock_bps across all tenors), key rate duration (individual tenor shocks), and butterfly scenario; reprices full book via Rayon+C++ FFI; results shown as P&L attribution waterfall by duration bucket (0-2yr, 2-5yr, 5-10yr, 10yr+). Portfolio Summary: bond position table with ISIN, face value, modified duration, convexity, DV01, last price, and live mark-to-market P&L. Workspace Management: save/restore panel layout, pinned tenors, and active scenario params to SQLite. MiFID II Audit View: read-only log of scenario runs with trader_id, timestamp, scenario params, and result summary.
target_output.system_components: Tauri App Shell (Rust process hosting WebView2 for React UI and Rust command handlers). Bloomberg B-PIPE WebSocket Consumer (Rust async task consuming tick events, updating in-process bond HashMap, emitting yield_curve_update Tauri events). In-Process Bond Position Cache (Rust HashMap<Isin, BondPosition> with RwLock; writer in B-PIPE consumer, readers in IPC command handlers). Rayon DV01 Thread Pool (num_cpus - 2 threads; parallel_iter over Vec<BondPosition> calling C++ FFI repriceWithShock per bond; catch_unwind per item). kdb+ Query Client (kdbplus Rust crate connecting to trading floor kdb+ cluster; used for historical curve queries and end-of-day position snapshots). OffscreenCanvas Web Worker (JavaScript worker receiving YieldCurveUpdate ArrayBuffer via postMessage; runs D3.js v7 selection and path generators against OffscreenCanvas; no DOM access). Prometheus Metrics HTTP Server (Rust, localhost:9093; tauri_yield_curve_refresh_ms, tauri_scenario_repricing_ms histograms). SQLite Persistence (rusqlite; scenario_runs and trader_workspaces tables in Tauri app_data_dir).
target_output.workflows: 10Hz Yield Curve Update: Bloomberg B-PIPE emits tick for a benchmark rate; Rust B-PIPE consumer updates in-process HashMap and serializes updated YieldCurveUpdate{par_curve, zero_curve, forward_curve, as_of_ts} to SharedArrayBuffer; Tauri emits yield_curve_update event; React listener postMessages ArrayBuffer to OffscreenCanvas Web Worker; worker deserializes, calls D3 scale/line generators against OffscreenCanvas context, draws updated curves; main thread frame budget: under 8ms. Scenario Stress Run: trader selects 'Parallel Shift +50bps' and clicks Run Scenario; React invokes Tauri command run_stress_scenario{scenario_type:'parallel_shift', shock_bps:50}; Rust handler snapshots bond HashMap, dispatches Rayon par_iter, each bond calls C++ FFI repriceWithShock (catch_unwind per item), aggregates P&L by duration bucket; returns StressResult{pnl_attribution, repricing_ms} within 200ms; React renders P&L waterfall via D3 horizontal bars; result appended to SQLite scenario_runs with trader_id. Historical Curve Query: trader opens date picker, selects 2026-03-15; React invokes query_kdb_history{from_ts, to_ts}; Rust kdbplus client sends q query to kdb+ cluster; returns Vec<HistoricalCurveSnapshot>; React renders overlay on existing yield curve canvas.
target_output.data_model: bond_positions (in-process RwLock<HashMap<String, BondPosition>>: isin String, cusip String, face_value_usd f64, coupon_rate f64, maturity_date NaiveDate, modified_duration f64, convexity f64, dv01_usd f64, last_price f64, last_updated_ms i64). yield_curves (in-process: curve_type ENUM par/zero/forward, tenors Vec<TenorPoint{years:f64, rate_bps:f64}>, as_of_ts i64). stress_scenarios (SQLite: scenario_id TEXT PK, trader_id TEXT, scenario_type TEXT, shock_bps INTEGER, book_snapshot_hash TEXT, pnl_total_usd REAL, pnl_by_bucket_json TEXT, repricing_ms INTEGER, run_at INTEGER). trader_workspaces (SQLite: workspace_id TEXT PK, trader_id TEXT, panel_layout_json TEXT, active_curve_types TEXT, pinned_tenors_json TEXT, last_saved_at INTEGER). mifid_audit_log (append-only local file + syslog: trader_id, event_type, event_ts, instrument_set_hash, params_json, hmac_chain TEXT). Fixed income financial entities: trade_transactions (id UUID PK, isin VARCHAR, cusip VARCHAR, trade_date DATE, settlement_date DATE, trade_type ENUM buy/sell/repo/reverse_repo, face_value_cents BIGINT, price NUMERIC, accrued_interest_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), counterparty_id VARCHAR, account_id VARCHAR), account_ledger_entries (id UUID PK, account_id VARCHAR, trade_id FK, entry_type ENUM debit/credit, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), balance_after_cents BIGINT, posted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), settlement_instructions (id UUID PK, trade_id FK, settlement_status ENUM pending/matched/settled/failed, custodian VARCHAR, settlement_amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC commands (Rust handlers, invoked from React): invoke('subscribe_yield_curve', {curve_types:['par','zero','forward'], tenors:[0.25,0.5,1,2,5,10,30]}) -> void (starts B-PIPE subscription, emits yield_curve_update events). invoke('run_stress_scenario', {scenario_type:'parallel_shift'|'key_rate_duration'|'butterfly', shock_bps:number, duration_bucket_filter?:string}) -> Promise<{pnl_attribution:BucketPnl[], repricing_ms:number, scenario_id:string}>. invoke('query_kdb_history', {from_ts:number, to_ts:number, curve_type:string}) -> Promise<HistoricalCurveSnapshot[]>. invoke('save_workspace', {layout:WorkspaceLayout}) -> void. invoke('list_scenario_runs', {limit:number}) -> Promise<ScenarioRunSummary[]>. All commands accept trader_id injected from MSAL token in the Rust command handler context.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Active Directory authentication via MSAL for Windows; Windows Hello hardware-backed PIN/biometric for strong authentication at app launch. Every Tauri IPC command handler extracts trader_id from the Windows identity context and embeds it in the MiFID II audit log entry. Audit log entries protected by HMAC-SHA256 chain: each entry includes the HMAC of the previous entry, making log tampering detectable.
