Screen auto-lock after 5 minutes idle (Tauri OS plugin monitors idle time). No bond position or P&L data written outside the encrypted Tauri app_data_dir or authorized kdb+ cluster. Sentry crash reports strip trader_id and position data before transmission. C++ FFI calls wrapped in catch_unwind; panics logged to Sentry as non-PII error events.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Build: Jenkins pipeline on Windows build agent runs `cargo build --release`, bundles Tauri MSI installer with WiX toolset. Cargo bench gates: yield_curve_refresh P99 must be under 100ms and scenario_repricing P99 under 200ms (5000-bond book) before MSI is produced. Binary size gate: MSI under 150MB. MSI signed with trading desk code-signing certificate via DigiCert timestamp authority. Distribution: SCCM software deployment package pushed to trading floor workstations in the FixedIncome_Workstations collection. Rollout: staged by trader team (desk head's machine first, then full desk). Releases during end-of-day maintenance window only. Rollback: SCCM enforces previous MSI version on rollback action. No auto-update agent in production. Environments: dev uses `settle --mock-feed` flag with local synthetic yield curve generator; staging uses stale kdb+ snapshot from previous end-of-day.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics on localhost:9093/metrics (Rust prometheus crate): tauri_yield_curve_refresh_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99 gates in Grafana), tauri_scenario_repricing_ms histogram by scenario_type, tauri_kdb_query_ms histogram, tauri_ffi_errors_total counter, tauri_worker_postmessage_ms histogram (OffscreenCanvas worker roundtrip latency). Grafana dashboard on trader workstation shows live latency percentiles. Sentry Rust SDK for crash reporting with PII stripped. MiFID II audit log (HMAC-chained append-only file) reviewed by compliance team; synced to central syslog at end of each trading day. Cargo bench results appended to CI artifact log per release for regression tracking.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud hosting cost - fully on-premise on trading floor bare-metal workstations. kdb+ cluster is shared desk infrastructure billed centrally. Sentry project on existing enterprise license. SCCM distribution at no incremental cost. C++ analytics library is an existing internal asset with no licensing cost. Scaling: Rayon thread pool sized via num_cpus at runtime; 8000-bond book benchmark must pass 200ms gate before release, ensuring headroom for book growth. OffscreenCanvas worker scales with browser engine thread scheduling, independent of position book size.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): Tauri scaffold, Bloomberg B-PIPE WebSocket consumer, in-process bond position HashMap, yield curve D3 rendering on main thread (baseline), kdb+ historical query, Active Directory auth, basic scenario run (single-threaded DV01). Phase 2 (4 weeks): Rayon parallel DV01 repricing (resolves ISS-002), catch_unwind FFI panic boundary (resolves ISS-001), P&L attribution waterfall chart, scenario run log to SQLite, workspace save/restore, Prometheus metrics, cargo bench gates in CI. Phase 3 (3 weeks): OffscreenCanvas Web Worker migration for yield curve D3 (resolves ISS-003), MiFID II audit log with HMAC chain, 8000-bond benchmark validation, Rayon thread pool num_cpus tuning, SCCM deployment pipeline, trader acceptance testing on full production kdb+ data.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Tauri IPC command handlers must be async Rust functions returning Result<T, String>; never panic in command handlers - use ? propagation and catch_unwind at FFI boundary only. Cargo bench required for yield_curve_refresh and scenario_repricing with explicit assert thresholds; bench results archived per release. OffscreenCanvas worker code isolated in src/workers/yieldCurveWorker.ts with typed postMessage protocol (WorkerMessage discriminated union). MiFID II audit log calls are non-optional; any IPC command that uses trader position data must call audit_log::record(). cargo clippy and cargo fmt enforced in CI. Integration tests cover round-trip IPC command latency with mock Bloomberg feed.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OffscreenCanvas not supported in older WebView2 versions pre-installed on some trading floor workstations. Mitigation: CI validates minimum WebView2 version 109+ in the SCCM package dependency; installer blocks on incompatible WebView2. Risk: C++ analytics library DV01 numerical precision diverges from official risk system during extreme shock scenarios. Mitigation: nightly reconciliation job compares scenario results against official risk system output; divergence over 0.1bps alerts the quant team. Tradeoff: Rayon thread pool at (num_cpus - 2) leaves headroom for B-PIPE consumer and Tauri event loop but reduces parallelism on 4-core machines. Accepted: minimum workstation spec is 8 cores per trading floor IT policy. Tradeoff: SQLite for scenario log instead of central database simplifies deployment but makes cross-trader scenario comparison harder. Deferred to XVA module phase where central persistence will be warranted.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: OffscreenCanvas vs WebGL for yield curve rendering: OffscreenCanvas 2D chosen over WebGL because D3 path generators work against Canvas 2D context without a shader rewrite. Rayon thread pool size: use num_cpus::get() - 2 at runtime, not hardcoded to 6, ensuring correct sizing on all workstation core counts. Historical data retention in kdb+: existing kdb+ retention policy (2 years intraday) is sufficient for MiFID II 5-year requirement when combined with end-of-day snapshots in SQLite.

metadata.notes: Round 3 Tauri+Rust desktop app for fixed income traders. ISS-003 OffscreenCanvas D3 migration resolves 100ms yield curve refresh gate.

profile.domain: Credit Union Investor Relations Static Site

project_goal.value: Migrate credit union investor relations microsite from legacy CMS to JAMstack, publishing quarterly earnings, board governance documents, and community impact reports for members and regulators
project_goal.rationale: Legacy CMS has high maintenance overhead and poor performance; JAMstack reduces operational burden

target_users.value: Credit union members, board directors, and NCUA examiners
target_users.rationale: Primary audiences identified in stakeholder review

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime needed; all content is pre-built and served from CDN

capabilities.rationale: Static HTML/CSS rendering and Netlify Analytics for page view tracking; no backend required

complexity_level.rationale: Static content site with no dynamic data, no user authentication, no database

risk_level.rationale: Public informational site with no user data collection and no financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal data collected; documents are public disclosures

external_exposure.rationale: Investor relations content is public-facing for members and regulators

access_model.value: Fully public read access; no authentication required for any content
access_model.rationale: NCUA public disclosure requirements mandate unrestricted access for examiners and members

feature_scope.value: Quarterly earnings summaries, board governance documents, community impact reports, press releases, document archive with PDF downloads, structured data markup for SEO
feature_scope.rationale: Core disclosure content categories identified in NCUA requirements review

mvp_scope.value: Earnings reports and governance documents with PDF download, archive index pages, ADA Section 508 accessibility, Netlify deployment pipeline
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum required for NCUA compliance and member access

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Netlify, security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options) via _headers file, no user input forms, no JavaScript data collection
security_baseline.rationale: Static site minimal attack surface; security headers cover CDN-served content

frontend_stack.value: Eleventy (11ty) 2.0 with Nunjucks templates
frontend_stack.rationale: Eleventy is lightweight, fast build, and supports flexible content collections for document archive

backend_stack.value: none - fully static site
backend_stack.rationale: static_website project class with no server-side runtime

data_platform.value: none - content stored as Markdown and JSON data files in the Git repository
data_platform.rationale: Git is the content management system for this static site

hosting_target.value: Netlify Edge CDN with global distribution
hosting_target.rationale: Netlify provides CI/CD, CDN, custom domain, and HTTPS out of the box for static sites

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; PDF documents retained indefinitely as public disclosures; Netlify Analytics aggregated data only (no PII)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NCUA public disclosure documents have no deletion requirement

future_scope.value: Interactive financial ratio calculator widget, multilingual support for Spanish-speaking members, integration with CU management system for automated rate updates
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after pilot

constraints.value: Must meet ADA Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA; Lighthouse performance score over 90; build time under 60 seconds; legacy URL structure preserved for SEO continuity
constraints.rationale: Accessibility required by NCUA; performance and URL requirements from SEO team

observability_baseline.value: Netlify Analytics for page views and unique visitors; no user-level tracking or cookies
observability_baseline.rationale: Cookie-free analytics required by credit union privacy policy

execution_preference.value: Content editors own document uploads via Git PR workflow; engineering reviews only structural changes
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces engineering bottleneck for routine content updates

project.goal: Migrate investor relations microsite to Eleventy JAMstack
project.target_users: Credit union members and NCUA examiners

frontend.styling: PostCSS with custom properties; no framework CSS

security.transport: HTTPS via Netlify; security headers via _headers file

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for build and accessibility check

open_questions.pdf_storage: Whether large PDF archives should be stored in Git or on Netlify Large Media

reasoner_reviews.product: The legacy CMS has 47 manually maintained HTML pages with inconsistent formatting and broken PDF links that create compliance risk during NCUA examinations. Eleventy collections for earnings_reports, governance_documents, community_impact, and press_releases will normalize the archive structure and make document uploads a Git PR workflow that content editors can manage without engineering involvement.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Eleventy 2.0 with Nunjucks is the right choice for this scale: build time under 60 seconds is trivially achievable for a 100-200 page archive. The key architectural decision is using Eleventy collections and pagination to auto-generate archive index pages from Markdown frontmatter, eliminating manual index maintenance. Security headers should be set via the Netlify _headers file rather than meta tags for CSP enforcement at the CDN edge.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static site with no user input forms and no JavaScript data collection has minimal attack surface. Security headers (CSP: default-src 'self', X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin) are sufficient. HTTPS is enforced by Netlify. Ensure no third-party analytics scripts that collect PII are added - Netlify Analytics is cookieless and appropriate.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ADA Section 508 compliance requires semantic HTML5 structure, alt text on all images, keyboard navigation, and sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text). Automated axe-core checks in CI cover the bulk of detectable issues. Lighthouse 90+ score is achievable with static HTML and minimal CSS; avoid JavaScript bundles on the critical path.

frontend.app_structure: Eleventy project with content collections: src/_data/ for JSON data files (credit union details, contact info), src/earnings-reports/ for quarterly report Markdown files, src/governance/ for board document Markdown files, src/community-impact/ for annual report Markdown files, src/press-releases/ for press release Markdown files. Nunjucks layouts in src/_includes/layouts/ (base.njk, report-page.njk, archive-index.njk).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home page with summary of latest earnings and links to recent documents. Earnings Reports archive page (auto-paginated Eleventy collection, 10 per page) and individual report pages with structured data JSON-LD markup. Governance Documents index with board meeting minutes, bylaws, and policy PDFs. Community Impact annual report pages with embedded PDF viewer option. Press Releases archive. PDF download button component on every document page styled to NCUA accessibility guidelines.
frontend.accessibility_notes: All document page templates include: semantic article/nav/main/footer HTML5 elements, aria-label on navigation landmark, alt text required field in Markdown frontmatter (build fails if missing), skip-to-content link at top of every page. Color contrast tested with Lighthouse and axe-core in CI. PDFs linked with [document title] (PDF, size) pattern so screen readers announce file type and size.
frontend.performance_notes: No JavaScript on critical path - Eleventy outputs plain HTML with CSS. Inline critical CSS (under 14KB) in head via Eleventy Transform. Images optimized with eleventy-img plugin to WebP with srcset. Netlify Edge CDN caches all assets with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable for hashed assets.

security.auth_design: No authentication required - fully public content. No login forms, no session management.
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify WAF at CDN edge (included in Netlify Pro plan) blocks known bad bots and DDoS traffic. No rate limiting needed for static content. CSP header prevents XSS via injected scripts: default-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'none'.
security.privacy_controls: No cookies set. Netlify Analytics uses server-side request log analysis; no JavaScript tracker injected. Privacy policy page documents that no personal data is collected. No third-party embeds (social share buttons, YouTube) that set tracking cookies.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repository -> GitHub Actions on PR: run eleventy --dryrun, axe-core accessibility scan, Lighthouse CI. On merge to main: Netlify build hook triggers production deploy. Netlify Deploy Previews for every PR branch. Custom domain: ir.creditunion.org with Netlify-managed Let's Encrypt TLS certificate.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, npm ci (install Eleventy and plugins), npx @11ty/eleventy --dryrun (validate build), axe-core CLI scan on built HTML (fail on critical violations), Lighthouse CI (fail if performance or accessibility score under 90). On main merge: POST to Netlify build hook. Build time gate: fail if Eleventy build exceeds 60 seconds.
devops.rollback_strategy: Netlify maintains full deployment history; rollback to any prior deploy is a one-click operation in the Netlify dashboard or via `netlify rollback` CLI. No database to roll back. Previous deploy is served instantly from CDN on rollback.

data.entities: earnings_reports (Markdown with frontmatter: title, quarter, year, summary, pdf_path, published_date, tags), governance_documents (frontmatter: title, doc_type ENUM minutes/bylaw/policy, meeting_date, pdf_path, published_date), community_impact_summaries (frontmatter: title, year, highlights_list, pdf_path), press_releases (frontmatter: title, published_date, summary, body_markdown), seo_metadata (per-page: og_title, og_description, schema_type JSON-LD)
data.storage_design: All content stored as Markdown files and JSON data files in the Git repository under src/. PDFs stored in the repository for small files (under 5MB) or on Netlify Large Media (Git LFS) for larger archives. No database, no CMS API calls at build time.
data.migration_notes: Legacy HTML pages scraped and converted to Markdown via turndown npm package in a one-time migration script. Legacy PDF URLs mapped to new paths in Netlify _redirects file to preserve SEO link equity. 47 legacy page URL redirects documented in src/_redirects.

target_output.thinking_summary: Straightforward JAMstack migration for a credit union investor relations site. Key decisions: Eleventy 2.0 with Nunjucks for content collections that auto-generate archive pages, Netlify Edge CDN for hosting and deploy pipeline, CSP and security headers via _headers file (not meta tags), and axe-core + Lighthouse CI gates to enforce ADA Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. No backend runtime, no database - the spec explicitly calls for static_website which means no server-side components. Data sensitivity is none so no compliance_context field required.
target_output.title: Credit Union Investor Relations JAMstack Site
target_output.executive_summary: A static Eleventy 2.0 website migrating the credit union's legacy investor relations microsite to a JAMstack architecture, serving quarterly earnings summaries, board governance documents, community impact annual reports, and press releases to credit union members and NCUA examiners. Built with Nunjucks templates, auto-paginated document archive collections, and structured data markup for SEO. Deployed to Netlify Edge CDN with ADA Section 508 accessibility compliance enforced in CI via axe-core and Lighthouse gates. Content editors manage document uploads through a Git PR workflow.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static site with no server-side runtime. Eleventy 2.0 static site generator transforms Markdown content files and Nunjucks templates into pre-rendered HTML at build time. Built HTML, CSS, and PDF documents are deployed to Netlify Edge CDN with global distribution. GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs Eleventy build validation, axe-core accessibility scanning, and Lighthouse CI performance checks on every pull request. Production deploys trigger via Netlify build hook on merge to main. Legacy URL redirects handled by Netlify's _redirects file at the CDN edge. Security headers served via Netlify _headers file, not injected by JavaScript. No cookies, no JavaScript analytics tracker, no backend API calls.
target_output.technology_stack: Static Site Generator: Eleventy 2.0 with Nunjucks template engine. Plugins: eleventy-img (WebP optimization with srcset), eleventy-plugin-syntaxhighlight (code blocks in docs), @11ty/eleventy-navigation (breadcrumb navigation). CSS: PostCSS with custom properties; no CSS framework. CI: GitHub Actions with @axe-core/cli and @lhci/cli. Hosting: Netlify Edge CDN. DNS: Netlify-managed with Let's Encrypt TLS (auto-renewed). Analytics: Netlify Analytics (server-side, cookieless).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Earnings Reports: archive index auto-generated from Eleventy earnings_reports collection, paginated 10 per page, sorted by published_date desc. Each report page shows quarter/year, summary prose, and PDF download button. Governance Documents: index grouped by doc_type (Board Minutes, Bylaws, Policies); PDF links annotated with file size for screen readers. Community Impact: annual report pages with highlights list and full PDF download. Press Releases: archive with published_date and summary excerpt. SEO: JSON-LD structured data markup (Organization, BreadcrumbList, NewsArticle) on every page. Legacy URL redirects: 47 _redirects entries preserving prior URL structure.
target_output.system_components: Eleventy Build Process (transforms Markdown + Nunjucks into static HTML; collections API auto-discovers content files; pagination plugin generates archive index pages). eleventy-img Transform (converts source images to WebP with srcset during build; outputs responsive markup). Netlify CDN (serves built HTML and assets with edge caching; enforces HTTPS; applies _headers and _redirects rules). GitHub Actions CI (build validation, axe-core accessibility scan, Lighthouse CI performance gate). Netlify Analytics Dashboard (aggregated page view and visit data; no client-side JS injected).
target_output.workflows: Document Upload Workflow: content editor creates a new Markdown file in src/earnings-reports/2026-Q1.md with frontmatter (title, quarter, year, summary, pdf_path, published_date), commits the corresponding PDF to src/pdfs/ (or Netlify Large Media for files over 5MB), opens a GitHub PR. GitHub Actions runs Eleventy dry-run build to validate frontmatter, axe-core scan on generated HTML, and Lighthouse CI gate. On PR approval and merge to main, Netlify build hook triggers production rebuild and CDN deploy within 2 minutes. Legacy URL Migration: a one-time migration script (scripts/migrate-legacy.js) scrapes 47 legacy CMS HTML pages using node-html-parser, converts body content to Markdown via turndown, extracts metadata for frontmatter, and maps legacy URLs to new paths generating the _redirects file. Script run once and then archived.
target_output.data_model: earnings_reports (Markdown frontmatter: title STRING, quarter STRING Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4, year INTEGER, summary TEXT, pdf_path STRING relative to site root, published_date DATE, featured BOOLEAN, og_description STRING). governance_documents (frontmatter: title, doc_type ENUM minutes/bylaw/policy/charter, meeting_date DATE nullable, pdf_path, published_date, active BOOLEAN). community_impact_summaries (frontmatter: title, year INTEGER, highlights STRING[], pdf_path, published_date). press_releases (frontmatter: title, published_date, summary TEXT, tags STRING[], body Markdown content). seo_metadata (per-page _data file: og_title, og_description, schema_type, canonical_url).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Static URLs served from CDN: /earnings-reports/ (paginated archive), /earnings-reports/2026-q1/ (individual report), /governance/ (document index), /governance/board-minutes/ (minutes archive), /community-impact/ (annual report index), /community-impact/2025/ (individual year), /press-releases/ (paginated archive). PDF files served from /pdfs/ path. Legacy redirects: e.g. /investor-relations/quarterly-reports.html -> /earnings-reports/ (301). All URLs are lowercase with hyphens, matching NCUA link citation patterns.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify for all requests; HTTP redirects to HTTPS at CDN edge. Security headers via src/_headers file applied at Netlify CDN: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'none'; form-action 'none'. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin. Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=(). No cookies set; no JavaScript analytics tracker injected. WCAG 2.1 AA compliance enforced via axe-core CI gate (zero critical violations required). ADA Section 508: semantic HTML5 structure, skip-to-content link, alt text required in Markdown frontmatter (Eleventy build fails if alt_text field is missing on image data).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on PR: npm ci, npx @11ty/eleventy --dryrun (build validation), @axe-core/cli --dir=_site (fail on any critical violations), Lighthouse CI (performance and accessibility must score >= 90). Build time gate: Eleventy build must complete in under 60 seconds (checked via time command in CI). On merge to main: POST to Netlify build hook triggers production build. Netlify build runs Eleventy, optimizes images, deploys to global CDN. Custom domain ir.creditunion.org configured with Netlify DNS. Let's Encrypt TLS certificate auto-renewed by Netlify. Rollback: Netlify dashboard one-click rollback to any prior successful deploy; takes effect in under 30 seconds. Netlify Deploy Previews generated for every PR branch for stakeholder review before merge.
target_output.observability: Netlify Analytics: aggregated page views, unique visitors, top referrers, and 404 error report - no PII, no cookies, no JavaScript tracker. GitHub Actions build logs show Eleventy build time, axe-core violation count, and Lighthouse score per PR. Netlify build notifications via Slack webhook on build failure or deploy success. Broken PDF link detection: weekly GitHub Actions scheduled job runs linkinator npm package against production URL, reports 404s as GitHub issues.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify Pro plan at $19/month covers custom domain, Netlify Analytics, Netlify Large Media for PDF storage, and unlimited builds. No database, no compute instances, no server-side runtime costs. CDN edge scaling is automatic and unlimited on Netlify. Content team uses existing GitHub accounts at no additional cost. Total estimated cost: $19/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): Eleventy project scaffold with Nunjucks base layout, earnings_reports and governance_documents collections, archive index pages with pagination, PDF download buttons, HTTPS + security headers via _headers, axe-core and Lighthouse CI gates, GitHub Actions pipeline, Netlify deploy. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Community impact and press release collections, JSON-LD structured data markup, legacy URL migration script and _redirects file, eleventy-img WebP optimization, broken link detection scheduled job. Phase 3 (1 week): Netlify Analytics integration, content editor documentation, stakeholder review of all 47 legacy URL redirects, production launch with SEO handoff.
target_output.development_guidelines: All document content types must define required frontmatter fields enforced via Eleventy computed data and build-fail on missing required fields (title, published_date, pdf_path). No inline JavaScript on any page; CSP allows no script-src by default. Nunjucks macros used for reusable PDF download button and breadcrumb components - no duplication across templates. Images must go through eleventy-img plugin; raw img tags with static src are disallowed. Commit message convention: type(scope): description (e.g. content(earnings): add 2026-Q1 report). PRs require one review from engineering for template/config changes; content-only PRs (Markdown + PDF) can be merged by content editors.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PDFs stored in Git LFS (Netlify Large Media) can cause slow clone times if archive grows beyond 2GB. Mitigation: audit PDF archive size annually; migrate historical PDFs to an S3 bucket with CloudFront if Git LFS bandwidth exceeds Netlify plan limits. Risk: Legacy URL regex redirect rules in _redirects may not cover all legacy paths, causing 404s for bookmarked NCUA examiner links. Mitigation: weekly linkinator scan emails compliance team with any new 404s for review. Tradeoff: Nunjucks templates over Markdown-only approach provides richer layout control but adds template complexity for content editors. Mitigation: provide a content editor guide with copy-paste frontmatter templates for each document type.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: PDF storage: large PDFs (over 5MB) stored via Netlify Large Media (Git LFS); smaller PDFs committed directly to repository. Multilingual support deferred to Year 2 roadmap - Eleventy i18n plugin will support Spanish without a rebuild of the template system. Interactive calculator widget deferred pending credit union management system API availability.

metadata.notes: Round 1 static JAMstack migration for credit union investor relations. Eleventy 2.0, Netlify CDN, ADA 508 compliance. No backend runtime - static_website project class.

profile.domain: Financial Services - Card Data Tokenization Pipeline

project_goal.value: Build a security-hardened data pipeline that ingests customer transaction records from Visa and Mastercard network data feeds, tokenizes sensitive PANs using format-preserving encryption via Vault Transit, and loads de-identified datasets into the Snowflake analytics warehouse for fraud analytics teams.
project_goal.rationale: Fraud analytics teams require access to transaction patterns without handling raw card numbers; FPE tokenization satisfies PCI-DSS scope reduction goals.

target_users.value: Fraud analytics engineers and data scientists who query de-identified transaction datasets in Snowflake; data engineering team responsible for pipeline operations and on-call response.
target_users.rationale: Two distinct consumer groups: engineers running analytics queries and ops team maintaining the pipeline.

project_class.rationale: No user-facing UI; the system is a scheduled batch ingestion and transformation pipeline.

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline ingests external network files, runs Spark transforms, and integrates with Vault Transit and Snowflake.

complexity_level.rationale: FPE tokenization with Vault Transit, PCI-DSS scope management, and distributed Spark processing on EMR Serverless add significant architectural complexity.

risk_level.rationale: Raw card data in flight creates Level 1 PCI-DSS scope; a breach or misconfiguration could expose millions of PANs.

data_sensitivity.rationale: PAN data is classified as cardholder data under PCI-DSS; requires maximum data-at-rest and in-transit protections.

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline ingests external network files over private SFTP circuits; analytics output is accessible only to authenticated internal users in Snowflake.

access_model.value: IAM role-based access; Snowflake RBAC grants to analytics_role and data_engineering_role; KMS key policies restrict decryption to pipeline execution role ARN only. No human IAM user has direct PAN decryption access.
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access model required for PCI-DSS Requirement 7; separation between analytics consumers and pipeline operators enforced at Snowflake role level.

feature_scope.value: Nightly ingestion of Visa and Mastercard network settlement files from encrypted S3 landing zone; PAN tokenization via Vault Transit FPE (FF1 algorithm); data quality assertions (row counts, schema validation, null checks); Snowflake upsert of tokenized transaction records; data lineage tracking per pipeline run; PagerDuty alerting on failure or data quality breach.
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope of the tokenization pipeline; downstream ML scoring is out of scope.

mvp_scope.value: Single-network ingestion (Visa only), PAN tokenization via Vault Transit, Snowflake load, and row-count reconciliation with CloudWatch alerting.
mvp_scope.rationale: Mastercard feed deferred to Phase 2 to limit PCI audit surface for initial delivery.

security_baseline.value: All S3 buckets encrypted with customer-managed KMS CMKs; TLS 1.3 for all data in transit; Vault Transit FPE for PAN tokenization; no raw PAN written to disk outside Vault-protected processing; EMR instance isolation via VPC private subnets with no internet egress; CloudTrail logging for all S3 and KMS API calls.
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS Requirements 3 and 4 mandate encryption at rest and in transit; Vault Transit is the approved tokenization service.

frontend_stack.value: none - data pipeline with no user-facing interface; operations teams use CloudWatch dashboards and Datadog for monitoring.
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required; pipeline is a batch ETL system triggered by EventBridge.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Spark 3.5 on EMR Serverless
backend_stack.rationale: Spark handles large-volume card network files efficiently; EMR Serverless eliminates cluster management overhead and costs zero between runs.

data_platform.value: Snowflake (analytics target); S3 (landing zone and staging); PostgreSQL 16 on RDS (pipeline metadata and lineage records)
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake is the enterprise analytics warehouse; S3 provides durable staging; Postgres stores lightweight operational metadata for QSA reporting.

hosting_target.value: EMR Serverless for Spark jobs; S3 for landing, staging, and archive zones; AWS Lambda for orchestration triggers; all resources in us-east-1 VPC private subnets.
hosting_target.rationale: EMR Serverless scales to zero between nightly runs, eliminating idle cluster costs; us-east-1 collocates with Snowflake PrivateLink endpoint.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw card network files retained in S3 landing zone for 7 days before deletion; tokenized transaction records retained in Snowflake for 24 months for fraud model training; pipeline run logs retained for 12 months per PCI audit requirements.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: PCI-DSS Requirement 9.4 limits raw cardholder data retention; fraud model training requires at least 18 months of history.

future_scope.value: Add Mastercard and Amex network feeds in Phase 2; implement real-time tokenization via Kinesis for card-present authorization events; add differential privacy noise injection for external data sharing with fintech partners.
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expands network coverage; real-time streaming is a longer-term architectural evolution once batch pipeline is proven.

constraints.value: Must achieve PCI-DSS Level 1 certification; no raw PANs may persist in Snowflake, S3 staging, or EMR job logs; all processing must remain within us-east-1 for data residency; pipeline must complete nightly run within a 4-hour batch window.
constraints.rationale: Card network rules and internal PCI policy impose strict PAN handling constraints; 4-hour window preserves analytics team SLA.

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for EMR Serverless job metrics and Lambda triggers; Datadog for pipeline SLO tracking (run duration, row throughput, error rate); structured JSON logs for every Spark job stage; PagerDuty alert on pipeline failure or data quality assertion breach.
observability_baseline.rationale: Pipeline failures during the batch window require immediate on-call response to meet daily analytics SLA.

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code via Terraform for all AWS resources; GitOps deployment via GitHub Actions; no manual AWS console changes in production.
execution_preference.rationale: PCI-DSS Requirement 6.4 mandates change management controls; IaC and GitOps satisfy this with full audit trail.

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS Level 1 (card data handling per Visa/Mastercard network rules); GLBA Safeguards Rule (financial data protection); SOC 2 Type II for the data handling environment; annual QSA assessment required.
compliance_context.rationale: Processing Visa/Mastercard settlement data triggers PCI-DSS Level 1 scope; GLBA applies as a financial institution; SOC 2 required by enterprise vendor policy.

project.goal: Tokenize card PAN data from network feeds and load de-identified records into Snowflake for fraud analytics
project.target_users: Fraud analytics engineers and data engineering ops team
project.timeline: Phase 1 MVP in 10 weeks; Mastercard feed in Phase 2

backend.framework: Apache Spark 3.5 on EMR Serverless
backend.orchestration: AWS Lambda triggered by EventBridge cron at 01:00 UTC
backend.tokenization: Vault Transit FPE (FF1 algorithm, card-pan-v1 key)

security.auth: IAM roles with least-privilege; KMS key policies
security.encryption: SSE-KMS CMK per S3 bucket; TLS 1.3 in transit; Vault Transit FPE for PANs

data.metadata: PostgreSQL 16 RDS for pipeline_run_logs and network_feed_manifests

constraints.pci_constraint: no raw PAN in Snowflake, S3 staging, or EMR logs

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is appropriate for a first-pass PCI-DSS-scoped data pipeline. Limiting MVP to Visa-only ingestion is a sound risk reduction approach that shrinks the initial audit surface. The 4-hour batch window SLA is achievable given EMR Serverless scaling but should be validated against peak file sizes from Visa network settlement volumes before finalizing.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: EMR Serverless with Spark 3.5 is the right choice for large network file processing without persistent cluster costs. The critical design decision is the Vault Transit integration point: tokenization must happen within the Spark executor context before any PAN is staged to S3. The PostgreSQL lineage store is lightweight and appropriate; avoid putting lineage in Snowflake to prevent a circular dependency during recovery scenarios when Snowflake may itself be unavailable.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS Requirement 3.5 mandates that PANs are never stored unprotected. The architecture must guarantee zero raw PAN persistence to S3 staging, EMR logs, or Snowflake. EMR Spark logging must be configured to suppress task-level stdout for the tokenization stage. Vault Transit key rotation policy must be defined and the tokenization mapping table must itself be encrypted with a separate KMS CMK.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour nightly batch window is the binding constraint. Spark parallelism configuration on EMR Serverless must be tuned to process peak daily Visa volumes (typically 50-100M records) within this window. CloudWatch alarms should fire at the 2-hour mark if progress is behind projection so on-call can intervene before window expiry.

backend.service_design: Single EMR Serverless Spark application with three sequential stages: (1) Ingest - read raw network files from S3 landing zone, validate schema and row counts against manifest, (2) Tokenize - invoke Vault Transit FPE batch API to replace PANs with format-preserving tokens preserving BIN prefix and card length, (3) Load - write tokenized records to Snowflake via Spark-Snowflake JDBC connector in upsert mode with deduplication on transaction_id.
backend.background_jobs: EventBridge cron triggers Lambda orchestrator nightly at 01:00 UTC; Lambda submits the EMR Serverless job and polls for completion, writing final status to the pipeline_run_logs table in PostgreSQL.
backend.failure_handling: Spark job partitioned into checkpointed stages; on failure the pipeline restarts from the last successful checkpoint rather than reprocessing the full file. Vault Transit errors trigger immediate job abort with PagerDuty P1 alert rather than partial tokenization.
backend.backend_risks: Vault Transit API rate limits could bottleneck batch tokenization at high volumes; mitigation is bulk FPE batch calls (up to 100 PANs per request) to reduce API call overhead by 99x compared to per-PAN calls.

security.auth_design: IAM roles with least-privilege; EMR Serverless execution role has S3 GetObject on landing bucket, Vault policy allowing transit/encrypt on the card-pan-v1 key only, and Snowflake JDBC credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
security.secrets_management: All secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation; KMS CMK for S3 bucket encryption with key policy restricting Decrypt to EMR execution role ARN; Vault Transit key version tracked per run in pipeline_run_logs for QSA evidence.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail records all S3 API calls and KMS key usage; Spark application structured logs record job stage, row counts, and Vault call counts without PAN values; PostgreSQL pipeline_run_logs captures input file hash, tokenized output hash, and run duration as QSA audit artifacts.
security.security_risks: EMR Serverless log configuration must explicitly suppress task stdout to prevent accidental PAN leakage into CloudWatch Logs; this must be validated in CI via a log inspection test against synthetic card data before any production run.

data.entities: card_transactions (token_id UUID, network ENUM visa/mastercard, transaction_id VARCHAR PK, masked_pan VARCHAR last-4-only, transaction_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, merchant_id VARCHAR, merchant_category_code CHAR(4), settlement_file_id UUID FK, loaded_at TIMESTAMP), network_feed_manifests (id UUID, network ENUM, s3_key TEXT, file_hash_sha256 CHAR(64), expected_row_count INT, actual_row_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM), pipeline_run_logs (run_id UUID, triggered_at TIMESTAMP, stage VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/success/failed, rows_processed BIGINT, duration_seconds INT, error_message TEXT).
data.storage_design: S3 landing bucket (raw network files, 7-day lifecycle, SSE-KMS landing CMK, VPC-endpoint-only bucket policy); S3 staging bucket (intermediate Spark Parquet output, 24-hour lifecycle deletion); Snowflake analytics schema (card_transactions table, PrivateLink endpoint); PostgreSQL RDS multi-AZ for pipeline metadata.
data.data_risks: Storing pipeline_run_logs in Snowflake instead of PostgreSQL creates a circular dependency: if Snowflake is unavailable the run status cannot be written, making automated recovery impossible. All pipeline metadata must reside in PostgreSQL.

devops.deployment_topology: Terraform modules: S3 buckets with KMS key associations and lifecycle rules, EMR Serverless application config, EventBridge rule, Lambda function, IAM roles, Secrets Manager entries. Snowflake objects (database, schema, tables, roles, grants) managed via separate Terraform with Snowflake provider.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR pipeline runs pytest unit tests (mock Vault and Snowflake via moto/responses), Terraform fmt/validate/plan, and a synthetic end-to-end test with fake PANs verifying tokenization round-trip. Merge to main deploys to dev then staging. Production requires manual approval gate.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch for EMR Serverless job metrics (runtime, vCPU-hours, shuffle bytes); Datadog custom metrics pushed by Lambda (rows_ingested_total, rows_tokenized_total, rows_loaded_total, assertions_failed_count, vault_api_latency_p99); PagerDuty on CloudWatch alarms for pipeline failure and data quality assertion breach.
devops.devops_risks: EMR Serverless cold-start latency of 2-4 minutes must be accounted for in the 4-hour batch window budget; pre-warming via a lightweight probe job submitted 5 minutes before the main job trigger reduces effective cold-start impact.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a PCI-DSS Level 1 data pipeline where the primary architectural concern is ensuring zero raw PAN persistence anywhere in the processing chain. Vault Transit FPE integration must be the first transformation applied before any data touches S3 staging or EMR logs. EMR Serverless is correct for nightly batch economics. The intentional flaw for training signal: the data_model section suggests storing pipeline_run_logs in Snowflake, which contradicts the architect_reasoner review that clearly warns against this circular dependency on Snowflake availability.
target_output.title: Card Transaction Tokenization Pipeline - PCI-DSS Level 1 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly batch data pipeline that ingests Visa and Mastercard settlement files from encrypted S3, tokenizes PANs via Vault Transit format-preserving encryption (FF1), and loads de-identified transaction records into Snowflake for fraud analytics consumption. Built on Apache Spark 3.5 on EMR Serverless with full PCI-DSS Level 1 scope controls, the pipeline guarantees zero raw PAN persistence in any downstream store. PostgreSQL stores operational metadata and data lineage; CloudWatch and Datadog provide observability with PagerDuty escalation on data quality breach.
target_output.architecture_overview: Batch-oriented ETL pipeline running nightly on EMR Serverless. Raw Visa and Mastercard settlement files arrive via encrypted SFTP circuit to an S3 landing bucket (SSE-KMS customer-managed CMK). An EventBridge cron at 01:00 UTC triggers a Lambda orchestrator that submits the Spark job. The Spark application reads raw files, invokes Vault Transit FPE to replace each PAN with a format-preserving token (preserving BIN prefix and card length), and writes tokenized records to Snowflake via JDBC over PrivateLink. No raw PAN exits Spark executor memory; Spark task logging is configured to suppress stdout for tokenization stages. Pipeline run metadata and data lineage (input file hashes, row counts, Vault key versions used) are stored in PostgreSQL RDS. All resources reside in us-east-1 VPC private subnets with no internet egress path.
target_output.technology_stack: Processing: Apache Spark 3.5 on EMR Serverless (Python 3.12 PySpark). Tokenization: HashiCorp Vault Transit FPE (FF1 algorithm, card-pan-v1 key with 90-day rotation). Analytics target: Snowflake via Spark-Snowflake connector over PrivateLink. Staging: S3 with SSE-KMS customer-managed CMKs (separate CMK per bucket). Orchestration: AWS Lambda (Python 3.12) triggered by EventBridge scheduled rule. Pipeline metadata: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS multi-AZ. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Infrastructure: Terraform modules + GitHub Actions CI/CD. Monitoring: CloudWatch + Datadog custom metrics + PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Network File Ingestion: read Visa and Mastercard settlement files from S3 landing zone, validate schema against expected column definitions and row count manifest before tokenization begins. PAN Tokenization Engine: batch-invoke Vault Transit FPE API at 100 PANs per call to produce format-preserving tokens preserving BIN prefix and card length for fraud model feature compatibility. Data Quality Assertions: 12 configurable rules covering null transaction_id, invalid merchant category codes, amount range violations, and expected-vs-actual row count reconciliation. Snowflake Loader: upsert tokenized records with deduplication on transaction_id. Lineage Tracker: record input file hash, Spark application version, Vault key version, and output row counts per run in PostgreSQL. Alerting Engine: PagerDuty P1 on job failure; P2 on assertion breach or batch window overrun.
target_output.system_components: S3 Landing Bucket (SSE-KMS CMK, 7-day lifecycle rule, VPC-endpoint-only bucket policy). S3 Staging Bucket (intermediate Spark Parquet output, 24-hour lifecycle deletion). EMR Serverless Application (Spark 3.5, Python 3.12, private VPC subnet, no public IP, 400 vCPU max concurrency). Lambda Orchestrator (submits EMR job, polls status, writes run metadata to PostgreSQL). EventBridge Rule (nightly cron 01:00 UTC). Vault Transit (FPE key card-pan-v1, 90-day rotation, audit logging enabled). Snowflake Analytics Schema (card_transactions table, PrivateLink endpoint, RBAC with analytics_role and data_engineering_role). PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ (pipeline_run_logs, network_feed_manifests). AWS Secrets Manager (Snowflake JDBC credentials, Vault token). CloudWatch + Datadog Lambda extension for custom metrics.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Ingestion and Tokenization: EventBridge fires at 01:00 UTC; Lambda checks S3 landing zone for new Visa settlement file, validates file hash against manifest, submits EMR Serverless Spark job with job parameters (file S3 key, run_id). Spark reads raw CSV records in 10M-row partitions, validates schema per partition, calls Vault Transit FPE batch API to tokenize PANs (100 PANs per call), writes tokenized Parquet partitions to S3 staging. On Spark completion, Lambda triggers Snowflake COPY INTO from staging, runs 12 data quality assertions, writes pipeline_run_logs record to PostgreSQL with SUCCESS status and row counts. Data Quality Breach Response: if any assertion fails, Lambda halts the Snowflake load, writes FAILED status to pipeline_run_logs with the failing assertion and counts, fires PagerDuty P2 alert. On-call engineer inspects S3 staging (tokenized data only, no PANs) and can rerun the Snowflake load stage independently without re-tokenizing.
target_output.data_model: card_transactions in Snowflake: (transaction_id VARCHAR PK, network ENUM('visa','mastercard'), token_id UUID NOT NULL, masked_pan VARCHAR(19) last-4-digits only, transaction_date DATE, posting_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, currency_code CHAR(3), merchant_id VARCHAR(15), merchant_category_code CHAR(4), authorization_code VARCHAR(6), acquirer_bin CHAR(6), settlement_file_id UUID FK, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). network_feed_manifests in PostgreSQL: (id UUID PK, network ENUM, s3_key TEXT, file_hash_sha256 CHAR(64), expected_row_count INT, actual_row_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/ingested/failed). pipeline_run_logs in Snowflake -- NOTE: storing pipeline operational metadata in Snowflake creates a circular dependency; if Snowflake is unavailable during a run failure the status record cannot be written and automated recovery is blocked. This table should reside in PostgreSQL as the architect reasoner recommended. data_quality_assertions (assertion_id UUID, run_id UUID FK, rule_name VARCHAR, expected_value TEXT, actual_value TEXT, passed BOOLEAN, evaluated_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: Internal interfaces only. Lambda orchestrator calls EMR Serverless API: POST /applications/{app-id}/jobruns (submit Spark job with PySpark entry point and job parameters). GET /applications/{app-id}/jobruns/{run-id} (polled every 60 seconds for status). Vault Transit calls: POST /v1/transit/encrypt/card-pan-v1 with batch_input array of {plaintext: base64(PAN)} up to 100 items per call. Snowflake load: COPY INTO card_transactions FROM @staging_stage FILE_FORMAT=(TYPE=PARQUET) PURGE=TRUE executed via JDBC. No public-facing API; all integrations are internal AWS service calls and Snowflake JDBC over PrivateLink.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS Level 1 controls: PANs tokenized via Vault Transit FPE before any write to persistent storage (satisfies Requirement 3.5 render PANs unreadable). EMR Spark log4j config filters suppress tokenization stage task stdout (prevents PAN leakage to CloudWatch Logs). S3 bucket policies deny access from non-VPC principals and enforce SSE-KMS with separate CMKs for landing vs. staging. KMS key policy restricts Decrypt action to EMR execution role ARN only; no human IAM user can decrypt without explicit break-glass procedure. Vault Transit key version tracked in pipeline_run_logs for QSA evidence of rotation compliance. CloudTrail enabled on all S3, KMS, and Secrets Manager API calls with 12-month log retention. Annual QSA artifacts: pipeline_run_logs exports, CloudTrail reports, Vault audit logs, Terraform state history.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure managed as Terraform modules: S3 buckets with KMS key associations and lifecycle policies, EMR Serverless application definition, EventBridge rule, Lambda function (Python 3.12, 512MB, 15-minute timeout), IAM roles and policies, Secrets Manager entries. Snowflake objects (database, schema, tables, warehouse, roles, grants) managed via Terraform Snowflake provider in a separate state file. CI/CD via GitHub Actions: PR pipeline runs pytest unit tests with moto/responses mocking Vault and Snowflake, Terraform fmt/validate/plan. Merge to main applies Terraform to dev and runs synthetic end-to-end test using fake BIN-range PANs. Staging promotion requires manual approval from data engineering lead. Production deployment tracked in GitHub with PR audit trail satisfying PCI Requirement 6.4. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, reduced EMR concurrency limit), staging (full scale, real Visa test file provided by Visa developer portal), production (multi-AZ RDS, 400 vCPU EMR concurrency, Snowflake PrivateLink). Rollback: Terraform state rollback for infrastructure; data issues resolved by rerunning load stage from persisted S3 staging output.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch: EMR Serverless job duration, vCPU-hours consumed, driver and executor failure counts published as CloudWatch metrics. Datadog custom metrics pushed by Lambda completion handler: rows_ingested_total, rows_tokenized_total, rows_loaded_total, assertions_failed_count, vault_api_latency_p99, pipeline_duration_seconds. SLO: 95% of nightly runs complete within 3.5 hours (leaving 30-minute buffer before 4-hour window). PagerDuty integration: P1 on EMR job failure or Lambda timeout; P2 on data quality assertion breach or batch window overrun (pipeline still running at 3-hour mark). PostgreSQL pipeline_run_logs provides QSA-auditable record of every run with input file hashes, row counts, and Vault key version used.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EMR Serverless scales to zero between nightly runs; estimated 400 vCPU-hours per run at $0.052/vCPU-hour equals approximately $20/night ($600/month). S3 storage for 7-day landing retention plus 24-month Snowflake history estimated $150/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small multi-AZ approximately $80/month. Total estimated $850/month. Scaling path: EMR Serverless auto-scales within the 400 vCPU cap; Snowflake warehouse size upgraded independently from XS to S if load stage duration exceeds 30 minutes during Phase 2 Mastercard volume doubling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Visa-only settlement file ingestion, Vault Transit FPE tokenization (100-PAN batch calls), Snowflake load with 12 data quality assertions, PostgreSQL pipeline metadata store, CloudWatch and Datadog observability, PagerDuty alerting, Terraform IaC, GitHub Actions CI with synthetic PAN end-to-end test, PCI-DSS QSA evidence collection framework. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Add Mastercard settlement file ingestion as a parallel Spark stage, extend data quality rules for Mastercard-specific field set, expand lineage tracking to cover dual-network runs. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Evaluate real-time tokenization path via Kinesis Data Streams for card-present authorization events; assess differential privacy noise injection for external dataset sharing with fintech partners.
target_output.development_guidelines: All PAN-touching Spark code must be reviewed by a Security-designated engineer before merge to main. Spark tokenization stage must have unit tests using synthetic PANs with fake BIN ranges not matching real card issuer ranges. Terraform changes require DevOps lead review before staging promotion. No direct AWS console changes in staging or production environments; all changes via Terraform or GitHub Actions pipeline. Log statements in the tokenization stage require explicit Security sign-off in the PR review confirming no PAN values appear in any log output.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vault Transit API rate limiting bottlenecks peak nightly batch if Visa file volumes spike seasonally. Mitigation: batch FPE calls at 100 PANs per request; pre-negotiate rate limit increase with Vault platform team before Phase 2 Mastercard addition doubles volume. Risk: EMR Serverless cold-start of 2-4 minutes consumes the batch window; mitigation is a lightweight probe job submitted 5 minutes before main job to pre-warm the Serverless application. Tradeoff: FPE tokenization preserves BIN prefix and card length for fraud model feature compatibility but means tokens are structurally distinguishable from random identifiers; this is acceptable within the PCI scope because tokens cannot be reversed without Vault access. Risk: pipeline_run_logs incorrectly modeled in Snowflake in the data_model section; this must be corrected to PostgreSQL before implementation to avoid circular dependency on Snowflake availability during failure recovery.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Vault Transit FPE over AWS Tokenization Service because Vault supports FF1 format-preserving encryption that preserves BIN structure required by fraud feature engineering. Confirmed Snowflake PrivateLink over public endpoint to avoid S3 egress through an internet gateway. Deferred Mastercard feed to Phase 2 to limit PCI audit surface for initial QSA assessment. Confirmed PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for pipeline metadata due to relational query requirements in QSA reporting dashboards.

metadata.notes: Intentional flaw: data_model section routes pipeline_run_logs to Snowflake, contradicting architect_reasoner and data specialist warnings about circular dependency on Snowflake availability. The risks_and_tradeoffs section flags this inconsistency explicitly, making this a training example of an internally inconsistent plan.

profile.domain: Financial Services - Real-Time Streaming Fraud Detection Research

project_goal.value: Build a real-time streaming research prototype that evaluates whether sub-second transaction fraud detection using a Flink-backed feature store materially improves AUC versus the existing nightly batch-scored fraud model, with a production shadow-mode A/B experiment framework.
project_goal.rationale: The risk analytics team hypothesizes that streaming feature freshness (30s velocity windows) captures fraud patterns invisible to 24-hour-old batch features; this prototype validates that hypothesis before committing to a full production streaming rollout.

target_users.value: Data scientists and ML engineers on the fraud models team who build and operate experiments; risk analytics stakeholders reviewing daily AUC reports via Grafana dashboards.
target_users.rationale: Two distinct audiences: engineers iterating on the streaming pipeline and business stakeholders consuming experiment results.

project_class.rationale: Experimental system operating in shadow mode only; not a production decision system. Architecture prioritizes experiment validity and observability over production SLAs.

capabilities.rationale: Flink streaming backend, Cassandra feature store, ClickHouse analytics, Grafana frontend dashboards, and real-time data processing all required for a complete experiment platform.

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-window Flink aggregations, Cassandra feature store integration, deterministic A/B routing, model serving with sub-50ms latency, and SR 11-7 governance artifacts combine into a genuinely complex streaming architecture.

risk_level.rationale: Live transaction data flows through this prototype in shadow mode; any data breach or mishandling of cardholder transaction records creates GLBA regulatory exposure.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Real transaction events contain tokenized card identifiers, merchant details, and amounts; classified as financial data requiring GLBA protections even in research context.

external_exposure.rationale: Prototype operates in shadow mode on internal transaction stream; Grafana dashboards accessible only to authenticated internal users via Google IAP.

access_model.value: Google IAP for Grafana dashboard access; GCP Workload Identity for service-to-service authentication between Flink, Cassandra, ClickHouse, and Redis; separate GCP service accounts per component with least-privilege IAM bindings. No long-lived service account keys.
access_model.rationale: Workload Identity eliminates long-lived service account keys; IAP provides zero-trust access control for the analytics frontend without a separate auth service.

feature_scope.value: Real-time Kafka consumer ingesting live transaction stream in shadow mode; Flink jobs computing 30s/5min/1hr velocity features per card and merchant; Cassandra feature store with point-in-time lookup; fraud model scoring within 50ms end-to-end; deterministic A/B experiment routing for 5% of transactions; ClickHouse analytics for AUC/precision/recall/PSI; Grafana dashboards with daily experiment reports; automated SR 11-7 artifact generation per model version.
feature_scope.rationale: Full experimental platform required to produce statistically valid comparison between streaming and batch feature regimes.

mvp_scope.value: Single Flink job computing 30s card-velocity features, basic gradient boosting fraud scoring via FastAPI, deterministic A/B routing for 5% of live transaction volume, ClickHouse AUC tracking, Grafana experiment dashboard.
mvp_scope.rationale: 5% traffic shadow allows statistical significance within 2 weeks without impacting production fraud decisioning.

security_baseline.value: All data in transit encrypted via TLS 1.3; Cassandra and ClickHouse at-rest encryption enabled; no cardholder PAN stored in any component (tokenized card_id only); GCP Workload Identity for all service auth; network policies restricting Flink cluster egress to approved internal endpoints only.
security_baseline.rationale: GLBA Safeguards Rule requires encryption at rest and in transit for customer financial data even in research contexts.

frontend_stack.value: Grafana 10 dashboards provisioned as code via grafonnet; ClickHouse data source plugin; three dashboard files for experiment results, latency SLO, and feature health.
frontend_stack.rationale: Grafana provides sufficient analytics visualization capability without custom frontend development overhead for a research prototype.

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Apache Flink 1.19 for streaming pipeline; Python 3.12 FastAPI for model scoring microservice
backend_stack.rationale: Flink 1.19 on Java 21 provides virtual thread support for high-concurrency state operations; Python FastAPI wraps the scikit-learn gradient boosting model for scoring.

data_platform.value: Apache Cassandra 4 for feature store (low-latency point lookups, composite partition key); ClickHouse for analytics (AUC and PSI metrics over billions of scoring events); Redis 7 for feature hot-cache; Kafka as transaction event source.
data_platform.rationale: Cassandra provides sub-5ms feature lookup required for 50ms end-to-end scoring budget; ClickHouse handles analytical aggregations over billions of scoring events efficiently.

hosting_target.value: Dataproc for Flink cluster; GKE Autopilot for FastAPI scoring service, ClickHouse, Cassandra, Redis, Grafana, and MLflow; all in GCP us-central1.
hosting_target.rationale: Dataproc simplifies Flink cluster lifecycle management; GKE Autopilot handles scaling for auxiliary services without node pool management.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw transaction events retained in Kafka for 7 days (consumer group replay window); Cassandra feature store records retained for 90 days with TTL (experiment duration); ClickHouse experiment metrics retained for 24 months; no cardholder PAN stored in any system component.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GLBA limits unnecessary retention of customer financial data; experiment metrics must be retained for model governance review and SR 11-7 audit.

future_scope.value: Graduate streaming feature model to production inference path if AUC improvement validated above threshold; extend Flink jobs to compute merchant-network graph features; add SHAP explainability layer to scoring service for SR 11-7 model documentation.
future_scope.rationale: Prototype success criteria defined upfront; production graduation path already designed to avoid throwaway architecture.

constraints.value: End-to-end scoring latency must be under 50ms at p95 (Kafka receipt to ClickHouse score write); prototype must operate in shadow mode only with no decisions affecting production fraud scores; GCP us-central1 only; no raw PAN stored in Cassandra, ClickHouse, or any log output.
constraints.rationale: 50ms latency is the threshold at which streaming features can be incorporated into synchronous authorization decisioning in the future production path.

observability_baseline.value: Flink metrics UI for job DAG health, backpressure, and checkpoint duration; Prometheus scraping Flink and FastAPI metrics; Grafana dashboards for streaming latency p50/p95/p99, feature store hit rate, A/B routing distribution, and daily AUC delta; PagerDuty P1 if scoring p95 latency exceeds 75ms for 5 minutes; P2 if Flink checkpoint fails 3 consecutive times.
observability_baseline.rationale: Latency SLA is the primary health signal; checkpoint failures indicate state backend issues that could corrupt feature store consistency.

execution_preference.value: Iterative experimentation cycles; Flink job changes deployed via Flink REST API savepoint-based rolling upgrade; model versions registered in MLflow before serving; no production traffic impact during prototype updates.
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype must support rapid iteration without multi-week deployment cycles typical of production systems.

compliance_context.value: SR 11-7 model risk management applies to experimental models operating on live production transaction data in shadow mode; GLBA Safeguards Rule for financial transaction data handling throughout the pipeline; internal model governance requires experiment design approval and results review before any production graduation decision.
compliance_context.rationale: Shadow mode on live transaction data triggers SR 11-7 model risk governance regardless of experimental status; GLBA applies to financial data processing in any context.

project.goal: Validate that streaming feature freshness improves fraud AUC versus batch baseline via A/B shadow experiment on live transaction stream
project.target_users: Fraud ML engineers and risk analytics stakeholders
project.timeline: Round 3 revision; MVP in Round 1, Rounds 2-3 addressing latency and feature consistency issues

frontend.framework: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse data source plugin

backend.framework: Apache Flink 1.19 on Dataproc

security.auth: GCP Workload Identity + Google IAP
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit; Cassandra and ClickHouse at-rest encryption

data.feature_store: Apache Cassandra 4 (composite partition key card_id, feature_window)
data.stream: Kafka managed (existing production consumer group isolation)

devops.monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus + Flink metrics UI + PagerDuty

constraints.mode: shadow only, no production decisions affected

confirmed_decisions.experiment_routing: deterministic hash of transaction_id mod 100 (resolved ISS-003)

reasoner_reviews.product: By Round 3 the core A/B framework is operational and delivering daily AUC reports. The focus issues from Round 2 -- feature inconsistency under Flink checkpoint recovery (ISS-002) and A/B routing skew (ISS-003) -- are addressed in this revision. The 5% shadow volume is now producing statistically significant AUC deltas after 3 weeks of operation. The plan should document the graduation criteria explicitly so stakeholders understand what AUC threshold and significance level trigger a production rollout proposal.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Round 2 issue of Cassandra hot partition under high card-velocity write load was resolved by switching to composite (card_id, feature_window) partition key distributing write load across the ring. The Flink checkpoint interval tuning from 60s to 10s with incremental RocksDB checkpoints directly bounds the feature freshness gap after recovery to 10 seconds, resolving ISS-002. The scoring service now implements a 15-second feature freshness guard that falls back to batch features and sets used_fallback=true in ClickHouse, giving experiment analysts visibility into fallback frequency.
reasoner_reviews.security: GLBA compliance is maintained: no PAN is stored in Cassandra (only tokenized card_id from the upstream FPE tokenization pipeline). The A/B routing decision recorded in ClickHouse contains only experiment_id, transaction_id, routing_group, score, and outcome -- no cardholder PAN or full account identifier. SR 11-7 model governance artifacts are now generated automatically by the scoring service: each model version records input feature distributions and output score distributions to MLflow for model risk team review.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 3 p95 latency measurements show 38ms end-to-end (Kafka receipt to ClickHouse write), comfortably within the 50ms SLA after the Cassandra composite partition key fix reduced write contention. The 10-second Flink checkpoint interval adds approximately 3ms latency variance at p99 but does not breach the p95 target. The deterministic hash routing for ISS-003 adds zero latency compared to the previous Redis TTL lookup path.

backend.service_design: Three Flink jobs on Dataproc: (1) FeatureComputeJob (parallelism 24) -- consumes Kafka transaction topic, computes 30s/5min/1hr tumbling window aggregates per (card_id, feature_window), writes to Cassandra with 10s incremental RocksDB checkpoint to GCS. (2) ScoringJob (parallelism 16) -- consumes same Kafka topic, computes routing_group via deterministic hash(transaction_id) mod 100, performs async Cassandra feature lookup with 30ms timeout and 15s freshness guard, calls FastAPI scoring microservice, writes experiment record to ClickHouse. (3) ExperimentMetricsJob (parallelism 4, batch mode daily at 06:00 UTC) -- joins ClickHouse experiment_scores with 24-hour-delayed ground truth fraud labels, computes AUC/precision/recall/PSI, writes to experiment_results.
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI internal scoring endpoint: POST /v1/score with feature vector returns score, model_version, feature_freshness_ms, used_fallback. GET /v1/health returns model_version and cassandra_latency_p99_ms. Called only by Flink ScoringJob via HTTP async sink; no external consumers.
backend.failure_handling: Flink jobs use 10-second incremental RocksDB checkpoints to GCS; on restart FeatureComputeJob replays Kafka from last checkpoint offset, bounding feature recomputation gap to 10 seconds. ScoringJob uses async I/O operator with 30ms Cassandra timeout; timeout triggers fallback to batch score cache in Redis with used_fallback=true logged. Routing assignment is deterministic (hash of transaction_id) so A/B group is stable across Flink restarts with no state dependency.
backend.scaling_notes: Flink parallelism 24 for FeatureComputeJob and 16 for ScoringJob on 8 x n2-standard-4 Dataproc nodes; autoscaling to 16 nodes based on Kafka consumer lag metric. Cassandra 6-node cluster RF=3; write throughput headroom to 50k TPS against peak Kafka consumer lag observed in Round 2 experiments.
backend.backend_risks: Cassandra async I/O operator in Flink can accumulate backpressure if feature lookup latency spikes during GC pauses; Prometheus alert on async operator queue depth exceeding 80% capacity triggers on-call investigation before it affects end-to-end latency SLA.

security.auth_design: GCP Workload Identity bindings: Flink Dataproc service account with Cassandra read/write IAM binding; FastAPI scoring GKE service account with Cassandra read-only and MLflow read access; ClickHouse Grafana service account with ClickHouse read-only; Kafka consumer SASL/SCRAM credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager with 60-day rotation.
security.privacy_controls: No PAN stored anywhere in the system; Kafka topic contains tokenized card_id from the upstream Vault Transit FPE pipeline. ClickHouse experiment_scores stores only transaction_id (not card_id or PAN), routing_group, score, and outcome for experiment analysis. SR 11-7 audit artifacts written to GCS bucket with 24-month retention and object-level access logging.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every FastAPI scoring call logs model_version, feature_freshness_ms, used_fallback, and score to ClickHouse without cardholder identifiers. MLflow tracking server records feature distribution histograms and score distributions per model version per day for model risk team weekly review. Weekly SR 11-7 performance report auto-generated by ExperimentMetricsJob and archived to GCS audit bucket.
security.security_risks: Kafka consumer group ACLs must prevent non-prototype services from reading the shadow transaction stream copy; misconfigured ACLs could expose transaction data to unauthorized consumers. Mitigation: dedicated Kafka consumer group with SASL/SCRAM credentials scoped to Dataproc service account only, validated via Kafka ACL audit in CI integration test.

data.entities: streaming_features in Cassandra: (card_id VARCHAR, feature_window ENUM 30s/5min/1hr, txn_count INT, total_amount_cents BIGINT, distinct_merchants INT, max_amount_cents BIGINT, computed_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY ((card_id, feature_window), computed_at) CLUSTERING ORDER BY computed_at DESC, TTL 90 days). experiment_scores in ClickHouse: (transaction_id UUID, experiment_id VARCHAR, routing_group ENUM streaming/batch, streaming_score FLOAT32, batch_score FLOAT32, actual_fraud_label Nullable(Int8), score_latency_ms UInt16, feature_freshness_ms Nullable(UInt32), used_fallback UInt8, scored_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(scored_at). experiment_results in ClickHouse: (experiment_id VARCHAR, evaluation_date Date, routing_group VARCHAR, auc Float64, precision Float64, recall Float64, psi Float64, sample_count UInt32) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree().
data.storage_design: Cassandra cluster (6 nodes GKE StatefulSet, RF=3, GCP persistent SSD, LZ4 compression, 90-day TTL on streaming_features). ClickHouse (2-node cluster GKE StatefulSet, MergeTree partitioned by scored_at month, pre-aggregated daily materialized views for AUC and PSI queries, 24-month retention). Redis Cluster (GKE, feature hot-cache for batch scores used by fallback path, 24hr TTL).
data.analytics_events: Daily experiment reports: AUC delta streaming vs. batch, PSI for input feature distributions over rolling 13-week window, recall at 10% FPR, average score latency by routing group, fallback rate (used_fallback=true percentage as signal of Cassandra health), Flink checkpoint success rate and average duration.

devops.deployment_topology: Dataproc cluster (Flink 1.19, Java 21, 8 x n2-standard-4 autoscaling to 16 nodes on Kafka consumer lag). GKE Autopilot: FastAPI scoring service (2+ replicas, HPA CPU 60%), ClickHouse StatefulSet (2 nodes, persistent SSD), Cassandra StatefulSet (6 nodes, persistent SSD), Redis Cluster, Grafana (Google IAP ingress), MLflow tracking server (GCS artifact backend). All in GCP us-central1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Flink job unit tests with FlinkMiniCluster fixtures for windowed aggregation correctness, FastAPI pytest suite covering valid lookup/timeout fallback/staleness guard, Grafana dashboard validation via grafonnet lint. Flink job deployment via Flink REST API savepoint-triggered upgrade preserving RocksDB state without reprocessing Kafka. FastAPI and GKE services updated via rolling deployment.
devops.rollback_strategy: Flink jobs: savepoint-based rollback to previous version within 10 minutes preserving feature state. FastAPI: GKE rollout undo to previous Deployment revision. Cassandra partition key schema migration uses blue-green approach: dual-write to old and new partition scheme in parallel for 24 hours, validate consistency, then cut over and drop old partition format.
devops.devops_risks: Dataproc Flink cluster restart during high Kafka consumer lag causes state replay bounded to the last 10-second checkpoint window; if lag is very high (>1 million messages) replay may briefly exceed the 50ms p95 latency target during catch-up before stabilizing. Preemptible VMs on ExperimentMetricsJob (batch, non-latency-sensitive) reduce that job cost by 60%.

frontend.app_structure: Grafana dashboards provisioned as code via grafonnet; three dashboard JSON files committed to repo: experiment-overview.jsonnet, latency-slo.jsonnet, feature-health.jsonnet. Deployed to GKE Grafana instance via Argo CD from the repo.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Experiment Overview dashboard: daily streaming vs. batch AUC and precision/recall delta bar charts; PSI chart for input feature distribution stability over 13-week rolling window; sample count by routing group confirming statistical balance at 5% streaming allocation. Latency SLO dashboard: score latency p50/p95/p99 over 24h rolling window; streaming vs. batch path latency side-by-side; fallback rate trend line. Feature Health dashboard: Cassandra write throughput and p99 write latency per feature_window; feature freshness distribution (time between computed_at and scoring request); Flink job checkpoint success rate and duration histogram.
frontend.performance_notes: ClickHouse materialized views pre-aggregate daily AUC and PSI values to avoid full-scan queries on the multi-billion-row experiment_scores table; Grafana query response time under 2 seconds for all dashboard panels.

ISS-001.title: Cassandra hot partition under high card-velocity write load
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan used card_id alone as Cassandra partition key, concentrating writes for high-velocity BINs on a single node, causing write latency spikes to 120ms+ and breaching the 50ms scoring SLA.

ISS-002.title: Feature freshness gap after Flink checkpoint recovery
ISS-002.detail: 60-second checkpoint interval caused up to 60 seconds of feature state loss on Flink restart, inflating fallback rate to 35% during recovery and invalidating AUC comparison results for those transactions.

ISS-003.title: A/B routing skew due to Redis TTL expiry during peak traffic
ISS-003.detail: A/B routing assignment stored in Redis with 24hr TTL expired for some cards during peak transaction hours, causing those cards to be re-assigned and receive mixed feature regimes within a single day, biasing the AUC comparison.

focus_issues.title: Feature freshness gap after Flink checkpoint recovery
focus_issues.detail: Resolved in Round 3: Flink checkpoint interval reduced from 60s to 10s with incremental RocksDB checkpointing to GCS; feature freshness guard added to FastAPI scoring service rejecting lookups older than 15 seconds and logging used_fallback=true.

focus_issues.title: A/B routing skew due to Redis TTL expiry
focus_issues.detail: Resolved in Round 3: A/B routing switched to deterministic hash of transaction_id mod 100, eliminating all Redis state dependency for routing assignment. Redis retained only for batch score hot-caching in the fallback path.

latest_recommendations: Reduce Flink checkpoint interval to 10 seconds with incremental RocksDB to bound feature freshness gap on recovery
Add a feature freshness guard in the FastAPI scoring service to reject stale Cassandra lookups older than 15 seconds
Switch A/B routing to deterministic hash of transaction_id to eliminate Redis TTL-driven routing instability
Add PSI monitoring panels to Grafana for input feature distributions to detect Cassandra state drift over time
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Streaming Fraud Feature Store - Round 2 Architecture

convergence_state.reason: Two high and medium severity issues unresolved after Round 2; latency SLA met after Cassandra partition key fix but feature freshness gap and routing skew require additional architectural changes

previous_audits.summary: Core streaming architecture is sound but two critical issues prevent valid experiment results: Cassandra hot partition causes latency SLA breaches and 60s checkpoint interval creates unacceptable feature freshness gaps on recovery.
strengths: Flink window aggregation design correctly covers all three required velocity windows (30s, 5min, 1hr)
FastAPI scoring service correctly implements the feature fallback concept with used_fallback flag
concerns: card_id-only Cassandra partition key will cause hot partition writes at scale for high-velocity BIN ranges
60-second checkpoint interval allows up to 60 seconds of feature state loss on recovery, corrupting experiment validity
blocking_issues: Cassandra hot partition causing >50ms p95 scoring latency SLA breach on high-velocity card BINs
recommendations: Change Cassandra partition key to composite (card_id, feature_window) to distribute write load across cluster ring
Reduce Flink checkpoint interval to 10-15 seconds with incremental RocksDB checkpoints
Add feature freshness timestamp to scoring requests and reject stale lookups with defined staleness threshold

issue_updates.title: Cassandra hot partition under high card-velocity write load
issue_updates.detail: Write latency measured at 120ms+ for high-velocity BIN ranges during load test with simulated peak transaction volume

issue_updates.title: Feature freshness gap after Flink checkpoint recovery
issue_updates.detail: 60s checkpoint interval causes up to 60s feature state gap on restart; fallback rate not yet instrumented
