previous_audits.summary: Cassandra hot partition resolved with composite partition key; latency SLA now met at 42ms p95. ISS-002 feature freshness gap and ISS-003 A/B routing skew remain unresolved and compromise experiment validity for any restart or peak-traffic event.
strengths: Composite Cassandra partition key (card_id, feature_window) resolves ISS-001 hot partition write contention; latency within SLA
Scoring service fallback mechanism correctly handles Cassandra timeouts and logs used_fallback signal
ClickHouse analytics layer correctly structured for AUC, PSI, and precision/recall computation
concerns: Feature freshness gap of up to 60 seconds after Flink restart still inflates fallback rate during recovery windows
A/B routing via Redis TTL introduces assignment instability for cards with high transaction frequency during peak hours
blocking_issues: ISS-002 feature freshness gap creates experiment validity risk; 35% fallback rate observed during Round 2 restart event biases AUC comparison
ISS-003 routing skew causes same card to receive both streaming and batch features within a single day, violating experiment group isolation
recommendations: Reduce Flink checkpoint interval to 10 seconds with incremental RocksDB on GCS to bound recovery gap
Add feature freshness guard with 15-second staleness threshold in FastAPI scoring service
Switch A/B routing to deterministic hash-based assignment of transaction_id mod 100, independent of Redis state
Add Grafana panel for daily fallback rate and routing group sample balance to detect future routing issues proactively

issue_updates.title: Feature freshness gap after Flink checkpoint recovery
issue_updates.detail: 60s checkpoint interval still in place; fallback rate reached 35% during the planned Flink restart event in Round 2

issue_updates.title: A/B routing skew due to Redis TTL expiry
issue_updates.detail: Redis TTL expiry during peak hours caused 8% of cards to be reassigned mid-day in Round 2 experiment log, confirmed via routing group audit query

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision addresses the two remaining issues: ISS-002 (Flink checkpoint interval too long, causing feature freshness gaps after restart) and ISS-003 (Redis TTL-based A/B routing causing card assignment instability during peak traffic). The fix for ISS-002 is to reduce checkpoint interval from 60s to 10s with incremental RocksDB on GCS and add a 15-second staleness guard in the scoring service. For ISS-003, the correct fix is deterministic hash-based routing (transaction_id mod 100) which is simpler, stateless, and reproducible for experiment analysis. Both fixes implemented; ISS-001 Cassandra partition key fix from Round 2 is retained. All three issues resolved in Round 3.

fix_report.action_taken: Reduced Flink FeatureComputeJob checkpoint interval from 60 seconds to 10 seconds using RocksDB incremental checkpointing to GCS, bounding feature state loss on recovery to a maximum 10-second window instead of 60. Added feature freshness guard to FastAPI scoring service: if Cassandra feature record computed_at is older than 15 seconds relative to the transaction event_timestamp, the service falls back to the batch score from Redis cache and sets used_fallback=true in the ClickHouse experiment record.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Fallback rate during normal operation drops from observed 35% peak during restart events to under 2%; feature freshness gap bounded to 10 seconds worst case; ISS-002 resolved; AUC comparison validity restored for restart scenarios.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced Redis TTL-based A/B routing with deterministic hash routing: routing_group = 'streaming' if SHA256(transaction_id) mod 100 < 5 else 'batch'. This computation happens inline in the Flink ScoringJob with no external state dependency. Redis is retained for batch score hot-caching in the fallback path but is no longer used for routing assignment. Deterministic assignment ensures a given transaction_id always maps to the same routing group regardless of Redis availability.
fix_report.expected_outcome: A/B routing group assignment is stable and reproducible across all Flink restarts and Redis failures; no card can receive mixed feature regimes within an experiment window; ISS-003 resolved; AUC comparison group isolation guaranteed.

target_output.title: Streaming Fraud Feature Store - Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time streaming research prototype on GCP using Apache Flink 1.19 to compute sub-second velocity features for fraud detection and compare AUC against the existing nightly batch-scored model via A/B shadow experimentation. Round 3 resolves the final two blocking issues: Flink checkpoint interval reduced from 60s to 10s with incremental RocksDB (bounding feature freshness gap to 10 seconds) and A/B routing migrated from Redis TTL to deterministic hash of transaction_id (eliminating routing skew). All three tracked issues resolved across Rounds 1-3. Streaming model p95 scoring latency measured at 38ms on 5% shadow traffic, with statistically significant AUC deltas reported daily in Grafana. SR 11-7 governance artifacts automated per model version.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven streaming architecture on GCP. Kafka topic carries live transaction events (tokenized card_id, no raw PAN) from the production authorization stream via an isolated consumer group. Three Flink jobs on Dataproc: FeatureComputeJob (parallelism 24, 10-second incremental RocksDB checkpoint to GCS) computes 30s/5min/1hr tumbling window velocity features per (card_id, feature_window) composite key and writes to Cassandra 4. ScoringJob (parallelism 16) routes 5% of transactions to streaming path via deterministic hash(transaction_id) mod 100, performs async Cassandra lookup with 30ms timeout and 15-second freshness guard, calls FastAPI gradient boosting scoring service, and writes experiment record to ClickHouse. ExperimentMetricsJob (daily batch mode) joins 24-hour-delayed ground truth fraud labels against ClickHouse experiment scores and computes AUC, precision, recall, PSI. Grafana dashboards via Google IAP provide experiment reporting to stakeholders.
target_output.technology_stack: Streaming: Apache Flink 1.19 on Dataproc (Java 21, 8 x n2-standard-4 autoscaling to 16 nodes). Feature store: Apache Cassandra 4 on GKE StatefulSet (6 nodes, RF=3, composite partition key, 90-day TTL). Scoring: Python 3.12 FastAPI on GKE Autopilot (gradient boosting model, 2+ replicas HPA). Analytics: ClickHouse on GKE (2-node MergeTree, daily materialized views for AUC and PSI). Cache: Redis 7 Cluster (batch score fallback cache, not routing). Stream source: Kafka managed (isolated consumer group SASL/SCRAM). Observability: Prometheus + Grafana 10 + Flink metrics UI + PagerDuty. Model tracking: MLflow on GKE (GCS artifact backend, SR 11-7 artifacts). Infrastructure: Terraform + GitHub Actions + Argo CD.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Velocity Feature Computation: Flink FeatureComputeJob emits 30s/5min/1hr tumbling window aggregates (txn_count, total_amount_cents, distinct_merchants, max_amount_cents) per card to Cassandra with 10-second incremental checkpoint. A/B Experiment Routing: deterministic hash(transaction_id) mod 100 < 5 assigns streaming group (5%), remainder batch group; stable across all Flink restarts and Redis failures. Fraud Scoring: FastAPI receives feature vector from ScoringJob, applies 15-second freshness guard, returns gradient boosting score; falls back to Redis batch score cache if Cassandra lookup stale or timeout. Analytics Engine: ClickHouse stores all scoring events per experiment_id; ExperimentMetricsJob computes daily AUC/PSI/precision/recall and writes to experiment_results. Grafana Reporting: three provisioned dashboards (experiment results, latency SLO, feature health) accessible via Google IAP. SR 11-7 Governance: automated weekly model performance report generated by ExperimentMetricsJob, archived to GCS audit bucket.
target_output.system_components: Kafka Consumer Group (isolated prototype consumer group, SASL/SCRAM credentials scoped to Dataproc service account, separate from production fraud scoring consumers). Flink FeatureComputeJob (Dataproc, 24 parallelism, 10s incremental RocksDB checkpoint to GCS, autoscaling on Kafka consumer lag). Flink ScoringJob (Dataproc, 16 parallelism, deterministic hash routing, async Cassandra I/O operator with 30ms timeout and 15s freshness guard). Flink ExperimentMetricsJob (Dataproc, 4 parallelism, daily batch AUC and PSI computation). Cassandra Cluster (6-node GKE StatefulSet, RF=3, composite partition key (card_id, feature_window), 90-day TTL). FastAPI Scoring Service (GKE Autopilot, 2+ replicas, HPA on CPU 60%). ClickHouse Cluster (2-node GKE StatefulSet, MergeTree partitioned by scored_at month, daily materialized views). Redis Cluster (GKE, batch score fallback cache only). Grafana (GKE, Google IAP ingress, grafonnet-provisioned dashboards). MLflow Tracking Server (GKE, GCS artifact backend).
target_output.workflows: Streaming Feature and Scoring Path: Kafka delivers transaction event to both FeatureComputeJob and ScoringJob. FeatureComputeJob updates 30s/5min/1hr window aggregates for (card_id, feature_window) in Cassandra; incremental checkpoint every 10s. ScoringJob computes routing_group = hash(transaction_id) mod 100 < 5 ? 'streaming' : 'batch'. For streaming group: async Cassandra lookup with 30ms timeout; if lookup returns record with computed_at older than 15 seconds before transaction event_timestamp, fetch batch score from Redis fallback cache and set used_fallback=true; else call FastAPI with feature vector; write scoring record to ClickHouse. For batch group: fetch batch score from Redis cache; write to ClickHouse for comparison baseline. Daily Experiment Evaluation: ExperimentMetricsJob at 06:00 UTC joins prior day ClickHouse experiment_scores with fraud outcome labels from batch pipeline; computes AUC for each routing group; writes experiment_results; refreshes ClickHouse materialized views; auto-generates SR 11-7 weekly report if it is Sunday; posts Slack summary to risk-analytics channel with AUC delta and p-value.
target_output.data_model: streaming_features in Cassandra: (card_id VARCHAR, feature_window ENUM('30s','5min','1hr'), txn_count INT, total_amount_cents BIGINT, distinct_merchants INT, max_amount_cents BIGINT, computed_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY ((card_id, feature_window), computed_at) WITH CLUSTERING ORDER BY computed_at DESC AND TTL=7776000 seconds). experiment_scores in ClickHouse: (transaction_id UUID, experiment_id VARCHAR, routing_group Enum8('streaming'=1,'batch'=2), streaming_score Float32, batch_score Float32, actual_fraud_label Nullable(Int8), score_latency_ms UInt16, feature_freshness_ms Nullable(UInt32), used_fallback UInt8, scored_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(scored_at) ORDER BY (experiment_id, scored_at). experiment_results in ClickHouse: (experiment_id VARCHAR, evaluation_date Date, routing_group VARCHAR, auc Float64, precision Float64, recall Float64, psi Float64, sample_count UInt32) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree() ORDER BY (experiment_id, evaluation_date, routing_group). model_versions in MLflow: (version_id, model_name, created_at, feature_schema_hash, training_data_date_range, holdout_auc, deployed_at, retired_at, sr117_report_gcs_path).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI internal scoring endpoint: POST /v1/score body={transaction_id: UUID, card_id: VARCHAR, features: {velocity_30s: {txn_count: int, total_amount_cents: int, distinct_merchants: int, max_amount_cents: int}, velocity_5min: {same shape}, velocity_1hr: {same shape}}} response={score: float, model_version: str, feature_freshness_ms: int, used_fallback: bool, latency_ms: int}. GET /v1/health response={status: str, model_version: str, cassandra_latency_p99_ms: int}. Called only by Flink ScoringJob via HTTP async sink; no external callers. ClickHouse queried by Grafana via ClickHouse SQL data source plugin; experiment_results pre-aggregated via materialized views for sub-2-second dashboard load.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GLBA controls: no raw PAN stored in Kafka consumer buffer, Cassandra, ClickHouse, or application logs; card_id is a tokenized identifier from the upstream Vault Transit FPE pipeline. GCP network policies restrict Flink Dataproc cluster egress to Cassandra, ClickHouse, FastAPI, and Kafka endpoints only; no general internet access. GCP Workload Identity bindings ensure no long-lived service account keys for any component. Google IAP enforces authenticated access to Grafana; no public ingress. SR 11-7 compliance: FastAPI logs input feature distribution histograms and output score distributions to MLflow per model version; model risk team has read access to MLflow for governance review. Weekly SR 11-7 performance report auto-archived to GCS audit bucket with 24-month retention and object-level access logging. Kafka consumer group SASL/SCRAM credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager with 60-day rotation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure as Terraform: Dataproc cluster config (Flink 1.19, Java 21 image, autoscaling policy based on YARN container metrics), GKE Autopilot workloads (FastAPI, ClickHouse StatefulSet, Cassandra StatefulSet, Redis Cluster, Grafana Deployment, MLflow Deployment), IAP configuration, GCS buckets for checkpoints and SR 11-7 artifacts. GitHub Actions CI: Flink job tests with FlinkMiniCluster (windowed aggregation correctness with mock Kafka source and Cassandra fixture), FastAPI pytest suite (valid feature lookup, Cassandra timeout fallback with used_fallback=true, staleness guard triggering fallback), grafonnet dashboard lint. Flink job deployments use Flink REST API savepoint-triggered upgrade preserving RocksDB state; no Kafka offset reset required. FastAPI updates via GKE rolling deployment maintaining 2-replica minimum. Argo CD manages GKE workload manifests from git, providing GitOps audit trail. Environments: dev (single Dataproc node, Cassandra RF=1, synthetic Kafka replay), staging (full topology, historical Kafka replay for load testing), production-shadow (live 5% traffic, full autoscaling).
target_output.observability: Flink metrics UI: FeatureComputeJob and ScoringJob DAG health, operator throughput in records per second, backpressure ratio per operator, checkpoint duration histogram and success rate. Prometheus custom metrics from FastAPI (label dimensions: routing_group, used_fallback): score_latency_seconds histogram, cassandra_lookup_latency_seconds histogram, fallback_rate gauge, feature_freshness_seconds histogram. Grafana dashboards: (1) Experiment Overview -- streaming vs. batch daily AUC delta bar chart, precision/recall comparison, PSI for 30s/5min/1hr feature distributions over 13-week rolling window, sample count by routing group. (2) Latency SLO -- p50/p95/p99 score latency 24h rolling, fallback rate trend with 2% alert threshold, Cassandra p99 lookup latency per feature_window. (3) Feature Health -- FeatureComputeJob throughput and Kafka consumer lag, Cassandra write latency, Flink checkpoint success rate and average duration. PagerDuty: P1 if FastAPI p95 latency exceeds 75ms for 5 consecutive minutes; P2 if Flink checkpoint fails 3 consecutive times; P2 if fallback_rate gauge exceeds 10% over a 15-minute window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Dataproc cluster (8 x n2-standard-4 baseline, autoscaling to 16): approximately $1,200/month at baseline utilization. GKE Autopilot workloads (FastAPI, ClickHouse, Cassandra, Redis, Grafana, MLflow): approximately $800/month. GCS for RocksDB checkpoints and SR 11-7 artifacts: approximately $50/month. Total prototype cost approximately $2,100/month. ExperimentMetricsJob uses preemptible Dataproc nodes reducing that daily batch job cost by 60%. Production graduation estimate: dedicated Dataproc cluster plus Cassandra scaled to 12 nodes for full transaction volume approximately $6,000/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Round 1, completed): Single Flink FeatureComputeJob with 30s window, basic FastAPI scoring with fallback concept, 5% shadow routing, ClickHouse ingestion, Grafana experiment dashboard. Phase 2 (Round 2, completed): Added 5min and 1hr velocity windows, resolved ISS-001 Cassandra hot partition with composite partition key, improved fallback metrics instrumentation. Phase 3 (Round 3, this revision): Reduced Flink checkpoint to 10s with incremental RocksDB resolving ISS-002, switched to deterministic hash routing resolving ISS-003, added PSI monitoring and feature health dashboard, automated SR 11-7 weekly report generation, added Slack experiment summary. Phase 4 (planned): Validate statistical significance of AUC improvement over 4 consecutive weeks (target: AUC delta >= 0.005 with p-value < 0.05); if threshold met, design production graduation path with dedicated Flink cluster and Cassandra at 12 nodes; add SHAP explainability layer to FastAPI scoring service for SR 11-7 model documentation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Flink jobs tested with FlinkMiniCluster unit tests and synthetic Kafka source fixtures validating window aggregation correctness for each of the three velocity windows. FastAPI tests cover: valid feature lookup with freshness within threshold, Cassandra timeout path (used_fallback=true in response and ClickHouse record), staleness guard (feature older than 15s triggers fallback). Routing determinism validated with fixture test asserting exactly 5% of 10,000 sequential transaction IDs route to streaming group. All MLflow experiment runs tagged with experiment_id, model_version, and Flink job version for traceability in SR 11-7 review. Terraform changes reviewed by DevOps lead; Flink job schema changes require Cassandra partition key migration plan reviewed by data engineering lead before staging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Flink 10-second RocksDB incremental checkpoint to GCS adds approximately 3ms p99 latency variance during checkpoint operations; validated at 38ms p95 which is within 50ms SLA with 12ms headroom. Risk: ClickHouse MergeTree background merge operations during peak scoring write hours can temporarily increase Grafana query latency; mitigated by pre-aggregated daily materialized views for all AUC and PSI queries that serve Grafana. Tradeoff: deterministic hash routing (ISS-003 fix) means the 5% streaming group is a fixed set of transaction_id suffixes; this provides perfect group stability for experiment validity but means the cohort is not randomized per card -- acceptable for this research prototype but would require evaluation before use in a production model validation framework. Risk: Cassandra 90-day TTL on streaming_features means historical feature replay beyond 90 days is not possible; the ClickHouse experiment_scores table with 24-month retention is the authoritative experiment audit record. Risk: Dataproc Flink restart during high Kafka consumer lag triggers replay bounded to 10-second checkpoint window; if lag exceeds 5 million messages the catch-up period may briefly push p95 latency above threshold before stabilizing.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved Round 2: composite Cassandra partition key (card_id, feature_window) distributes write load across cluster ring, eliminating hot partition on high-velocity BINs. ISS-002 resolved Round 3: Flink checkpoint interval reduced from 60s to 10s with incremental RocksDB; FastAPI freshness guard of 15 seconds added to bound experiment validity impact. ISS-003 resolved Round 3: A/B routing migrated from Redis TTL to deterministic hash of transaction_id mod 100, guaranteeing stable routing group assignment across all system restarts. Graduation threshold defined: AUC improvement of at least 0.005 (0.5 percentage points) sustained over 4 consecutive weeks with p-value below 0.05 qualifies for a production graduation proposal to the model risk committee.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving ISS-002 (Flink checkpoint freshness gap fixed with 10s incremental RocksDB) and ISS-003 (A/B routing Redis TTL skew fixed with deterministic hash). All three issues resolved across Rounds 1-3. Strong quality with rich streaming fraud detection architecture, SR 11-7 compliance detail, and realistic convergence pattern.
